And remember the favour of Allah upon you, when you were enemies and He joined your hearts together, so by His favour you became brothers. And you were on the edge of a pit of Fire, and He saved you from it. Thus Allah makes His signs clear to you, so that you may be guided.
And let there arise from among you a community that calls to what is good, commands what is right, and forbids what is wrong. And those are the ones who are successful.
And do not be like those who became divided and differed after clear proofs had come to them. And for those there is a great punishment.

On the Day when some faces will turn white and some faces will turn black. As for those whose faces turn black, “Did you disbelieve after your belief? Then taste the punishment for what you used to disbelieve.”
But as for those whose faces turn white, they will be in the mercy of Allah, remaining in it forever.
These are the signs of Allah which We recite to you in truth. And Allah does not intend injustice for the worlds.
T o Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And to Allah all matters are returned.

You are the best community brought forth for humanity. You command what is right, forbid what is wrong, and believe in Allah. If the People of the Book had believed, it would have been better for them. Among them are believers, but most of them are rebellious.
They will not harm you except with minor annoyance. And if they fight you, they will turn their backs, then they will not be helped.

Humiliation has been placed upon them wherever they are found, except with a bond from Allah and a bond from people. And they have returned with anger from Allah, and poverty has been laid upon them. That is because they rejected the signs of Allah and ki lled the prophets without right. That is because they disobeyed and kept transgressing.
They are not all the same. Among the People of the Book is a community standing upright, reciting the signs of Allah during the hours of the night, and they prostrate.
They believe in Allah and the Last Day, command what is right, forbid what is wrong, and hasten to good deeds. And those are among the righteous.
And whatever good they do will never be denied to them. And Allah knows the mindful well.
Indeed, those who disbelieve, neither their wealth nor their children will benefit them in any way against Allah. Those are the companions of the Fire. They will remain in it forever.

The example of what they spend in this worldly life is like a wind containing icy cold that strikes the harvest of a people who wronged themselves and destroys it. Allah did not wrong them, but they were wronging themselves.

O you who believe, do not take as close confidants those outside your ranks. They will spare no effort to cause you harm. They wish for your hardship. Hatred has already appeared from their mouths, and what their hearts conceal is greater. We have made the signs clear to you, if you use reason.

Here you are, you love them, but they do not love you, while you believe in the Book, all of it. When they meet you, they say, “We believe,” but when they are alone, they bite their fingertips at you in rage. Say, “Die in your rage.” Indeed, Allah knows what is within the hearts.

If good touches you, it distresses them. And if harm touches you, they rejoice in it. But if you are patient and mindful, their plotting will not harm you in any way. Indeed, Allah encompasses what they do.
And remember when you set out early from your household to assign the believers to their positions for battle. And Allah is All -Hearing, All - Knowing.
When two groups among you were about to lose heart, while Allah was their protector. And upon Allah let the believers rely.
And Allah had already given you victory at Badr, while you were weak. So be mindful of Allah, so that you may be grateful.
When you said to the believers, “Is it not enough for you that your Lord should support you with three thousand angels sent down?”
Y es, if you are patient and mindful, and they come upon you suddenly, your Lord will support you with five thousand marked angels.
And Allah did not make it except as good news for you, and so that your hearts may be reassured by it. And victory is only from Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise,
so that He may cut off a portion of those who disbelieve  or suppress them, so that they turn back disappointed.
You have no part in the decision, whether He turns to them in mercy or punishes them, for they are wrongdoers.
And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. He forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
O you who believe, do not consume usury, doubled and multiplied, and be mindful of Allah, so that you may succeed.
And guard yourselves against the Fire which has been prepared for those who disbelieve.
And obey Allah and the messenger, so that you may be shown mercy.
And hasten towards forgiveness from your Lord and a Garden as wide as the heavens and the earth, prepared for the mindful,
those who spend in ease and hardship, restrain anger, and pardon people. And Allah loves those who do good.

And those who, when they commit an indecency or wrong themselves, remember Allah and seek forgiveness for their sins, and who can forgive sins except Allah? And they do not persist knowingly in what they have done.
Those, their reward is forgiveness from their Lord and gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever. And how excellent is the reward of those who act.
Many ways of life have passed on before you. So travel through the land and see how was the end of those who denied.
This is a clear statement for people, and guidance and instruction for the mindful.
Do not weaken or grieve, for you will be superior if you are believers.

If a wound has touched you, a similar wound has touched the people. And these days We alternate among people, so that Allah may know those who believe and take witnesses from among you. And Allah does not love the wrongdoers.
And so that Allah may purify those who believe and erase those who disbelieve.
Or did you think that you would enter the Garden while Allah had not yet made evident those among you who strive, and made evident the patient?
You had certainly wished for death before you met it, but now you have seen it while you were looking on.

And Muhammad is only a messenger. Messengers have passed on before him. So if he were to die or be killed, would you turn back on your heels? And whoever turns back on their heels will never harm Allah in any way. And Allah will reward the grateful.

And no soul can die except by permission of Allah, at an appointed time. And whoever desires the reward of this world, We give him from it. And whoever desires the reward of the Hereafter, We give him from it. And We will reward the grateful.
And how many a prophet fought, with many devoted followers with him. They did not weaken because of what struck them in the way of Allah, nor did they falter or submit. And Allah loves the patient.
And their words were nothing but that they said, “Our Lord, forgive us our sins and our excesses in our affairs, make our steps firm, and give us victory over the disbelieving people.”
So Allah gave them the reward of this world and the excellent reward of the Hereafter. And Allah loves those who do good.
O you who believe, if you obey those who disbelieve, they will turn you back on your heels, and you will return as losers.
Rather, Allah is your protector, and He is the best of helpers.

We will cast fear into the hearts of those who disbelieve, because they associated others with Allah for which He sent down no authority. Their refuge will be the Fire, and how miserable is the resting place of the wrongdoers.

Allah had already fulfilled His promise to you when you were striking them down by His permission, until you lost courage, disputed about the command, and disobeyed after He had shown you what you love. Among you are those who desire this world, and among you are those who desire the Hereafter. Then He turned you away from them in order to test you, but He has already pardoned you. And Allah is full of bounty towards the believers.

When you were climbing without looking back at anyone, while the messenger was calling you from behind, He repaid you with distress upon distress, so that you would not grieve over what escaped you nor over what struck you. And Allah is fully aware of what you do.

Then after distress, He sent down upon you security, a drowsiness that overtook a group of you. Another group was worried about themselves, thinking wrongly about Allah, the thought of ignorance. They said, “Do we have any part in the matter?” Say, “The m atter belongs entirely to Allah.” They conceal within themselves what they do not reveal to you. They say, “If we had any part in the matter, we would not have been killed here.” Say, “Even if you had remained in your homes, those for whom killing was decreed would have gone out to their places of death.” And so Allah may test what is in your breasts and purify what is in your hearts. And Allah knows what is within the hearts.

Indeed, those of you who turned back on the day the two groups met, Shayṭān caused them to slip because of some of what they had earned. But Allah has pardoned them. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Most-Forbearing.

O you who believe, do not be like those who disbelieve and say about their brothers, when they travel through the land or go out to fight, “If they had been with us, they would not have died or been killed,” so that Allah may make it a regret in their hearts. And Allah gives life and causes death. And Allah sees all that you do.
And if you are killed in the way of Allah or die, then forgiveness from Allah and mercy are better than what they accumulate.
And whether you die or are killed, it is to Allah that you will be gathered.

It is by mercy from Allah that you were gentle with them. And if you had been harsh and hard -hearted, they would have dispersed from around you. So pardon them, seek forgiveness for them, and consult them in the matter. Then when you decide, place your trust in Allah. Indeed, Allah loves those who place their trust in Him.
If Allah supports you, none can overcome you. And if He forsakes you, who is there who can support you after Him? And upon Allah let the believers rely.

It is not for a prophet to act dishonestly. And whoever acts dishonestly will come with what they took on the Day of Resurrection. Then every soul will be repaid in full for what it earned, and they will not be wronged.
Is one who follows the pleasure of Allah like one who brings upon himself anger from Allah, whose refuge is Hell, and how miserable is the destination?
They are in degrees with Allah. And Allah sees all that they do.

Allah has certainly shown favour to the believers when He sent among them a messenger from themselves, reciting His signs to them, purifying them, and teaching them the Book and wisdom, even though before that they were in clear error.
When a disaster struck you, though you had struck twice as much, you said, “From where is this?” Say, “It is from yourselves.” Indeed, Allah has power over everything.
And what struck you on the day the two groups met was by permission of Allah, so that He might make evident the believers,

and make evident those who were hypocritical. And it was said to them, “Come, fight in the way of Allah, or defend.” They said, “If we knew how to fight, we would follow you.” They were nearer to disbelief that day than to belief. They say with their mouths what is not in their hearts. And Allah knows best what they conceal.
Those who said about their brothers, while they sat back, “If they had obeyed us, they would not have been killed.” Say, “Then avert death from yourselves, if you are truthful.”
Do not think that those who were killed in the way of Allah are dead. Rather, they are alive with their Lord, provided for,
rejoicing in what Allah has given them of His bounty, and rejoicing for those who have not yet joined them, from behind them, that there will be no fear upon them, nor will they grieve.
They rejoice in a blessing from Allah and favour, and that Allah does not allow the reward of the believers to be lost.
Those who responded to Allah and the messenger after the wound had touched them. For those who did good and were mindful among them is a great reward.

Those to whom people said, “The people have gathered against you, so fear them,” but it only increased them in faith, and they said, “ Allah is sufficient for us, and He is the best disposer of affairs.”
So they returned with a favour from Allah and bounty, no harm having touched them. They followed the pleasure of Allah. And Allah is Possessor of great bounty.
It is only Shayṭān who frightens his allies. So do not fear them, but fear Me, if you are believers.
And do not let those who rush into disbelief grieve you. They will never harm Allah in any way. Allah intends not to give them any share in the Hereafter, and for them is a great punishment.
Indeed, those who have purchased disbelief in exchange for belief will never harm Allah in any way, and for them is a painful punishment.
And do not let those who disbelieve  think that Our granting them respite is good for them. We only grant them respite so that they may increase in sin. And for them is a humiliating punishment.

Allah would never leave the believers in the state you are in until He separates the bad from the good. And Allah would never reveal the unseen to you, but Allah chooses of His messengers whom He wills. So believe in Allah and His messengers. And if you b elieve and are mindful, then for you is a great reward.

And let not those who withhold what Allah has given them of His bounty think that it is good for them. Rather, it is bad for them. What they withheld will be hung around their necks on the Day of Resurrection. And to Allah belongs the inheritance of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is fully aware of what you do.

Allah has certainly heard the statement of those who said, “ Allah is poor, and we are rich.” We will record what they said and their killing of the prophets without right, and We will say, “T aste the punishment of the burning Fire.”
That is because of what your hands have sent ahead, and because Allah is never unjust to the servants.

Those who said, “ Allah has instructed us not to believe in any messenger until he brings us an offering consumed by fire.” Say, “Messengers before me came to you with clear proofs and with what you mentioned, so why did you kill them, if you are truthful?”
Then if they deny you, messengers before you were also denied, who came with clear proofs, scriptures, and the enlightening Book.

Every soul will taste death. And you will only be given your full rewards on the Day of Resurrection. So whoever is kept away from the Fire and admitted into the Garden has indeed succeeded. And the life of this world is nothing but the enjoyment of delusion.

You will surely be tested in your wealth and yourselves, and you will surely hear much harm from those who were given the Book before you and from those who associate others with Allah. But if you are patient and mindful, then that is from the matters of firm resolve.

And remember when Allah took a covenant from those who were given the Book, “You must make it clear to people and not conceal it.” But they threw it behind their backs and traded it for a small price. Miserable is what they purchased.
Do not think that those who rejoice in what they have done and love to be praised for what they did not do will escape the punishment. For them is a painful punishment.
And to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. And Allah has power over everything.
Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the alternation of night and day, are signs for people of understanding,

Those who remember Allah while standing, sitting, and lying on their sides, and who reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth, saying, “Our Lord, You did not create this without purpose. Glory be to You, so protect us from the punishment of the Fire.”
“Our Lord, whoever You admit into the Fire, You have surely disgraced him. And for the wrongdoers there are no helpers.”
“Our Lord, we have heard a caller calling to faith, saying, ‘Believe in your Lord,’ and we have believed. Our Lord, forgive us our sins, wipe away our wrongs, and cause us to die among the virtuous.”
“Our Lord, grant us what You promised us through Your messengers, and do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed, You do not break Your promise.”

So their Lord responded to them, “I will never allow the work of any worker among you to be lost, male or female. You are from one another. So those who migrated, were driven out of their homes, were harmed in My way, fought, and were killed, I will surel y remove their wrongs from them and admit them into gardens beneath which rivers flow, as a reward from Allah.” And with Allah is the best reward.
Do not let the movement of those who disbelieve  through the land deceive you.
It is only a brief enjoyment, then their refuge is Hell, and how miserable is the resting place.
But those who are mindful of their Lord, for them are gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever, as a hospitality from Allah. And what is with Allah is best for the righteous.

And among the People of the Book are those who believe in Allah and in what has been sent down to you and what was sent down to them, humbling themselves before Allah. They do not trade the signs of Allah for a small price. For them is their reward with their Lord. Indeed, Allah is swift in reckoning.
O you who believe, be patient, urge one another to patience, remain steadfast, and be mindful of Allah, so that you may succeed.

surahs.name: An-Nisāʾ (Women)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: O people, be mindful of your Lord, who created you from a single soul, created from it its mate, and spread from them countless men and women. Be mindful of Allah, in whose name you ask one another, and of the wombs. Indeed, Allah is ever watchful over you.
Give the orphans their property, and do not exchange what is bad for what is good, and do not consume their wealth by adding it to your own. That would be a grave sin.

If you fear that you will not deal justly with the orphans, then marry women of your choice, two, three, or four. But if you fear that you will not be just, then marry only one, or those your right hands possess. That is closer to preventing injustice.
Give women their bridal gifts graciously. But if they willingly give up something of it for you, then enjoy it with ease and contentment.
Do not give the weak -minded your property which Allah has made a means of support for you, but provide for them from it, clothe them, and speak to them with kindness.

T est the orphans until they reach the age of marriage. If you find sound judgement in them, then hand over their property to them, and do not consume it wastefully or hastily before they grow up. Whoever is well -off should refrain, and whoever is poor may take what is reasonable. When you hand over their property to them, bring witnesses over them. And Allah is sufficient as a Reckoner.
For men there is a share of what parents and close relatives leave, and for women there is a share of what parents and close relatives leave, whether little or much, a fixed share.
When relatives, orphans, and the needy are present at the division, provide for them from it and speak to them kindly.
Let those fear for orphans as they would fear for their own helpless children if they were to leave them behind. So let them be mindful of Allah and speak words of justice.
Those who consume the property of orphans unjustly are only consuming fire into their bellies, and they will burn in a blazing fire.

Allah instructs you concerning your children, for the male is a share equal to that of two females. If there are only daughters, two or more, then for them is two thirds of what is left. If there is only one, then for her is half. For the parents, each one receives a sixth if the deceased has a child. If the deceased has no child and the parents inherit alone, then for the mother is a third. If the deceased has siblings, then for the mother is a sixth, after any will that was made or debt. Your parents and your children, you do not know which of them is nearer to you in benefit. This is an obligation from Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

For you is half of what your wives leave if they have no child. But if they have a child, then for you is a quarter of what they leave, after any will that was made or debt. And for them is a quarter of what you leave if you have no child. But if you have  a child, then for them is an eighth of what you leave, after any will that was made or debt. If a man or woman leaves neither parents nor children, but has a brother or a sister, then for each one of them is a sixth. If they are more than that, then they share a third, after any will that was made or debt, without harm. This is a command from Allah. And Allah is All - Knowing, Most-Forbearing.
These are the limits set by Allah. Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, He will admit them into gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever. That is the great success.
And whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger and oversteps His limits, He will admit them into a Fire, remaining in it forever. And for them is a humiliating punishment.
As for those women who commit indecency, call four witnesses from among you against them. If they testify, then confine them to the houses until death takes them or Allah makes a way for them.
And the two among you who commit it, punish them both. But if they repent and set things right, then turn away from them. Indeed, Allah is Ever- Accepting of repentance, Ever-Merciful.
Acceptance of repentance is only for those who commit wrongdoing out of ignorance, then repent soon after. For such people, Allah accepts their repentance. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

But repentance is not for those who persist in wrongdoing until, when death comes to one of them, they say, “Now I repent,” nor for those who die while disbelieving. For them We have prepared a painful punishment.

O you who believe, it is not lawful for you to inherit women by force. And do not constrain them in order to take back part of what you gave them, unless they commit a clear indecency. Live with them honourably. If you dislike them, then it may be that yo u dislike something while Allah places much good in it.
And if you wish to replace one wife with another, and you have given one of them a great amount, do not take anything from it. W ould you take it by slander and a clear sin?
And how could you take it back when you have gone into one another and they have taken from you a firm covenant?
And do not marry women whom your fathers married, except what has already passed. That was indeed a shameful act, hateful, and an evil path.

Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your paternal aunts, your maternal aunts, your brothers’ daughters, your sisters’ daughters, your foster mothers who nursed you, your foster sisters, your wives’ mothers, your stepdaughters under your care born of your wives with whom you have consummated marriage, but if you have not consummated marriage with them then there is no blame upon you, the wives of your sons who are from your own loins, and that you take two sisters together, e xcept what has already passed. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

And forbidden to you are married women, except those whom your right hands possess. This is the decree of Allah upon you. Lawful for you beyond these are those you seek with your wealth, desiring chastity, not immorality. So for whatever you enjoy from them, give them their due reward as an obligation. And there is no blame upon you for what you mutually agree upon beyond the obligation. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

Whoever among you cannot afford to marry free believing women, then marry believing women from those whom your right hands possess. Allah knows best about your faith, you are from one another. Marry them with the permission of their families, and give the m their bridal gifts honourably, as chaste women, not openly immoral nor taking secret lovers. But if, after marriage, they commit indecency, then for them is half the punishment of free women. That is for those among you who fear falling into hardship. But to be patient is better for you. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
Allah intends to make matters clear to you, guide you to the ways of those before you, and accept your repentance. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
Allah wants to accept your repentance, but those who follow their desires want you to deviate greatly.
Allah wants to lighten your burden, for the human being was created weak.
O you who believe, do not consume one another’s wealth unjustly, except through trade by mutual consent. And do not kill yourselves. Indeed, Allah is Ever-Merciful to you.
And whoever does that in aggression and wrongdoing, We will cast him into a Fire. And that is easy for Allah.
If you avoid the major sins which you are forbidden, We will wipe away your lesser sins and admit you into a noble entrance.

And do not wish for what Allah has given some of you over others. For men is a share of what they earn, and for women is a share of what they earn. And ask Allah of His bounty. Indeed, Allah has full knowledge of everything.

For everyone, We have appointed heirs to what parents and close relatives leave. And to those with whom you have made binding pledges, give them their share. Indeed, Allah is a witness over everything.

Men are caretakers of women by what Allah has given some over others and by what they spend of their wealth. So righteous women are devoted and guard what Allah has entrusted, in the absence of their husbands. As for those from whom you fear defiance, adv ise them, then leave them alone in bed, then discipline them. But if they obey you, seek no means against them. Indeed, Allah is Most-High, Most-Great.

If you fear a split between them, appoint an arbiter from his family and an arbiter from her family. If they desire reconciliation, Allah will bring harmony between them. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Aware.

W orship Allah and associate nothing with Him, and show kindness to parents, relatives, orphans, the needy, the near neighbour, the distant neighbour, the companion at your side, the traveller, and those whom your right hands possess. Indeed, Allah does not love the arrogant, the boastful.
Those who are miserly and urge others to miserliness, and conceal what Allah has given them of His bounty. And We have prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment.
And those who spend their wealth to be seen by people, and do not believe in Allah nor the Last Day. Whoever takes Shayṭān as a companion, then what an evil companion he is.
And what would it have cost them if they had believed in Allah and the Last Day, and spent from what Allah has provided them? And Allah is fully aware of them.
Indeed, Allah does not wrong even the weight of an atom. And if there is a good deed, He multiplies it and gives from Himself a great reward.
How will it be when We bring a witness from every nation, and We bring you as a witness over these?
On that Day, those who disbelieved  and disobeyed the Messenger will wish that the earth were levelled over them, and they will not hide from Allah a single word.

O you who believe, do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated until you know what you are saying, nor while in a state of major impurity, unless you are passing through, until you have washed. And if you are ill, or on a journey, or one of you comes from relieving himself, or you have touched women and do not find water, then seek clean earth and wipe your faces and your hands. Indeed, Allah is Ever-Pardoning, Most-Forgiving.
Have you not seen those who were given a portion of the Book, purchasing misguidance and wishing that you would stray from the path?
And Allah knows best your enemies. And Allah is sufficient as a Protector, and Allah is sufficient as a Helper.

Among those who are Jews are some who distort words from their proper places and say, “We hear and disobey,” and “Hear without being made to hear,” and “Listen to us,” twisting with their tongues and attacking the religion. But if they had said, “We hear and obey,” and “Hear,” and “Give us your attention,” it would have been better for them and more upright. But Allah has cursed them for their disbelief, so they do not believe except a little.

O you who were given the Book, believe in what We have sent down, confirming what is with you, before We erase faces and turn them backward or curse them as We cursed the people of the Sabbath. And the command of Allah is always carried out.
Indeed, Allah does not forgive associating partners with Him, but He forgives what is less than that for whom He wills. And whoever associates partners with Allah has surely committed a great sin.
Have you not seen those who declare themselves pure? Rather, Allah purifies whom He wills, and they will not be wronged even by a thread.
Look how they invent lies against Allah. And that alone is enough as a clear sin.
Have you not seen those who were given a portion of the Book, believing in superstition and false gods, and saying to those who disbelieve, “These are better guided than the believers”?
They are the ones whom Allah has cursed. And whoever Allah curses, you will never find for him a helper.
Or do they have a share in authority? If so, they would not give people even a speck.
Or do they envy people for what Allah has given them of His bounty? We gave the family of Ibrāhīm the Book and wisdom, and We gave them a great kingdom.
So among them are those who believe in it, and among them are those who turn away from it. And Hell is sufficient as a blazing fire.

Those who disbelieve Our signs, We will soon cast them into a Fire. Every time their skins are burned away, We will replace them with other skins so they may taste the punishment. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

But those who believe and do righteous deeds, We will admit them into gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever. For them are purified spouses, and We will admit them into deep shade.

Indeed, Allah commands you to return trusts to those entitled to them, and when you judge between people, to judge with justice. How excellent is what Allah instructs you with. Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

O you who believe, obey Allah, and obey the Messenger, and those in authority among you. If you dispute over anything, refer it to Allah and the Messenger, if you truly believe in Allah and the Last Day. That is best and most fitting in outcome.

Have you not seen those who claim that they believe in what was sent down to you and what was sent down before you, yet they seek judgement from false authorities, though they were commanded to reject them? And Shayṭān wants to lead them far astray.
When it is said to them, “Come to what Allah has sent down and to the Messenger,” you see the hypocrites turning away from you in aversion.
Then how will it be when a disaster strikes them because of what their own hands have sent ahead, then they come to you swearing by Allah, “We intended nothing but good and reconciliation”?
Those are the ones Allah knows what is in their hearts. So turn away from them, warn them, and speak to them with words that reach their souls.

We did not send any messenger except to be obeyed by the permission of Allah. And if, when they wronged themselves, they had come to you and sought forgiveness from Allah, and the Messenger had sought forgiveness for them, they would have found Allah Ever -Accepting of repentance, Ever- Merciful.
No, by your Lord, they will not truly believe until they make you judge in all disputes between them, then find no resistance within themselves against what you decide, and submit fully.

If We had decreed for them, “Kill yourselves” or “Leave your homes,” they would not have done it, except a few of them. But if they had done what they were advised, it would have been better for them and stronger in affirmation.
And then We would surely have given them from Us a great reward.
And We would have guided them to a straight path.
And whoever obeys Allah and the Messenger will be with those whom Allah has favoured, the prophets, the truthful, the martyrs, and the righteous. And how excellent are those as companions.
That is the bounty from Allah. And Allah is sufficient as All-Knowing.
O you who believe, take your precautions, then advance in groups or advance all together.
And among you is one who will surely hold back. Then if disaster strikes you, he says, “ Allah has favoured me since I was not present with them.”
But if bounty comes to you from Allah, he will surely say, as though there had been no affection between you and him, “If only I had been with them, I would have attained a great success.”
So let those fight in the cause of Allah who sell the life of this world for the Hereafter. And whoever fights in the cause of Allah and is killed or gains victory, We will grant him a great reward.

And what is the matter with you that you do not fight in the cause of Allah and for the helpless among men, women, and children, who say, “Our Lord, bring us out of this town whose people are oppressors, and appoint for us from You a protector, and appoint for us from You a helper”?
Those who believe fight in the cause of Allah, and those who disbelieve fight in the cause of falsehood. So fight against the allies of Shayṭān. Indeed, the plot of Shayṭān is weak.

Have you not seen those to whom it was said, “Restrain your hands, uphold the prayer, and give zakah”? But when fighting was prescribed for them, a group of them feared people as they should have feared Allah, or even more. And they said, “Our Lord, why have You prescribed fighting for us? If only You had delayed us for a short time.” Say, “The enjoyment of this world is little, and the Hereafter is better for those who are mindful. And you will not be wronged even by a thread.”

Wherever you may be, death will reach you, even if you are in lofty fortified towers. If good comes to them, they say, “This is from Allah.” And if evil comes to them, they say, “This is from you.” Say, “ All is from Allah.” So what is wrong with these peo ple that they can hardly understand any statement?
Whatever good comes to you is from Allah, and whatever evil comes to you is from yourself. And We have sent you as a Messenger to the people. And Allah is sufficient as a Witness.
Whoever obeys the Messenger has obeyed Allah. And whoever turns away, We have not sent you as a guardian over them.

And they say, “Obedience.” But when they leave you, a group of them plan by night something other than what you say. And Allah records what they plan by night, so turn away from them and place your trust in Allah. And Allah is sufficient as a T rustee.
Do they not reflect on the Qur’an? If it were from anyone other than Allah, they would have found in it many contradictions.

When news comes to them of security or fear, they spread it. But if they had referred it to the Messenger and to those in authority among them, those who can draw conclusions from it would have known it. And if not for the grace of Allah and His mercy upo n you, you would have followed Shayṭān, except a few.

So fight in the cause of Allah, you are only accountable for yourself. And encourage the believers. It may be that Allah will restrain the power of those who disbelieve. And Allah is greater in might and severe in retribution.
Whoever intercedes with a good intercession will have a share of it, and whoever intercedes with an evil intercession will have a share of it. And Allah is Ever-Sustainer over all things.
When you are greeted with a greeting, respond with one better than it or return it equally. Indeed, Allah is Ever-Reckoner over everything.
Allah, there is no god except Him. He will surely gather you for the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt. And who is more truthful than Allah in speech?

So why are you divided into two groups concerning the hypocrites, while Allah has turned them back for what they earned? Do you want to guide those whom Allah has let go astray? And whoever Allah lets go astray, you will never find for him a way.

They wish that you would disbelieve as they have disbelieved, so that you would be alike. So do not take them as allies until they migrate in the cause of Allah. But if they turn away, then seize them and kill them wherever you find them, and do not take from them any ally or helper,

except those who join a people with whom you have a treaty, or those who come to you with hearts unwilling to fight you or to fight their own people. If Allah had willed, He could have given them power over you, and they would have fought you. So if they withdraw from you and do not fight you and offer you peace, then Allah has not made for you a way against them.

You will find others who wish to be safe from you and safe from their own people. Whenever they are returned to temptation, they fall back into it. So if they do not withdraw from you, nor offer you peace, nor restrain their hands, then seize them and kill them wherever you encounter them. Against such people, We have given you clear authority.

It is not for a believer to kill a believer except by mistake. And whoever kills a believer by mistake must free a believing slave and pay blood money to the family, unless they forgo it as charity. If the deceased belonged to a hostile people but was a b eliever, then freeing a believing slave is required. If he belonged to a people with whom you have a treaty, then blood money must be paid to the family and a believing slave freed. Whoever cannot find one must fast for two consecutive months as repentance from Allah. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
And whoever kills a believer deliberately, his recompense is Hell, remaining in it forever. Allah is angry with him, has cursed him, and has prepared for him a great punishment.

O you who believe, when you go forth in the cause of Allah, be careful, and do not say to one who offers you peace, “You are not a believer,” seeking the fleeting gains of this world. For with Allah are abundant gains. You yourselves were like that before, then Allah showed you favour. So be careful. Indeed, Allah is fully aware of what you do.

Not equal are the believers who sit back, except those with valid excuse, and those who strive in the cause of Allah with their wealth and their lives. Allah has given those who strive with their wealth and lives a rank above those who sit back. Y et Allah has promised good to all. And Allah has favoured those who strive over those who sit back with a great reward,
ranks from Him, forgiveness, and mercy. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

Indeed, those whom the angels take while wronging themselves, they will say, “In what condition were you?” They will say, “We were oppressed in the land.” They will say, “W as not the earth of Allah spacious enough for you to migrate?” For such people, their refu ge is Hell, and how miserable is the destination,
except the helpless among men, women, and children who cannot devise a plan nor find a way,
so it may be that Allah will pardon them. And Allah is Ever -Pardoning, Most-Forgiving.

And whoever migrates in the cause of Allah will find many places of refuge and abundance in the land. And whoever leaves his home migrating to Allah and His Messenger, then death overtakes him, his reward has already fallen upon Allah. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
And when you travel through the land, there is no blame upon you for shortening the prayer if you fear that those who disbelieve  may harm you. Indeed, those who disbelieve are clear enemies to you.

And when you are among them and lead them in prayer, let a group of them stand with you, taking their arms. When they have prostrated, let them move to the rear, and let another group who have not prayed come and pray with you, taking their precautions and arms. Those who disbelieve wish that you would neglect your arms and belongings so they could launch a single attack against you. But there is no blame upon you if you are harmed by rain or are ill, to lay aside your arms, but take your precautions. Indeed, Allah has prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment.

When you have finished the prayer, remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying on your sides. Then when you are secure, uphold the prayer. Indeed, prayer is prescribed for the believers at fixed times.
And do not weaken in pursuing the enemy. If you suffer, they too suffer as you suffer, but you hope from Allah what they do not hope. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
Indeed, We have sent down the Book to you in truth so that you may judge between people by what Allah has shown you. And do not be an advocate for the treacherous.
And seek forgiveness from Allah. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever- Merciful.
And do not argue on behalf of those who betray themselves. Indeed, Allah does not love one who is treacherous and sinful.
They hide from people, but they cannot hide from Allah. And He is with them when they plan by night what He does not approve of in speech. And Allah encompasses what they do.
Here you are, you argue on their behalf in this worldly life, but who will argue on their behalf with Allah on the Day of Resurrection, or who will be their advocate?
And whoever commits evil or wrongs himself, then seeks forgiveness from Allah, will find Allah Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
And whoever commits a sin only commits it against himself. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
And whoever commits a wrongdoing or a sin, then accuses an innocent person of it, has surely taken upon himself slander and a clear sin.

And if not for the grace of Allah upon you and His mercy, a group of them would have tried to mislead you. But they mislead only themselves, and they cannot harm you at all. Allah has sent down upon you the Book and wisdom, and taught you what you did not  know. And the grace of Allah upon you is immense.

There is no good in much of their private talk, except for one who orders charity, or what is right, or reconciliation between people. And whoever does that seeking the pleasure of Allah, We will give him a great reward.

And whoever opposes the Messenger after guidance has become clear to him, and follows a path other than that of the believers, We will turn him to what he has chosen and burn him in Hell. And how evil is the destination.
Indeed, Allah does not forgive associating partners with Him, but He forgives what is less than that for whom He wills. And whoever associates partners with Allah has surely gone far astray.
They call upon none besides Him except female deities, and they call upon none except a rebellious Shayṭān,
whom Allah has cursed. He said, “I will surely take from Your servants a set share,

and I will surely mislead them, fill them with false hopes, command them so they will slit the ears of cattle, and command them so they will alter the creation of Allah.” And whoever takes Shayṭān as a protector instead of Allah has surely suffered a clear loss.
He promises them and fills them with false hopes. And Shayṭān promises them nothing but deception.
For such people, their refuge is Hell, and they will find from it no escape.

But those who believe and do righteous deeds, We will admit them into gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever. The promise of Allah is true. And who is more truthful than Allah in word?
It is not by your wishes nor by the wishes of the People of the Book. Whoever does evil will be repaid for it, and he will find besides Allah no protector and no helper.
And whoever does righteous deeds, whether male or female, while being a believer, such people will enter Paradise, and they will not be wronged even by a speck.
And who is better in religion than one who submits himself to Allah, while doing good, and follows the way of Ibrāhīm, inclining to truth? And Allah took Ibrāhīm as a close friend.
And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah encompasses everything.

And they ask you concerning women. Say, “ Allah gives you a ruling about them, and what is recited to you in the Book concerning the orphan girls to whom you do not give what is prescribed for them while you desire to marry them, and concerning helpless children, and that you stand firmly for justice with orphans. And whatever good you do, Allah is fully aware of it.”

And if a woman fears ill-treatment or neglect from her husband, there is no blame upon them if they seek reconciliation between themselves. And reconciliation is better. And souls are inclined to greed. But if you do good and are mindful of Allah, then indeed, Allah is fully aware of what you do.

And you will never be able to deal with your wives in perfect fairness, even if you desire it. So do not lean entirely toward one and leave the other suspended. But if you make things right and remain mindful, then indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
But if they separate, Allah will enrich each of them from His abundance. And Allah is All-Encompassing, All-Wise.

And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. We instructed those who were given the Book before you, and you yourselves, to be mindful of Allah. But if you disbelieve, then to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is Free of need, All-Praiseworthy
And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is sufficient as a T rustee.
If He wills, He can remove you, O people, and bring others. And Allah is fully able to do that.
Whoever desires the reward of this world, then with Allah is the reward of this world and the Hereafter. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

O you who believe, stand firmly for justice as witnesses for Allah, even against yourselves, or parents, or close relatives, whether one is rich or poor. Allah is more worthy of both. So do not follow desires lest you deviate. And if you distort or turn away, then indeed, Allah is fully aware of what you do.

O you who believe, believe in Allah, His Messenger, the Book He sent down upon His Messenger, and the Book He sent down before. And whoever disbelieves in Allah, His angels, His books, His messengers, and the Last Day has surely gone far astray.
Indeed, those who believe, then disbelieve, then believe, then disbelieve, then increase in disbelief, Allah will never forgive them nor guide them to a way
Give news to the hypocrites that for them is a painful punishment.
Those who take the disbelievers as allies instead of the believers. Do they seek honour with them? Indeed, all honour belongs to Allah.

And He has already revealed to you in the Book that when you hear the signs of Allah being rejected and mocked, do not sit with them until they engage in another topic. Otherwise, you would be like them. Indeed, Allah will gather the hypocrites and the disbelievers together in Hell.

Those who watch you closely, then if victory comes to you from Allah, they say, “Were we not with you?” But if the disbelievers gain a share, they say, “Did we not gain the upper hand over you and protect you from the believers?” Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection. And Allah will never give the disbelievers a way over the believers.
Indeed, the hypocrites seek to deceive Allah, but He is deceiving them. And when they stand for prayer, they stand lazily, showing themselves to people, and they remember Allah only a little,
wavering between this and that, belonging neither to these nor to those. And whoever Allah lets go astray, you will never find for him a way.
O you who believe, do not take the disbelievers as allies instead of the believers. Do you want to give Allah clear proof against yourselves?
Indeed, the hypocrites will be in the lowest depths of the Fire, and you will never find for them a helper,
except those who repent, set things right, hold firmly to Allah, and devote their religion sincerely to Allah. Those will be with the believers. And Allah will give the believers a great reward.
Why would Allah punish you if you are grateful and believe? And Allah is Ever-Appreciative, All-Knowing.
Allah does not love the public utterance of evil speech, except by one who has been wronged. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
If you show good or conceal it, or pardon an evil, then indeed, Allah is Ever-Pardoning, All-Powerful.

Indeed, those who disbelieve  Allah and His messengers, and wish to make a distinction between Allah and His messengers, saying, “We believe in some and disbelieve in others,” and wish to take a path in between,
Those are the true disbelievers. And We have prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment.
But those who believe in Allah and His messengers and do not make any distinction between them, He will give them their rewards. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

The People of the Book ask you to bring down to them a book from the sky. They already asked Mūsā for something even greater than that, and said, “Show us Allah openly. ” So the thunderbolt struck them for their wrongdoing. Then they took the calf for worship after clear signs had come to them, yet We pardoned that. And We gave Mūsā clear authority.
And We raised the Mount Ṭūr above them for their covenant, and We said to them, “Enter the gate prostrating,” and We said to them, “Do not violate the Sabbath,” and We took from them a firm covenant.

So for their breaking of their covenant, their disbelief in the signs of Allah, their killing of the prophets without right, and their saying, “Our hearts are covered,” rather, Allah sealed them because of their disbelief, so they do not believe except a little,
and for their disbelief and their saying against Maryam a great slander,

and their saying, “We killed the Messiah, ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, the Messenger of Allah.” But they did not kill him, nor did they crucify him, but it was made to appear so to them. And those who differ over it are in doubt about it. They have no knowledge of  it except following assumption. And they certainly did not kill him.
Rather, Allah raised him up to Himself. And Allah is All-Mighty, All- Wise.
And there is none among the People of the Book except that he will surely believe in him before his death. And on the Day of Resurrection, he will be a witness against them.
So for the wrongdoing of those who were Jews, We forbade them good things which had previously been made lawful for them, and for their blocking many people from the way of Allah,
and for their taking of interest while they had been forbidden from it, and for their consuming people’s wealth unjustly. And We have prepared for the disbelievers among them a painful punishment.

But those firmly grounded in knowledge among them, and the believers, believe in what has been sent down to you and what was sent down before you, and those who uphold the prayer, give zakah, and believe in Allah and the Last Day. Those, We will give them a great reward.

Indeed, We have revealed to you as We revealed to Nūḥ and the prophets after him. And We revealed to Ibrāhīm, Ismāʿīl, Isḥāq, Y aʿqūb, and the tribes, and ʿĪsā, Ayyūb, Yūnus, Hārūn, and Sulaymān. And We gave Dāwūd the Zabūr.
And messengers We have already told you about, and messengers We have not told you about. And Allah spoke directly to Mūsā.
Messengers bringing good news and warnings, so that people would have no argument against Allah after the messengers. And Allah is All- Mighty, All-Wise.
But Allah bears witness to what He has sent down to you,  He sent it down with His knowledge. And the angels bear witness, and Allah is sufficient as a Witness.
Indeed, those who disbelieve  and turn people away from the path of Allah have surely gone far astray.
Indeed, those who disbelieve and commit wrongdoing, Allah will never forgive them nor guide them to any path,
except the path of Hell, remaining in it forever. And that is easy for Allah.

O people, the Messenger has come to you with the truth from your Lord, so believe, it is better for you. But if you disbelieve, then to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

O People of the Book, do not go to extremes in your religion, and do not say about Allah anything except the truth. The Messiah  ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, was only a Messenger of Allah, His word which He cast to Maryam, and a spirit from Him. So believe in Allah and His messengers, and do not say, “Three.” Stop, it is better for you. Allah is only One God. Glory be to Him, far exalted above having a son. T o Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is sufficient as a T rustee.
The Messiah will never refuse to be a servant of Allah, nor will the angels who are brought near. And whoever refuses His worship and acts arrogantly, He will gather them all to Himself.

As for those who believe and do righteous deeds, He will give them their rewards in full and increase them from His bounty. But as for those who refuse and act arrogantly, He will punish them with a painful punishment, and they will not find for themselves besides Allah any protector or helper.
O people, a clear proof has come to you from your Lord, and We have sent down to you a clear light.
So those who believe in Allah and hold firmly to Him, He will admit them into mercy from Him and bounty, and guide them to Himself on a straight path.

They ask you for a ruling. Say, “ Allah gives you a ruling concerning the one who leaves no parent or child. If a man dies leaving no child but has a sister, for her is half of what he leaves. And he inherits from her if she has no child. If there are two sisters, for them is two thirds of what he leaves. If there are brothers and sisters, then for the male is a share equal to that of two females.” Allah makes things clear to you so that you do not go astray. And Allah has full knowledge of everything.

surahs.name: Al-Māʾidah (The Table Spread)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: O you who believe, fulfil your obligations. Lawful to you are grazing livestock, except what is recited to you, not permitting hunting while you are in a state of pilgrimage. Indeed, Allah decrees what He wills.

O you who believe, do not violate the symbols of Allah, nor the sacred month, nor the sacrificial animals, nor the garlands, nor those heading to the Sacred House seeking bounty and pleasure from their Lord. But when you come out of pilgrimage, then you m ay hunt. And do not let the hatred of a people for having barred you from the Sacred Mosque lead you to transgress. Cooperate in righteousness and mindfulness, and do not cooperate in sin and aggression. And be mindful of Allah. Indeed, Allah is severe in punishment.

Forbidden to you is carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, and what has been dedicated to other than Allah, and the strangled, the beaten, the fallen, the gored, and what wild animals have eaten, except what you properly slaughter, and what is sacrificed on stone altars, and that you seek division by arrows. That is corruption. T oday those who disbelieve  have despaired of your religion, so do not fear them but fear Me. T oday I have perfected your religion for you, completed My favour upon you, and approved Islām as your religion. But whoever is compelled by hunger without inclination to sin, then indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

They ask you what is lawful for them. Say, “Lawful for you are good things, and what trained animals catch for you, which you train as Allah has taught you. So eat from what they catch for you and mention the name of Allah over it, and be mindful of Allah. Indeed, Allah is swift in reckoning.”

T oday, all good and wholesome things have been made lawful for you. The food of those who were given the Scripture is lawful for you, and your food is lawful for them. Lawful for you are chaste believing women and chaste women from those who were given the Scripture before you, when you give them their bridal gifts, seeking chastity, not committing immorality nor taking secret lovers. Whoever rejects faith, his deeds are brought to nothing, and in the Hereafter he will be among the losers.

O you who believe, when you rise for prayer, wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, wipe your heads, and wash your feet up to the ankles. And if you are in a state of major impurity, then purify yourselves. But if you are ill, or on a journey, o r one of you comes from relieving himself, or you have touched women and do not find water, then seek clean earth and wipe your faces and your hands with it. Allah does not intend to place hardship upon you, but He intends to purify you and complete His favour upon you, so that you may be grateful.
And remember the favour of Allah upon you and His covenant with which He bound you when you said, “We hear and we obey.” And be mindful of Allah. Indeed, Allah knows what is within the hearts.

O you who believe, stand firm for Allah as witnesses in justice. Do not let the hatred of a people drive you away from being just. Be just; that is nearer to mindfulness. And be mindful of Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
Allah has promised those who believe and do righteous deeds forgiveness and a great reward.
But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs, those are the companions of the Fire.

O you who believe, remember the favour of Allah upon you when a people intended to stretch out their hands against you, but He restrained their hands from you. So be mindful of Allah. And upon Allah let the believers place their trust.

And Allah certainly took a covenant from the Children of Isrāʾīl, and We appointed twelve leaders among them. And Allah said, “I am with you. If you uphold the prayer, give zakah, believe in My messengers, support them, and lend Allah a good loan, I will surely remove your wrongs from you and admit you into gardens beneath which rivers flow. But whoever among you disbelieves after that has surely strayed from the straight path.”

So for their breaking of their covenant, We cursed them and made their hearts hard. They distort words from their proper places and forgot a portion of what they were reminded of. And you will continue to discover betrayal from them, except a few of them.  But pardon them and overlook. Indeed, Allah loves those who do good.

And from those who say, “We are Christians,” We took their covenant, but they forgot a portion of what they were reminded of. So We stirred up enmity and hatred among them until the Day of Resurrection. And Allah will inform them of what they used to do.
O People of the Book, Our Messenger has come to you, making clear to you much of what you used to conceal of the Book, and overlooking much. There has come to you from Allah a light and a clear Book,
by which Allah guides those who seek His pleasure to the ways of peace, brings them out of darkness into light by His permission, and guides them to a straight path.

They have surely disbelieved who say, “ Allah is the Messiah, son of Maryam.” Say, “Who then has any power against Allah if He intended to destroy the Messiah, son of Maryam, and his mother, and everyone on the earth?” T o Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth and whatever is between them. He creates what He wills. And Allah has power over everything.

And the Jews and the Christians say, “We are the children of Allah and His beloved.” Say, “Then why does He punish you for your sins?” Rather, you are human beings from among those He created. He forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills. And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth and whatever is between them, and to Him is the final return.

O People of the Book, Our Messenger has come to you, clarifying things for you after a break in messengers, so that you may not say, “No bringer of good news or warner came to us.” So T ruly, a bringer of good news and a warner has come to you. And Allah has power over everything.

And remember when Mūsā said to his people, “O my people, remember the favour of Allah upon you when He placed prophets among you, made you kings, and gave you what He had not given anyone else in the worlds.”
O my people, enter the holy land which Allah has prescribed for you, and do not turn back, or you will become losers.
They said, “O Mūsā, in it are a people of great strength, and we will never enter it until they leave it. If they leave it, then we will enter.”

T wo men from among those who feared Allah, upon whom Allah had bestowed favour, said, “Enter upon them through the gate. If you enter it, you will surely be victorious. And upon Allah place your trust, if you are believers.”
They said, “O Mūsā, we will never enter it as long as they remain in it. So go, you and your Lord, and fight. We are sitting right here.”
He said, “My Lord, I have no power except over myself and my brother, so separate us from the disobedient people.”
He said, “Then it is forbidden to them for forty years. They will wander through the land. So do not grieve over the people who are disobedient.”

And recite to them the story of the two sons of Ādam in truth. When they both offered a sacrifice, it was accepted from one of them and not accepted from the other. He said, “I will surely kill you.” He said, “ Allah only accepts from the mindful.
If you stretch out your hand to kill me, I will not stretch out my hand to kill you. Indeed, I fear Allah, Lord of the worlds.
I want you to bear my sin and your sin, so you will be among the companions of the Fire. And that is the recompense of the wrongdoers.”
Then his soul urged him to kill his brother, so he killed him and became among the losers.

Then Allah sent a crow scratching in the ground to show him how to conceal the body of his brother. He said, “W oe to me. W as I unable to be like this crow and conceal the body of my brother?” So he became among the regretful.

Because of that, We decreed upon the Children of Isrāʾīl that whoever kills a soul, unless for a soul or for corruption in the land, it is as if he has killed all of humanity. And whoever saves one, it is as if he has saved all of humanity. Our messengers came to them with clear proofs, yet even after that many of them continued to commit excesses in the land.

The recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger and strive to spread corruption in the land is only that they be killed, or crucified, or have their hands and feet cut off on opposite sides, or be banished from the land. That is disgr ace for them in this world, and for them in the Hereafter is a great punishment,
except for those who repent before you gain power over them. So know that Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
O you who believe, be mindful of Allah and seek a means of closeness to Him, and strive in His way, so that you may succeed.

Indeed, those who disbelieve, if they had all that is on the earth and the like of it with it to ransom themselves from the punishment of the Day of Resurrection, it would not be accepted from them. And for them is a painful punishment.
They will wish to come out of the Fire, but they will never come out of it. And for them is a lasting punishment.
As for the male thief and the female thief, cut off their hands as a recompense for what they earned, as a deterrent from Allah. And Allah is All- Mighty, All-Wise.
But whoever repents after his wrongdoing and set things right, then indeed, Allah will turn to him in forgiveness. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
Do you not know that to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth? He punishes whom He wills and forgives whom He wills. And Allah has power over everything.

O Messenger, do not be grieved by those who rush into disbelief among those who say, “We believe,” with their mouths while their hearts do not believe, and among the Jews, listeners to falsehood, listeners for another people who have not come to you. They distort words from their proper places, saying, “If you are given this, then take it, but if you are not given it, then beware.” And whoever Allah intends to test, you will never have any power for him against Allah. Those are the ones whose hearts Allah does not intend to purify. For them is disgrace in this world, and for them in the Hereafter is a great punishment.

They are listeners to falsehood, consumers of unlawful gain. So if they come to you, judge between them or turn away from them. And if you turn away from them, they will not harm you at all. And if you judge, then judge between them with justice. Indeed, Allah loves those who are just.
And how do they come to you for judgement while they have the Tawrāh, in which is the judgement of Allah? Then they turn away after that. And those are not believers.

Indeed, We sent down the Tawrāh, in which was guidance and light. The prophets who submitted judged by it for those who were Jews, as did the scholars and rabbis, by what they were entrusted with of the Book of Allah, and they were witnesses to it. So do not fear people, but fear Me, and do not trade My signs for a small price. And whoever does not judge by what Allah has sent down, those are the disbelievers.

And We prescribed for them in it: a life for a life, an eye for an eye, a nose for a nose, an ear for an ear, a tooth for a tooth, and for wounds equal retaliation. But whoever waives it as charity, then it is an expiation for him. And whoever does not ju dge by what Allah has sent down, those are the wrongdoers.

And We sent, following in their footsteps, ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, confirming what came before him of the Tawrāh. And We gave him the Injīl, in which was guidance and light, confirming what came before it of the Tawrāh, and as guidance and instruction for the mindful.
And let the People of the Injīl judge by what Allah has sent down in it. And whoever does not judge by what Allah has sent down, those are the rebellious.

And We have sent down to you the Book in truth, confirming what came before it of the Scripture and guarding over it. So judge between them by what Allah has sent down, and do not follow their desires away from the truth that has come to you. For each of you We have made a law and a path. If Allah had willed, He could have made you one community, but He tests you in what He has given you. So compete in good works. T o Allah is your return all together, and He will inform you of what you used to differ over.

And judge between them by what Allah has sent down, and do not follow their desires, and beware of them lest they turn you away from some of what Allah has sent down to you. But if they turn away, then know that Allah intends to strike them for some of their sins. And indeed, many of the people are rebellious.
Is it the judgement of ignorance that they seek? And who is better than Allah in judgement for a people who are certain?

O you who believe, do not take the Jews and the Christians as allies. They are allies of one another. And whoever among you takes them as allies, then he is surely one of them. Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

So you see those in whose hearts is disease rushing toward them, saying, “We fear that a misfortune may strike us.” But it may be that Allah will bring victory or a command from Him, and they will become regretful over what they concealed within themselves.
And those who believe will say, “ Are these the ones who swore by Allah their strongest oaths that they were with you?” Their deeds have failed, and they have become losers.

O you who believe, whoever among you turns back from his religion, Allah will bring a people He loves and who love Him, humble toward the believers, firm against the disbelievers, striving in the way of Allah, and not fearing the blame of a critic. That is the bounty of Allah, He gives it to whom He wills. And Allah is All-Encompassing, All-Knowing.
Your ally is only Allah, His Messenger, and those who believe, those who uphold the prayer and give zakah while bowing down.
And whoever takes Allah, His Messenger, and those who believe as allies, then indeed the party of Allah are the victorious.

O you who believe, do not take as allies those who take your religion in mockery and play from among those who were given the Book before you and the disbelievers. And be mindful of Allah, if you are believers.
And when you call to prayer, they take it in mockery and play. That is because they are a people who do not reason.
Say, “O People of the Book, do you resent us except that we believe in Allah and what was sent down to us and what was sent down before, and that most of you are rebellious?”

Say, “Shall I inform you of something worse than that in recompense from Allah? Those whom Allah has cursed and with whom He is angry, and from whom He made apes and swine, and those who worship false gods, those are worse in rank and farther astray from the straight path.”
And when they come to you, they say, “We believe,” but they entered with disbelief and they left with it. And Allah knows best what they were concealing.
And you see many of them rushing into sin and aggression and their consuming of unlawful gain. How evil is what they were doing.
Why do the rabbis and scholars not forbid them from sinful speech and consuming unlawful gain? How evil is what they used to do.

And the Jews say, “The hand of Allah is chained.” Their hands are chained, and they are cursed for what they said. Rather, His hands are outspread, He spends how He wills. And what has been sent down to you from your Lord will surely increase many of them in rebellion and disbelief. And We have cast among them enmity and hatred until the Day of Resurrection. Every time they kindle a fire for war, Allah extinguishes it. And they strive for corruption in the land. And Allah does not love the corrupters.
And if the People of the Book had believed and been mindful, We would surely have wiped away their sins and admitted them into gardens of delight.

And if they had upheld the Tawrāh, and the Injīl and what was sent down to them from their Lord, they would have eaten from above them and from beneath their feet. Among them is a balanced community, but many of them, evil is what they do.

O Messenger, convey what has been sent down to you from your Lord. And if you do not, then you have not conveyed His message. And Allah will protect you from the people. Indeed, Allah does not guide the disbelieving people.

Say, “O People of the Book, you have nothing to stand upon until you uphold the Tawrāh, the Injīl, and what has been sent down to you from your Lord.” And what has been sent down to you from your Lord will surely increase many of them in transgression and disbelief. So do not grieve over the disbelieving people.

Indeed, those who believe, those who are Jews, the Sabians, and the Christians, whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day and does righteous deeds, there will be no fear upon them, nor will they grieve.

We certainly took a covenant from the Children of Isrāʾīl and sent messengers to them. Whenever a messenger came to them with what their souls did not desire, a group of them they denied, and another group they killed.

And they thought there would be no trial, so they became blind and deaf. Then Allah turned to them in forgiveness, then many of them again became blind and deaf. And Allah is fully aware of what they do.

They have surely disbelieved who say, “ Allah is al-Masīḥ, son of Maryam.” But al-Masīḥ said, “O Children of Isrāʾīl, worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.” Indeed, whoever associates partners with Allah, then Allah has forbidden Paradise to him, and his refuge is the Fire. The wrongdoers will have no helpers.

They have surely disbelieved who say, “ Allah is the third of three.” There is no god except One God. And if they do not stop what they say, a painful punishment will surely touch those who disbelieve among them.
Will they not turn to Allah in repentance and seek His forgiveness? And Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

The Messiah, son of Maryam, was only a Messenger. Messengers passed away before him. His mother was truthful. They both used to eat food. Look how We make the signs clear to them, then look how they are turned away.
Say, “Do you worship besides Allah what has no power to harm you nor benefit you?” And Allah is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.

Say, “O People of the Book, do not go to extremes in your religion beyond the truth, and do not follow the desires of a people who went astray before, misled many, and strayed from the straight path.”
Those who disbelieved among the Children of Isrāʾīl were cursed by the tongue of Dāwūd and ʿĪsā, son of Maryam. That was because they disobeyed and used to transgress.
They did not forbid one another from the wrongdoing they committed. How evil was what they used to do.
You see many of them taking the disbelievers as allies. How evil is what their souls have put forward for them, that Allah is angry with them, and in punishment they will remain.
And if they had believed in Allah, the Prophet, and what was sent down to him, they would not have taken them as allies. But many of them are rebellious.

You will surely find the most intense in hostility toward the believers to be the Jews, and those who associate partners with Allah. And you will surely find the nearest of them in affection to the believers those who say, “We are Christians.” That is because among them are priests and monks, and because they are not arrogant.

And when they hear what has been sent down to the Messenger, you see their eyes overflowing with tears because of the truth they recognise. They say, “Our Lord, we believe, so record us among the witnesses.
And why should we not believe in Allah and the truth that has come to us, while we hope that our Lord will admit us with the righteous people?”
So Allah rewarded them for what they said with gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever. And that is the reward of those who do good.
But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs, those are the companions of the Fire.
O you who believe, do not forbid the good things Allah has made lawful for you, and do not transgress. Indeed, Allah does not love the transgressors.
And eat from what Allah has provided for you, lawful and good, and be mindful of Allah in whom you believe.

Allah does not hold you accountable for unintentional oaths, but He holds you accountable for oaths you bind intentionally. Its expiation is feeding ten needy people from the average of what you feed your families, or clothing them, or freeing a slave. Wh oever cannot find that must fast three days. That is the expiation for your oaths when you have sworn. And guard your oaths. Thus Allah makes clear His signs to you so that you may be grateful.
O you who believe, intoxicants, gambling, stone altars, and divining arrows are filth from the work of Shayṭān, so avoid them, so that you may succeed.
Shayṭān only wants to stir up enmity and hatred among you through intoxicants and gambling, and to turn you away from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer. So will you not desist?
Obey Allah and obey the Messenger, and be cautious. But if you turn away, then know that upon Our Messenger is only to convey the message clearly.

There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they previously consumed, if they are mindful of Allah, believe, and do righteous deeds, then remain mindful and believe, then remain mindful and do good. And Allah loves those who do good.

O you who believe, Allah will surely test you with some hunted animals within reach of your hands and your spears, so that Allah may know who fears Him unseen. Whoever transgresses after that, for him is a painful punishment.

O you who believe, do not kill hunted animals while you are in a state of pilgrimage. And whoever among you kills it intentionally, the penalty is livestock equal to what he killed, judged by two just men among you, as an offering delivered to the Sacred House, or an expiation of feeding the needy or fasting an equivalent, so that he may taste the consequence of his action. Allah has pardoned what has passed. But whoever returns, Allah will take retribution from him. And Allah is All-Mighty, Possessor of retribution.

Lawful to you is the catch of the sea and its food as provision for you and for travellers. But forbidden to you are hunted animals on land while you are in a state of pilgrimage. And be mindful of Allah, to whom you will be gathered.

Allah has made the Kaʿbah, the Sacred House, a means of stability for the people, and the sacred month, the sacrificial animals, and the garlands. That is so that you may know that Allah knows what is in the heavens and what is on the earth, and that Allah has full knowledge of everything.
Know that Allah is severe in punishment and that Allah is Most- Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
Upon the Messenger is only the delivery of the message, and Allah knows what you reveal and what you conceal.
Say, “The bad and the good are not equal, even if the abundance of the bad impresses you.” So be mindful of Allah, O people of understanding, so that you may succeed.

O you who believe, do not ask about matters which, if made clear to you, would trouble you. But if you ask about them while the Qur’an is being revealed, they will be made clear to you. Allah has pardoned that. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, Most-Forbearing.
A people before you asked about such matters, then they became disbelievers because of them.
Allah has not appointed any such thing as Baḥīrah, Sāʾibah, W aṣīlah, or Ḥām. But those who disbelieve invent lies against Allah, and most of them do not reason.

And when it is said to them, “Come to what Allah has sent down and to the Messenger,” they say, “Enough for us is what we found our fathers upon.” Even if their fathers knew nothing and were not guided?

O you who believe, upon you is responsibility for yourselves. Those who go astray will not harm you when you are guided. T o Allah is your return all together, and He will inform you of what you used to do.

O you who believe, testimony among you when death approaches one of you at the time of making a will is two just men from among you, or two others from outside if you are travelling through the land and disaster strikes you. Detain them after prayer and let them swear by Allah if you doubt, “We will not sell it for any price, even if it were a close relative, nor will we conceal the testimony of Allah, for then we would be among the sinners.”

But if it is discovered that the two committed a sin, then two others from those most entitled to it shall stand in their place and swear by Allah, “Our testimony is truer than their testimony, and we have not transgressed, for then we would be among the wrongdoers.”
