That makes it more likely that they will give testimony in its proper form, or that they will fear that oaths will be taken after their oaths. And be mindful of Allah and listen. And Allah does not guide the rebellious people.
On the Day Allah gathers the messengers and says, “What response were you given?” they will say, “We have no knowledge. Indeed, You are the Knower of the unseen.”

When Allah will say, “O ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, remember My favour upon you and upon your mother when I supported you with the Holy Spirit, you spoke to the people in the cradle and in maturity, and when I taught you the Book, wisdom, the Tawrāh, and the Injīl. And when you shaped from clay the form of a bird by My permission, then you breathed into it and it became a bird by My permission. And you healed the blind and the leper by My permission. And when you brought forth the dead by My permissio n. And when I restrained the Children of Isrāʾīl from you when you came to them with clear proofs, and those who disbelieved among them said, ‘This is nothing but clear magic.’”
And when I inspired the disciples, “Believe in Me and in My Messenger,” they said, “We believe. Bear witness that we are those who submit.”
When the disciples said, “O ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, can your Lord send down to us a table spread from heaven?” He said, “Be mindful of Allah, if you are believers.”
They said, “We wish to eat from it, and to reassure our hearts, and to know that you have spoken truthfully to us, and to be witnesses to it.”

ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, said, “O Allah, our Lord, send down to us a table spread from heaven, to be for us a festival, for the first of us and the last of us, and a sign from You. And provide for us, for You are the best of providers.
Allah said, “I will send it down to you. But whoever among you disbelieves after that, I will punish him with a punishment by which I have not punished anyone among the worlds.”

And when Allah will say, “O ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, did you say to the people, ‘T ake me and my mother as two gods besides Allah’?” He will say, “Glory be to You. It was not for me to say what I had no right to say. If I had said it, You would surely have known it. You know what is within myself, and I do not know what is within Yourself. Indeed, You are the Knower of the unseen.

I did not say to them except what You commanded me, ‘W orship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.’ And I was a witness over them while I remained among them. But when You took me up, You were the W atcher over them. And You are a Witness over everything.
If You punish them, they are Your servants. And if You forgive them, then indeed, You are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.”

Allah will say, “This is the Day when the truthful will benefit from their truthfulness. For them are gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever.” Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. That is the great success.
And to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever is in them. And He has power over everything.

surahs.name: Al-Anʿām (The Cattle)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: All praise belongs to Allah, who created the heavens and the earth, and made darkness and light. Y et those who disbelieve  set up equals with their Lord.
He is the One who created you from clay, then set a fixed term, and a specified term known only to Him. Y et you still doubt.
And He is Allah in the heavens and on the earth. He knows what you conceal and what you reveal, and He knows what you earn.
No sign comes to them from the signs of their Lord except that they turn away from it.
They have certainly denied the truth when it came to them, but the news of what they used to mock will soon come to them.

Have they not seen how many generations We destroyed before them? We had established them in the land more firmly than We have established you, and We sent the sky upon them in abundant rain, and made rivers flow beneath them. Then We destroyed them for their sins and brought forth after them other generations.
Even if We had sent down to you a Book on parchment and they had touched it with their hands, those who disbelieve  would still say, “This is nothing but clear magic.”
And they say, “Why has an angel not been sent down to him?” But if We had sent down an angel, the matter would have been decided, then they would not be given respite.
And if We had made him an angel, We would have made him a man, and We would have confused them just as they confuse themselves.
And messengers before you were certainly mocked, but those who mocked them were surrounded by what they used to ridicule.
Say, “T ravel through the land and observe how the outcome was for those who denied.”

Say, “T o whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth?” Say, “T o Allah.” He has prescribed mercy for Himself. He will surely gather you on the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt. Those who have lost themselves do not believe.
And to Him belongs whatever dwells in the night and the day. And He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.

Say, “Shall I take as an ally other than Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth, who feeds and is not fed?” Say, “I have been commanded to be the first of those who submit, and do not be among the associators.”
Say, “I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous Day.”
Whoever is turned away from it on that Day, He has surely shown him mercy. And that is the clear success.
And if Allah touches you with harm, there is none to remove it except Him. And if He touches you with good, then He has power over everything.
And He is the Subduer above His servants. And He is the All -Wise, the All-Aware.

Say, “What thing is greatest in testimony?” Say, “ Allah is witness between me and you. And this Qur’an has been revealed to me that I may warn you by it and whoever it reaches. Do you truly testify that there are other gods with Allah?” Say, “I do not tes tify.” Say, “He is only One God, and I am free of what you associate.”
Those to whom We gave the Book recognise it as they recognise their own children. Those who have lost themselves do not believe.
And who is more unjust than one who invents a lie against Allah or denies His signs? Indeed, the wrongdoers will not succeed.
And on the Day We gather them all together, then We will say to those who associated partners  with Allah, “Where are your partners whom you used to claim?”
Then their only plea will be, “By Allah, our Lord, we were never among those who associated partners with Him.”
Look how they lied against themselves, and what they used to invent has vanished from them.

And among them are those who listen to you, but We have placed coverings over their hearts so they do not understand it, and heaviness in their ears. And if they were to see every sign, they would not believe in it. Until, when they come to you arguing, those who disbelieve say, “This is nothing but tales of the former people.”
And they prevent others from it and turn away from it. And they only destroy themselves, but they do not realise.
If only you could see when they are made to stand before the Fire and they will say, “If only we could be returned, then we would not deny the signs of our Lord and would be among the believers.”
Rather, what they used to conceal before has become clear to them. And even if they were returned, they would surely return to what they were forbidden. And indeed, they are liars.
And they say, “There is nothing but our worldly life, and we will not be resurrected.”

If only you could see when they are made to stand before their Lord. He will say, “Is this not the truth?” They will say, “Y es, by our Lord.” He will say, “Then taste the punishment for what you used to disbelieve.”

Those have surely lost who deny the meeting with Allah. Until, when the Hour comes upon them suddenly, they will say, “W oe to us for what we neglected concerning it,” while they carry their burdens on their backs. How evil is what they carry.
And the life of this world is nothing but play and distraction. And the Home of the Hereafter is better for those who are mindful. Will you not then understand?
We certainly know that what they say grieves you. But it is not you they deny, rather it is the signs of Allah that the wrongdoers deny.

Messengers before you were certainly denied, but they remained patient over being denied and harmed until Our help came to them. And none can alter the words of Allah. And the news of the messengers has certainly come to you.

And if their turning away is hard upon you, then if you can seek a tunnel into the earth or a ladder into the sky to bring them a sign, then do so. But if Allah had willed, He would have gathered them upon guidance. So do not be among the ignorant.
Only those who listen will respond. As for the dead, Allah will raise them, then to Him they will be returned.
And they say, “Why has a sign not been sent down to him from his Lord?” Say, “ Allah is fully capable of sending down a sign, but most of them do not know.”
There is no creature on the earth nor bird flying with its wings except that they are communities like you. We have not neglected anything in the Book. Then to their Lord they will be gathered.
And those who deny Our signs are deaf and mute, in darkness. Whomever Allah wills, He lets go astray, and whomever He wills, He places on a straight path.
Say, “Do you see, if the punishment of Allah comes to you or the Hour comes to you, will you call upon other than Allah, if you are truthful?”
Rather, it is Him alone you would call upon. Then He would remove what you called upon Him for, if He willed, and you would forget what you associate with Him.
And We certainly sent messengers to nations before you, then We seized them with hardship and adversity so that they might humble themselves.
If only, when Our punishment came to them, they had humbled themselves. But their hearts became hardened, and Shayṭān made what they were doing seem pleasing to them.

Then when they forgot what they were reminded of, We opened for them the gates of everything, until, when they rejoiced in what they were given, We seized them suddenly, and they were left in despair.
So the root of the people who did wrong was cut off. And all praise belongs to Allah, Lord of the worlds.
Say, “Do you see, if Allah were to take away your hearing and your sight and seal your hearts, which god other than Allah could restore them to you?” Look how We turn the signs, then they turn away.
Say, “Do you see, if the punishment of Allah were to come to you suddenly or openly, would any be destroyed except the wrongdoing people?”
We do not send the messengers except as bearers of good news and as warners. So whoever believes and sets things right, there will be no fear upon them, nor will they grieve.
But those who deny Our signs, punishment will touch them for their rebellion.

Say, “I do not say to you that I possess the treasures of Allah, nor do I know the unseen, nor do I say to you that I am an angel. I only follow what is revealed to me.” Say, “ Are the blind and the seeing equal?” Will you not then reflect?
And warn by it those who fear that they will be gathered to their Lord, they will have no protector or intercessor besides Him, so that they may be mindful.

And do not drive away those who call upon their Lord morning and evening, seeking His pleasure. You are not accountable for them in anything, nor are they accountable for you in anything. If you were to drive them away, you would then be among the wrongdoers.
And thus We have tested some of them by others so that they may say, “ Are these the ones Allah has favoured among us?” Does Allah not know best those who are grateful?

And when those who believe in Our signs come to you, say, “Peace be upon you. Your Lord has prescribed mercy for Himself. Whoever among you does wrong out of ignorance, then repents after that and set things right, then indeed, He is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.”
And thus We make clear the signs, so that the path of the criminals may become evident.
Say, “I have been forbidden from worshipping those whom you call upon besides Allah.” Say, “I do not follow your desires, for then I would have gone astray and would not be among the guided.”
Say, “I stand upon clear proof from my Lord, yet you deny it. What you seek to hasten is not in my control. Judgement belongs only to Allah. He declares the truth, and He is the best of judges.”
Say, “If what you seek to hasten were in my control, the matter would have been settled between me and you. And Allah knows best the wrongdoers.”

And with Him are the keys of the unseen, none knows them except Him. And He knows what is on the land and in the sea. Not a leaf falls but He knows it, nor a grain in the darkness of the earth, nor anything fresh or dry, except that it is in a clear record.

And He is the One who takes you by night and knows what you commit by day. Then He raises you up in it so that a fixed term may be fulfilled. Then to Him is your return. Then He will inform you of what you used to do.
And He is the Subduer above His servants, and He sends guardians over you. Then when death comes to one of you, Our messengers take him, and they do not fall short.
Then they are returned to Allah, their true Master. Surely, judgement belongs to Him, and He is the swiftest in reckoning.
Say, “Who rescues you from the darkness of the land and the sea when you call upon Him humbly and secretly, ‘If You save us from this, we will surely be among the grateful’?”
Say, “ Allah rescues you from it and from every distress, yet you still associate partners with Him.”

Say, “He is able to send punishment upon you from above you or from beneath your feet, or to divide you into factions and make you taste the violence of one another.” Look how We turn the signs, so that they may understand.
But your people have denied it, while it is the truth. Say, “I am not a guardian over you.”
For every message there is a fixed time, and you will come to know.

And when you see those who engage in mocking Our signs, turn away from them until they move on to another topic. And if Shayṭān causes you to forget, then do not sit after remembering with the wrongdoing people.
Those who are mindful are not accountable for them in any way, but only to remind, so that they may become mindful of Allah.

And leave those who take their religion as play and amusement, and  are deceived by the worldly life. And remind by it, lest a soul be delivered to destruction for what it has earned. It will have no protector nor intercessor besides Allah. And if it offers every ransom, it will not be accepted. Those are the ones who are delivered to destruction for what they earned. For them is a drink of boiling water and a painful punishment because they used to reject.

Say, “Shall we call upon besides Allah what can neither benefit us nor harm us, and be turned back on our heels after Allah has guided us, like one whom Shayṭān has lured on the earth, confused, while he has companions calling him to guidance, ‘Come to us’?” Say, “Indeed, the guidance of Allah is the guidance, and we have been commanded to submit to the Lord of the worlds,
and to uphold the prayer and be mindful of Him. And He is the One to whom you will be gathered.”

And He is the One who created the heavens and the earth in truth. And the Day He says, “Be,” and it is, His word is the truth. And to Him belongs the dominion on the Day the trumpet is blown. He is the Knower of the unseen and the seen. And He is the All-Wise, the All-Aware.
And when Ibrāhīm said to his father Āzar, “Do you take idols as gods? Indeed, I see you and your people in clear error.”
And thus We showed Ibrāhīm the dominion of the heavens and the earth so that he might be among those who are certain.
So when the night covered him, he saw a star and said, “This is my Lord.” But when it set, he said, “I do not love those that set.”
Then when he saw the moon rising, he said, “This is my Lord.” But when it set, he said, “If my Lord does not guide me, I will surely be among the misguided people.”
Then when he saw the sun rising, he said, “This is my Lord, this is greater.” But when it set, he said, “O my people, I am free of what you associate.
I have turned my face toward the One who created the heavens and the earth, inclining to truth, and I am not among those who associate partners.”

And his people argued with him. He said, “Do you argue with me about Allah while He has guided me? And I do not fear what you associate with Him unless my Lord wills something. My Lord encompasses everything in knowledge. Will you not then remember?”

And how should I fear what you associate, while you do not fear associating with Allah that for which He has sent down no authority? So which of the two parties has more right to security, if you know?
Those who believe and do not mix their belief with wrongdoing, for them is security, and they are rightly guided.
And that was Our argument which We gave to Ibrāhīm against his people. We raise in degrees whom We will. Indeed, your Lord is All-Wise, All- Knowing.

And We granted him Isḥāq and Y aʿqūb, and We guided each of them. And Nūḥ We guided before, and among his descendants Dāwūd, Sulaymān, Ayyūb, Yūsuf, Mūsā, and Hārūn. And thus We reward those who do good.
And Zakariyyā, Yaḥyā, ʿĪsā, and Ilyās, all were among the righteous.
And Ismāʿīl, al-Y asaʿ, Yūnus, and Lūṭ. And all of them We favoured above the worlds.
And from their fathers, their descendants, and their brothers. And We chose them and guided them to a straight path.
That is the guidance of Allah by which He guides whom He wills among His servants. If they had associated partners with Him, all they used to do would have come to nothing.
Those are the ones to whom We gave the Book, judgement, and prophethood. But if these disbelieve in it, then We have entrusted it to a people who do not disbelieve in it.
Those are the ones whom Allah has guided, so follow their guidance. Say, “I ask of you no reward for it. It is only a reminder for the worlds.”

And they did not honour Allah with the honour due to Him when they said, “ Allah has not sent down anything to any human being.” Say, “Who sent down the Book that Mūsā brought as a light and guidance for people, which you make into sheets, revealing some of it and concealing much, and you were taught what neither you nor your fathers knew?” Say, “ Allah.” Then leave them in their false talk, playing.

And this is a blessed Book We have sent down, confirming what was before it, so that you may warn the Mother of Cities and those around it. Those who believe in the Hereafter believe in it, and they guard their prayers.

And who is more unjust than one who invents a lie against Allah or says, “It has been revealed to me,” while nothing has been revealed to him, or who says, “I will send down the like of what Allah has sent down”? And if only you could see when the wrongdoers are in the throes of death and the angels stretch out their hands, saying, “Give up your souls. T oday you will be repaid with the punishment of humiliation for what you used to say against Allah without truth and for being arrogant toward His signs.”

And you have certainly come to Us alone, just as We created you the first time, and you have left behind what We had granted you. And We do not see with you your intercessors whom you claimed were partners among you. indeed, the bonds between you have been severed, and what you used to claim has vanished from you.
Indeed, Allah is the Splitter of the grain and the seed. He brings the living out of the dead and brings the dead out of the living. That is Allah, so how are you turned away?
He is the Splitter of the dawn, and He has made the night for rest, and the sun and the moon for calculation. That is the decree of the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.
And He is the One who made the stars for you, so that you may be guided by them through the darkness of the land and the sea. We have made the signs clear for a people who know.
And He is the One who produced you from a single soul, then a place of settlement and a place of storage. We have made the signs clear for a people who understand.

And He is the One who sends down water from the sky. Then We bring forth with it growth of every kind, then We bring out from it greenery from which We bring forth clustered grain. And from the palm trees, from their spathes, are hanging clusters, and gar dens of grapes, olives, and pomegranates, similar yet different. Look at their fruit when it bears fruit and when it ripens. Indeed, in that are signs for a people who believe.

And they have made  the jinn partners with Allah, though He created them, and they have falsely attributed to Him sons and daughters without knowledge. Glory be to Him. He is far exalted above what they describe.
The Originator of the heavens and the earth. How could He have a child when He has no companion? He created everything, and He has full knowledge of everything.
That is Allah, your Lord. There is no god except Him, the Creator of everything, so worship Him. And He is a Guardian over everything.
Vision does not grasp Him, but He grasps all vision. And He is the All- Subtle, the All-Aware.
Clear insights have come to you from your Lord. Whoever sees, it is for his own benefit, and whoever is blind, it is against himself. And I am not a keeper over you.
And thus We vary the signs, so that they may say, “You have studied,” and so that We may make it clear for a people who know.
Follow what has been revealed to you from your Lord. There is no god except Him. And turn away from those who associate partners with Him.
And if Allah had willed, they would not have associated partners with Him. We have not made you a guardian over them, nor are you responsible for them.

And do not insult those whom they call upon besides Allah, lest they insult Allah in hostility without knowledge. Thus We have made appealing to every community their deeds. Then to their Lord is their return, and He will inform them of what they used to do.

And they swear by Allah their strongest oaths that if a sign came to them, they would surely believe in it. Say, “Signs are only with Allah.” And what will make you realise that if it came, they would not believe?
And We turn their hearts and their sights away, just as they did not believe in it the first time, and We leave them wandering blindly in their transgression.

Even if We had sent down to them the angels, and the dead had spoken to them, and We had gathered everything before them face to face, they would not have believed unless Allah willed. But most of them are ignorant.

And thus We have appointed for every prophet enemies, devils from humankind and jinn, inspiring one another with embellished speech as deception. But if your Lord had willed, they would not have done it. So leave them and what they invent.
So that the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter may incline to it, be pleased with it, and commit what they are committing.

So shall I seek a judge other than Allah, when it is He who has sent down to you the Book, clearly explained? And those to whom We gave the Book know that it has been sent down from your Lord in truth. So do not be among the doubters.
And the word of your Lord has been completed in truth and justice. None can change His words. And He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
And if you obey most of those on the earth, they will lead you astray from the way of Allah. They follow nothing but assumption, and they are only guessing.
Indeed, your Lord knows best who strays from His way, and He knows best those who are guided.
So eat from that over which the name of Allah has been mentioned, if you truly believe in His signs.

And what is the matter with you that you do not eat from that over which the name of Allah has been mentioned, when He has already made clear to you what He has forbidden to you, except what you are compelled to? And indeed, many lead others astray by their desires without knowledge. Indeed, your Lord knows best the transgressors.
And leave what is apparent of sin and what is hidden. Indeed, those who earn sin will be repaid for what they used to commit.

And do not eat from that over which the name of Allah has not been mentioned, for it is disobedience. And indeed, the devils inspire their allies to argue with you. And if you obey them, you would surely be associators.

Is one who was dead and We gave him life and made for him a light by which he walks among the people like one who is in darkness, never to come out of it? Thus it has been made appealing to the disbelievers what they used to do.
And thus We have placed in every town its greatest criminals to plot within it. But they plot only against themselves, and they do not realise.

And when a sign comes to them, they say, “We will not believe until we are given the like of what was given to the messengers of Allah.” Allah knows best where to place His message. Those who commit crimes will be struck by humiliation from Allah and a severe punishment for what they used to plot.

And whoever Allah intends to guide, He opens his chest to Islām. And whoever He intends to let go astray, He makes his chest tight and constricted, as though he were climbing into the sky. Thus Allah places defilement upon those who do not believe.
And this is the straight path of your Lord. We have made the signs clear for a people who remember.
For them is the Home of Peace with their Lord. And He is their Protector because of what they used to do.

And on the Day He gathers them all together, “O assembly of jinn, you have taken much from humankind.” And their allies among humankind will say, “Our Lord, some of us benefited from others, and we have reached our appointed term which You appointed for us.” He will say, “The Fire is your refuge, remaining in it forever, except what Allah wills.” Indeed, your Lord is All-Wise, All-Knowing.
And thus We make some of the wrongdoers allies of others because of what they used to earn.

O assembly of jinn and humankind, did messengers not come to you from among you, relating to you My signs and warning you of the meeting of this Day of yours?” They will say, “We bear witness against ourselves.” And the life of this world deceived them, and they bore witness against themselves that they were disbelievers.
That is because your Lord would not destroy the towns unjustly while their people were unaware.
And for all are degrees according to what they have done. And your Lord is not unaware of what they do.
And your Lord is Free of need, Possessor of Mercy. If He wills, He can remove you and replace you with whom He wills, just as He brought you forth from the descendants of another people.
Indeed, what you are promised will surely come, and you cannot prevent it.
Say, “O my people, act according to your position. Indeed, I am acting. Soon you will know who will have the best outcome in the Hereafter. Indeed, the wrongdoers will not succeed.”

And they assign to Allah a portion of what He has produced of crops and livestock, saying, “This is for Allah,” according to their claim, “and this is for our partners.” But what is for their partners does not reach Allah, while what is for Allah reaches their partners. How evil is what they judge.

And likewise, their partners made appealing to many of the associators the killing of their children, to ruin them and confuse their religion for them. And if Allah had willed, they would not have done it. So leave them and what they invent.

And they say, “These livestock and crops are forbidden. None may eat from them except whom we will,” according to their claim. And livestock whose backs are forbidden, and livestock over which they do not mention the name of Allah, inventing lies against Him. He will repay them for what they used to invent.

And they say, “What is in the wombs of these livestock is exclusive for our males and forbidden to our females.” But if it is born dead, then they all share in it. He will repay them for their description. Indeed, He is All -Wise, All-Knowing.
Those have surely lost who killed their children foolishly without knowledge, and forbade what Allah had provided for them, inventing lies against Allah. They have gone astray and were not guided.

And He is the One who produces gardens, trellised and untrellised, and date palms, crops of diverse yield, olives and pomegranates, similar yet different. Eat from their fruit when they bear fruit, and give its due on the day of harvest. And do not be wasteful. Indeed, He does not love the wasteful.
And of the livestock are carriers of burdens and those for slaughter. Eat from what Allah has provided for you, and do not follow the footsteps of Shayṭān. Indeed, he is to you a clear enemy.
Eight pairs, of sheep two and of goats two. Say, “Has He forbidden the two males, or the two females, or what the wombs of the two females contain? Inform me with knowledge, if you are truthful.”

And of camels two and of cattle two. Say, “Has He forbidden the two males, or the two females, or what the wombs of the two females contain? Or were you witnesses when Allah commanded you this?” So who is more unjust than one who invents a lie against All ah to mislead people without knowledge? Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

Say, “I do not find in what has been revealed to me anything forbidden for one who eats it, except carrion, flowing blood, the flesh of swine, for it is impure, or what has been dedicated to other than Allah, for it is rebellious. But whoever is compelled by necessity, without desire nor transgression, then indeed, your Lord is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.”

And to those who were Jews, We forbade every animal with claws. And of cattle and sheep, We forbade to them their fat, except what their backs carry, or the entrails, or what is mixed with bone. That is how We repaid them for their wrongdoing. And indeed, We are truthful.
So if they deny you, say, “Your Lord possesses vast mercy, but His punishment will not be turned back from the criminal people.”

Those who associate partners will say, “If Allah had willed, we would not have associated partners, nor would our fathers, nor would we have forbidden anything.” Likewise, those before them denied until they tasted Our punishment. Say, “Do you have any knowledge that you can bring forth for us? You follow nothing but assumption, and you are only guessing.”
Say, “T o Allah belongs the decisive proof. If He had willed, He would have guided you all.”

Say, “Bring your witnesses who testify that Allah has forbidden this.” If they testify, do not testify with them. And do not follow the desires of those who deny Our signs, and those who do not believe in the Hereafter, and they set up equals with their Lord.

Say, “Come, I will recite what your Lord has forbidden to you: that you associate nothing with Him, and show kindness to parents, and do not kill your children out of poverty, We provide for you and for them, and do not approach indecencies, what is apparent of them and what is hidden, and do not kill the soul which Allah has made sacred except by right. That He has instructed you with, so that you may understand.”

“ And do not approach the property of the orphan except in the best manner until he reaches maturity. And give full measure and weight with justice. We do not burden any soul except to its capacity. And when you speak, be just, even if it concerns a close relative. And fulfil the covenant of Allah. That He has instructed you with, so that you may remember.”
“ And this is My straight path, so follow it. And do not follow other paths, for they will separate you from His path. That He has instructed you with, so that you may be mindful of Allah.”

Then We gave Mūsā the Book, completing favour upon the one who did good, and as a detailed explanation of everything, and as guidance and mercy, so that they may believe in the meeting with their Lord.
And this is a Book We have sent down, blessed, so follow it and be mindful of Allah, so that you may receive mercy.
So that you may not say, “The Book was only sent down to two groups before us, and we were unaware of their study,”

or say, “If the Book had been sent down to us, we would have been better guided than them.” So there has now come to you clear proof from your Lord, and guidance and mercy. So who is more unjust than one who denies the signs of Allah and turns away from them? We will repay those who turn away from Our signs with the worst punishment for what they used to turn away.

Are they waiting except for the angels to come to them, or for your Lord to come, or for some of the signs of your Lord to come? On the Day when some of the signs of your Lord come, belief will not benefit a soul that did not believe before or earn good t hrough its faith. Say, “W ait. Indeed, we are waiting.”
Indeed, those who divided their religion and became sects, you have nothing to do with them. Their matter is only with Allah. Then He will inform them of what they used to do.
Whoever comes with a good deed will have ten times its like, and whoever comes with an evil deed will not be repaid except with its like. And they will not be wronged.
Say, “Indeed, my Lord has guided me to a straight path, an upright religion, the way of Ibrāhīm, inclining to truth. And he was not among those who associate partners.”
Say, “Indeed, my prayer, my sacrifice, my living, and my dying are for Allah, Lord of the worlds.
He has no partner. And with that I have been commanded, and I am the first of those who submit.”

Say, “Shall I seek a lord other than Allah, while He is the Lord of everything? And no soul earns except against itself. And no bearer of burdens bears the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you of what you used to differ over.”

And He is the One who made you successors on the earth and raised some of you above others in degrees, to test you in what He has given you. Indeed, your Lord is swift in punishment, and indeed, He is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

surahs.name: Al-Aʿrāf (The Heights)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Mīm Ṣād.
This is a Book sent down to you, so let there be no tightness in your chest because of it, so that you may warn by it, and as a reminder for the believers.
Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, and do not follow allies besides Him. Little do you remember.
And how many towns We destroyed. Our punishment came upon them by night or while they were resting at midday.
Their only cry when Our punishment came upon them was that they said, “Indeed, we were wrongdoers.”
So We will surely question those to whom messengers were sent, and We will surely question the messengers.
Then We will surely relate to them with knowledge. And We were never absent.
And the weighing on that Day is true. So those whose scales are heavy, they are the successful.
And those whose scales are light, those are the ones who have lost themselves because they used to wrong Our signs.
And We have certainly established you upon the earth and placed for you within it means of living. Little are you grateful.
And We created you, then shaped you, then said to the angels, “Prostrate to Ādam.” So they prostrated, except Iblīs. He was not among those who prostrated.
He said, “What prevented you from prostrating when I commanded you?” He said, “I am better than him. You created me from fire, and You created him from clay.”
He said, “Then descend from it. It is not for you to be arrogant here. So get out. You are among the disgraced.”
He said, “Grant me respite until the Day they are resurrected.”
He said, “You are among those given respite.”
He said, “Because You have let me go astray, I will surely lie in wait for them on Your straight path.
Then I will come to them from before them and from behind them, from their right and from their left, and You will find most of them ungrateful.”
He said, “Get out from it, disgraced and expelled. Whoever among them follows you, I will surely fill Hell with you all.”
And O Ādam, dwell, you and your wife, in the Garden, and eat from wherever you wish, but do not approach this tree, or you will be among the wrongdoers.

Then Shayṭān whispered to them so that he might uncover to them what had been concealed from them of their shame. And he said, “Your Lord has only forbidden you this tree so that you do not become angels or become among those who live forever.”
And he swore to them, “Indeed, I am to you a sincere adviser.”

So he led them on by deception. And when they tasted of the tree, their shame became apparent to them, and they began to cover themselves with leaves of the Garden. And their Lord called out to them, “Did I not forbid you from that tree and say to you that Shayṭān is a clear enemy to you?”
They said, “Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves. And if You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we will surely be among the losers.”
He said, “Descend, some of you enemies to others. And for you on the earth is a dwelling place and provision for a time.”
He said, “In it you will live, and in it you will die, and from it you will be brought forth.”
O children of Ādam, We have sent down to you clothing to cover your shame and as adornment. But the garment of mindfulness, that is best. That is from the signs of Allah, so that they may remember.

O children of Ādam, let not Shayṭān tempt you as he removed your parents from the Garden, stripping them of their clothing to show them their shame. Indeed, he and his kind see you from where you do not see them. We have made the devils allies of those who do not believe.
And when they commit an indecency, they say, “We found our fathers doing it, and Allah commanded us to do it.” Say, “ Allah does not command indecency. Do you say about Allah what you do not know?”
Say, “My Lord has commanded justice. And set your faces toward Him at every place of worship, and call upon Him sincerely devoted to Him in religion. Just as He originated you, so you will return.”
A group He has guided, and a group has deserved misguidance. They took devils as allies instead of Allah, and they think that they are guided.
O children of Ādam, take your adornment at every place of worship, eat and drink, but do not be excessive. Indeed, He does not love those who are excessive.

Say, “Who has forbidden the adornment of Allah which He has brought forth for His servants, and the good provisions?” Say, “They are for those who believe in the life of this world, exclusively theirs on the Day of Resurrection.” Thus We make the signs clear for a people who know.

Say, “My Lord has only forbidden indecencies, what is apparent of them and what is hidden, and sin, and oppression without right, and that you associate with Allah that for which He has sent down no authority, and that you say about Allah what you do not know.”
And for every community there is a fixed term. When their term comes, they cannot delay it for an hour, nor can they bring it forward.
O children of Ādam, if messengers come to you from among you, relating My signs to you, then whoever is mindful of Allah and set things right, there will be no fear upon them, nor will they grieve.
But those who deny Our signs and are arrogant toward them, those are the companions of the Fire, remaining in it forever.

And who is more unjust than one who invents a lie against Allah or denies His signs? Their share will reach them from the Book until, when Our messengers come to take them in death, they will say, “Where are those you used to call upon besides Allah?” The y will say, “They have vanished from us,” and they will bear witness against themselves that they were disbelievers.

He will say, “Enter among communities of jinn and humankind that have passed before you into the Fire.” Every time a community enters, it curses its sister, until when they have all joined one another in it, the last of them will say about the first of th em, “Our Lord, these led us astray, so give them a double punishment of the Fire.” He will say, “For each is double, but you do not know.”
And the first of them will say to the last of them, “You had no advantage over us, so taste the punishment for what you used to earn.”

Indeed, those who deny Our signs and are arrogant toward them, the gates of heaven will not be opened for them, nor will they enter the Garden until the camel passes through the eye of the needle. Thus We repay the criminals.
For them are beds from Hell, and above them coverings. Thus We repay the wrongdoers.
But those who believe and do righteous deeds, We do not burden any soul beyond its capacity, those are the companions of the Garden, remaining in it forever.

And We will remove whatever resentment is in their chests. Rivers will flow beneath them, and they will say, “ All praise belongs to Allah, who guided us to this. We would never have been guided had Allah not guided us. The messengers of our Lord certainly came with the truth.” And they will be called, “This is the Garden which you have been made to inherit for what you used to do.”

And the companions of the Garden will call out to the companions of the Fire, “We have found what our Lord promised us to be true, have you found what your Lord promised to be true?” They will say, “Y es.” Then an announcer will proclaim among them, “The c urse of Allah is upon the wrongdoers,
those who turn people away from the path of Allah and seek to make it distorted, while they disbelieve in the Hereafter.”

And between them will be a barrier, and upon the heights are men who recognise everyone by their marks. And they will call out to the companions of the Garden, “Peace be upon you.” They will not have entered it, though they hope.
And when their eyes are turned toward the companions of the Fire, they will say, “Our Lord, do not place us with the wrongdoing people.”
And the people of the heights will call out to men whom they recognise by their marks, saying, “Your gathering and your arrogance have not benefited you.
Are these the ones you swore Allah would never grant mercy?” “Enter the Garden. There will be no fear upon you, nor will you grieve.”

And the companions of the Fire will call out to the companions of the Garden, “Pour upon us some water or some of what Allah has provided you.” They will say, “Indeed, Allah has forbidden both to the disbelievers,
those who took their religion as play and amusement, and whom the worldly life deceived. So today We will forget them as they forgot the meeting of this Day of theirs and for denying Our signs.
And We certainly brought them a Book which We explained with knowledge, as guidance and mercy for a people who believe.

Are they waiting except for its outcome? On the Day its outcome comes, those who forgot it before will say, “The messengers of our Lord certainly came with the truth. So are there any intercessors who can intercede for us, or can we be sent back so that w e may do other than what we used to do?” They have certainly lost themselves, and what they used to invent has vanished from them.

Indeed, your Lord is Allah, who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then established Himself over the Throne. He covers the night with the day, chasing it swiftly, and the sun, the moon, and the stars are subjected by His command. Surely, to Him belongs creation and command. Blessed is Allah, Lord of the worlds.
Call upon your Lord humbly and privately. Indeed, He does not love the transgressors.
And do not cause corruption on the earth after it has been set right. And call upon Him in fear and hope. Indeed, the mercy of Allah is near to those who do good.

And He is the One who sends the winds as good news before His mercy, until, when they carry heavy clouds, We drive them to a dead land and send down rain upon it, bringing forth with it fruits of every kind. Thus We bring forth the dead, so that you may remember.
And the good land brings forth its vegetation by the permission of its Lord. But that which is bad brings forth nothing except sparsely. Thus We turn the signs for a people who are grateful.
We certainly sent Nūḥ to his people. He said, “O my people, worship Allah, you have no god other than Him. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a mighty Day.”
The leaders among his people said, “Indeed, we see you in clear error.”
He said, “O my people, there is no error in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds.
I convey to you the messages of my Lord, advise you sincerely, and I know from Allah what you do not know.
Do you find it strange that a reminder has come to you from your Lord through a man from among you, to warn you and so that you may be mindful of Allah, and that you may receive mercy?”
But they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a blind people.
And to ʿĀd We sent their brother Hūd. He said, “O my people, worship Allah, you have no god other than Him. Will you not then be mindful?”
The leaders who disbelieved among his people said, “Indeed, we see you in foolishness, and truly, we think you are among the liars.”
He said, “O my people, there is no foolishness in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds.
I convey to you the messages of my Lord, and I am to you a trustworthy adviser.

Do you find it strange that a reminder has come to you from your Lord through a man from among you, to warn you? And remember when He made you successors after the people of Nū ḥ and increased you greatly in stature. So remember the favours of Allah, so that you may succeed.
They said, “Have you come to us that we should worship Allah alone and abandon what our fathers used to worship? Then bring us what you promise us, if you are truthful.”

He said, “Punishment and anger have already fallen upon you from your Lord. Do you argue with me over names you and your fathers invented, for which Allah sent down no authority? So wait. Indeed, I am with you among those who wait.”
So We saved him and those with him by mercy from Us, and We cut off the last of those who denied Our signs and were not believers.

And to Thamūd We sent their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said, “O my people, worship Allah, you have no god other than Him. There has come to you clear proof from your Lord. This she-camel of Allah is a sign for you, so leave her to eat in the land of Allah and do not touch her with harm, or a painful punishment will seize you.”

And remember when He made you successors after ʿĀd and settled you in the land, building palaces on its plains and carving homes in the mountains. So remember the favours of Allah and do not act corruptly in the land.

The leaders who were arrogant among his people said to those who were considered weak, those who believed among them, “Do you truly know that Ṣāliḥ is sent from his Lord?” They said, “ Indeed, we believe in what he has been sent with.”
Those who were arrogant said, “Indeed, we disbelieve in what you believe in.”
So they hamstrung the she-camel, defied the command of their Lord, and said, “O Ṣāliḥ, bring us what you threaten us with, if you are among the messengers.”
Then the tremor seized them, and they became lifeless in their homes.
So he turned away from them and said, “O my people, I certainly conveyed to you the message of my Lord and advised you sincerely, but you do not love sincere advisers.”
And Lūṭ, when he said to his people, “Do you commit an indecency such as none among the worlds has preceded you in?”
You approach men with desire instead of women. Rather, you are a people who transgress all bounds.
And the only answer of his people was that they said, “Expel them from your town. They are people who seek purity.”
So We saved him and his family, except for his wife. She was among those who remained behind.
And We sent down upon them a rain. So see how was the outcome of the criminals.

And to Madyan We sent their brother Shuʿayb. He said, “O my people, worship Allah, you have no god except Him. Clear proof has come to you from your Lord. So give full measure and full weight, and do not deprive people of what is rightfully theirs, and do not spread corruption on the earth after it has been set right. That is better for you, if you are believers.”

And do not sit along every path, threatening and turning away from the path of Allah those who believe in Him, and seeking to distort it. And remember when you were few and He increased you. And see how was the outcome of the corrupters.
And if there is a group among you who believe in what I have been sent with, and a group who do not believe, then be patient until Allah judges between us. And He is the best of judges.

The leaders who were arrogant among his people said, “We will surely expel you, O Shuʿayb, and those who believe with you from our town, or you must return to our religion.” He said, “Even if we detest it?

We would have invented a lie against Allah if we were to return to your religion after Allah has saved us from it. And it is not for us to return to it unless Allah, our Lord, wills. Our Lord encompasses everything in knowledge. Upon Allah we place our trust. Our Lord, decide between us and our people with truth. And You are the best of those who decide.”
And the leaders who disbelieved among his people said, “If you follow Shuʿayb, then you will surely be losers.”
So the mighty tremor seized them, and they lay lifeless in their homes.
Those who denied Shuʿayb, it was as though they had never lived there. Those who denied Shuʿayb, they were the losers.
So he turned away from them and said, “O my people, I certainly conveyed to you the messages of my Lord and advised you sincerely. So how should I grieve for a people who disbelieve?”
And We did not send any prophet to a town except that We seized its people with hardship and adversity so that they might humble themselves.
Then We replaced the bad with good until they multiplied and said, “Hardship and ease touched our fathers.” So We seized them suddenly while they were unaware.
And if the people of the towns had believed and been mindful, We would have opened for them blessings from the heavens and the earth. But they denied, so We seized them for what they used to earn.
Do the people of the towns feel secure that Our punishment will not come to them by night while they are asleep?
Or do the people of the towns feel secure that Our punishment will not come to them in daylight while they are at play?
Do they feel secure from the plan of Allah? None feels secure from the plan of Allah except the losing people.
Has it not become clear to those who inherited the earth after its people that if We willed, We could strike them for their sins and seal their hearts so that they do not hear?

Those were the towns whose stories We relate to you. Their messengers certainly came to them with clear proofs, but they were not going to believe in what they had denied before. Thus Allah seals the hearts of the disbelievers.
And We did not find for most of them any covenant. And indeed, We found most of them rebellious.
Then We sent after them Mūsā with Our signs to Firʿawn and his leaders, but they wronged them. So see how was the outcome of the corrupters.
And Mūsā said, “O Firʿawn, I am indeed a messenger from the Lord of the worlds,
bound to say nothing about Allah except the truth. I have come to you with clear proof from your Lord, so send with me the Children of Isrāʾīl.”
He said, “If you have come with a sign, then bring it, if you are among the truthful.”
So he threw down his staff, and it was clearly a serpent.
And he drew out his hand, and it was white for the onlookers.
The leaders of the people of Firʿawn said, “ Indeed, this is a skilled magician,
who wants to drive you out of your land. So what do you advise?”
They said, “Delay him and his brother, and send gatherers to the cities,
to bring you every skilled magician.”
And the magicians came to Firʿawn. They said, “Will there surely be a reward for us if we are the victors?”
He said, “Y es. And you will surely be among those brought near.”
They said, “O Mūsā, either you throw, or we will be the ones to throw.”
He said, “You throw.” So when they threw, they bewitched the eyes of the people, struck fear into them, and produced a great magic.
And We inspired Mūsā, “Throw your staff.” Then it swallowed what they were falsifying.
So the truth prevailed, and what they were doing was exposed as false.
And they were defeated there, and they turned back humbled.
And the magicians fell down in prostration.
They said, “We believe in the Lord of the worlds,
the Lord of Mūsā and Hārūn.”
Firʿawn said, “You believed in Him before I gave you permission? This is a plot you plotted in the city to drive its people out of it. But you will soon know.”
I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, then I will surely crucify you all.”
They said, “Indeed, to our Lord we will return.
You take revenge on us only because we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord, pour patience upon us and cause us to die as those who submit.”

And the leaders of the people of Firʿawn said, “Will you leave Mūsā and his people to cause corruption in the land and abandon you and your gods?” He said, “We will kill their sons and keep their women alive. And indeed, we are dominant over them.”
Mūsā said to his people, “Seek help through Allah and be patient. Indeed, the earth belongs to Allah. He gives it in inheritance to whom He wills of His servants. And the outcome is for the mindful.”
They said, “We were harmed before you came to us and after you came to us.” He said, “It may be that your Lord will destroy your enemy and make you successors in the land, then see how you act.”
And We certainly seized the people of Firʿawn with years of drought and loss of fruits so that they might remember.
But when good came to them, they said, “This is ours.” And if harm struck them, they blamed it on Mūsā and those with him. Indeed, their misfortune was with Allah, but most of them did not know.
And they said, “Whatever sign you bring us to bewitch us with it, we will not believe you.”
So We sent upon them the flood, the locusts, the lice, the frogs, and the blood as clear signs, but they were arrogant and became a criminal people.

And when the punishment fell upon them, they said, “O Mūsā, call upon your Lord for us by what He has promised you. If you remove the punishment from us, we will surely believe you and will surely send the Children of Isrāʾīl with you.”
But when We removed the punishment from them until a term they were to reach, then they broke their word.
So We took retribution from them and drowned them in the sea because they denied Our signs and were heedless of them.

And We made the people who were oppressed inherit the eastern parts of the land and the western parts of it which We had blessed. And the good word of your Lord was fulfilled upon the Children of Isrāʾīl because of their patience. And We destroyed what Firʿawn and his people had been making and what they had been building.

And We brought the Children of Isrāʾīl across the sea, and they came upon a people devoted to idols they had. They said, “O Mūsā, make for us a god just as they have gods.” He said, “Indeed, you are an ignorant people.
Indeed, what these people are upon will be destroyed, and what they are doing is false.”
He said, “Shall I seek for you a god other than Allah, while He has favoured you over the worlds?”
And remember when We saved you from the people of Firʿawn, afflicting you with the worst punishment, slaughtering your sons and sparing your women. And in that was a great trial from your Lord.

And We appointed for Mūsā thirty nights and completed them with ten, so the appointed time of his Lord was forty nights. And Mūsā said to his brother Hārūn, “T ake my place among my people, act rightly, and do not follow the way of the corrupters.”

And when Mūsā came at the appointed time and his Lord spoke to him, he said, “My Lord, show Yourself to me so that I may look at You.” He said, “You will not see Me, but look at the mountain. If it remains in place, then you will see Me.” But when his Lord manifested Himself to the mountain, He made it crumble, and Mūsā fell unconscious. Then when he recovered, he said, “Glory be to You. I turn to You in repentance, and I am the first of the believers.”
He said, “O Mūsā, I have chosen you above the people with My messages and My speech. So take what I have given you and be among the grateful.”

And We wrote for him on the tablets instruction of every matter, and clarification of everything, “So take them firmly and command your people to take the best of it. I will show you the home of the rebellious.”

I will turn away from My signs those who are arrogant on the earth without right. Even if they see every sign, they will not believe in it. And if they see the path of guidance, they will not take it as a path. But if they see the path of error, they will take it as a path. That is because they denied Our signs and were heedless of them.
And those who deny Our signs and the meeting of the Hereafter, their deeds have failed. Are they repaid except for what they used to do?

And the people of Mūsā took after him from their ornaments a calf, a body with a lowing sound. Did they not see that it did not speak to them nor guide them to a path? They took it, and they were wrongdoers.
And when regret seized them and they saw that they had gone astray, they said, “If our Lord does not have mercy on us and forgive us, we will surely be among the losers.”

And when Mūsā returned to his people angry and sorrowful, he said, “How evil is what you did after me. Did you hasten the command of your Lord?” And he threw down the tablets and seized his brother by the head, pulling him toward himself. He said, “Son of  my mother, the people overpowered me and nearly killed me, so do not make the enemies rejoice over me, and do not place me among the wrongdoing people.”
He said, “My Lord, forgive me and my brother, and admit us into Your mercy. And You are the Ever-Merciful of those who show mercy.”
Indeed, those who took the calf will be overtaken by anger from their Lord and humiliation in the life of this world. Thus We repay those who invent lies.
But those who commit evil deeds, then repent after that and believe, then indeed, your Lord after that is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
And when the anger subsided from Mūsā, he took the tablets. And in their inscription was guidance and mercy for those who fear their Lord.

And Mūsā chose from his people seventy men for Our appointed time. Then when the mighty tremor seized them, he said, “My Lord, if You had willed, You could have destroyed them before, and me as well. Will You destroy us for what the foolish among us have done? It is only Your trial by which You let go astray whom You will and guide whom You will. You are our Protector, so forgive us and have mercy on us, and You are the best of forgivers.

And decree for us good in this world and in the Hereafter. Indeed, we have turned to You.” He said, “My punishment, I strike with it whom I will, but My mercy encompasses everything. So I will decree it for those who are mindful, give charity, and those who believe in Our signs,

those who follow the Messenger, the unlettered Prophet, whom they find written with them in the Tawrāh and the Injīl, who commands them to what is right and forbids them from what is wrong, makes lawful for them the good things and forbids them from the impure, and relieves them of their burdens and the shackles that were upon them. So those who believe in him, honour him, support him, and follow the light that was sent down with him, those are the successful.”

Say, “O people, indeed I am the Messenger of Allah to you all, to Him belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. There is no god except Him. He gives life and causes death. So believe in Allah and His Messenger, the unlettered Prophet, who believes in Allah and His words, and follow him so that you may be guided.”
And among the people of Mūsā is a community that guides by the truth and establishes justice by it.

And We divided them into twelve tribes as communities. And We inspired Mūsā when his people asked him for water, “Strike the rock with your staff.” So twelve springs burst forth from it. Each people knew its drinking place. And We shaded them with clouds and sent down upon them manna and quails, “Eat from the good things We have provided for you.” And they did not wrong Us, but they were wronging themselves.

And remember when it was said to them, “Dwell in this town and eat from it wherever you wish, and say, ‘ Remove our sins,’ and enter the gate bowing, so We may forgive your wrongs. We will increase those who do good.”
But those who did wrong among them changed the word to something other than what had been said to them, so We sent upon them punishment from the sky for what they used to do wrong.

And ask them about the town that was by the sea, when they transgressed concerning the Sabbath, when their fish came to them openly on their Sabbath day, and on the day they did not observe the Sabbath, they did not come. Thus We tested them because they persisted in rebellion.

And when a group among them said, “Why do you warn a people Allah is about to destroy or punish with a severe punishment?” They said, “ As an excuse before your Lord, and perhaps they may be mindful of Allah.”
So when they forgot what they were reminded of, We saved those who forbade evil, and We seized those who did wrong with a miserable punishment because they used to rebel.
Then when they were insolent about what they had been forbidden, We said to them, “Be apes, despised.”

And remember when your Lord proclaimed that He would surely send upon them, until the Day of Resurrection, those who would afflict them with the worst punishment. Indeed, your Lord is swift in punishment, and indeed, He is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
And We divided them throughout the earth into communities. Among them are the righteous, and among them are others less than that. And We tested them with good and bad so that they might return.

Then there came after them successors who inherited the Book, taking the gains of this lower life and saying, “We will be forgiven.” But if similar gain comes to them, they take it again. W as not the covenant of the Book taken from them that they would no t say about Allah except the truth, and they studied what is in it? And the Home of the Hereafter is better for those who are mindful. Will you not then understand?
And those who hold firmly to the Book and uphold the prayer, indeed We do not allow the reward of those who set things right to be lost.

And when We raised the mountain above them as if it were a canopy, and they thought it would fall upon them, “Hold firmly what We have given you and remember what is in it, so that you may be mindful of Allah.”

And when your Lord took from the children of Ādam, from their backs, their descendants, and made them bear witness over themselves, “ Am I not your Lord?” They said, “Y es, we bear witness.” Lest you should say on the Day of Resurrection, “Indeed, we were unaware of this,”
or say, “It was only our fathers who associated partners before, and we were descendants after them. Will You then destroy us for what the falsifiers did?”
And thus We make the signs clear, so that they may return.
And recite to them the story of the one to whom We gave Our signs, but he detached himself from them, so Shayṭān pursued him and he became among the misguided.

And if We had willed, We could have raised him by them, but he clung to the earth and followed his desire. So his example is like that of a dog, if you drive it away, it pants, and if you leave it, it pants. That is the example of the people who deny Our signs. So relate the stories, so that they may reflect.
Evil is the example of the people who deny Our signs and wrong themselves.
Whoever Allah guides, he is the guided. And whoever He lets go astray, those are the losers.

And We have certainly created for Hell many of the jinn and humankind. They have hearts with which they do not understand, eyes with which they do not see, and ears with which they do not hear. Those are like cattle, rather they are more astray. Those are the heedless.
And to Allah belong the most beautiful names, so call upon Him by them, and leave those who deviate concerning His names. They will be repaid for what they used to do.
And among those We created is a community that guides by the truth and establishes justice by it.
And those who deny Our signs, We will lead them on gradually from where they do not know.
And I grant them respite. Indeed, My plan is firm.
Have they not reflected? There is no madness in their companion. He is only a clear warner.
Have they not looked into the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever Allah has created, and that perhaps their term has drawn near? Then in what speech after this will they believe?
Whomever Allah lets go astray, there is no guide for him. And He leaves them wandering blindly in their transgression.

They ask you about the Hour, “When will it take place?” Say, “Its knowledge is only with my Lord. None will reveal it at its time except Him. It weighs heavy in the heavens and the earth. It will not come upon you except suddenly.” They ask you as though you are eager for it. Say, “Its knowledge is only with Allah, but most people do not know.”

Say, “I possess no power to benefit myself or harm myself except what Allah wills. And if I knew the unseen, I would have acquired much good, and no harm would have touched me. I am only a warner and a bearer of good news for a people who believe.”

He is the One who created you from a single soul and made from it its mate, so that he might find rest in her. Then when he covered her, she carried a light burden and passed on with it. But when it grew heavy, they both called upon Allah, their Lord, “If  You grant us a righteous child, we will surely be among the grateful.”
But when He granted them a righteous child, they associated partners with Him in what He gave them. Exalted is Allah above what they associate.
Do they associate what creates nothing and themselves are created,
and cannot help them, nor can they help themselves?
And if you call them to guidance, they do not follow you. It is the same for them whether you call them or remain silent.
Indeed, those you call upon besides Allah are servants like you. So call upon them and let them respond to you, if you are truthful.

Do they have feet with which they walk, or hands with which they strike, or eyes with which they see, or ears with which they hear? Say, “Call your partners, then plot against me and give me no respite.
Indeed, my Protector is Allah, who sent down the Book. And He protects the righteous.
And those you call upon besides Him cannot help you, nor can they help themselves.”
And if you call them to guidance, they do not hear. And you see them looking at you, but they do not see.
T ake forgiveness, command what is right, and turn away from the ignorant.
And if a prompting from Shayṭān touches you, then seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
Indeed, those who are mindful  of Allah, when a prompting from Shayṭān touches them, they remember, and then they see clearly.
But their brothers draw them deeper into error, then they do not stop.

And when you do not bring them a sign, they say, “Why did you not choose it?” Say, “I only follow what is revealed to me from my Lord.” This is insight from your Lord, and guidance and mercy for a people who believe.
And when the Qur’an is recited, listen to it and remain silent, so that you may receive mercy.
And remember your Lord within yourself, humbly and in awe, without loudness in words, morning and evening, and do not be among the heedless.
Indeed, those who are with your Lord are not arrogant toward His worship. They glorify Him, and to Him they prostrate.

surahs.name: Al-Anfāl (The Spoils of War)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: They ask you about the spoils of war. Say, “The spoils belong to Allah and the Messenger.” So be mindful of Allah, set matters right among yourselves, and obey Allah and His Messenger, if you are believers.
The believers are only those whose hearts tremble when Allah is mentioned, whose faith increases when His signs are recited to them, and who place their trust in their Lord,
those who uphold the prayer  and spend from what We have provided them.
Those are truly the believers. For them are degrees with their Lord, forgiveness, and a generous provision.
Just as your Lord brought you out from your home in truth, while a group among the believers were averse,
arguing with you about the truth after it had become clear, as though they were being driven toward death while they were looking on.

And when Allah promised you one of the two groups, that it would be yours, and you wished that the one without strength would be yours. But Allah intended to establish the truth by His words and cut off the last of the disbelievers,
so that He might establish the truth and nullify falsehood, even if the criminals disliked it.
When you sought help from your Lord, and He responded to you, “I will reinforce you with a thousand of the angels, coming one after another.”
And Allah made it only as good news and so that your hearts might be reassured by it. And victory is only from Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

When He covered you with drowsiness as security from Him, and sent down upon you rain from the sky to purify you with it, remove from you the defilement of Shayṭān, strengthen your hearts, and make firm your steps with it.

When your Lord inspired the angels, “I am with you, so make firm those who believe. I will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve. So strike above the necks and strike from them every fingertip.”
That is because they opposed Allah and His Messenger. And whoever opposes Allah and His Messenger, then  indeed, Allah is severe in punishment.
That is for you, so taste it. And for the disbelievers is the punishment of the Fire.
O you who believe, when you meet those who disbelieve advancing, do not turn your backs to them.

And whoever turns his back to them on that day, unless maneuvering for battle or joining another group, has certainly incurred anger from Allah, and his refuge is Hell. And how miserable is the destination.

You did not kill them, but Allah killed them. And you did not throw when you threw, but Allah threw, so that He might test the believers with a good test from Him. Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
That is so, and Allah weakens the plot of the disbelievers.

If you sought victory, victory has now come to you. And if you desist, it is better for you. But if you return, We will return. And your forces will not benefit you at all, even if numerous. And indeed, Allah is with the believers.
O you who believe, obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not turn away from him while you hear.
And do not be like those who say, “We hear,” while they do not hear.
Indeed, the worst of creatures in the sight of Allah are the deaf and mute who do not reason.
And if Allah had known any good in them, He would have made them hear. And if He had made them hear, they would still have turned away while refusing.
O you who believe, respond to Allah and to the Messenger when he calls you to what gives you life. And know that Allah comes between a person and his heart, and that to Him you will be gathered.
And beware of a trial which will not strike exclusively those who do wrong among you. And know that Allah is severe in punishment.

And remember when you were few, oppressed in the land, fearing that people might snatch you away, then He sheltered you, supported you with His help, and provided you with good things so that you might be grateful.
O you who believe, do not betray Allah and the Messenger, nor betray your trusts while you know.
And know that your wealth and your children are a trial, and that with Allah is a great reward.
O you who believe, if you are mindful of Allah, He will grant you a criterion, remove your wrongs from you, and forgive you. And Allah is Possessor of great bounty.
And remember when those who disbelieve plotted against you to restrain you, or kill you, or expel you. They plotted, and Allah plotted. And Allah is the best of planners.
And when Our signs are recited to them, they say, “We have heard. If we wished, we could say the like of this. This is nothing but tales of the former people.”
And when they said, “O Allah, if this is the truth from You, then rain down stones upon us from the sky or bring upon us a painful punishment.”
But Allah would not punish them while you are among them, and Allah would not punish them while they seek forgiveness.
And why should Allah not punish them while they bar people from the Sacred Mosque, though they are not its guardians? Its guardians are only the mindful, but most of them do not know.
And their prayer at the House was nothing but whistling and clapping. So taste the punishment for what you used to disbelieve.

Indeed, those who disbelieve spend their wealth to turn people away from the path of Allah. They will spend it, then it will become regret for them, then they will be overcome. And those who disbelieve will be gathered to Hell,
so that Allah may separate the impure from the pure, place the impure on top of one another, heap them together, and place them in Hell. Those are the losers.
Say to those who disbelieve, “If they desist, what has passed will be forgiven for them. But if they return, then the way of the former peoples has already passed.”
And fight them until there is no persecution and religion is for Allah alone. But if they cease, then indeed, Allah sees what they do.
And if they turn away, then know that Allah is your Protector. How excellent a Protector and how excellent a Helper.

And know that whatever spoils you gain, a fifth of it is for Allah, the Messenger, close relatives, orphans, the needy, and the traveller, if you believe in Allah and in what We sent down upon Our servant on the Day of Criterion, the Day the two forces met. And Allah has power over everything.

When you were on the nearer side of the valley, and they were on the farther side, and the caravan was lower than you. And if you had made an appointment together, you would have differed over the appointment. But it was so that Allah might accomplish a matter already decreed, so that whoever perished would perish upon clear proof, and whoever lived would live upon clear proof. And indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

When Allah showed them to you in your dream as few. And if He had shown them to you as many, you would have lost courage and disputed over the matter. But Allah saved you. Indeed, He knows what is within the chests.
And when He showed them to you, when you met, as few in your eyes, and He made you few in their eyes, so that Allah might accomplish a matter already decreed. And to Allah are returned all matters.
O you who believe, when you meet a force, remain firm and remember Allah much, so that you may succeed.
And obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not dispute, lest you lose courage and your strength depart. And be patient. Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
And do not be like those who came out of their homes boastfully and to show off before people, and who turn others away from the path of Allah. And Allah encompasses what they do.

And when Shayṭān made their deeds appear pleasing to them and said, “No one can overcome you today, and I am your protector.” But when the two forces came into sight, he turned back on his heels and said, “Indeed, I am free of you. Indeed, I see what you do not see. Indeed, I fear Allah.” And Allah is severe in punishment.
When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts was disease said, “Their religion has deceived them.” But whoever places his trust in Allah, then indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.
And if you could see when the angels take the souls of those who disbelieve, striking their faces and their backs, and saying, “T aste the punishment of the Burning Fire.”
That is for what your hands have sent ahead, and because Allah is not unjust to the servants.
Like the way of the people of Firʿawn and those before them. They rejected the signs of Allah, so Allah seized them for their sins. Indeed, Allah is All-Strong, severe in punishment.
That is because Allah would not change a blessing He has bestowed upon a people until they change what is within themselves. And indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

Like the way of the people of Firʿawn and those before them. They denied the signs of their Lord, so We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned the people of Firʿawn. And all of them were wrongdoers.
Indeed, the worst of creatures in the sight of Allah are those who disbelieve, so they do not believe,
those with whom you made a covenant, then they break their covenant every time, and they are not mindful of Allah.
So if you encounter them in war, scatter by them those behind them, so that they may remember.
And if you fear betrayal from a people, then throw it back to them on equal terms. Indeed, Allah does not love the betrayers.
And let not those who disbelieve think that they have escaped. Indeed, they will not frustrate.

And prepare against them whatever force you can and steeds of war, to deter the enemy of Allah and your enemy and others besides them whom you do not know, Allah knows them. And whatever you spend in the way of Allah will be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged.
And if they incline toward peace, then incline to it, and place your trust in Allah. Indeed, He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
And if they intend to deceive you, then indeed, Allah is sufficient for you. He is the One who supported you with His help and with the believers,
and brought their hearts together. If you had spent all that is on the earth, you could not have brought their hearts together, but Allah brought them together. Indeed, He is All-Mighty, All-Wise.
O Prophet, Allah is sufficient for you and for those of the believers who follow you.

O Prophet, urge the believers to fight. If there are twenty among you who are steadfast, they will overcome two hundred. And if there are one hundred among you, they will overcome one thousand of those who disbelieve, because they are a people who do not understand.

Now Allah has lightened your burden, knowing that there is weakness in you. So if there are one hundred among you who are steadfast, they will overcome two hundred. And if there are one thousand among you, they will overcome two thousand by the permission  of Allah. And Allah is with the steadfast.
It is not for a prophet to have captives until he has thoroughly subdued the land. You desire the goods of this world, but Allah desires the Hereafter. And Allah is Mighty, All-Wise.
Had it not been for a prior decree from Allah, a great punishment would have touched you for what you took.
So eat from what you have taken as spoils, lawful and good, and be mindful of Allah. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

O Prophet, say to those captives in your hands, “If Allah knows any good in your hearts, He will give you better than what has been taken from you and forgive you.” And Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
