And if they intend to betray you, they have already betrayed Allah before, so He gave you power over them. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

Indeed, those who believed, migrated, and strove with their wealth and their lives in the way of Allah, and those who sheltered and supported them, those are allies of one another. But those who believed and did not migrate, you have no alliance with them until they migrate. And if they seek your help in religion, then help is due, except against a people between whom and you is a treaty. And Allah sees what you do.
And those who disbelieve are allies of one another. If you do not do this, there will be turmoil in the land and great corruption.
And those who believed, migrated, and struggled in the way of Allah, and those who sheltered and supported, those are the true believers. For them is forgiveness and a noble provision.

And those who believed afterwards, migrated, and struggled with you, they are among you. And blood relatives are closer to one another in the Book of Allah. Indeed, Allah has full knowledge of everything.
A declaration of disavowal from Allah and His Messenger to those among the associators with whom you made a treaty.
So travel freely in the land for four months, and know that you cannot escape Allah, and that Allah will bring disgrace upon the disbelievers.

And a proclamation from Allah and His Messenger to the people on the Day of the Great Pilgrimage, that Allah is free from obligation to the associators, and so is His Messenger. So if you repent, that is better for you. But if you turn away, then know tha t you cannot escape Allah. And give tidings of a painful punishment to those who disbelieve.

Except for those among the associators with whom you made a treaty, and who did not fail you in anything, nor supported anyone against you. Fulfil your treaty with them until its term. Indeed, Allah loves those who are mindful of Him.

Then, when the sacred months have passed, kill the associators wherever you find them, capture them, besiege them, and lie in wait for them at every place of ambush. But if they repent, uphold the prayer, and give zakah, then leave their way free. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

And if any one of the associators seeks your protection, then grant him protection so that he may hear the words of Allah. Then escort him to a place of safety. That is because they are a people who do not know.

How can there be a treaty with Allah and with His Messenger for the associators, except for those with whom you made a treaty at the Sacred Mosque? So long as they remain upright with you, remain upright with them. Indeed, Allah loves those who are mindful of Him.
How so, when if they gain the upper hand over you, they respect neither bond nor treaty concerning you? They please you with their mouths, but their hearts refuse, and most of them are rebellious.
They have sold the signs of Allah for a small gain and barred others from His path. Indeed, evil is what they have been doing.
They respect neither bond nor obligation concerning a believer. Those are the transgressors.
But if they repent, uphold the prayer, and give zakah, then they are your brothers in faith. And We set out the signs clearly for a people who understand.
And if they break their oaths after their treaty and attack your religion, then fight the leaders of disbelief, for there are no binding oaths for them, so that they may desist.
Will you not fight a people who broke their oaths, sought to expel the Messenger, and were the first to attack you? Do you fear them? Allah is more deserving that you fear Him, if you are believers.
Fight them, Allah will punish them by your hands, disgrace them, give you victory over them, and heal the hearts of believing people,
and remove the rage from their hearts. And Allah turns in mercy to whom He wills. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

Or did you think that you would be left alone while Allah has not yet made evident those among you who strive, and who take no close allies besides Allah, His Messenger, and the believers? And Allah is fully aware of what you do.
It is not for the associators to maintain the mosques of Allah while bearing witness against themselves to disbelief. Those, their deeds have come to nothing, and they will remain in the Fire.
Only those who believe in Allah and the Last Day, uphold the prayer, give zakah, and fear none but Allah, are fit to maintain the mosques of Allah. It may be that they are among the rightly guided.

Do you consider the providing of water to pilgrims and the maintenance of the Sacred Mosque equal to belief in Allah and the Last Day, and striving in the way of Allah? They are not equal with Allah. And Allah does not guide wrongdoing people.
Those who believed, migrated, and strove in the way of Allah with their wealth and their lives are greater in rank with Allah. Those are the successful.
Their Lord gives them good news of mercy from Him, pleasure, and gardens in which they will have lasting delight,
remaining in them forever. Indeed, with Allah is a great reward.
O you who believe, do not take your fathers and your brothers as close allies if they prefer disbelief over faith. And whoever among you takes them as allies, those are the wrongdoers.

Say, “If your fathers, your sons, your brothers, your spouses, your relatives, the wealth you have acquired, the trade you fear may decline, and the homes you love are dearer to you than Allah, His Messenger, and striving in His way, then wait until Allah brings His command.” And Allah does not guide rebellious people.

Allah has already given you victory on many occasions, and on the Day of Ḥunayn, when your great numbers pleased you, but they did not benefit you at all, and the land became narrow for you despite its vastness, then you turned back fleeing.
Then Allah sent down His tranquillity upon His Messenger and upon the believers, sent down forces you did not see, and punished those who disbelieved. That is the recompense of the disbelievers.
Then Allah turns in mercy after that to whom He wills. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

O you who believe, those who associate with Allah are indeed unclean, so let them not approach the Sacred Mosque after this year. And if you fear poverty, Allah will enrich you from His bounty if He wills. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

Fight those from among the People of the Book who do not believe in Allah or the Last Day, who do not consider unlawful what Allah and His Messenger have made unlawful, and who do not follow the religion of truth, until they pay the jizyah willingly, while being subdued.

And the Jews say, “ʿUzayr is the son of Allah,” and the Christians say, “The Messiah is the son of Allah.” That is what they say with their mouths, imitating the words of those who disbelieved  before. May Allah condemn them. How they are turned away.

They have taken their rabbis and monks as lords besides Allah, and also the Messiah, son of Maryam, though they were commanded to worship only One God. There is no god except Him. Glory be to Him above what they associate.
They want to extinguish the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah will perfect His light, even though the disbelievers hate it.
He is the One who sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth, to make it prevail over all religion, even though the associators hate it.

O you who believe, many of the rabbis and monks consume people’s wealth unjustly and bar others from the way of Allah. And those who hoard gold and silver and do not spend it in the way of Allah, give them news of a painful punishment.
On the Day when it will be heated in the Fire of Hell, and their foreheads, sides, and backs will be branded with it: “This is what you hoarded for yourselves, so taste what you used to hoard.”

Indeed, the number of months with Allah is twelve, in the decree of Allah, from the day He created the heavens and the earth. Of them, four are sacred. That is the upright religion, so do not wrong yourselves within them. And fight the associators collectively as they fight you collectively. And know that Allah is with those who are mindful of Him.

Indeed, postponing is an increase in disbelief, by which those who disbelieve are led further astray. They make it lawful one year and unlawful another year, to match the number of months Allah has made sacred, and so make lawful what Allah has made unlawful. Their evil deeds are made appealing to them. And Allah does not guide the disbelieving people.

O you who believe, what is the matter with you that when it is said to you, “Go forth in the way of Allah,” you cling heavily to the earth? Are you pleased with the life of this world over the Hereafter? But the enjoyment of this world is little compared to the Hereafter.
If you do not go forth, He will punish you with a painful punishment, replace you with another people, and you will not harm Him at all. And Allah has power over everything.

If you do not support him, Allah has already supported him, when those who disbelieved drove him out, as the second of the two, when they were in the cave, and he said to his companion, “Do not grieve, Allah is with us.” Then Allah sent down His tranquillity upon him, supported him with forces you did not see, and made the word of those who disbelieved the lowest. And the word of Allah is the highest. And Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.
Go forth, whether light or heavy, and strive with your wealth and your lives in the way of Allah. That is better for you, if you only knew.

If it had been an easy gain and a short journey, they would have followed you, but the distance was long for them. And they will swear by Allah, “If we were able, we would have gone out with you.” They destroy themselves, and Allah knows that they are lying.
May Allah pardon you. Why did you permit them before it had become clear to you who was truthful and before you knew the liars?
Those who believe in Allah and the Last Day do not ask you for permission to hold back from striving with their wealth and their lives. And Allah knows well those who are mindful of Him.
Only those ask your permission who do not believe in Allah and the Last Day, whose hearts are in doubt, so they waver in their doubt.
And if they had intended to go out, they would have made preparation for it. But Allah disliked their going forth, so He held them back, and it was said, “Remain with those who remain.”

Had they gone out with you, they would have added nothing but corruption, and they would have hurried among you seeking discord. And among you are those who would have listened to them. And Allah knows the wrongdoers.
They had already sought discord before and turned matters upside down for you, until the truth came and the command of Allah prevailed, while they were resentful.
And among them is one who says, “Grant me permission and do not put me to trial.” Indeed, they have already fallen into trial. And Hell will indeed encompass the disbelievers.
If something good happens to you, it distresses them. But if a hardship befalls you, they say, “We took our precautions before,” and they turn away rejoicing.
Say, “Nothing will ever befall us except what Allah has decreed for us. He is our Protector. And upon Allah let the believers place their trust.”

Say, “Do you await for us anything other than one of the two best outcomes? But we await for you that Allah will strike you with a punishment from Him or by our hands. So wait; we too are waiting with you.”
Say, “Spend willingly or unwillingly, it will never be accepted from you. Indeed, you are a rebellious people.”
And nothing prevents their spending from being accepted except that they disbelieved in Allah and His Messenger, they do not come to prayer except lazily, and they do not spend except unwillingly.
So do not let their wealth or their children impress you. Allah only intends to punish them through them in this worldly life, and that their souls depart while they are disbelievers.
And they swear by Allah that they are from among you, but they are not from among you. Rather, they are a people who are afraid.
If they could find a refuge, or caves, or a place to enter, they would turn to it while rushing wildly.
And among them are those who criticise you concerning the distribution. If they are given from it, they are pleased, but if they are not given from it, they become angry.

If only they had been content with what Allah and His Messenger had given them and said, “ Allah is sufficient for us. Allah will give us from His bounty, and so will His Messenger. Indeed, to Allah we turn in hope.”

Charity is only for the poor, the needy, those employed to administer it, those whose hearts are to be reconciled, to free those in bondage, for those in debt, in the way of Allah, and for the traveller. This is an obligation from Allah. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

And among them are those who harm the Messenger and say, “He listens to everyone.” Say, “He listens for your good. He believes in Allah, trusts the believers, and is a mercy to those among you who believe.” And for those who harm the Messenger of Allah is a painful punishment.
They swear by Allah to you to please you, but Allah and His Messenger are more deserving that they please Him, if they are believers.
Do they not know that whoever opposes Allah and His Messenger, for him is the Fire of Hell, remaining in it forever? That is the great disgrace.
The hypocrites fear that a surah will be sent down about them, informing them of what is in their hearts. Say, “Mock if you wish. Allah will surely bring out what you fear.”
And if you ask them, they will surely say, “We were only talking idly and joking.” Say, “W as it Allah, His signs, and His Messenger that you were mocking?”
Make no excuses. You have disbelieved after your belief. If We pardon a group among you, We will punish another group, because they were criminals.

The hypocrite men and hypocrite women belong to one another. They command what is wrong and forbid what is right, and they keep their hands closed. They forgot Allah, so He forgot them. Indeed, the hypocrites are the rebellious.

Allah has promised the hypocrite men and the hypocrite women and the disbelievers the Fire of Hell, remaining in it forever. It is sufficient for them. And Allah has cursed them, and for them is a lasting punishment.

Like those before you, they were stronger than you in power and greater in wealth and children. They enjoyed their share, so you have enjoyed your share just as those before you enjoyed their share, and you have plunged into idle talk as they plunged. Tho se are the ones whose deeds have come to nothing in this world and the Hereafter. Those are the losers.

Has the news of those before them not reached them, the people of Nūḥ, ʿĀd, Thamūd, the people of Ibrāhīm, the people of Madyan, and the overturned cities? Their messengers came to them with clear signs. Allah did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves.

The believing men and the believing women are protectors of one another. They command what is right and forbid what is wrong, uphold the prayer, give zakah, and obey Allah and His Messenger. Those, Allah will have mercy on them. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

Allah has promised the believing men and the believing women gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever, and pleasant dwellings in the gardens of lasting residence. And the pleasure of Allah is greater. That is the great success.
O Prophet, strive against the disbelievers and the hypocrites, and be firm with them. Their dwelling is Hell, and an evil destination it is.

They swear by Allah that they did not say it, but they surely spoke the word of disbelief and disbelieved after their belief, and they intended what they could not achieve. And they found fault only because Allah and His Messenger enriched them from His bounty. So if they repent, that is better for them. But if they turn away, Allah will punish them with a pa inful punishment in this world and the Hereafter, and they will have no protector or helper on the earth.
And among them are those who made a pledge to Allah, “If He gives us from His bounty, we will surely give charity and surely be among the righteous.”
But when He gave them from His bounty, they became miserly with it and turned away, while refusing.
So He caused hypocrisy to follow in their hearts until the Day they will meet Him, because they broke their promise to Allah and because they used to lie.
Do they not know that Allah knows their secrets and their private counsel, and that Allah is the Knower of all unseen things?

Those who criticise the believers who give freely in charity, and those who find fault with those who have nothing except what they earn with effort, and they mock them, Allah mocks them. And for them is a painful punishment.

Ask forgiveness for them, or do not ask forgiveness for them. Even if you ask forgiveness for them seventy times, Allah will never forgive them. That is because they disbelieved in Allah and His Messenger. And Allah does not guide rebellious people.

Those who stayed behind rejoiced in remaining behind the Messenger of Allah, and they disliked striving with their wealth and their lives in the way of Allah. They said, “Do not go out in the heat.” Say, “The Fire of Hell is more intense in heat,” if only they understood.
So let them laugh a little and weep much, as recompense for what they used to earn.

If Allah brings you back to a group of them and they ask your permission to go out, then say, “You will never go out with me, nor will you ever fight an enemy with me. You were content to remain behind the first time, so remain with those who stay behind.”
And do not ever pray over any of them who dies, nor stand at his grave. They disbelieved in Allah and His Messenger and died while rebellious.
And do not let their wealth or their children impress you. Allah only intends to punish them through them in this world, and that their souls depart while they are disbelievers.
And when a surah is sent down, “Believe in Allah and strive with His Messenger,” those of means among them ask your permission and say, “Leave us to be with those who remain.”
They were pleased to be with those who stayed behind, and their hearts were sealed, so they do not understand.
But the Messenger and those who believed with him strove with their wealth and their lives. Those, for them are all good things, and those are the successful.
Allah has prepared for them gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever. That is the great success.

And those of the Bedouin  Arabs who made excuses came to be granted permission, and those who lied to Allah and His Messenger stayed behind. A painful punishment will strike those among them who disbelieved.

There is no blame upon the weak, the sick, or those who find nothing to spend, so long as they are sincere to Allah and His Messenger. There is no cause against those who do good. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever- Merciful.

Nor upon those who came to you so that you might provide mounts for them, and you said, “I cannot find anything to mount you on,” so they turned back while their eyes overflowed with tears out of sorrow that they could not find anything to spend.
The cause is only against those who ask your permission while they are wealthy. They were pleased to be with those who stayed behind, and Allah sealed their hearts, so they do not know.

They will make excuses to you when you return to them. Say, “Do not make excuses. We will never believe you. Allah has already informed us about you. Allah will see your deeds, and so will His Messenger, then you will be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the seen, and He will inform you of what you used to do.”

They will swear by Allah to you, when you return to them, so that you may turn away from them. So turn away from them. They are defiled, and their dwelling is Hell, as recompense for what they used to earn.
They swear to you so that you may be pleased with them. But even if you are pleased with them, Allah is not pleased with rebellious people.
The Bedouin Arabs are more intense in disbelief and hypocrisy, and more likely to be unaware of the limits Allah has sent down to His Messenger. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
And among the Bedouins are those who consider what they spend as a loss and await misfortune for you. Upon them is the misfortune of evil. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

And among the Bedouins are those who believe in Allah and the Last Day and consider what they spend as a means of closeness to Allah and the prayers of the Messenger. Indeed, it is a means of closeness for them. Allah will admit them into His mercy. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

And the first to lead the way among the emigrants and the supporters, and those who followed them with excellence, Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him. And He has prepared for them gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever. That is the great success.

And among those around you of the Bedouin Arabs are hypocrites, and among the people of Madinah are those who persist in hypocrisy. You do not know them, We know them. We will punish them twice, then they will be returned to a great punishment.
And others have admitted their sins. They mixed a righteous deed with another that was evil. It may be that Allah will turn to them in mercy. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
T ake charity from their wealth to purify them and cleanse them by it, and pray for them. Indeed, your prayer is a source of tranquillity for them. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
Do they not know that Allah accepts repentance from His servants and accepts charities, and that Allah is the One who accepts repentance, the Ever- Merciful?

And say, “Do deeds. Allah will see your deeds, and so will His Messenger and the believers. Then you will be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the seen, and He will inform you of what you used to do.”
And others are deferred until the command of Allah, whether He will punish them or turn to them in mercy. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

And those who took a mosque for harm, disbelief, division among the believers, and as an outpost for those who previously waged war against Allah and His Messenger, they will surely swear, “We intended nothing but good.” And Allah bears witness that they are liars.

Do not stand in it at all. A mosque founded on mindfulness of Allah from the first day is more deserving that you stand there. In it are men who love to purify themselves. And Allah loves those who purify themselves.

Is one who founded his building on mindfulness of Allah and His pleasure better, or one who founded his building on the edge of a crumbling bank, so it collapsed with him into the Fire of Hell? And Allah does not guide wrongdoing people.
The building they built will not cease to be a source of doubt in their hearts, until their hearts are cut to pieces. And Allah is All -Knowing, All- Wise.

Indeed, Allah has purchased from the believers their lives and their wealth in exchange for Paradise. They fight in the way of Allah, so they kill and are killed. This is a true promise binding upon Him in the Tawrāh, the Injīl, and the Qur’an. And who is more faithful to His promise than Allah? So rejoice in the transaction you have made. That is the great success.

Those who repent, those who worship, those who praise, those who journey, those who bow, those who prostrate, those who command what is right and forbid what is wrong, and those who guard the limits of Allah. And give good news to the believers.
It is not for the Prophet and those who believe to seek forgiveness for the associators, even if they are close relatives, after it has become clear to them that they are companions of the Fire.

And the seeking of forgiveness by Ibrāhīm for his father was only because of a promise he had made to him. But when it became clear to him that he was an enemy of Allah, he disowned him. Indeed, Ibrāhīm was tender- hearted and forbearing.
And Allah would not lead a people astray after He has guided them, until He makes clear to them what they should be mindful of. Indeed, Allah has full knowledge of everything.
Indeed, to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and causes death. And you have no protector or helper besides Allah.

Allah has turned in mercy to the Prophet, and to the emigrants and the supporters who followed him in the hour of hardship, after the hearts of a group among them had nearly swerved. Then He turned to them in mercy. Indeed, He is to them Most-Kind, Ever-Merciful.

And to the three who were left behind, until the earth became narrow for them despite its vastness, and their souls became constricted, and they realised that there is no refuge from Allah except in Him. Then He turned to them in mercy, so that they might  repent. Indeed, Allah is the One who accepts repentance, the Ever-Merciful.
O you who believe, be mindful of Allah and be with the truthful.

It was not for the people of Madinah and those around them of the Bedouins to stay behind the Messenger of Allah, nor to prefer their own lives over his. That is because no thirst, hardship, or hunger strikes them in the way of Allah, nor do they take any  step that angers the disbelievers, nor do they inflict any loss upon an enemy, except that it is written for them as a righteous deed. Indeed, Allah does not let the reward of those who do good be lost.
Nor do they spend any small or great amount, nor do they cross any valley, except that it is written for them, so that Allah may reward them for the best of what they used to do.

And it is not for the believers to go forth all together. So why should not a group from every community go forth to gain understanding of the religion and to warn their people when they return to them, so that they may be mindful?
O you who believe, fight those among the disbelievers who are nearest to you, and let them find firmness in you. And know that Allah is with those who are mindful of Him.
And whenever a surah is sent down, among them are those who say, “Which of you has this increased in faith?” As for those who believe, it has increased them in faith, and they rejoice.
But as for those in whose hearts is disease, it adds impurity to their impurity, and they die while they are disbelievers.
Do they not see that they are tested once or twice every year? Y et they do not repent, nor do they take heed.
And whenever a surah is sent down, they look at one another, “Does anyone see you?” Then they turn away. Allah has turned their hearts away, because they are a people who do not understand.
There has certainly come to you a Messenger from among yourselves. Your hardship weighs heavily upon him. He is deeply concerned for you, and to the believers he is gentle and merciful.
But if they turn away, then say, “ Allah is sufficient for me. There is no god except Him. Upon Him I place my trust. And He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne.”

surahs.name: Yūnus (Jonah)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Rā. These are the verses of the wise Book.

Is it surprising to the people that We have revealed to a man from among them: “W arn the people, and give good news to those who believe that they will have a sure standing with their Lord”? The disbelievers say, “This is clearly a magician.”

Indeed, your Lord is Allah, who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then established Himself over the Throne, directing all matters. There is no intercessor except after His permission. That is Allah, your Lord, so worship Him. Will you not then reflect?

T o Him is your return, all of you. This is the true promise of Allah. He begins creation, then repeats it, so that He may reward those who believe and do righteous deeds with justice. And for those who disbelieve, for them is a drink of boiling water and a painful punishment because they used to disbelieve.

He is the One who made the sun a shining light and the moon a reflected light, and determined its phases, so that you may know the number of years and the reckoning. Allah did not create this except in truth. He sets out the signs clearly for a people who know.
Indeed, in the alternation of night and day, and in what Allah has created in the heavens and the earth, are signs for a people who are mindful of Him.
Those who do not expect to meet Us, are pleased with the life of this world and feel secure in it, and those who are heedless of Our signs,
their dwelling will be the Fire, for what they used to earn.
Indeed, those who believe and do righteous deeds, their Lord will guide them by their faith. Rivers will flow beneath them in gardens of delight.
Their call therein will be, “Glory be to You, O Allah,” and their greeting therein will be, “Peace,” and the closing of their call will be, “ All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all worlds.”

And if Allah were to hasten harm for people as they hasten good, their appointed time would have been ended for them. But We leave those who do not expect to meet Us in their transgression, wandering blindly.

And when harm touches a person, he calls upon Us lying on his side, sitting, or standing. But when We remove the harm from him, he passes on as though he had never called upon Us concerning harm that touched him. Thus the deeds of those who go beyond bounds are made appealing to them.
And We destroyed generations before you when they wronged themselves. Their messengers came to them with clear signs, but they were not ones to believe. Thus We recompense the criminal people.
Then We made you successors on the earth after them, so that We might see how you would act.

And when Our clear signs are recited to them, those who do not expect to meet Us say, “Bring a Qur’an other than this, or change it.” Say, “It is not for me to change it of my own will. I follow only what is revealed to me. Indeed, I fear, if I were to disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous Day.”
Say, “If Allah had willed, I would not have recited it to you, nor would He have made it known to you. I lived among you a lifetime before it. Will you not then reason?”
So who is more wrong than one who invents a lie against Allah or denies His signs? Indeed, the criminals will not succeed.

And they worship besides Allah what neither harms them nor benefits them, and they say, “These are our intercessors with Allah.” Say, “Do you inform Allah of something He does not know in the heavens or on the earth?” Glory be to Him, and exalted is He above what they associate.

And mankind was not but one community, then they differed. And if it had not been for a word that had already gone forth from your Lord, it would have been judged between them concerning what they differed about.
And they say, “Why has a sign not been sent down to him from his Lord?” Say, “The unseen belongs only to Allah. So wait; Indeed, I am with you among those who wait.”
And when We let people taste mercy after hardship has touched them, at once they plot against Our signs. Say, “ Allah is swifter in planning.” Indeed, Our messengers record what you plot.

He is the One who enables you to travel on land and sea. Until, when you are in ships and they sail with them by a pleasant wind and they rejoice in it, a violent wind comes upon them and waves come to them from every side, and they think they are surroun ded. They call upon Allah, devoting the religion purely to Him, “If You save us from this, we will surely be among the grateful.”

But when He saves them, they return to committing wrongdoing on the earth without right. O people, your wrongdoing is only against yourselves, an enjoyment of the life of this world. Then to Us is your return, and We will inform you of what you used to do.

The likeness of the life of this world is like water We send down from the sky, by which the plants of the earth mingle, from which people and livestock eat. Until, when the earth takes on its adornment and is beautified, and its people think they have power over it, Our command comes to it by night or by day, and We make it as a harvested field, as though it had not flourished yesterday. Thus We set out the signs clearly for a people who reflect.
And Allah calls to the Home of Peace and guides whom He wills to a straight path.
For those who do good is the best reward, and more. No darkness nor humiliation will cover their faces. Those are the companions of Paradise, remaining in it forever.

And for those who earn evil deeds, the recompense of an evil deed is its like, and humiliation will cover them. They will have no protector from Allah. It is as though their faces are covered with pieces of the darkness of night. Those are the companions of the Fire, remaining in it forever.

And on the Day We gather them all together, then We will say to those who associated partners, “Remain in your place, you and your partners.” Then We will separate them, and their partners will say, “It was not us that you used to worship.
So Allah is sufficient as a witness between us and you. We were indeed unaware of your worship.”
There, every soul will be tested for what it sent ahead. And they will be returned to Allah, their true Master, and what they used to invent will abandon them.

Say, “Who provides for you from the heavens and the earth? Or who controls hearing and sight? And who brings the living out of the dead and brings the dead out of the living? And who directs all affairs?” They will say, “ Allah.” So say, “Will you not then be mindful?”
That is Allah, your true Lord. So what is there after truth except error? So how are you turned away?
Thus the word of your Lord has been fulfilled against those who are rebellious, that they will not believe.
Say, “Is there any of your partners who begins creation, then repeats it?” Say, “ Allah begins creation, then repeats it. So how are you turned away?”

Say, “Is there any of your partners who guides to the truth?” Say, “ Allah guides to the truth. So is the One who guides to the truth more deserving to be followed, or one who does not guide unless he is guided? So what is wrong with you? How do you judge?”
And most of them follow nothing but assumption. Indeed, assumption does not avail anything against the truth. Indeed, Allah knows well what they do.

And this Qur’an could not have been invented apart from Allah, but it is a confirmation of what came before it and a detailed explanation of the Book, in which there is no doubt, from the Lord of all worlds.
Or do they say, “He has invented it”? Say, “Then bring a surah like it and call upon whomever you can besides Allah, if you are truthful.”
But they denied what they did not encompass in knowledge, and whose interpretation has not yet come to them. Thus those before them denied. So see how the outcome of the wrongdoers was.
And among them are those who believe in it, and among them are those who do not believe in it. And your Lord knows best the corrupters.
And if they deny you, then say, “For me are my deeds, and for you are your deeds. You are free of what I do, and I am free of what you do.”
And among them are those who listen to you. But can you make the deaf hear, even if they do not understand?
And among them are those who look at you. But can you guide the blind, even if they do not see?
Indeed, Allah does not wrong the people at all, but it is the people who wrong themselves.

And on the Day He gathers them, it will be as though they had not remained except for an hour of a day, recognising one another. Those who denied the meeting with Allah will have lost, and they were not guided.
Whether We show you some of what We promise them, or cause you to die before that, to Us is their return. Then Allah is a witness over what they do.
And for every community there is a messenger. Then, when their messenger comes, judgment is passed between them with justice, and they are not wronged.
And they say, “When will this promise be, if you are truthful?”

Say, “I have no power over harm or benefit for myself, except what Allah wills. For every community there is an appointed time. When their time comes, they cannot delay it by an hour, nor can they bring it forward.”
Say, “Have you considered, if His punishment were to come upon you by night or by day, what part of it would the criminals seek to hasten?”
Then, when it falls upon you, will you then believe in it? Now? While before you were seeking to hasten it?
Then it will be said to those who wronged themselves, “T aste the punishment of eternity. Are you recompensed except for what you used to earn?”
And they ask you, “Is it true?” Say, “Y es, by my Lord. It is surely true, and you cannot escape.”

And if every soul that wronged itself had all that is on the earth, it would offer it in ransom. And they will conceal regret when they see the punishment. And judgment will be passed between them with justice, and they will not be wronged.
Be aware: to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Be aware: the promise of Allah is true, but most of them do not know.
He gives life and causes death, and to Him you will be returned.
O people, there has come to you an admonition from your Lord, a healing for what is in the hearts, and guidance and mercy for the believers.
Say, “In the bounty of Allah and in His mercy, in that let them rejoice. It is better than what they accumulate.”
Say, “Have you considered what Allah has sent down for you of provision, and you have made some of it unlawful and some lawful?” Say, “Has Allah permitted you, or are you inventing against Allah?”
And what do those who invent lies against Allah think of the Day of Resurrection? Indeed, Allah is full of bounty toward the people, but most of them are not grateful.

And you are not engaged in any matter, nor do you recite from it any portion of the Qur’an, nor do you do any deed, except that We are witnesses over you while you are occupied with it. Not even the weight of an atom escapes your Lord on the earth or in th e heavens, nor anything smaller than that or greater, except that it is in a clear record.
Surely, the allies of Allah have no fear, nor will they grieve,
those who believed and were mindful of Him.
For them is good news in the life of this world and in the Hereafter. There is no change in the words of Allah. That is the great success.
And do not let their words grieve you. Indeed, all honour belongs to Allah. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.

Surely, to Allah belongs whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth. And those who call upon partners besides Allah do not follow partners; they follow nothing but assumption, and they do nothing but guess.
He is the One who made the night for you to rest in, and the day to see. Indeed, in that are signs for a people who listen.

They say, “ Allah has taken a son.” Glory be to Him. He is Free of need. T o Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. You have no authority for this. Do you say about Allah what you do not know?
Say, “Indeed, those who invent lies against Allah will not succeed.”
An enjoyment in this world, then to Us is their return, then We will make them taste the severe punishment because they used to disbelieve.

And recite to them the account of Nū ḥ, when he said to his people, “O my people, if my standing and my reminding you of the signs of Allah have become burdensome to you, then upon Allah I place my trust. So resolve your plan together with your partners, then let your plan not be unclear to you. Then carry it out against me, and give me no respite.
But if you turn away, then I have not asked you for any reward. My reward is only from Allah, and I have been commanded to be among those who submit.”
But they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and We made them successors, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. So see how the outcome was of those who were warned.

Then after him We sent messengers to their peoples. They came to them with clear signs, but they were not ones to believe in what they had already denied before. Thus We seal the hearts of those who transgress.
Then after them We sent Mūsā and Hārūn to Firʿawn and his chiefs with Our signs, but they were arrogant and were a criminal people.
So when the truth came to them from Us, they said, “This is clearly magic.”
Mūsā said, “Do you say this about the truth when it has come to you? Is this magic? And magicians do not succeed.”
They said, “Have you come to us to turn us away from what we found our fathers upon, so that greatness may belong to you two in the land? We will not believe you.”
And Firʿawn said, “Bring to me every knowledgeable magician.”
So when the magicians came, Mūsā said to them, “Cast what you are going to cast.”
Then when they had cast, Mūsā said, “What you have brought is magic. Indeed, Allah will bring it to nothing. Allah does not set right the work of corrupters.
And Allah establishes the truth by His words, even though the criminals detest it.”

But no one believed Mūsā except a small group from among his people, out of fear of Firʿawn and their chiefs, lest they persecute them. And indeed, Firʿawn was exalted in the land, and indeed, he was among those who went beyond bounds.
And Mūsā said, “O my people, if you have believed in Allah, then place your trust in Him, if you are those who submit.”
So they said, “Upon Allah we place our trust. Our Lord, do not make us a trial for the wrongdoing people,
and save us by Your mercy from the disbelieving people.”
And We revealed to Mūsā and his brother: “Settle your people in houses in Egypt, make your houses places of prayer, uphold the prayer, and give good news to the believers.”

And Mūsā said, “Our Lord, You have given Firʿawn and his chiefs adornment and wealth in the life of this world, our Lord, so that they may lead astray from Your path. Our Lord, wipe out their wealth and harden their hearts, so that they will not believe until they see the painful punishment.”
He said, “Your supplication has been answered. So remain upright, and do not follow the way of those who do not know.”

And We brought the Children of Isrā’īl across the sea. Then Firʿawn and his forces pursued them in tyranny and hostility. Until, when drowning overtook him, he said, “I believe that there is no god except the One in whom the Children of Isrā’īl believe, and I am among those who submit.”
Now? While before you rebelled and were among the corrupters?
So today We will preserve your body, so that you may be a sign for those after you. And indeed, many people are heedless of Our signs.

And We certainly settled the Children of Isrā’īl in a secure place, and provided them with good things. And they did not differ until knowledge came to them. Indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning what they used to differ about.

So if you are in doubt about what We have revealed to you, then ask those who have been reading the Book before you. The truth has certainly come to you from your Lord, so do not be among the doubters.
And do not be among those who deny the signs of Allah, lest you be among the losers.
Indeed, those against whom the word of your Lord has been fulfilled will not believe,
even if every sign comes to them, until they see the painful punishment.

Then why was there not a single town that believed and benefited from its belief, except the people of Yūnus? When they believed, We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in the life of this world, and We allowed them enjoyment for a time.
And if your Lord had willed, all those on the earth would have believed, all of them entirely. So will you compel the people until they become believers?
And it is not for any soul to believe except by the permission of Allah. And He places defilement upon those who do not use reason.
Say, “Look at what is in the heavens and the earth.” But signs and warnings do not benefit a people who do not believe.
So do they await anything except the likes of the days of those who passed before them? Say, “Then wait. Indeed, I am with you among those who wait.”
Then We save Our messengers and those who believe. Thus it is binding upon Us that We save the believers.

Say, “O people, if you are in doubt about my religion, then I do not worship those whom you worship besides Allah. But I worship Allah, who will take your souls. And I have been commanded to be among the believers,
and to set your face toward the religion, upright, and never be among the associators.”
And do not call upon besides Allah what neither benefits you nor harms you. For if you do, then you would be among the wrongdoers.

And if Allah touches you with harm, none can remove it except Him. And if He intends good for you, none can repel His bounty. He causes it to reach whom He wills among His servants. And He is Most-Forgiving, Ever- Merciful.
Say, “O people, the truth has come to you from your Lord. So whoever is guided is guided for his own soul, and whoever goes astray goes astray only against it. And I am not a guardian over you.”
And follow what has been revealed to you, and be patient until Allah judges. And He is the best of judges.

surahs.name: Hūd
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Rā.  A Book whose verses have been perfected, then set out clearly, from One who is All-Wise, All-Aware,
that you worship none but Allah. Indeed, I am to you from Him a warner and a bringer of good news.

And that you seek forgiveness from your Lord, then turn to Him in repentance. He will grant you good enjoyment for an appointed time and give His bounty to everyone of merit. But if you turn away, then indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a great Day.
T o Allah is your return, and He has power over everything.

Unquestionably, they fold their chests to hide from Him. But even when they cover themselves with their garments, He knows what they conceal and what they reveal. Indeed, He knows what is within the hearts.
And there is no creature on the earth except that its provision is upon Allah. And He knows its dwelling place and its resting place. All of it is in a clear record.

And He is the One who created the heavens and the earth in six days, while His Throne was upon the water, so that He may test you as to which of you is best in deeds. But if you say, “You will surely be raised after death,” those who disbelieve will surely say, “This is nothing but clear magic.”

And if We delay the punishment from them until an appointed time, they will surely say, “What is holding it back?” Surely, on the Day it comes to them, it will not be turned away from them, and what they used to mock will surround them.
And if We let a person taste mercy from Us, then We withdraw it from him, he becomes despairing, ungrateful.
But if We let him taste blessing after hardship has touched him, he surely says, “T roubles have departed from me.” Indeed, he is exultant, boastful.
Except for those who are patient and do righteous deeds. Those will have forgiveness and a great reward.

So perhaps you would leave out some of what is revealed to you, and your chest would be constrained by it, because they say, “Why has a treasure not been sent down to him, or an angel not come with him?” You are only a warner, and Allah is a guardian over everything.
Or do they say, “He has invented it”? Say, “Then bring ten invented surahs like it, and call upon whomever you can besides Allah, if you are truthful.”
But if they do not respond to you, then know that it has been sent down with the knowledge of Allah, and that there is no god except Him. So will you then submit?
Those who desire the life of this world and its adornment, We will fully repay them for their deeds in it, and they will not be deprived therein.
Those are the ones for whom there is nothing in the Hereafter except the Fire. What they worked for has come to nothing, and what they used to do is void.

Is the one who stands on clear proof from his Lord, confirmed by a witness from Him, and preceded by the Book of Mūsā as a guide and a mercy, like one who is not? Those believe in it. And whoever disbelieves in it from among the factions, the Fire is his appointed place. So do not be in doubt about it. Surely, it is the truth from your Lord, but most people do not believe.

And who is more wrong than one who invents a lie against Allah? Those will be presented before their Lord, and the witnesses will say, “These are the ones who lied against their Lord.” Surely, the curse of Allah is upon the wrongdoers,
those who bar others from the way of Allah and seek to make it crooked, and they disbelieve in the Hereafter.
They will not escape on the earth, and they have no protectors besides Allah. The punishment will be doubled for them. They were not able to hear, nor did they see.
Those are the ones who have lost their own souls, and what they used to invent has vanished from them.
Without doubt, in the Hereafter they will be the greatest losers.
Indeed, those who believe, do righteous deeds, and humble themselves before their Lord, those are the companions of Paradise, remaining in it forever.
The likeness of the two groups is like the blind and the deaf compared with the seeing and the hearing. Are they equal in likeness? Will you not then reflect?
And We certainly sent Nūḥ to his people: “ Indeed, I am to you a clear warner,
that you worship none but Allah. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a painful Day.”

But the chiefs among those who disbelieved from his people said, “We do not see you as anything but a man like ourselves, and we do not see that anyone follows you except the lowest among us, rash in judgment. And we do not see that you have any advantage over us. Rather, we think you are liars.”

He said, “O my people, have you considered, if I am upon clear proof from my Lord and He has given me mercy from Himself, but it has been hidden from you, should we force it upon you while you are averse to it?

And O my people, I do not ask you for wealth in return for it. My reward is only from Allah. And I will not drive away those who believe. Indeed, they will meet their Lord. But I see that you are an ignorant people.
And O my people, who would protect me from Allah if I were to drive them away? Will you not then reflect?”

And I do not say to you that I possess the treasures of Allah, nor that I know the unseen, nor do I say that I am an angel. Nor do I say of those whom your eyes look down upon that Allah will never grant them any good. Allah knows best what is in their so uls. Indeed, then I would be among the wrongdoers.”
They said, “O Nū ḥ, you have disputed with us and prolonged dispute with us, so bring us what you promise, if you are among the truthful.”
He said, “ Allah will bring it to you if He wills, and you will not escape.
And my advice will not benefit you, even if I wish to advise you, if Allah intends to leave you astray. He is your Lord, and to Him you will be returned.”
Or do they say, “He has invented it”? Say, “If I have invented it, then upon me is my crime, and I am free of the crimes you commit.”
And it was revealed to Nūḥ that none of your people will believe except those who have already believed, so do not grieve over what they used to do.
And construct the Ark under Our watch and by Our instruction, and do not address Me concerning those who wronged themselves. They are to be drowned.
And he was constructing the Ark, and whenever chiefs from his people passed by him, they mocked him. He said, “If you mock us, then we will mock you just as you mock,
and you will soon know upon whom a punishment will come that will disgrace him, and upon whom a lasting punishment will fall.”

Until, when Our command came and the oven burst forth, We said, “T ake on board a pair from every kind, and your family, except those against whom the decree has already been given, and those who believe.” And none believed with him except a few.
And he said, “Embark upon it. In the name of Allah is its course and its anchoring. Indeed, my Lord is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.”
And it sailed with them amid waves like mountains, and Nūḥ called out to his son, who was apart, “O my son, embark with us and do not be with the disbelievers.”

He said, “I will take refuge on a mountain that will protect me from the water.” He said, “There is no protector today from the command of Allah, except for whom He shows mercy.” And the waves came between them, and he was among the drowned.

And it was said, “O earth, swallow your water, and O sky, withhold.” And the water subsided, the matter was concluded, and it came to rest upon al - Jūdī. And it was said, “ Away with the wrongdoing people.”
And Nūḥ called upon his Lord and said, “My Lord, my son is from my family, and Your promise is true, and You are the most just of judges.”
He said, “O Nū ḥ, he is not from your family. Indeed, his conduct was unrighteous. So do not ask Me about that of which you have no knowledge. I advise you, lest you be among the ignorant.”
He said, “My Lord, I seek refuge in You from asking You about that of which I have no knowledge. And if You do not forgive me and have mercy upon me, I will be among the losers.”

It was said, “O Nūḥ, disembark in peace from Us, and blessings upon you and upon communities from those with you. And there are communities whom We will grant enjoyment, then a painful punishment will touch them from Us.”
That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal to you. You did not know it, nor did your people, before this. So be patient. Indeed, the outcome belongs to those who are mindful.
And to ʿĀd We sent their brother Hūd. He said, “O my people, worship Allah. You have no god other than Him. You are nothing but inventors.”
He said, “O my people, I do not ask you for any reward for it. My reward is only from the One who created me. Will you not then reason?

And O my people, seek forgiveness from your Lord, then turn to Him in repentance. He will send the sky upon you in abundant rain and increase you in strength upon your strength. And do not turn away as criminals.”
They said, “O Hūd, you have not brought us any clear proof, and we will not abandon our gods for your word, nor are we believers in you.
We say only that some of our gods have struck you with evil.” He said, “Indeed, I call Allah to witness, and you bear witness, that I am free from what you associate,
besides Him. So plot against me, all of you, then give me no respite.
Indeed, I have placed my trust in Allah, my Lord and your Lord. There is no creature except that He holds it by its forelock. Indeed, my Lord is upon a straight path.”

But if you turn away, then I have conveyed to you what I was sent with. And my Lord will replace you with another people, and you will not harm Him at all. Indeed, my Lord is a Guardian over everything.
And when Our command came, We saved Hūd and those who believed with him by mercy from Us, and We saved them from a severe punishment.
And that was ʿĀd. They denied the signs of their Lord, disobeyed His messengers, and followed the command of every obstinate tyrant.
And they were followed in this world by a curse, and on the Day of Resurrection as well. Unquestionably, ʿĀd disbelieved in their Lord. Away with ʿĀd, the people of Hūd.

And to Thamūd We sent their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said, “O my people, worship Allah. You have no god other than Him. He brought you into being from the earth and settled you upon it. So seek forgiveness from Him, then turn to Him in repentance. Indeed, my Lord is near and responsive.”

They said, “O Ṣāliḥ, you were among us one we placed hope in before this. Do you now forbid us from worshipping what our fathers worshipped? And we are in serious doubt about what you are calling us to.”

He said, “O my people, have you considered: if I am upon clear proof from my Lord, and He has granted me mercy from Himself, who would protect me from Allah if I were to disobey Him? You would only increase me in loss.
And O my people, this is the she-camel of Allah, a sign for you. So let her graze freely on the earth of Allah, and do not harm her, or a swift punishment will seize you.”
But they slaughtered her. So he said, “Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days. That is a promise that will not be denied.”
So when Our command came, We saved Ṣāliḥ and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us, and from the disgrace of that Day. Indeed, your Lord is the All-Strong, the All-Mighty.
And the blast seized those who wronged themselves, and they fell lifeless in their homes,
as though they had never lived there. Surely, Thamūd disbelieved in their Lord. Away with Thamūd
And indeed, Our messengers came to Ibrāhīm with good news. They said, “Peace.” He said, “Peace,” and he did not delay in bringing a roasted calf.
But when he saw their hands not reaching toward it, he felt uneasy about them and sensed fear. They said, “Do not fear. We have been sent to the people of Lūṭ.”
And his wife was standing nearby, and she laughed. Then We gave her good news of Isḥāq, and after Isḥāq, Y aʿqūb.
She said, “W oe to me! Shall I give birth when I am an old woman, and this husband of mine is an old man? Indeed, this is something astonishing.”
They said, “ Are you astonished at the command of Allah? The mercy of Allah and His blessings are upon you, people of the household. Indeed, He is All-Praiseworthy, Most-Glorious.”
Then when the fear left Ibrāhīm and the good news came to him, he began to plead with Us concerning the people of Lūṭ.
Indeed, Ibrāhīm was forbearing, tender-hearted, and constantly turning to Allah.
“O Ibrāhīm, turn away from this. The command of your Lord has already come, and a punishment that cannot be turned back is coming to them.”
And when Our messengers came to Lūṭ, he was distressed for them and felt helpless to protect them. He said, “This is a difficult day.”

And his people came rushing toward him, and they had long been committing evil deeds. He said, “O my people, these are my daughters. They are purer for you. So be mindful of Allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests. Is there not among you a man of sound judgment?”
They said, “You know well that we have no claim on your daughters, and you surely know what we desire.”
He said, “If only I had strength against you, or could take refuge in a strong support.”

They said, “O Lūṭ, we are messengers of your Lord. They will never reach you. So set out with your family during the night, and let none of you look back, except your wife. What will strike them will strike her. Their appointed time is the morning. Is not the morning near?”
So when Our command came, We turned it upside down, and We rained upon it stones of baked clay, one after another,
marked with your Lord. And it is not far from the wrongdoers.

And to Madyan We sent their brother Shuʿayb. He said, “O my people, worship Allah. You have no god other than Him. And do not give short measure or weight. I see you in prosperity, but I fear for you the punishment of an all-encompassing Day.
And O my people, give full measure and weight with justice, and do not deprive people of their due, and do not spread corruption in the land.
What remains with Allah is better for you, if you are believers. And I am not a guardian over you.”
They said, “O Shuʿayb, does your prayer command you that we leave what our fathers worshipped, or that we not do with our wealth what we please? Indeed, you are the forbearing, the wise.”

He said, “O my people, have you considered: if I am upon clear proof from my Lord, and He has provided me with good provision from Him, should I disobey Him? I do not wish to oppose you in what I forbid you. I only seek set things right as far as I am able. And my success is only through Allah. Upon Him I rely, and to Him I turn.

And O my people, do not let hostility toward me lead you into sin, lest what struck the people of Nūḥ, or the people of Hūd, or the people of Ṣāliḥ strike you. And the people of Lūṭ are not far from you.
And seek forgiveness from your Lord, then turn to Him in repentance. Indeed, my Lord is Ever-Merciful, Most-Loving.”
They said, “O Shuʿayb, we do not understand much of what you say, and we see you as weak among us. Were it not for your clan, we would have stoned you. You are not powerful against us.”
He said, “O my people, is my clan more honoured by you than Allah, that you have cast Him behind your backs? Indeed, my Lord encompasses what you do.
And O my people, act according to your position, and I will act. You will soon know upon whom a punishment will come that will disgrace him, and who is a liar. And watch; I too am watching with you.”
And when Our command came, We saved Shuʿayb and those who believed with him by mercy from Us, and the blast seized those who wronged themselves, and they fell lifeless in their homes,
as though they had never lived there. Away with Madyan, just as Thamūd was cast away.
And We certainly sent Mūsā with Our signs and a clear authority,
to Firʿawn and his chiefs, but they followed the command of Firʿawn, and the command of Firʿawn was not rightly guided.
He will lead his people on the Day of Resurrection and bring them into the Fire. And how wretched is the place they are brought to.
And they were followed in this world by a curse, and on the Day of Resurrection as well. How wretched is the gift that is given.
That is from the news of the towns which We recount to you. Among them are some still standing and some reduced to ruins.

And We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves. And their gods whom they called upon besides Allah did not benefit them at all when the command of your Lord came. They only increased them in ruin.
Such is the seizing of your Lord when He seizes the towns while they are doing wrong. Indeed, His seizing is painful and severe.
Indeed, in that is a sign for one who fears the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a Day when the people will be gathered, and that is a Day witnessed.
And We do not delay it except for a term determined.
On the Day it comes, no soul will speak except by His permission. And among them will be the wretched and the joyful.
As for those who are wretched, they will be in the Fire. For them therein is sighing and crying,
remaining in it as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord wills. Indeed, your Lord does what He intends.
And as for those who are joyful, they will be in Paradise, remaining in it as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord wills, a gift that will never end.
So do not be in doubt about what these people worship. They worship only as their fathers worshipped before. And We will surely give them their full share, without reduction.

And We certainly gave Mūsā the Book, but differences arose concerning it. And if it had not been for a word that had already gone forth from your Lord, it would have been judged between them. And indeed, they are in troubling doubt about it.
And indeed, your Lord will fully repay each of them for their deeds. He is fully aware of what they do.
So remain upright as you have been commanded, and those who turn back with you, and do not overstep. Indeed, He sees what you do.
And do not incline toward those who do wrong, lest the Fire touch you. And you will have no protectors besides Allah, nor will you be helped.
And uphold the prayer at the two ends of the day and in the early part of the night. Indeed, good deeds remove bad deeds. That is a reminder for those who remember.
And be patient. Indeed, Allah does not let the reward of those who do good be lost.

So why were there not, among the generations before you, people of insight who forbade corruption on the earth, except a few whom We saved among them? But those who did wrong pursued the luxury they were given, and they were criminals.
And your Lord would never destroy towns unjustly while their people were acting rightly.
And if your Lord had willed, He would have made people one community. But they continue to differ,
except those upon whom your Lord has mercy. And for that He created them. And the word of your Lord has been fulfilled: “I will surely fill Hell with jinn and people altogether.”
And all that We recount to you of the news of the messengers is to strengthen your heart by it. And in this has come to you the truth, an instruction, and a reminder for the believers.
And say to those who do not believe, “ Act according to your position. We too are acting.
And wait. Indeed, we too are waiting.”
And to Allah belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth, and to Him all matters return. So worship Him and rely upon Him. And your Lord is not unaware of what you do.

surahs.name: Yūsuf (Joseph)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Rā. These are the verses of the clear Book.
We have sent it down as an Arabic Qur’an, so that you may understand.
We relate to you the best of stories through what We have revealed to you of this Qur’an, though before it you were unaware.
When Yūsuf said to his father, “O my father, I saw eleven stars, and the sun and the moon; I saw them prostrating before me.”
He said, “My son, do not tell your vision to your brothers, or they will plot against you. Shayṭān is a clear enemy to mankind.

And so your Lord will choose you, teach you the understanding of events, and complete His blessing upon you and upon the family of Y aʿqūb, as He completed it before upon your fathers, Ibrāhīm and Isḥāq. Your Lord is All- Knowing and All-Wise.”
In Yūsuf and his brothers there are signs for those who ask.
When they said, “Yūsuf and his brother are more loved by our father than we are, even though we are a strong group. Our father is clearly mistaken.”
“Kill Yūsuf or cast him away to some distant land, so that your father’s attention may be for you alone, and after that you may become righteous people.”
One of them said, “Do not kill Yūsuf, but throw him into the depths of the well, so that some travellers may pick him up, if you are going to act.”
They said, “Our father, why do you not trust us with Yūsuf, when we truly wish him well?
Send him with us tomorrow so that he may enjoy himself and play, and we will surely take care of him.”
He said, “It grieves me that you should take him away, and I fear that a wolf may eat him while you are careless of him.”
They said, “If a wolf were to eat him while we are a strong group, then we would truly be losers.”
When they took him away and agreed to throw him into the depths of the well, We revealed to him, “You will surely inform them of this affair of theirs, while they do not realise.”
And they came to their father in the evening, weeping.
They said, “Our father, we went racing and left Yūsuf with our belongings, and a wolf ate him. But you would not believe us, even if we were telling the truth.”

And they brought his shirt with false blood upon it. He said, “No, your souls have persuaded you to something. So patience is most fitting. And Allah is the One whose help is sought against what you describe.”
And travellers came by, and they sent their water-drawer. He lowered his bucket and said, “Good news, here is a boy.” They hid him as merchandise, and Allah was fully aware of what they were doing.
And they sold him for a small price, a few counted coins, for they had little regard for him.

And the one from Egypt who bought him said to his wife, “Give him an honourable place. He may benefit us, or we may take him as a son.” And so We established Yūsuf in the land, and to teach him the understanding of events. Allah prevails over His affairs, but most people do not know.
And when he reached full maturity, We  gave him judgement and knowledge. This is how We reward those who do good.

And the woman in whose house he lived sought to draw him to herself. She locked the doors and said, “Come to me.” He said, “I seek refuge with Allah. He is my master, who has given me a good place. Indeed, the wrongdoers do not succeed.”

She certainly desired him, and he would have inclined towards her had he not seen a sign from his Lord. So it was, that We might turn away from him evil and immorality. Indeed, he was among Our chosen servants.

They both rushed to the door, and she tore his shirt from behind. They found her husband at the door. She said, “What should be the punishment of one who intended evil against your wife, except imprisonment or a painful punishment?”
He said, “It was she who sought to draw me to herself.” And a witness from her family testified, “If his shirt is torn from the front, then she is truthful and he is among the liars.
But if his shirt is torn from behind, then she has lied and he is among the truthful.”
So when he saw his shirt torn from behind, he said, “This is from the scheming of you women. Indeed, your scheming is great.”
“Yūsuf, turn away from this. And you, ask forgiveness for your sin. Indeed, you were among those who erred.”
And women in the city said, “The wife of the governor is trying to seduce her young servant. He has captured her heart with love. Indeed, we see her clearly astray.”

When she heard of their scheming, she sent for them and prepared a banquet for them. She gave each of them a knife, then said, “Come out before them.” When they saw him, they were struck by him and cut their hands, and they said, “ Allah forbid! This is not a human being. This is none but a noble angel.”

She said, “This is the one for whom you blamed me. I did seek to draw him to myself, but he held firm. And if he does not do what I command him, he will surely be imprisoned and will be among the disgraced.”
He said, “My Lord, prison is more dear to me than what they call me to. If You do not turn their plotting away from me, I may incline towards them and become among the ignorant.”
So his Lord responded to him and turned their plotting away from him. Indeed, He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
Then it seemed to them, after they had seen the signs, that they should imprison him for a time.

And two young men entered the prison with him. One of them said, “I see myself pressing wine.” And the other said, “I see myself carrying bread on my head, from which birds are eating. T ell us its interpretation. Indeed, we see you among those who do good.”

He said, “No food will come to you as provision except that I will inform you of its interpretation before it reaches you. This is from what my Lord has taught me. Indeed, I have left the way of people who do not believe in Allah and who deny the Hereafter.

And I follow the way of my fathers, Ibrāhīm, Isḥāq, and Y aʿqūb. It is not for us to associate anything with Allah. That is from the favour of Allah upon us and upon the people, but most people are not grateful.
O my fellow prisoners, are many divided lords better, or Allah, the One, the Overpowering?

You worship nothing besides Him except names you have named, you and your fathers, for which Allah has sent down no authority. Judgement belongs to Allah alone. He has commanded that you worship none but Him. That is the upright way, but most people do not know.

O my fellow prisoners, as for one of you, he will serve wine to his lord. As for the other, he will be crucified, and birds will eat from his head. The matter about which you both asked has been decided.
And he said to the one whom he thought would be saved, “Mention me to your lord.” But Shayṭān caused him to forget mentioning him to his lord, so he remained in prison for a few years.
And the king said, “I see seven fat cows eaten by seven lean ones, and seven green ears and others dry. O nobles, explain to me my vision, if you can interpret visions.”
They said, “Confused dreams, and we are not skilled in interpreting dreams.”
And the one who was saved among them remembered after a time and said, “I will inform you of its interpretation, so send me.”
“Yūsuf, man of truth, explain to us seven fat cows eaten by seven lean ones, and seven green ears and others dry, so that I may return to the people and that they may know.”
He said, “You will plant for seven years as usual. Whatever you harvest, leave it in its ears, except a little from which you eat.
Then after that will come seven hard years that will consume what you have stored for them, except a little which you will preserve.
Then after that will come a year in which people will be given rain, and in it they will press.”
And the king said, “Bring him to me.” But when the messenger came to him, he said, “Return to your lord and ask him about the women who cut their hands. Indeed, my Lord knows well their plotting.”

He said, “What was your situation when you tried to draw Yūsuf to yourselves?” They said, “ Allah forbid! We knew no evil of him.” The wife of the governor said, “Now the truth has come to light. I was the one who tried to draw him to myself, and he is surely among the truthful.”
“This is so that he may know that I did not betray him in his absence, and that Allah does not guide the scheming of the betrayers.”
“Y et I do not declare myself innocent. The soul certainly urges towards evil, except where my Lord shows mercy. Indeed, my Lord is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.”
And the king said, “Bring him to me; I will reserve him for myself.” Then when he spoke to him, he said, “T oday you are firmly established and trusted with us.”
He said, “Place me in charge of the storehouses of the land. Indeed, I am a careful guardian, knowledgeable.”
And so We established Yūsuf in the land, settling wherever he wished. We grant Our mercy to whom We will, and We do not allow the reward of those who do good to be lost.
And the reward of the Hereafter is better for those who believe and are mindful.
And Yūsuf’s brothers came and entered upon him. He recognised them, but they did not recognise him.
When he had supplied them with their provisions, he said, “Bring me a brother of yours from your father. Do you not see that I give full measure and that I am the best of hosts?
But if you do not bring him to me, then there will be no measure for you with me, and you must not come near me.”
They said, “We will try to persuade his father about him, and we will surely do so.”
And he said to his attendants, “Put their goods back into their saddlebags, so that they may recognise them when they return to their family, and perhaps they will come back.”
So when they returned to their father, they said, “Our father, the measure has been denied to us, so send our brother with us, and we will receive full measure. We will surely guard him.”
He said, “Should I trust you with him as I trusted you before with his brother? But Allah is the best guardian, and He is the Ever-Merciful of the merciful.”

And when they opened their baggage, they found their goods returned to them. They said, “Our father, what more could we want? These are our goods, returned to us. We will bring provisions for our family, guard our brother, and receive an extra camel-load. That is an easy measure.”

He said, “I will never send him with you until you give me a firm pledge before Allah that you will bring him back to me, unless you are completely overcome.” And when they gave him their pledge, he said, “ Allah is witness over what we say.”

And he said, “My sons, do not enter from one gate, but enter from different gates. I cannot protect you at all from Allah. Judgement belongs to Allah alone. In Him I place my trust, and in Him let all who trust place their trust.”

And when they entered as their father had instructed them, it did not protect them in any way from Allah, except for a need within Y aʿqūb which he fulfilled. Indeed, he possessed knowledge because of what We had taught him, but most people do not know.
And when they entered upon Yūsuf, he took his brother close to him and said, “I am your brother, so do not be distressed by what they used to do.”
Then when he had supplied them with their provisions, he placed the drinking cup in his brother’s bag. Then a caller announced, “O caravan, you are surely thieves.”
They said, turning towards them, “What is it that you are missing?”
They said, “We are missing the king’s cup. Whoever brings it will have a camel-load, and I guarantee it.”
They said, “By Allah, you know well that we did not come to cause corruption in the land, and we are not thieves.”
They said, “Then what will be its penalty, if you are liars?”
They said, “Its penalty is that the one in whose bag it is found will himself be the penalty. That is how we punish the wrongdoers.”

So he began with their bags before the bag of his brother, then he brought it out from his brother’s bag. Thus We planned for Yūsuf. He could not have taken his brother under the law of the king, except that Allah willed it. We raise in rank whom We will, and above everyone who has knowledge is One who knows more.

They said, “If he has stolen, then a brother of his stole before.” Yūsuf kept it within himself and did not reveal it to them. He said, “You are in a worse position, and Allah knows best what you describe.”
They said, “O governor, he has an elderly father, so take one of us in his place. Indeed, we see you among those who do good.”
He said, “ Allah forbid that we should take anyone except the one with whom we found our property. Indeed, we would then be wrongdoers.”

So when they lost hope in him, they withdrew to confer privately. The eldest of them said, “Do you not know that your father took a pledge from you before Allah, and before that you failed regarding Yūsuf? I will never leave this land until my father permits me, or Allah judges for me. And He is the best of judges.”
“Return to your father and say, ‘Our father, your son has stolen. We only testify to what we know, and we were not guardians of the unseen.
Ask the town in which we were, and the caravan with which we travelled. We are surely truthful.’”
He said, “No, your souls have persuaded you to something. So patience is most fitting. Perhaps Allah will bring them all back to me. Indeed, He is the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.”
And he turned away from them and said, “Oh my sorrow for Yūsuf,” and his eyes turned white from grief, as he was filled with sorrow.
They said, “By Allah, you will not cease remembering Yūsuf until you waste away or become among the perished.”
He said, “I only complain of my sorrow and grief to Allah, and I know from Allah what you do not know.
My sons, go and search for Yūsuf and his brother, and do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, none despairs of the mercy of Allah except the disbelieving people.”

Then when they entered upon him, they said, “O governor, hardship has touched us and our family, and we have brought goods of little worth. So give us full measure and be charitable to us. Indeed, Allah rewards those who give charity.”
He said, “Do you know what you did to Yūsuf and his brother when you were ignorant?”

They said, “ Are you truly Yūsuf?” He said, “I am Yūsuf, and this is my brother. Allah has favoured us. Surely, whoever is mindful and patient, then Allah does not allow the reward of those who do good to be lost.”
They said, “By Allah, Allah has certainly preferred you over us, and we were surely at fault.”
He said, “There is no blame upon you today. Allah will forgive you, and He is the Ever-Merciful of the merciful.”
“T ake this shirt of mine and cast it over my father’s face; he will regain his sight. Then bring your entire family to me.”
And when the caravan set out, their father said, “ Indeed, I sense the presence of Yūsuf, if you do not think me mistaken.”
