They said, “By Allah, you are still in your old confusion.”
Then when the bearer of good news arrived, he cast it over his face, and he regained his sight. He said, “Did I not tell you that I know from Allah what you do not know?”
They said, “Our father, ask forgiveness for our sins. We were surely at fault.”
He said, “I will ask my Lord to forgive you. Indeed, He is the Most- Forgiving, the Ever-Merciful.”
Then when they entered upon Yūsuf, he drew his parents close to himself and said, “Enter Egypt, if Allah wills, in safety.”

And he raised his parents upon the throne, and they all fell down before him in prostration. And he said, “My father, this is the fulfilment of my earlier vision. My Lord has made it real. He was good to me when He took me out of the prison, and brought y ou from the desert, after Shayṭān had stirred discord between me and my brothers. Indeed, my Lord is All-Subtle in what He wills. He is the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.”

“My Lord, You have given me authority and taught me the understanding of events. Creator of the heavens and the earth, You are my guardian in this world and the Hereafter. T ake me in submission to You, and join me with the righteous.”
That is from the unseen accounts which We reveal to you. You were not with them when they agreed upon their plan while they were plotting.
And most people, even if you are eager, will not believe.
And you do not ask them for any reward for it. It is only a reminder for all worlds.
And how many signs in the heavens and the earth do they pass by, while turning away from them.
And most of them do not believe in Allah without associating partners.
Do they feel secure that an overwhelming punishment from Allah will not come upon them, or that the Hour will not come upon them suddenly while they are unaware?
Say, “This is my way. I call to Allah with clear insight, I and those who follow me. Glory be to Allah, and I am not among those who associate partners with Him.”

And We did not send before you except men to whom We revealed, from among the people of the towns. Have they not travelled through the earth and seen how the end was of those before them? And the Home of the Hereafter is better for those who are mindful. Will you not then understand?

Until, when the messengers lost hope and thought that they had been denied, Our help came to them, and whoever We willed was saved. And Our punishment is not turned back from the people who commit crimes.

In their stories there is a lesson for those of understanding. It is not a fabricated speech, but a confirmation of what came before it, a clear explanation of everything, and guidance and mercy for a people who believe.

surahs.name: Ar-Raʿd (Thunder)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Mīm Rā. These are the verses of the Book. What has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth, but most people do not believe.

Allah is the One who raised the heavens without pillars that you can see. Then established Himself over the Throne.  And subjected the sun and the moon, each running for a fixed term. He directs all matters, making the signs clear, so that you may be certain of meeting your Lord.

And He is the One who spread out the earth, placed firm mountains and rivers upon it, and made pairs of every fruit. He covers the night with the day. Indeed, in this are signs for people who reflect.

And on the earth are neighbouring tracts, vineyards, crops, and date palms, growing from the same roots or from separate roots, watered with the same water, yet We make some of them better than others in taste. Indeed, in this are signs for people who use reason.

If you are amazed, then amazing is their saying, “When we are dust, will we really be created anew?” Those are the ones who disbelieved in their Lord. Those are the ones with shackles around their necks, and those are the companions of the Fire, remaining in it forever.

They ask you to hasten the punishment before the good, even though examples have passed before them. Your Lord is full of forgiveness toward people despite their wrongdoing, and your Lord is severe in punishment.
Those who disbelieve say, “Why has a sign not been sent down to him from his Lord?” You are only a warner, and for every people there is a guide.
Allah knows what every female carries, what the wombs reduce and what they increase. Everything with Him is by precise measure.
He knows the unseen and the visible. He is the Great, the Most High.
It is the same whether one of you speaks secretly or openly, hides in the night or moves about in the day.

For each person there are angels in succession, before him and behind him, guarding him by the command of Allah. Allah does not change the condition of a people until they change what is within themselves. And when Allah intends harm for a people, there is no turning it back, and they have no protector besides Him.
He is the One who shows you lightning, bringing fear and hope, and He produces the heavy clouds.

And the thunder glorifies Him with His praise, and so do the angels out of awe of Him. He sends thunderbolts and strikes with them whom He wills, yet they argue about Allah, while He is severe in might.

T o Him belongs the call of truth. Those whom they call upon besides Him do not respond to them at all, except like one who stretches out his hands to water so that it may reach his mouth, but it never reaches it. The call of the disbelievers is nothing but loss.
T o Allah prostrates whatever is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly, and so do their shadows in the mornings and evenings.

Say, “Who is the Lord of the heavens and the earth?” Say, “ Allah.” Say, “Have you taken protectors besides Him who have no power over benefit or harm even for themselves?” Say, “ Are the blind and the seeing equal? Or are darkness and light equal?” Or have  they assigned partners to Allah who created like His creation, so that the creation seemed similar to them? Say, “ Allah is the Creator of everything, and He is the One, the Overpowering.”

He sends down water from the sky, and valleys flow according to their measure, and the flood carries rising foam. And from what they heat in fire, seeking ornaments or tools, comes foam like it. Thus Allah presents the example of truth and falsehood. As for the foam, it vanishes away; but what benefits people remains on the earth. Thus Allah sets out the examples.

For those who respond to their Lord is the best outcome. And those who do not respond to Him, if they had all that is on the earth and the same again with it, they would surely offer it to ransom themselves. Those will have a terrible reckoning, and their refuge is Hell, and what a wretched resting place.
Is one who knows that what has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth like one who is blind? Only those with understanding take heed,
those who fulfil the covenant of Allah and do not break the bond.
those who keep joined what Allah has commanded to be joined, fear their Lord, and are afraid of a severe reckoning,

and those who are patient, seeking the face of their Lord, uphold the prayer, give from what We have provided them, secretly and openly, and repel evil with good. For such people there is the final home,

the Gardens of lasting residence, which they will enter along with whoever was righteous among their fathers, their spouses, and their descendants. And the angels will enter upon them from every gate,
“Peace be upon you for your patience.” How excellent is the final home.

But those who break the covenant of Allah after it has been affirmed, sever what Allah has commanded to be joined, and spread corruption in the land, upon them is the curse, and for them is the worst abode.
Allah extends provision for whom He wills and restricts it. They rejoice in the life of this world, but the life of this world, compared to the Hereafter, is only brief enjoyment.
Those who disbelieve say, “Why has a sign not been sent down to him from his Lord?” Say, “ Allah leads astray whom He wills and guides to Himself whoever turns back.”
Those who believe and whose hearts find rest in the remembrance of Allah. Indeed, in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find rest.
Those who believe and do good deeds, for them is glad news and a beautiful return.

Thus We have sent you among a community before which other communities have passed away, so that you may recite to them what We have revealed to you, while they disbelieve in the Most-Merciful. Say, “He is my Lord. There is no god except Him. In Him I place my trust, and to Him is my return.”

Even if there were a Qur’an by which mountains were moved, or the earth was torn apart, or the dead were made to speak, still the command belongs entirely to Allah. Do those who believe not realise that if Allah willed, He would guide all people? As for those who disbelieve, disaster will not cease to strike them for what they have done, or fall near their homes, until the promise of Allah comes. Indeed, Allah never breaks His promise.
Messengers before you were also mocked, but I gave respite to those who disbelieved. Then I seized them, and how was My punishment.

Is He who stands over every soul in what it has earned like one who does not? Y et they assign partners to Allah. Say, “Name them. Or are you informing Him of something He does not know on the earth, or is it only empty words?” Rather, the plotting of those who disbelieve has been made appealing to them, and they have been barred from the path. And whoever Allah leads astray has no guide.
For them is punishment in the life of this world, and the punishment of the Hereafter is more severe. They will have no protector from Allah.

The likeness of the Garden promised to those who are mindful: rivers flow beneath it, its provision is constant, and its shade everlasting. That is the end of those who are mindful, and the end of the disbelievers is the Fire.

Those to whom We gave the Book rejoice in what has been sent down to you, while among the factions are those who deny part of it. Say, “I have only been commanded to worship Allah and not associate anything with Him. T o Him I call, and to Him is my return.”
And thus We have sent it down as a judgement in Arabic. If you were to follow their desires after the knowledge that has come to you, you would have no protector or defender against Allah.
And We certainly sent messengers before you and gave them wives and children. It was not for a messenger to bring a sign except by the permission of Allah. For every term there is a decree.
Allah erases what He wills and confirms what He wills, and with Him is the Mother of the Book.
Whether We show you some of what We promise them, or take you before that, your duty is only to convey, and upon Us is the reckoning.
Do they not see that We come to the land, reducing it from its edges? Allah judges; there is no one who can reverse His judgement, and He is swift in reckoning.
Those before them also plotted, but all planning belongs to Allah. He knows what every soul earns. The disbelievers will soon know who will have the final home.
Those who disbelieve  say, “You are not a messenger.” Say, “ Allah is sufficient as a witness between me and you, and those who have knowledge of the Book.”

surahs.name: Ibrāhīm (Abraham)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Rā. A Book which We have sent down to you so that you may bring people out of darkness into the light, by the permission of their Lord, to the path of the All-Mighty, the All-Praiseworthy,
Allah, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And woe to the disbelievers from a severe punishment,
those who prefer the life of this world over the Hereafter, turn people away from the path of Allah, and seek to make it distorted. They are far astray.

We did not send any messenger except in the language of his people, so that he might make things clear to them. Then Allah leads astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

We certainly sent Mūsā with Our signs, saying, “Bring your people out of darkness into the light, and remind them of the days of Allah.” Indeed, in that are signs for everyone who is patient and grateful.

And when Mūsā said to his people, “Remember the blessing of Allah upon you, when He saved you from the people of Firʿawn, who were afflicting you with severe torment, slaughtering your sons and sparing your women. In that was a great trial from your Lord.”
And when your Lord proclaimed, “If you are grateful, I will surely increase you; but if you are ungrateful, then My punishment is severe.”
And Mūsā said, “If you and all who are on the earth were to disbelieve, Allah is indeed Free of need, All-Praiseworthy.”

Has the news not reached you of those before you, the people of Nūḥ, ʿĀd, and Thamūd, and those after them? None knows them except Allah. Their messengers came to them with clear signs, but they put their hands to their mouths and said, “We disbelieve in what you have been sent with, and we are in serious doubt about what you call us to.”

Their messengers said, “Is there any doubt about Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth? He calls you so that He may forgive you your sins and give you respite for a fixed term.” They said, “You are only human beings like us. You want to turn us away from what our fathers used to worship. So bring us a clear authority.”

Their messengers said to them, “We are only human beings like you, but Allah favours whom He wills among His servants. It is not for us to bring you any authority except by the permission of Allah. And in Allah let the believers place their trust.
And why should we not place our trust in Allah, when He has guided us to our paths? We will surely be patient over the harm you cause us. And in Allah let those who trust place their trust.”
Those who disbelieved said to their messengers, “We will surely drive you out of our land, or you must return to our way.” So their Lord revealed to them, “We will surely destroy the wrongdoers,
and We will surely cause you to dwell in the land after them. That is for those who fear My standing and fear My warning.”
And they sought a decisive judgement, and every stubborn tyrant was brought to loss.
Ahead of him is Hell, and he will be given water of pus to drink.
He will sip it but can hardly swallow it. Death will come to him from every direction, yet he will not die, and ahead of him is a harsh punishment.
The example of those who disbelieve  in their Lord: their deeds are like ashes scattered by the wind on a stormy day. They have no power over anything they earned. That is the farthest misguidance.
Do you not see that Allah created the heavens and the earth in truth? If He wills, He can remove you and bring a new creation,
and that is not difficult for Allah.

And they will all appear before Allah. The weak will say to those who were arrogant, “We were following you, so can you relieve us of any part of the punishment of Allah?” They will say, “If Allah had guided us, we would have guided you. It is the same for us whether we panic or endure patiently. There is no escape for us.”

And Shayṭān will say, when the matter has been decided, “Indeed, Allah promised you a true promise, and I promised you, then I betrayed you. I had no authority over you except that I invited you and you responded to me. So do not blame me, but blame yourselves. I cannot help you, nor can you help me. I reject your associating me with Allah before. Indeed, for the wrongdoers is a painful punishment.”
And those who believed and did good deeds will be admitted into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever by the permission of their Lord. Their greeting there will be, “Peace.”
Do you not see how Allah sets forth an example: a good word is like a good tree, its root firm and its branches in the sky,
giving its fruit at all times by the permission of its Lord. And Allah sets forth examples for people, so that they may take heed.
And the example of a bad word is like a bad tree, uprooted from the surface of the earth, having no stability.
Allah keeps firm those who believe with the firm word, in the life of this world and in the Hereafter. And Allah leads astray the wrongdoers. And Allah does whatever He wills.
Have you not seen those who exchanged the blessing of Allah for disbelief, and settled their people in the home of ruin,
Hell, in which they will burn? And how wretched is the place of settlement.
And they set up equals to Allah to lead people astray from His path. Say, “Enjoy yourselves, for your destination is the Fire.”

Say to My servants who believe that they should uphold the prayer and give from what We have provided them, secretly and openly, before a Day comes in which there will be no trading and no friendships.

Allah is the One who created the heavens and the earth, sent down water from the sky, and brought forth fruits as provision for you. He subjected the ships for you to sail through the sea by His command, and He subjected the rivers for you.
And He subjected for you the sun and the moon, constant in their courses, and He subjected for you the night and the day.
And He gave you from all that you asked of Him. If you were to count the blessings of Allah, you could not enumerate them. Indeed, mankind is most unjust and ungrateful.
And when Ibrāhīm said, “My Lord, make this city secure, and keep me and my sons away from worshipping idols.
My Lord, they have led many people astray. So whoever follows me is truly from me, and whoever disobeys me, then You are Most-Forgiving, Ever- Merciful.

Our Lord, I have settled some of my descendants in a barren valley near Your Sacred House, our Lord, so that they may uphold the prayer. So make hearts among the people incline towards them, and provide for them from fruits, so that they may be grateful.
Our Lord, You know what we conceal and what we declare. Nothing is hidden from Allah on the earth or in the heavens.
All praise belongs to Allah, who has granted me, in old age, Ismāʿīl and Isḥāq. Indeed, my Lord is the Hearer of supplication.
My Lord, make me one who upholds the prayer, and also from my descendants. Our Lord, accept my supplication.
Our Lord, forgive me, my parents, and the believers on the Day the reckoning is established.
Do not think that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do. He only delays them to a Day when eyes will stare in horror,
rushing forward, their heads raised, their eyes not blinking, and their hearts empty.

W arn the people of a Day when the punishment will come upon them, and those who did wrong will say, “Our Lord, give us a short reprieve; we will respond to Your call and follow the messengers.” Did you not swear before that there would be no passing away for you?
You lived in the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, and it was made clear to you how We dealt with them, and We set forth examples for you.
They plotted their plots, but their plotting is known to Allah, even if their plotting were such that mountains could be moved by it.
So do not think that Allah will break His promise to His messengers. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, Possessor of retribution.
On the Day the earth is changed into another earth, and the heavens as well, and they will appear before Allah, the One, the Overpowering.
And you will see the criminals on that Day bound together in chains,
their garments of tar, and the Fire covering their faces,
so that Allah may repay every soul for what it earned. Indeed, Allah is swift in reckoning.
This is a message for people, so that they may be warned by it, know that He is One, and so that people of understanding may take heed.

surahs.name: Al-Ḥijr (The Rocky Tract)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Rā. These are the verses of the Book and a clear Qur’an.
There will be a time when those who disbelieved will wish that they had been those who submitted.
Leave them to eat and enjoy themselves, and let hope distract them. They will soon know.
We did not destroy any town except that it had a known appointed term.
No community can advance its term, nor can it delay it.
They say, “You to whom the Reminder has been sent down, you are surely mad.”
“Why do you not bring the angels to us, if you are truthful?”
We do not send down the angels except with the truth, and then they would not be given any respite.
Indeed, We have sent down the Reminder, and We will surely preserve it.
We certainly sent messengers before you among the earlier communities.
No messenger ever came to them except that they mocked him.
In this way We let it pass into the hearts of the criminals.
They do not believe in it, even though the way of earlier people has already passed.
Even if We were to open for them a gate from the sky and they were to keep ascending through it,
they would surely say, “Our eyes have been dazzled. Rather, we are a people under a spell.”
And We have placed constellations in the sky and made it pleasing to those who look,
and We have protected it from every accursed devil,
except one who snatches a fragment, and is then pursued by a clear flame.
And the earth We have spread out, placed firm mountains upon it, and caused everything to grow in balanced measure.
And We placed for you means of livelihood in it, and for those you do not provide for.
There is nothing whose stores are not with Us, and We do not send it down except in a known measure.
And We send the fertilising winds, then send down water from the sky and give it to you to drink, and it is not you who store it.
Indeed, it is We who give life and cause death, and We are the inheritors.
And We already know those among you who came before, and We already know those who will come later.
And your Lord will surely gather them all together. Indeed, He is All- Wise, All-Knowing.
And We created mankind from sounding clay, from dark moulded mud.
And the jinn We created before from scorching fire.
And when your Lord said to the angels, “I am creating a human being from sounding clay, from dark moulded mud.
So when I have shaped him and breathed into him from My spirit, then fall down before him in prostration.”
So the angels prostrated, all of them together,
except Iblīs; he refused to be among those who prostrated.
He said, “O Iblīs, what is it that you are not among those who prostrated?”
He said, “I am not one to prostrate to a human being whom You created from sounding clay, from dark moulded mud.”
He said, “Then get out of it, for you are accursed.
And upon you is the curse until the Day of Judgement.”
He said, “My Lord, then give me respite until the Day they are resurrected.”
He said, “You are among those given respite,
until the Day of the appointed time.”
He said, “My Lord, because You have led me astray, I will surely make things appealing to them on the earth, and I will mislead them all,
except Your devoted servants among them.”
He said, “This is a straight path to Me.
Indeed, you will have no authority over My servants, except those who follow you among the misguided.
And Hell is the promised place for them all.
It has seven gates; for each gate is an assigned portion of them.”
Those who are mindful of Allah will be among gardens and springs.
“Enter them in peace and security.”
And We will remove whatever is in their hearts of resentment. They will be brothers, upon couches, facing one another.
No fatigue will touch them there, nor will they ever be removed from it.
Inform My servants that I am the Most-Forgiving, the Ever-Merciful,
and that My punishment is the painful punishment.
And inform them about the guests of Ibrāhīm,
when they entered upon him and said, “Peace.” He said, “Indeed, we are fearful of you.”
They said, “Do not be afraid. We give you good news of a boy endowed with knowledge.”
He said, “Do you give me good news despite old age having reached me? So what good news is it that you bring?”
They said, “We give you good news in truth, so do not be among those who despair.”
He said, “ And who despairs of the mercy of his Lord except those who are astray?”
He said, “So what is your mission, O messengers?”
They said, “We have been sent to a people who are criminals,
except the family of Lūṭ. We will surely save them all,
except his wife. We have decreed that she will surely be among those who remain behind.”
Then when the messengers came to the family of Lūṭ,
he said, “You are a people unknown.”
They said, “Rather, we have come to you with what they used to doubt.
And we have come to you with the truth, and indeed we are truthful.
So set out with your family during a part of the night, and follow behind them, and let none of you look back, and go where you are commanded.”
And We conveyed to him that decree: that the last of those people would be cut off by morning.
And the people of the city came rejoicing.
He said, “These are my guests, so do not disgrace me.
Fear Allah and do not humiliate me.”
They said, “Did we not forbid you from protecting anyone?”
He said, “These are my daughters, if you are going to act.”
By your life, they were truly wandering blindly in their intoxication.
So the blast seized them at sunrise.
And We turned it upside down and rained upon them stones of baked clay.
Indeed, in that are signs for those who discern.
And indeed, it lies along a known route.
Indeed, in that is a sign for the believers.
And the people of the Thicket were surely wrongdoers,
so We took retribution from them. And both lie along a clear road.
And the people of Al-Ḥijr denied the messengers.
And We had given them Our signs, but they turned away from them.
And they carved out homes from the mountains, feeling secure.
But the blast seized them in the morning,
and what they used to earn did not benefit them at all.
We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them except with truth. And indeed, the Hour is surely coming, so overlook with gracious overlooking.
Indeed, your Lord is the All-Creating, the All-Knowing.
And We have certainly given you seven of the repeated verses and the great Qur’an.
Do not stretch your eyes toward what We have granted some groups of them to enjoy, and do not grieve over them. And lower your wing to the believers.
And say, “Indeed, I am the clear warner.”
Just as We sent down upon those who divided,
those who made the Qur’an into parts.
So by your Lord, We will surely question them all
about what they used to do.
So proclaim what you are commanded, and turn away from those who associate partners with Allah.
Indeed, We are sufficient for you against the mockers,
those who set up another god alongside Allah. They will soon know.
And We certainly know that your chest is strained by what they say.
So glorify the praise of your Lord and be among those who prostrate,
and worship your Lord until certainty comes to you.

surahs.name: An-Naḥl (The Bee)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: The command of Allah has come, so do not seek to hasten it. Glory be to Him, and exalted is He above what they associate.
He sends down the angels with the Spirit by His command upon whom He wills of His servants, saying, “W arn that there is no god except Me, so be mindful of Me.”
He created the heavens and the earth in truth. Exalted is He above what they associate.
He created mankind from a drop, yet suddenly he becomes an open disputant.
And He created the livestock; in them there is warmth and benefit for you, and from them you eat.
And for you in them is beauty when you bring them in and when you send them out to pasture.
And they carry your loads to lands you could not reach except with great hardship. Indeed, your Lord is Most-Gentle and Ever-Merciful.
And He created horses, mules, and donkeys for you to ride and as adornment. And He creates what you do not know.
And it is for Allah to make the path straight, and among the paths are those that turn away. And if He had willed, He would have guided you all.
He is the One who sent down water from the sky; from it you drink, and from it grow the plants in which you pasture.
With it He causes crops to grow for you, olives, date palms, grapes, and every kind of fruit. Indeed, in that is a sign for people who reflect.
And He has subjected for you the night and the day, the sun and the moon, and the stars are subjected by His command. Indeed, in that are signs for people who use reason.
And whatever He has scattered for you on the earth, diverse in its colours. Indeed, in that is a sign for people who take heed.

And He is the One who subjected the sea, so that you may eat from it tender meat and extract from it ornaments which you wear. And you see the ships ploughing through it, so that you may seek from His bounty and so that you may be grateful.
And He placed firm mountains upon the earth, so that it would not shake with you, and rivers and pathways, so that you may be guided,
and landmarks. And by the stars they are guided.
Is One who creates like one who does not create? Will you not then take heed?
And if you were to count the blessings of Allah, you could not enumerate them. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
And Allah knows what you conceal and what you reveal.
Those whom they call upon besides Allah create nothing, while they themselves are created.
They are lifeless, not alive, and they do not know when they will be raised.
Your god is One God. As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, their hearts deny it, and they are arrogant.
Without doubt, Allah knows what they conceal and what they reveal. Indeed, He does not love the arrogant.
And when it is said to them, “What has your Lord sent down?” they say, “Stories of the former people.”
So they will carry their full burdens on the Day of Resurrection, and from the burdens of those whom they misled without knowledge. How evil is what they carry.
Those before them also plotted, but Allah came upon their structures from the foundations, so the roof fell down upon them from above, and the punishment came to them from where they did not expect.

Then on the Day of Resurrection He will disgrace them and say, “Where are My partners for whose sake you used to argue?” Those who were given knowledge will say, “ Indeed, disgrace and evil today are upon the disbelievers,”
those whom the angels take in death while they are wronging themselves. Then they will offer submission, saying, “We were not doing any evil.” “No. Indeed, Allah knows well what you were doing.”
So enter the gates of Hell, remaining in it forever. How wretched is the dwelling of the arrogant.”

And it will be said to those who were mindful of Allah, “What has your Lord sent down?” They will say, “Good.” For those who do good in this world is good, and the home of the Hereafter is better. How excellent is the home of the mindful.
Gardens of lasting residence which they will enter, with rivers flowing beneath them. For them there will be whatever they wish. This is how Allah rewards those who are mindful,
those whom the angels take in death while they are pure, saying, “Peace be upon you. Enter the Garden for what you used to do.

Are they waiting for anything except that the angels should come to them, or that the command of your Lord should come? Those before them did the same. Allah did not wrong them, but they were wronging themselves.
So the evil consequences of what they did struck them, and they were surrounded by what they used to mock.

Those who associate partners say, “If Allah had willed, we would not have worshipped anything besides Him, neither we nor our fathers, nor would we have forbidden anything without His command.” Those before them did the same. So are the messengers responsi ble for anything except conveying the message clearly?

We certainly sent into every community a messenger, saying, “W orship Allah and avoid false gods.” Among them were those whom Allah guided, and among them were those upon whom misguidance was deserved. So travel through the earth and see how the end was of those who denied.
Even if you are eager for their guidance, Allah does not guide those whom He leads astray, and they will have no helpers.
They swear by Allah with their strongest oaths that Allah will not raise one who dies. No T ruly, it is a promise binding upon Him in truth, but most people do not know,
so that He may make clear to them what they differ about, and so that those who disbelieved may know that they were liars.
Our word to a thing when We intend it is only that We say to it, “Be,” and it is.
And those who emigrated for Allah after they were wronged, We will surely settle them well in this world, and the reward of the Hereafter is greater, if only they knew,
those who were patient and upon their Lord they place their trust.
And We did not send before you except men to whom We revealed. So ask the people of knowledge if you do not know.
With clear proofs and scriptures. And We sent down to you the Reminder, so that you may make clear to the people what was sent down to them, and so that they may reflect.
Do those who plot evil deeds feel secure that Allah will not cause the earth to swallow them, or that the punishment will not come to them from where they do not expect,
or that He will not seize them during their movements, while they cannot escape,
or that He will not seize them by gradual reduction? Indeed, your Lord is Most-Gentle and Ever-Merciful.
Do they not see the things Allah has created, how their shadows turn to the right and to the left, prostrating to Allah while they are humbled?
And to Allah prostrates whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth of every moving creature, and the angels, and they are not arrogant.
They fear their Lord above them, and they do what they are commanded.
And Allah has said, “Do not take two gods. He is only One God, so fear Me alone.”
T o Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and to Him belongs lasting obedience. Will you then fear anyone other than Allah?
Whatever blessing you have is from Allah. Then when hardship touches you, to Him you cry out.
Then when He removes the hardship from you, suddenly a group among you associate partners with their Lord,
so that they deny what We have given them. So enjoy yourselves; you will soon know.
And they assign a share of what We have provided them to what they do not know. By Allah, you will surely be questioned about what you used to invent.
And they assign daughters to Allah, glory be to Him, while for themselves is what they desire.
When one of them is given news of a female child, his face darkens, and he is filled with inward grief.
He hides from the people because of the bad news he has been given. Should he keep her in humiliation, or bury her in the ground? How evil is what they decide.
For those who do not believe in the Hereafter is an evil description, and for Allah is the highest description. He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

If Allah were to take people to account for their wrongdoing, He would not leave a single living creature upon it. But He delays them to a fixed term. Then when their term comes, they can neither delay it for a moment nor bring it forward.
And they assign to Allah what they dislike for themselves, and their tongues describe the lie that for them is the best. Without doubt, for them is the Fire, and they will be sent ahead into it.
By Allah, We certainly sent messengers to communities before you, but Shayṭān made their deeds appear appealing to them. So he is their ally today, and for them is a painful punishment.
And We have not sent down the Book to you except so that you may make clear to them what they differ about, and as guidance and mercy for a people who believe.
And Allah sent down water from the sky and gave life to the earth after its death. Indeed, in that is a sign for people who listen.
And indeed, in livestock there is a lesson for you. We give you drink from what is in their bellies, between waste and blood, pure milk, pleasant to those who drink.
And from the fruits of date palms and grapes you take intoxicants and good provision. Indeed, in that is a sign for people who use reason.
And your Lord inspired the bee: “T ake homes in the mountains, in the trees, and in what they build.

Then eat from all fruits and follow the paths of your Lord made smooth.” From their bellies comes a drink of different colours, in which there is healing for people. Indeed, in that is a sign for people who reflect.

And Allah created you, then He will take you in death. And among you is one who is returned to the weakest stage of life, so that after having knowledge he knows nothing. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Powerful.

And Allah has favoured some of you over others in provision. Y et those who were favoured do not pass on their provision to those whom their right hands possess, so that they would be equal in it. Do they then deny the blessing of Allah?

And Allah has made for you spouses from among yourselves, and from your spouses He has given you children and grandchildren, and provided you with good things. Do they then believe in falsehood and deny the blessing of Allah?
And they worship besides Allah that which has no power to provide them with any provision from the heavens or the earth, nor are they able.
So do not set up likenesses for Allah. Indeed, Allah knows, and you do not know.

Allah sets forth an example: a slave owned, having no power over anything, and one whom We have provided with good provision from Us, so he gives from it secretly and openly. Are they equal? Praise belongs to Allah. But most of them do not know.

And Allah sets forth an example of two men: one of them mute, unable to do anything, and a burden upon his master; wherever he directs him, he brings no good. Is he equal to one who commands justice and is upon a straight path?
And to Allah belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth. And the matter of the Hour is like the blink of an eye, or nearer still. Indeed, Allah has power over everything.
And Allah brought you out from the wombs of your mothers knowing nothing, and He gave you hearing, sight, and hearts, so that you may be grateful.
Do they not see the birds made subservient in the open sky? None holds them up except Allah. Indeed, in that are signs for people who believe.

And Allah has made for you your homes as places of rest, and He has made for you from the hides of livestock homes which you find light on the day of travel and on the day of settlement, and from their wool, fur, and hair furnishings and enjoyment for a time.

And Allah has made for you shade from what He created, and He has made for you shelters in the mountains, and He has made for you garments that protect you from the heat and garments that protect you in battle. Thus He completes His blessing upon you, so that you may submit.
But if they turn away, then your duty is only to convey the message clearly.
They recognise the blessing of Allah, then they deny it, and most of them are disbelievers.
And on the Day  We raise from every community a witness, then those who disbelieved will not be permitted to speak, nor will they be allowed to make amends.
And when those who wronged see the punishment, it will not be lightened for them, nor will they be given respite.

And when those who associated partners see their partners, they will say, “Our Lord, these are our partners whom we used to call upon besides You.” But they will throw back the statement at them, “Indeed, you are liars.”
And on that Day they will offer submission to Allah, and what they used to invent will abandon them.
Those who disbelieved and turned people away from the path of Allah, We will add punishment upon punishment for them, because they used to spread corruption.

And on the Day We raise in every community a witness against them from among themselves, and We bring you as a witness over these. And We have sent down to you the Book as a clear explanation of everything, and as guidance, mercy, and good news for those who submit.
Indeed, Allah commands justice, excellence, and giving to relatives, and He forbids immorality, wrongdoing, and oppression. He instructs you so that you may take heed.
And fulfil the covenant of Allah when you have made it, and do not break oaths after confirming them, while you have made Allah your guarantor. Indeed, Allah knows what you do.

And do not be like the woman who unravelled her spun thread after it was strong, taking your oaths as a means of deception between you because one community is more numerous than another. Allah only tests you through this, and on the Day of Resurrection H e will surely make clear to you what you used to differ over.
And if Allah had willed, He would have made you one community, but He leads astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills, and you will surely be questioned about what you used to do.

And do not take your oaths as a means of deception between you, lest a foot slip after it was firm, and you taste evil for having turned away from the path of Allah, and for you is a great punishment.
And do not trade the covenant of Allah for a small price. What is with Allah is better for you, if only you knew.
What is with you runs out, but what is with Allah remains. And We will surely give those who were patient their reward according to the best of what they used to do.
Whoever does good, whether male or female, while believing, We will surely grant them a good life, and We will surely give them their reward according to the best of what they used to do.
So when you recite the Qur’an, seek refuge in Allah from Shayṭān, the accursed.
Indeed, he has no authority over those who believe and place their trust in their Lord.
His authority is only over those who take him as an ally and those who associate partners through him.
And when We replace one verse with another, and Allah knows best what He sends down, they say, “You are only making it up.” But most of them do not know.
Say, “The Pure Spirit has brought it down from your Lord in truth, to make firm those who believe, and as guidance and good news for those who submit.”
And We certainly know that they say, “It is only a human being who teaches him.” The language of the one they refer to is foreign, while this is clear Arabic language.
Indeed, those who do not believe in the signs of Allah, Allah will not guide them, and for them is a painful punishment.
It is only those who do not believe in the signs of Allah who invent lies, and they are the liars.

Whoever disbelieves in Allah after having believed, except one who is compelled while his heart is at rest in faith, but whoever opens his chest to disbelief, upon them is anger from Allah, and for them is a tremendous punishment.
That is because they preferred the life of this world over the Hereafter, and because Allah does not guide the disbelieving people.
Those are the ones upon whose hearts, hearing, and sight Allah has set a seal, and they are the heedless.
Without doubt, in the Hereafter they will be the losers.
Then Indeed, your Lord, for those who emigrated after they were persecuted, then struggled and were patient, Indeed, your Lord after that is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
On the Day when every soul will come, arguing for itself, and every soul will be fully repaid for what it did, and they will not be wronged.

And Allah sets forth an example of a town that was secure and at ease, with its provision coming to it in abundance from every place, but it denied the blessings of Allah, so Allah made it taste hunger and fear because of what they used to do.
And a messenger had certainly come to them from among themselves, but they denied him, so the punishment seized them while they were wrongdoers.
So eat from what Allah has provided for you, lawful and good, and be grateful for the blessing of Allah, if it is Him that you worship.

He has only forbidden to you  carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, and what has been dedicated to other than Allah. But whoever is compelled, without desire or excess, then Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
And do not say about what your tongues falsely describe, “This is lawful and this is forbidden,” inventing lies against Allah. Indeed, those who invent lies against Allah will not succeed.
A brief enjoyment, and for them is a painful punishment.
And to those who were Jews We forbade what We have related to you before. We did not wrong them, but they were wronging themselves.
Then Indeed, your Lord, for those who did wrong out of ignorance, then repented after that and set matters right, Indeed, your Lord after that is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
Indeed, Ibrāhīm was a community in himself, devoted to Allah, upright, and he was not among those who associate partners.
Grateful for His blessings. He chose him and guided him to a straight path.
And We gave him good in this world, and in the Hereafter he will surely be among the righteous.
Then We revealed to you that you should follow the way of Ibrāhīm, upright. And he was not among those who associate partners with Allah.
The Sabbath was only imposed upon those who differed about it. And indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning what they used to differ over.
Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction, and argue with them in the best manner. Indeed, your Lord knows best who has strayed from His path, and He knows best who are guided.
And if you respond to harm, then respond with the like of what you were harmed with. But if you are patient, it is better for those who are patient.
Be patient, and your patience is only through Allah. Do not grieve over them, and do not be distressed by what they plot.
Indeed, Allah is with those who are mindful and those who do good.

surahs.name: Al-Isrāʾ (The Night Journey)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Glory be to the One who took His servant by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Furthest Mosque, whose surroundings We blessed, so that We might show him some of Our signs. Indeed, He is the All -Hearing, the All- Seeing.
And We gave Mūsā the Book and made it guidance for the Children of Isrāʾīl, saying, “Do not take any guardian besides Me.”
Descendants of those whom We carried with Nūḥ. Indeed, he was a grateful servant.
And We declared to the Children of Isrāʾīl in the Book that you would surely cause corruption in the land twice, and you would surely rise to great arrogance.
So when the promise of the first of them came, We sent against you servants of Ours, mighty in force, and they swept through the homes, and the promise was fulfilled.
Then We gave you the turn back over them, supported you with wealth and children, and made you greater in number.

If you do good, you do good for yourselves. And if you do evil, it is against yourselves. Then when the promise of the final time came, it was to darken your faces, to enter the Mosque as they entered it the first time, and to destroy completely what they overpowered.
Perhaps your Lord will have mercy upon you. But if you return, We will return. And We have made Hell a confinement for the disbelievers.
Indeed, this Qur’an guides to what is most upright and gives good news to the believers who do good deeds that for them is a great reward,
and that those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have prepared for them a painful punishment.
And the human being calls for evil as he calls for good, and the human being is ever hasty.

And We made the night and the day as two signs. We erased the sign of the night and made the sign of the day visible, so that you may seek bounty from your Lord and know the number of years and the reckoning. And We have made everything clear in detail.
And We have fastened every person’s deeds to his neck, and on the Day of Resurrection We will bring out for him a record which he will find laid open.
Read your record. You are sufficient today as a reckoner against yourself.

Whoever is guided is only guided for himself, and whoever goes astray goes astray only against himself. No bearer of burden will bear the burden of another. And We do not punish until We send a messenger.
And when We intend to destroy a town, We command its affluent ones, but they rebel within it, so the word becomes binding upon it, and We destroy it completely.
And how many generations We have destroyed after Nūḥ. Your Lord is sufficient as One who is fully aware and seeing of the sins of His servants.
Whoever desires this fleeting life, We hasten for him in it whatever We will for whom We will. Then We appoint for him Hell, in which he will burn, disgraced and cast out.
And whoever desires the Hereafter and strives for it as it should be striven for, while being a believer, those are the ones whose effort is appreciated.
T o each We extend, to these and to those, from the gift of your Lord. And the gift of your Lord is not restricted.
Look how We have favoured some of them over others. And the Hereafter is greater in degrees and greater in favour.
Do not set up another god alongside Allah, or you will sit condemned and abandoned.

And your Lord has decreed that you worship none but Him, and show kindness to parents. If one or both of them reach old age with you, do not say to them even “ugh”, and do not rebuke them, but speak to them graciously.
And lower for them the wing of humility out of mercy, and say, “My Lord, have mercy on them, just as they raised me when I was small.”
Your Lord knows best what is within your souls. If you are righteous, then indeed, He is Most-Forgiving to those who return.
And give the relative his due, and the poor, and the traveller, and do not squander wastefully.
Indeed, those who squander are brothers of Shayṭān, and Shayṭān has always been ungrateful to his Lord.
And if you turn away from them seeking mercy from your Lord which you hope for, then speak to them with gentle words.
And do not keep your hand chained to your neck, nor stretch it out fully, so that you become blameworthy and destitute.
Indeed, your Lord extends provision for whom He wills and restricts it. Indeed, He is fully aware and seeing of His servants.
And do not kill your children out of fear of poverty. We provide for them and for you. Indeed, killing them is a great sin.
And do not go near adultery. Indeed, it is an outrage and an evil path.

And do not kill the soul which Allah has made sacred, except with right. And whoever is killed unjustly, We have given his guardian authority, but let him not exceed limits in killing. Indeed, he is supported.
And do not approach the property of the orphan except in the best way, until he reaches maturity. And fulfil the covenant. Indeed, the covenant will be questioned about.
And give full measure when you measure, and weigh with an even balance. That is better and best in outcome.
And do not pursue what you have no knowledge of. Indeed, hearing, sight, and the heart, all of these will be questioned about.
And do not walk on the earth arrogantly. Indeed, you will never tear the earth apart, nor will you reach the mountains in height.
All of that, its evil is hateful to your Lord.
That is from the wisdom which your Lord has revealed to you. And do not set up another god alongside Allah, or you will be thrown into Hell, blamed and cast out.
Has your Lord favoured you with sons and taken daughters for Himself from among the angels? Indeed, you say a grave word.
We have set out matters in this Qur’an in many ways, so that they may take heed, but it only increases them in aversion.
Say, “If there were other deities with Him, as they claim, then they would have sought a way to the Possessor of the Throne.”
Glory be to Him. Exalted is He far above what they say, beyond all measure.

The seven heavens, the earth, and whatever is in them glorify Him. There is nothing that does not glorify Him with praise, but you do not understand their glorification. Indeed, He is Most-Forbearing, Most-Forgiving.
And when you recite the Qur’an, We place an unseen barrier between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter.
And We place coverings over their hearts, so they do not understand it, and heaviness in their ears. And when you mention your Lord alone in the Qur’an, they turn their backs in aversion.
We know best what they listen for when they listen to you, and when they confer privately, when the wrongdoers say, “You are only following a man who is bewitched.”
Look how they strike examples for you, so they have gone astray and cannot find a way.
And they say, “When we are bones and fragments, will we really be raised as a new creation?”
Say, “Be stones or iron,

or any creation that you consider great in your minds.” Then they will say, “Who will bring us back?” Say, “The One who created you the first time.” Then they will shake their heads at you and say, “When will it be?” Say, “Perhaps it is near.”
On the Day He calls you, you will respond with His praise, and you will think that you remained only a little while.
And say to My servants that they should speak what is best. Indeed, Shayṭān stirs discord between them. Indeed, Shayṭān is a clear enemy to the human being.
Your Lord knows you best. If He wills, He will have mercy upon you, and if He wills, He will punish you. And We have not sent you as a guardian over them.
And your Lord knows best those in the heavens and the earth. And We have favoured some prophets over others, and We gave Dāwūd the Zabūr.
Say, “Call upon those you claim besides Him. They have no power to remove harm from you or to change it.”
Those whom they call upon seek a means to their Lord, which of them is nearest, and they hope for His mercy and fear His punishment. Indeed, the punishment of your Lord is something to be feared.
There is no town except that We will destroy it before the Day of Resurrection, or punish it with severe punishment. That is written in the Book.

And nothing prevented Us from sending the signs except that the former people denied them. And We gave Thamūd the she camel as a clear sign, but they wronged her. And We do not send the signs except as a warning.

And when We said to you, “Indeed, your Lord encompasses the people.” And We did not make the vision We showed you except as a trial for the people, and the cursed tree mentioned in the Qur’an. We frighten them, but it only increases them in great transgression.
And when We said to the angels, “Prostrate to Ādam,” they prostrated, except Iblīs. He said, “Shall I prostrate to one You created from clay?”
He said, “Do You see this one whom You have honoured above me? If You give me respite until the Day of Resurrection, I will surely bring his descendants under my control, except a few.”
He said, “Go. Whoever among them follows you, then Hell is your recompense, a full recompense.

And incite whoever you can among them with your voice, rally against them your cavalry and infantry, share with them in wealth and children, and promise them.” But Shayṭān promises them nothing except deception.
Indeed, over My servants you have no authority. And your Lord is sufficient as a guardian.
Your Lord is the One who drives the ships for you in the sea, so that you may seek from His bounty. Indeed, He is Ever-Merciful towards you.
And when harm touches you at sea, those you call upon vanish, except Him. But when He brings you safely to land, you turn away. And the human being is ever ungrateful.
Do you feel secure that He will not cause a side of the land to swallow you, or send upon you a storm of stones, then you would find no one to protect you?
Or do you feel secure that He will not return you to it another time, then send upon you a violent wind and drown you for what you disbelieved, then you would find no one against Us to pursue?
And We have honoured the children of Ādam. We carried them on land and sea, provided them with good things, and favoured them greatly over many of those We created.
On the Day We will call every people with their leader. Whoever is given his record in his right hand, they will read their record, and they will not be wronged even by a thread.
And whoever is blind in this world will be blind in the Hereafter and even further astray from the path.
They almost tempted you away from what We revealed to you, so that you would invent something else against Us. If that had happened, they would have taken you as a close friend.
And if We had not made you firm, you would almost have inclined towards them a little.
Then We would have made you taste double punishment in life and double punishment in death, and you would have found no helper against Us.
And they almost drove you out of the land to expel you from it, but then they would not have remained after you except for a short time.
This was Our established way with those We sent before you of Our messengers, and you will not find any change in Our way.
Uphold the prayer at the decline of the sun until the darkness of night, and the recitation at dawn. Indeed, the recitation at dawn is witnessed.
And during part of the night, stand in prayer with it as extra worship for you. Perhaps your Lord will raise you to a praised station.
And say, “My Lord, let my entry be truthful and let my exit be truthful, and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority.”
And say, “T ruth has come, and falsehood has vanished. Indeed, falsehood is bound to vanish.”
And We send down from the Qur’an what is healing and mercy for the believers, but it increases the wrongdoers only in loss.
And when We show favour to mankind, he turns away and withdraws to the side. But when harm touches him, he becomes despairing.
Say, “Each acts according to his own way, and your Lord knows best who is most guided in path.”
And they ask you about the spirit. Say, “The spirit is from the command of my Lord, and you have been given only a little knowledge.”
And if We willed, We could surely take away what We have revealed to you. Then you would find no protector for yourself against Us,
except by mercy from your Lord. Indeed, His favour upon you is great.
Say, “If mankind and jinn gathered together to produce the like of this Qur’an, they could not produce its like, even if they were helpers to one another.”
And We have certainly set out for people in this Qur’an every kind of example, yet most people refuse except disbelief.
And they say, “We will not believe you until you cause a spring to gush forth for us from the earth.”
Or until you have a garden of date palms and grapes and cause rivers to flow through it abundantly.
Or until you make the sky fall upon us in pieces, as you claim, or you bring Allah and the angels before us.

Or until you have a house of gold, or you ascend into the sky. And even then we will not believe in your ascent unless you bring down to us a book that we can read. Say, “Glory be to my Lord. Am I anything but a human messenger?”
And nothing prevented people from believing when guidance came to them except that they said, “Has Allah sent a human messenger?”
Say, “If there were angels walking securely on the earth, We would have sent down to them an angel as a messenger.”
Say, “ Allah is sufficient as a witness between me and you. Indeed, He is fully aware and seeing of His servants.”

And whoever Allah guides, he is truly guided. And whoever He leaves astray, you will never find for them any protectors besides Him. And We will gather them on the Day of Resurrection on their faces, blind, mute, and deaf. Their refuge is Hell. Every time it subsides, We increase the blaze for them.
That is their recompense because they denied Our signs and said, “When we are bones and fragments, will we really be raised as a new creation?”

Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and the earth, is able to create the like of them? And He has set for them a term about which there is no doubt. But the wrongdoers refuse except disbelief.
Say, “If you possessed the treasuries of the mercy of my Lord, you would surely withhold them out of fear of spending.” And the human being is ever miserly.
And We certainly gave Mūsā nine clear signs. So ask the Children of Isrāʾīl when he came to them, and Firʿawn said to him, “I think that you, O Mūsā, are bewitched.”
He said, “You know well that none sent these down except the Lord of the heavens and the earth as clear proofs. And indeed, I think that you, O Firʿawn, are ruined.”
So he intended to drive them out of the land, but We drowned him and those who were with him, all together.
And after him We said to the Children of Isrāʾīl, “Settle in the land. Then when the promise of the Hereafter comes, We will bring you forth as a mixed gathering.”
And with truth We sent it down, and with truth it came down. And We did not send you except as a bearer of good news and a warner.
And a Qur’an which We have divided, so that you may recite it to the people gradually, and We sent it down step by step.
Say, “Believe in it or do not believe. Indeed, those who were given knowledge before it, when it is recited to them, fall upon their faces in prostration,”
and they say, “Glory be to our Lord. The promise of our Lord has surely been fulfilled.”
And they fall upon their faces weeping, and it increases them in humility.

Say, “Call upon Allah or call upon the Most-Merciful. Whichever you call upon, to Him belong the most beautiful names.” And do not be loud in your prayer, nor be silent in it, but seek a way between that.
And say, “All praise belongs to Allah, who has not taken a son, who has no partner in dominion, and who has no need of a protector out of weakness. And magnify Him with full magnificence.”

surahs.name: Al-Kahf (The Cave)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: All praise belongs to Allah, who sent down the Book to His servant and placed no distortion within it.
It stands straight, to warn of severe punishment from Him, and to give good news to the believers who do good deeds that for them is a lasting reward,
in which they will remain without end,
and to warn those who claim, “ Allah has taken a son.”
They have no knowledge of this, nor did their forefathers. A serious claim comes from their mouths. They say nothing but falsehood.
Then perhaps you would exhaust yourself with grief over them, if they do not believe in this speech.
We have made what is upon the earth an adornment for it, so that We may test which of them is best in action.
And We will surely reduce what is upon it to barren ground.
Or did you think that the people of the cave and the inscription were something extraordinary among Our signs?
When the young men took refuge in the cave and said, “Our Lord, grant us mercy from Yourself and prepare for us sound direction in our affair.”
So We sealed their hearing within the cave for many years.
Then We raised them, so that We might know which of the two groups was more accurate in counting how long they had remained.
We relate their story to you in truth. They were young men who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.

And We strengthened their hearts when they stood and said, “Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We will never call upon any god besides Him. If we did, we would have spoken an extreme falsehood.
These people of ours have taken deities besides Him. Why do they not bring clear authority for them? So who is more unjust than one who invents a lie against Allah?”
“When you withdraw from them and from what they worship besides Allah, then take refuge in the cave. Your Lord will spread His mercy over you and prepare ease for you in your affair.”

And you would see the sun, when it rose, inclining away from their cave to the right, and when it set, passing away from them to the left, while they were within an open space of it. That is among the signs of Allah. Whoever Allah guides is truly guided, and whoever He leaves astray, you will find no guiding ally for him.

You would think them awake, while they were asleep. We turned them to the right and to the left, and their dog stretched out its forelegs at the entrance. If you had looked at them, you would have turned away from them in flight, and you would have been filled with fear of them.

And in this way We raised them, so that they might question one another. One of them said, “How long have you remained?” They said, “We remained a day, or part of a day.” They said, “Your Lord knows best how long you remained. So send one of you with thes e coins of yours to the city, and let him look for the purest food and bring you provision from it. Let him be discreet and not make anyone aware of you.
For if they discover you, they will stone you or force you back into their way, and then you would never succeed.”

And in this way We brought them to be discovered, so that it would be known that the promise of Allah is true, and that there is no doubt about the Hour. When they were disputing among themselves about their matter, they said, “Build a structure over them. Their Lord knows them best.” Those whose word prevailed said, “We will surely build a place of worship over them.”

They will say, “They were three, their dog being the fourth,” and they will say, “Five, their dog being the sixth,” guessing at the unseen, and they will say, “Seven, and their dog the eighth.” Say, “My Lord knows best their number. Only a few know about them.” So do not argue about them except with what is clear, and do not seek information about them from anyone.
And never say about anything, “I will do that tomorrow,”
without adding, “if Allah wills.” And remember your Lord when you forget, and say, “Perhaps my Lord will guide me to what is closer than this in right direction.”
And they remained in their cave for three hundred years, and they added nine.

Say, “ Allah knows best how long they remained. T o Him belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth. How perfectly He sees, how perfectly He hears. They have no protector besides Him, and He shares His authority with no one.”
Recite what has been revealed to you from the Book of your Lord. There is no one who can alter His words, and you will never find any refuge besides Him.

And keep yourself patient with those who call upon their Lord morning and evening, seeking His pleasure. Do not let your eyes turn away from them, desiring the attraction of the life of this world. And do not obey one whose heart We have made unaware of Our remembrance, who follows his desire, and whose affair has gone beyond limits.

And say, “The truth is from your Lord. So whoever wishes, let him believe, and whoever wishes, let him disbelieve.” Indeed, We have prepared for the wrongdoers a Fire whose walls surrounds them. If they cry out for help, they will be given water like molten metal that scorches faces. What a miserable drink and what a wretched resting place.
Indeed, those who believe and do good deeds, We will never allow the reward of anyone who does good to be lost.

For them are Gardens of lasting stay, with rivers flowing beneath them. They will be adorned in them with bracelets of gold and will wear green garments of fine silk and rich brocade, reclining therein upon couches. How excellent is the reward, and how fine is the resting place.
Set forth for them the example of two men. For one of them We made two gardens of grapevines, surrounded them with date palms, and placed crops between them.
Both gardens produced their yield and fell short in nothing. And We caused a river to flow through them.
He had abundance, so he said to his companion while speaking with him, “I have more wealth than you and greater numbers in support.”
And he entered his garden while wronging himself. He said, “I do not think this will ever perish,
nor do I think the Hour will come. And even if I am returned to my Lord, I will surely find something better than this in return.”
His companion said to him, while reasoning with him, “Do you disbelieve in the One who created you from dust, then from a drop, then shaped you as a man?
But as for me, He is Allah, my Lord, and I do not associate anyone with my Lord.
Why did you not say, when you entered your garden, ‘What Allah wills comes to pass. There is no power except with Allah’? If you see me as lesser than you in wealth and children,
then perhaps my Lord will give me something better than your garden, and send upon it a reckoning from the sky, so that it becomes barren ground.”
Or its water may sink deep into the ground, and you would never be able to seek it out.”
And his harvest was ruined, so he began wringing his hands over what he had spent on it, while it lay collapsed upon its trellises. And he said, “I wish I had not associated anyone with my Lord.”
And he had no group to help him besides Allah, nor was he able to defend himself.
There, true authority belongs to Allah alone. He is best in reward and best in outcome.

Set forth for them the example of the life of this world. It is like water We send down from the sky, so the plants of the earth mix with it, then it becomes dry stalks scattered by the winds. And Allah has power over everything.
Wealth and children are the adornment of the life of this world, but lasting good deeds are better with your Lord in reward and better in hope.
And on the Day We cause the mountains to move, and you see the earth laid bare, and We gather them, leaving none of them behind,
they will be presented before your Lord in rows. “You have come to Us just as We created you the first time. Y et you claimed We would never appoint a time for you.”

And the record will be placed, and you will see the criminals fearful of what is in it. They will say, “What kind of record is this? It leaves nothing small or great without counting it.” And they will find whatever they did present before them. And your Lord wrongs no one.

And when We said to the angels, “Prostrate to Ādam,” they prostrated, except Iblīs. He was from the jinn, so he defied the command of his Lord. Will you then take him and his descendants as allies instead of Me, while they are enemies to you? How terrible is that exchange for the wrongdoers.
I did not make them witnesses to the creation of the heavens and the earth, nor to the creation of themselves, and I would not take those who mislead as helpers.
And on the Day He will say, “Call those whom you claimed as My partners,” and they will call them, but they will not respond to them, and We will place a barrier of destruction between them.
And the criminals will see the Fire and realise that they are about to fall into it, and they will find no way away from it.
And We have set out in this Qur’an every kind of example for people, yet the human being is the most argumentative of everything.

And nothing prevented people from believing when guidance came to them and from seeking forgiveness from their Lord, except that the way of the earlier people should come upon them, or that the punishment should confront them directly.

And We send the messengers only as bearers of good news and as warners. But those who disbelieve  argue with falsehood in order to undermine the truth with it, and they take My signs and what they are warned of in mockery.

And who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the signs of his Lord, yet turns away from them and forgets what his hands have sent ahead? We have placed coverings over their hearts, so they do not understand it, and heaviness in their ears. And if yo u call them to guidance, they will never be guided.

And your Lord is the Most-Forgiving, the Possessor of mercy. If He were to take them to account for what they have earned, He would hasten the punishment for them. But for them there is an appointed time, from which they will find no escape.
And those towns We destroyed when they did wrong, and We set for their destruction a fixed time.
And when Mūsā said to his attendant, “I will not stop until I reach the junction of the two seas, even if I continue for a long time.”
Then when they reached the meeting point of the two seas, they forgot their fish, and it took its way into the sea, slipping away.
When they had passed on, he said to his attendant, “Bring us our meal. We have certainly met fatigue from this journey of ours.”
He said, “Did you see when we rested by the rock? I forgot the fish, and none made me forget it except Shayṭān, that I should mention it. And it took its way into the sea in a remarkable manner.”
He said, “That is what we were seeking.” So they turned back, retracing their steps.
Then they found a servant from among Our servants, to whom We had given mercy from Us and taught knowledge from Our presence.
Mūsā said to him, “May I follow you so that you teach me some of what you have been taught of sound guidance?”
He said, “You will not be able to remain patient with me.
And how can you be patient over what you cannot fully comprehend?”
He said, “You will find me patient, if Allah wills, and I will not disobey you in any matter.”
He said, “Then if you follow me, do not ask me about anything until I mention it to you myself.”
So they set out until, when they boarded the boat, he damaged it. Mūsā said, “Have you damaged it to drown its people? You have certainly done a serious thing.”
He said, “Did I not say that you would not be able to remain patient with me?”
He said, “Do not take me to task for what I forgot, and do not burden me with hardship in my affair.”
So they set out until, when they met a young boy, he killed him. Mūsā said, “Have you killed a pure soul without right? You have certainly done a dreadful thing.”
He said, “Did I not tell you that you would not be able to remain patient with me?”
He said, “If I ask you about anything after this, then do not keep me in your company. You have now received an excuse from me.”

So they set out until, when they came to the people of a town, they asked its people for food, but they refused to host them. Then they found in it a wall about to collapse, and he restored it. Mūsā said, “If you wished, you could have taken reward for it.”
He said, “This is the point of separation between me and you. I will inform you of the meaning of what you were not able to remain patient over.
As for the boat, it belonged to poor people working at sea. I intended to damage it, because behind them was a king who was seizing every boat by force.
And as for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared that he would burden them with transgression and disbelief.
So we intended that their Lord should replace him for them with one better in purity and closer in compassion.

And as for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and beneath it was a treasure belonging to them. Their father had been righteous, so your Lord intended that they should reach maturity and bring out their treasure, as a mercy from your Lor d. I did not do it of my own will. That is the explanation of what you were not able to remain patient over.
And they ask you about Dhūl-Qarnayn. Say, “I will recite to you a remembrance of him.”
Indeed, We established him in the land and gave him means for everything.
So he followed a course,

until, when he reached the setting place of the sun, he found it setting in a dark body of water, and he found near it a people. We said, “O Dhūl- Qarnayn, you may punish, or you may treat them with kindness.”
He said, “ As for one who does wrong, we will punish him, then he will be returned to his Lord, and He will punish him with a terrible punishment.
But as for one who believes and does good deeds, for him is the best reward, and we will speak to him gently in our command.”
Then he followed a course,
until, when he reached the rising place of the sun, he found it rising upon a people for whom We had made no shelter against it.
So it was. And We had full knowledge of what was with him.
Then he followed another course,
until, when he reached between the two barriers, he found beside them a people who could scarcely understand speech.
They said, “O Dhūl -Qarnayn, indeed Y aʾjūj and Maʾjūj are spreading corruption in the land. So may we assign for you a payment so that you make a barrier between us and them?”
He said, “What my Lord has established me in is better. So help me with strength, and I will make a solid barrier between you and them.
Bring me blocks of iron.” Then when he had levelled between the two sides, he said, “Blow.” Then when he had made it fire, he said, “Bring me molten copper so that I may pour it over it.”
So they were unable to scale it, nor were they able to pierce it.
He said, “This is a mercy from my Lord. But when the promise of my Lord comes, He will level it to the ground. And the promise of my Lord is true.”
And on that Day We will let them surge over one another, and the trumpet will be blown, and We will gather them all together.
And on that Day We will present Hell openly to the disbelievers,
those whose eyes were veiled from My remembrance, and who were not able to listen.
Do those who disbelieve  think that they can take My servants as protectors instead of Me? We have prepared Hell as a dwelling for the disbelievers.
Say, “Shall We inform you of the greatest losers in deeds?
Those whose effort is lost in the life of this world, while they think they are doing good work.”
Those are the ones who disbelieved in the signs of their Lord and in meeting Him, so their deeds became worthless, and We will assign no weight to them on the Day of Resurrection.
That is their recompense, Hell, because they disbelieved and took My signs and My messengers in mockery.
Indeed, those who believe and do good deeds will have the Gardens of Paradise as a dwelling,
remaining in them forever. They will not desire any change from it.
Say, “If the sea were ink for the words of my Lord, the sea would run dry before the words of my Lord came to an end, even if We brought another sea like it as additional supply.”

Say, “I am only a human being like you. It is revealed to me that your god is One God. So whoever hopes to meet his Lord, let him do righteous deeds and not associate anyone in the worship of his Lord.”

surahs.name: Maryam (Mary)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Kāf Hā Yā ʿAyn Ṣād.
A mention of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zakariyyā
When he called upon his Lord with a quiet call.
He said, “My Lord, my bones have weakened, and my head has flared with grey hair, and I have never been disappointed in my call to You, my Lord.
I fear those who will come after me, and my wife is barren, so grant me from Yourself an heir,
who will inherit from me and inherit from the family of Y aʿqūb, and make him, my Lord, pleasing to You.”
“O Zakariyyā, We give you good news of a boy whose name will be Yaḥyā. We have not given this name to anyone before.”
He said, “My Lord, how can I have a boy when my wife is barren and I have reached extreme old age?”
He said, “Thus it will be. Your Lord says, ‘It is easy for Me, and I created you before, when you were nothing.’”
He said, “My Lord, appoint for me a sign.” He said, “Your sign is that you will not speak to people for three nights, while being sound.”
So he came out to his people from the sanctuary and signalled to them to glorify Allah morning and evening.
“O Yaḥyā, hold firmly to the Scripture.” And We gave him wisdom while he was still a child,
and tenderness from Us and purity. He was mindful of Alah,
devoted to his parents, and he was not arrogant or disobedient.
Peace was upon him on the day he was born, on the day he will die, and on the day he will be raised to life.
