And mention Maryam in the Book, when she withdrew from her family to an eastern place.
She took a screen apart from them, then We sent to her Our Spirit, and he appeared to her as a fully formed man.
She said, “I seek refuge in the Most-Merciful from you, if you are mindful.”
He said, “I am only a messenger from your Lord, to grant you a pure boy.”
She said, “How can I have a son when no man has touched me, and I have not been unchaste?”
He said, “Thus it will be. Your Lord says, ‘It is easy for Me. We will make him a sign for people and a mercy from Us. It is a matter already decided.’”
So she conceived him and withdrew with him to a distant place.
Then the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said, “If only I had died before this and been forgotten and erased from memory.”
Then a voice called to her from below her, “Do not grieve. Your Lord has placed a stream beneath you.
Shake the trunk of the palm tree toward you. It will drop fresh ripe dates upon you.
So eat, drink, and be at ease. And if you see anyone, say, ‘I have vowed silence to the Most-Merciful, so today I will not speak to any person.’”
Then she came to her people carrying him. They said, “O Maryam, you have done something unheard of.
O sister of Hārūn, your father was not an evil man, nor was your mother unchaste.”
So she pointed to him. They said, “How can we speak to one who is a child in the cradle?”
He said, “I am a servant of Allah. He has given me the Book and made me a prophet.
And He has made me blessed wherever I may be, and He has instructed me to uphold the prayer and give zakah for as long as I live,
and to be devoted to my mother, and He has not made me arrogant or wretched.
Peace is upon me on the day I was born, on the day I will die, and on the day I will be raised to life.”
That is ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, the word of truth about which they dispute.
It is not for Allah to take a son. Glory be to Him. When He decrees a matter, He only says to it, “Be,” and it is.
And indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight path.
Then the groups differed among themselves. So woe to those who disbelieve from the witnessing of a mighty Day.
How clearly they will hear and see on the Day they come to Us, but today the wrongdoers are in clear misguidance.
And warn them of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be decided, while they are heedless and do not believe.
Indeed, We will inherit the earth and whoever is upon it, and to Us they will be returned.
And mention Ibrāhīm in the Book. Indeed, he was a man of truth, a prophet.
When he said to his father, “My father, why do you worship what neither hears nor sees, and cannot benefit you in any way?
My father, knowledge has come to me that did not come to you, so follow me and I will guide you to a straight path.
My father do not worship Shayṭān. Indeed, Shayṭān has always been rebellious to the Most-Merciful.
My father, I fear that a punishment from the Most-Merciful may touch you, and that you may become an ally of Shayṭān.”
He said, “Do you turn away from my gods, O Ibrāhīm? If you do not stop, I will surely stone you. Leave me for a long time.”
He said, “Peace be upon you. I will ask my Lord to forgive you. Indeed, He has always been gracious to me.
And I will withdraw from you and from what you call upon besides Allah, and I will call upon my Lord. I hope that I will not be disappointed in calling upon my Lord.”
So when he withdrew from them and from what they worshipped besides Allah, We granted him Isḥāq and Y aʿqūb, and We made each of them a prophet.
And We granted them mercy from Us, and We gave them a truthful and lasting reputation.
And mention Mūsā in the Book. Indeed, he was chosen, and he was a messenger and a prophet.
And We called him from the right side of the mount, and We brought him near for intimate discourse.
And We granted him, out of Our mercy, his brother Hārūn as a prophet.
And mention Ismāʿīl in the Book. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet.
And he used to command his family to prayer and zakah, and he was pleasing to his Lord.
And mention Idrīs in the Book. Indeed, he was a man of truth, a prophet.
And We raised him to a high station.

Those are the ones upon whom Allah bestowed favour, from among the prophets, from the descendants of Ādam, and from those We carried with Nūḥ, and from the descendants of Ibrāhīm and Isrāʾīl, and from those We guided and chose. When the verses of the Most-Merciful were recited to them, they fell in prostration, weeping.
Then after them came later generations who neglected the prayer and followed their desires, so they will face ruin.
except those who repent, believe, and do good deeds. Those will enter the Garden and will not be wronged at all.
Gardens of lasting stay which the Most-Merciful has promised His servants in the unseen. Indeed, His promise is ever fulfilled.
They will not hear any empty talk there, only peace. And they will have their provision there, morning and evening.
That is the Garden which We will give as inheritance to those of Our servants who were mindful.
“We do not descend except by the command of your Lord. T o Him belongs what is before us, what is behind us, and what is between that. And your Lord is never forgetful.
Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, so worship Him and remain patient in His worship. Do you know of anyone comparable to Him?”
And the human being says, “When I have died, will I really be brought out alive?”
Does the human being not remember that We created him before, when he was nothing?
So by your Lord, We will surely gather them and the devils, then We will bring them around Hell, kneeling.
Then We will surely extract from every group those who were most stubborn against the Most-Merciful.
Then We know best those who are most deserving of being burned in it.
There is none of you except that he will come to it. This is a matter decided by your Lord, firmly decreed.
Then We will save those who were mindful, and We will leave the wrongdoers within it, on their knees.
And when Our verses are recited to them as clear signs, those who disbelieve say to those who believe, “Which of the two groups is better in position and finer in assembly?”
And how many generations before them We destroyed, who were better in possessions and appearance.

Say, “Whoever is in misguidance, let the Most-Merciful extend for him an extension, until when they see what they were promised, either the punishment or the Hour, then they will know who is worse in position and weaker in forces.”
And Allah increases those who are guided in guidance. And lasting good deeds are better with your Lord in reward and better in return.
Have you seen the one who disbelieved in Our signs and said, “I will surely be given wealth and children”?
Has he looked into the unseen, or has he taken a covenant with the Most- Merciful?
No T ruly. We will record what he says, and We will extend the punishment for him extensively.
And We will inherit from him what he speaks of, and he will come to Us alone.
And they have taken deities besides Allah, hoping that they would be a source of honour for them.
No T ruly. They will deny their worship and will become opponents against them.
Do you not see that We have sent the devils against the disbelievers, driving them on forcefully?
So do not be hasty against them. We are only counting out a fixed count for them.
On the Day We will gather the mindful to the Most-Merciful as honoured guests,
and We will drive the criminals to Hell, thirsty.
They will have no power of intercession, except for those who have taken a covenant with the Most-Merciful.
And they say, “The Most-Merciful has taken a son.”
You have certainly brought forth a terrible claim,
at which the heavens are almost torn apart, the earth splits open, and the mountains collapse in ruin,
because they attribute a son to the Most-Merciful,
while it is not fitting for the Most-Merciful to take a son.
There is no one in the heavens and the earth except that he comes to the Most-Merciful as a servant.
He has fully encompassed them and counted them precisely.
And all of them will come to Him on the Day of Resurrection alone.
Indeed, those who believe and do good deeds, the Most-Merciful will place affection for them.
So We have made it easy in your language, so that you may give good news to those who are mindful of Allah, and warn a stubborn people.
And how many generations before them We destroyed. Do you sense any of them now, or hear from them even a whisper?

surahs.name: Ṭā Hā
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Ṭā Hā.
We did not send down the Qur’an to you so that you would be distressed,
but as a reminder for those who stand in awe.
A revelation from the One who created the earth and the lofty heavens.
The Most-Merciful established authority over the Throne.
T o Him belongs whatever is in the heavens, whatever is on the earth, whatever is between them, and whatever is beneath the soil.
And if you speak aloud, then He knows the secret and what is even more hidden.
Allah, there is no god except Him. T o Him belong the most beautiful names.
Has the account of Mūsā reached you,
when he saw a fire and said to his family, “Remain here. I have noticed a fire. Perhaps I can bring you a burning brand from it, or find guidance at the fire.”
When he came to it, he was called, “O Mūsā.
Indeed, I am your Lord, so remove your sandals. You are in the sacred valley of Ṭuwā.
I have chosen you, so listen to what is being revealed.
Indeed, I am Allah. There is no god except Me. So worship Me and uphold the prayer for My remembrance.
Indeed, the Hour is coming. I keep it concealed, so that every soul may be repaid for what it strives for.
So do not let one who does not believe in it and follows his desire turn you away from it, or you will perish.
And what is that in your right hand, O Mūsā?”
He said, “It is my staff. I lean on it, I strike down leaves for my sheep with it, and I have other uses for it.”
He said, “Throw it down, O Mūsā.”
So he threw it down, and suddenly it was a snake, moving swiftly.
He said, “T ake hold of it and do not be afraid. We will return it to its former state.
And place your hand under your arm. It will come out white, without harm, as another sign,
so that We may show you some of Our greatest signs.
Go to Firʿawn. Indeed, he has overstepped all bounds.”
He said, “My Lord, expand my chest for me,
and make my task easy for me,
and loosen the knot from my tongue,
so that they may understand my speech.
And appoint for me a helper from my family,
Hārūn, my brother.
Strengthen me through him,
and let him share in my task,
so that we may glorify You greatly,
and remember You often.
Indeed, You have always been fully aware of us.”
He said, “Your request has been granted, O Mūsā.
And We showed you favour before,
when We inspired your mother with what was inspired,

saying, ‘Place him in the chest, then cast it into the river. The river will carry it to the shore, where an enemy of Mine and an enemy of his will take him.’ And I placed love upon you from Me, so that you would be formed under My watch.

When your sister went ahead and said, ‘Shall I guide you to someone who will care for him?’ So We returned you to your mother, so that her eye would be comforted and she would not grieve. And you killed a soul, and We delivered you from distress, and We tested you with severe trials. Then you remained for years among the people of Madyan. Then you came at the appointed time, O Mūsā.
And I have chosen you for Myself.
Go, you and your brother, with My signs, and do not be lax in remembering Me.
Go to Firʿawn. Indeed, he has overstepped all bounds.
So speak to him with gentle speech, so that he may take heed or fear.
They said, “Our Lord, we fear that he may act hastily against us or go even further.”
He said, “Do not fear. I am with you both. I hear and I see.

So go to him and say, ‘We are messengers of your Lord. Send the Children of Isrāʾīl with us, and do not torment them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. Peace is upon whoever follows guidance.
It has been revealed to us that punishment is upon whoever denies and turns away.’”
He said, “So who is your Lord, O Mūsā?”
He said, “Our Lord is the One who gave everything its form, then guided it.”
He said, “Then what is the case of the earlier generations?”
He said, “Knowledge of them is with my Lord, in a record. My Lord does not go astray, nor does He forget.”
He is the One who made the earth spread out for you, traced paths for you within it, and sent down water from the sky. With it We brought out varied pairs of plants.
Eat and graze your livestock. Indeed, in that are signs for people of understanding.
From it We created you, to it We will return you, and from it We will bring you out once more.
And We showed him Our signs, all of them, but he denied and refused.
He said, “Have you come to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Mūsā?
Then we will surely bring you magic like it. So set a meeting between us and you, which neither we nor you will fail to attend, at a place agreed upon.”
He said, “Your meeting is on the day of the festival, and let the people be gathered in the forenoon.”
So Firʿawn turned away, gathered his plan, then came.
Mūsā said to them, “W oe to you. Do not invent lies against Allah, or He will destroy you with punishment. Whoever invents lies has already failed.”
So they disputed among themselves about their affair and spoke privately.
They said, “These two are surely magicians who want to drive you out of your land by their magic and do away with your superior way.
So gather your plan, then come in a single line. And whoever prevails today will truly succeed.”
They said, “O Mūsā, either you throw first, or we will be the ones to throw.”
He said, “You throw.” Then suddenly their ropes and staffs appeared to him, by their magic, as if they were moving.
So Mūsā felt fear within himself.
We said, “Do not fear. You will be the one who prevails.
Throw what is in your right hand. It will swallow what they have produced. What they produced is only the trick of a magician, and a magician does not succeed wherever he may go.”
So the magicians fell down in prostration. They said, “We believe in the Lord of Hārūn and Mūsā.”

He said, “You believed in him before I gave you permission. He is surely your leader who taught you magic. So I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you on the trunks of palm trees. Then you will know whi ch of us is harsher and longer lasting in punishment.”

They said, “We will never prefer you over what has come to us of clear truth, nor over the One who created us. So decide whatever you are going to decide. You can only decide matters of this worldly life.
Indeed, we have believed in our Lord, so that He may forgive us our sins and the magic you forced us to practise. Allah is better and more lasting.”
Indeed, whoever comes to his Lord as a criminal, for him is Hell. He will neither die in it nor live.
And whoever comes to Him as a believer, having done good deeds, for them are the highest ranks,
Gardens of lasting stay, with rivers flowing beneath them, remaining in them forever. That is the reward of those who purify themselves.
And We revealed to Mūsā, “Set out with My servants by night, and strike for them a dry path through the sea. Do not fear being overtaken, and do not be afraid.”
Then Firʿawn pursued them with his forces, and the sea covered them with what covered them.
And Firʿawn led his people astray and did not guide them.
O Children of Isrāʾīl, We delivered you from your enemy, made a covenant with you on the right side of the mount, and sent down manna and quails upon you.
“Eat from the good things We have provided for you, and do not overstep within them, or My anger will descend upon you. And whoever My anger descends upon has fallen.
Y et I am truly Forgiving to the one who repents, believes, does good deeds, and then remains guided.
And what made you hasten ahead of your people, O Mūsā?”
He said, “They are close behind me, and I hurried to You, my Lord, so that You would be pleased.”
He said, “Then We have tested your people after you left, and the Sāmirī has led them astray.”

So Mūsā returned to his people angry and sorrowful. He said, “ O My people, did your Lord not make you a good promise? Did the time seem too long to you, or did you wish that anger from your Lord should fall upon you, so you broke your promise to me?”
They said, “We did not break our promise to you by our own will, but we were made to carry burdens of the people’s ornaments, so we threw them in, and that is what the Sāmirī cast.”
Then he brought out for them a calf, a body that made a lowing sound. They said, “This is your god and the god of Mūsā, but he forgot.”
Did they not see that it could not return any speech to them, nor control any harm or benefit for them?
And Hārūn had already said to them before, “O My people, you are only being tested by it. Indeed, your Lord is the Most-Merciful, so follow me and obey my command.”
They said, “We will not stop remaining devoted to it until Mūsā returns to us.”
He said, “O Hārūn, what prevented you, when you saw them go astray,
from following me? Did you then disobey my command?”
He said, “Son of my mother, do not seize me by my beard or my head. I feared that you would say, ‘You caused division among the Children of Isrāʾīl and did not observe my word.’”
He said, “Then what is your case, O Sāmirī?”
He said, “I perceived what they did not perceive, so I took a handful from the trace of the messenger and cast it. That is what my soul urged me to do.”

He said, “Then go. In this life you will say, ‘No contact.’ And indeed, for you is an appointed time that you will not escape. And look at your god to which you remained devoted. We will surely burn it, then scatter it into the sea completely.”
Your god is Allah, there is no god except Him. He has encompassed everything in knowledge.
In this way We recount to you accounts of what has passed, and We have given you from Us a reminder.
Whoever turns away from it will carry a burden on the Day of Resurrection.
They will remain bearing it, and how terrible that burden will be for them on the Day of Resurrection.
On the Day the trumpet is blown, and We gather the criminals that Day, blue eyed,
whispering among themselves, “You remained only ten days.”
We know best what they will say, when the most upright among them will say, “You remained only a single day.”
And they ask you about the mountains. So say, “My Lord will scatter them completely,
then He will leave them as a level plain,
in which you will see no curve and no rise.”
On that Day they will follow the caller, with no deviation from him, and voices will be hushed before the Most-Merciful, and you will hear nothing but a faint sound.
On that Day intercession will not benefit anyone except the one to whom the Most-Merciful has given permission and whose word He accepts.
He knows what is before them and what is behind them, and they do not encompass Him in knowledge.
Faces will be humbled before the Ever Living, the One who sustains all. And whoever carries wrongdoing has surely failed.
And whoever does good deeds while believing will fear neither injustice nor deprivation.
In this way We have sent it down as a Qur’an in clear Arabic, and We have set out warnings within it, so that they may become mindful  of Allah, or that it may bring about remembrance for them.
Exalted is Allah, the T rue King. And do not rush the Qur’an before its revelation is completed for you, and say, “My Lord, increase me in knowledge.”
And We had already taken a covenant from Ādam before, but he forgot, and We did not find in him firm resolve.
And when We said to the angels, “Prostrate to Ādam,” they prostrated, except Iblīs. He refused.
So We said, “O Ādam, Indeed this is an enemy to you and to your wife. So do not let him drive you out of the Garden, so that you suffer.
Indeed, for you in it is that you will neither go hungry nor be unclothed,
and that you will neither be thirsty in it nor will you be exposed to heat.”
Then Shayṭān whispered to him. He said, “O Ādam, shall I guide you to the tree of lasting life and a dominion that does not fade?”
So they both ate from it, and their shame became apparent to them, and they began covering themselves with leaves from the Garden. Ādam disobeyed his Lord and erred.
Then his Lord chose him, turned to him, and guided him.
He said, “Go down from it, all of you, as enemies to one another. Then when guidance comes to you from Me, whoever follows My guidance will not go astray nor suffer hardship.
And whoever turns away from My remembrance will have a life of distress, and We will raise him blind on the Day of Resurrection.”
He will say, “My Lord, why have You raised me blind when I used to see?”
He will say, “In this way Our signs came to you and you neglected them, and in the same way today you are neglected.”
And in this way We repay those who exceed limits and do not believe in the signs of their Lord. And the punishment of the Hereafter is more severe and more lasting.
Does it not guide them how many generations We destroyed before them, among whose dwellings they walk? Indeed, in that are signs for people of understanding.
And if not for a word that had already gone forth from your Lord, and a fixed term, it would surely have been settled.

So be patient over what they say, and  glorify the praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting, and glorify Him during parts of the night and at the edges of the day, so that you may be satisfied.

And do not let your eyes stretch toward what We have given some groups of them to enjoy, the splendour of the life of this world, by which We test them. The provision of your Lord is better and more lasting.
And command your family to prayer and remain steadfast in it. We do not ask you for provision. We provide for you. And the outcome belongs to mindfulness of Allah.
And they say, “Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?” Has there not come to them clear proof of what was in the earlier scriptures?

And if We had destroyed them with punishment before it, they would surely have said, “Our Lord, if only You had sent us a messenger, so that we could have followed Your signs before we were humiliated and disgraced.”
Say, “Each is waiting, so wait. You will soon know who are the people of the straight path and who is rightly guided.”

surahs.name: Al-Anbiyāʾ (The Prophets)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: The reckoning of the people has drawn near, yet they remain heedless, turning away.
No renewed reminder comes to them from their Lord except that they listen to it while playing about,
with distracted hearts. Those who do wrong speak secretly, saying, “Is this anything but a human like yourselves? Will you approach sorcery while you can see?”
He said, “My Lord knows every word spoken in the heaven and the earth. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.”
But they say, “Confused dreams, no, he invented it, no, he is a poet. Let him then bring us a sign, like those sent to earlier people.”
Not a single town We destroyed before them ever believed, so will they now believe?
We did not send before you anyone except men to whom We revealed. So ask the people of remembrance if you do not know.
We did not give them bodies that did not need food, nor were they immortal.
Then We fulfilled the promise to them. We saved them and whom We willed, and We destroyed those who went beyond all bounds.
We have sent down to you a Book containing your reminder. Will you then not use reason?
How many towns We shattered that were steeped in wrongdoing, and after them We raised up other people.
When they sensed Our force coming upon them, they began to flee from it.
“Do not flee. Return to the comforts you were given and to your dwellings, so that you may be questioned.”
They said, “W oe to us. We were indeed wrongdoers.”
Their cry did not cease until We made them like a mown field, lifeless and still.
We did not create the heavens and the earth and everything between them in play.
Had We wished to take amusement, We could have taken it from Our presence, if that were what We intended.
No, We strike falsehood with truth, and it shatters it, and there it vanishes. And woe to you for what you describe.
T o Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. Those who are near Him do not grow arrogant in His worship, nor do they grow weary.
They glorify Him night and day, without pause.
Or have they taken gods from the earth who can raise the dead?
Had there been in them gods besides Allah, both would surely have fallen into ruin. So glory be to Allah, Lord of the Throne, far above what they describe.
He is not questioned about what He does, but they will be questioned.
Or have they taken gods besides Him? Say, “Bring your proof. This is the reminder of those with me and the reminder of those before me.” But most of them do not know the truth, so they turn away.
We did not send any messenger before you except that We revealed to him that there is no god but Me, so worship Me.
And they say, “The Most-Merciful has taken a son.” Glory be to Him. No, they are honoured servants.
They do not speak ahead of Him, and they act only by His command.
He knows what lies before them and what lies behind them, and they do not intercede except for one He is pleased with, and they stand in awe of Him.
And whoever among them were to say, “I am a god besides Him,” We would repay him with Hell. In this way We repay the wrongdoers.
Have those who disbelieve not seen that the heavens and the earth were once joined together, then We split them apart, and We made from water every living thing? Will they then not believe?
We placed firm mountains in the earth so that it would not shake with them, and We made in it broad paths, so that they might find their way.
And We made the sky a protected roof, yet they turn away from its signs.
He is the One who created the night and the day, the sun and the moon, each moving in its own course.
We did not grant lasting life to any human before you. If you were to die, would they then live forever?
Every soul will taste death. We test you with harm and with good as a trial, and to Us you will be returned.
When those who disbelieve  see you, they take you only in mockery, saying, “Is this the one who speaks of your gods?” Y et they themselves reject the remembrance of the Most-Merciful.
Human beings were created with haste. I will soon show you My signs, so do not ask Me to hurry them.
And they say, “When will this promise be, if you are truthful?”
If only those who disbelieve knew the moment when they will not be able to shield their faces or their backs from the Fire, nor will they be helped.
No, it will come upon them suddenly and overwhelm them. They will not be able to turn it away, nor will they be given any delay.
Messengers before you were certainly mocked, but those who mocked them were engulfed by what they used to ridicule.
Say, “Who can protect you by night or by day from the Most-Merciful?” Y et they turn away from the remembrance of their Lord.
Or do they have gods who can defend them besides Us? They cannot even help themselves, nor can they be protected from Us.

No, We allowed these people and their forefathers to enjoy life until their time was prolonged. Do they not see that We come to the land, reducing it from its edges? Are they then the ones who prevail?
Say, “I only warn you by revelation.” But the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned.
If even a breath of the punishment of your Lord were to touch them, they would surely say, “W oe to us. We were indeed wrongdoers.”

We will set up the scales of justice for the Day of Resurrection, so no soul will be wronged in the least. Even if it is the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it forth. We are sufficient as Reckoners.
We certainly gave Mūsā and Hārūn the criterion, a light and a reminder for those who are mindful,
those who stand in awe of their Lord without seeing Him, and who are fearful of the Hour.
And this is a blessed reminder We have sent down. Will you then deny it?
And We had already given Ibrāhīm his right guidance before, and We were fully aware of him.
When he said to his father and his people, “What are these images to which you remain devoted?”
They said, “We found our forefathers worshipping them.”
He said, “You and your forefathers have clearly been astray.”
They said, “Have you come to us with the truth, or are you among those who jest?”
He said, “No, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the One who created them, and I am among those who bear witness to this.”
“And by Allah, I will surely devise a plan against your idols after you have turned away.”
So he broke them into pieces, except for the largest of them, so that they might turn back to it.
They said, “Who has done this to our gods? He is surely among the wrongdoers.”
They said, “We heard a young man mention them. He is called Ibrāhīm.”
They said, “Bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may bear witness.”
They said, “W as it you who did this to our gods, O Ibrāhīm?”
He said, “No, this one did it, the largest of them. Ask them, if they can speak.”
So they turned back upon themselves and said, “You yourselves are the wrongdoers.”
Then they were turned upside down and said, “You know very well that these do not speak.”
He said, “Do you then worship besides Allah that which can neither benefit you in any way nor harm you?
Shame on you and on what you worship besides Allah. Will you not then use reason?”
They said, “Burn him and support your gods, if you are going to act.”
We said, “O fire, be cool and safe for Ibrāhīm.”
They intended harm for him, but We made them the ones who lost most.
We saved him and Lūṭ and brought them to the land which We had blessed for all people.
And We granted him Isḥāq, and Y aʿqūb as an added gift, and We made all of them righteous.
And We made them leaders who guided by Our command, and We revealed to them to do good deeds, to uphold the prayer, and to give zakah. And they were devoted in worship to Us.
And to Lūṭ We gave sound judgement and knowledge, and We saved him from the town that practised indecencies. They were indeed an evil, rebellious people.
And We admitted him into Our mercy. He was among the righteous.
And Nūḥ, when he called out earlier, We answered him and saved him and his family from the great distress.
We helped him against the people who denied Our signs. They were an evil people, so We drowned them all.
And Dāwūd and Sulaymān, when they judged concerning the field into which the sheep of a people had strayed by night, and We were witnesses to their judgement.
We gave Sulaymān understanding of it, and to each We gave sound judgement and knowledge. We subjected the mountains and the birds to glorify with Dāwūd, and We were the doers.
We taught him the making of armour for you, to protect you in battle. Will you then be grateful?
And to Sulaymān We subjected the wind, raging at his command, flowing to the land which We had blessed. We have full knowledge of everything.
And among the devils were those who dived for him and carried out other work besides that, and We kept watch over them.
And Ayyūb, when he called out to his Lord, “Harm has touched me, and You are the Ever-Merciful of the merciful.”
So We answered him, removed the harm that was upon him, and restored his family to him, and the like of them with them, as a mercy from Us and a reminder for the worshippers.
And Ismāʿīl, and Idrīs, and Dhul -Kifl, all of them were among the steadfast.
We admitted them into Our mercy. They were among the righteous.

And Dhā n-Nūn, when he went off in anger, thinking that We would not restrain him. Then he called out in the depths of darkness, “There is no god but You. Glory be to You. I was Indeed among the wrongdoers.”
So We answered him and saved him from distress. In this way We save the believers.
And Zakariyyā, when he called out to his Lord, “My Lord, do not leave me alone, though You are the best of inheritors.”
So We answered him, granted him Y aḥyā, and restored his wife for him. They used to hasten to good deeds and call upon Us with hope and fear, and they were humble before Us.
And the one who guarded her chastity. We breathed into her of Our spirit, and We made her and her son a sign for all people.
This community of yours is one single community, and I am your Lord, so worship Me.
But they split their affairs among themselves. All will return to Us.
So whoever does good while being a believer, his effort will not be denied. We are surely recording it for him.
It is forbidden for a town that We destroyed that they should ever return,
until when Y aʾjūj and Maʾjūj are released and they surge from every elevation,
and the true promise draws near. Then suddenly the eyes of those who disbelieve will stare in horror, saying, “W oe to us. We were heedless of this. No, we were wrongdoers.”
You and what you worship besides Allah will be fuel for Hell. You will surely enter it.
If these had truly been gods, they would not have entered it. All will remain there forever.
For them there will be groaning within it, and they will not hear anything in it.
Those for whom the finest reward from Us has already gone ahead will be kept far away from it.
They will not hear its faintest sound, and they will remain forever in what their souls desire.
The greatest terror will not grieve them, and the angels will meet them, saying, “This is your Day which you were promised.”
The Day We roll up the heaven like a written page is rolled up. Just as We began the first creation, We will bring it back again. A promise binding upon Us. We are surely the Ones who will do it.
We have already written in the Zabūr, after the Reminder, that My righteous servants will inherit the earth.
In this there is truly a message for people devoted to worship.
We did not send you except as a mercy to all worlds.
Say, “It is only revealed to me that your god is One God. Will you then submit?”
If they turn away, then say, “I have informed you equally, and I do not know whether what you are promised is near or far.”
He knows what is spoken openly, and He knows what you conceal.
And I do not know, perhaps it is a trial for you, and an enjoyment for a time.
He said, “My Lord, judge with truth.” Our Lord is the Most-Merciful, the One whose help is sought against what you describe.

surahs.name: Al-Ḥajj (The Pilgrimage)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: O people, be mindful of your Lord. The shaking of the Hour is a tremendous thing.

On the Day you see it, every nursing mother will forget what she was nursing, every pregnant one will miscarry, and you will see people as if drunk, though they are not drunk. But the punishment of Allah is severe.
Among people are those who argue about Allah without knowledge, and follow every defiant devil,
for whom it has been decreed that whoever takes him as an ally, he will mislead him and lead him to the punishment of the Blaze.

O people, if you are in doubt about the resurrection, then consider that We created you from dust, then from a drop, then from a clinging form, then from a lump, formed and unformed, so that We may make clear to you. We settle in the wombs whatever We will for a fixed term, then We bring you out as infants, then you reach your full strength. Among you are those who are taken in death, and among you are those returned to the weakest stage of life, so that after knowledge they know nothing. You see the earth lifeless, then when We send down water upon it, it stirs, swells, and produces every kind of beautiful growth.
That is because Allah is the T ruth, and because He gives life to the dead, and because He has power over everything,
and because the Hour is coming, there is no doubt about it, and because Allah will raise those in the graves.
Among people is one who argues about Allah without knowledge, without guidance, and without an illuminating book,
turning away arrogantly to lead others astray from the path of Allah. For him there is disgrace in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection We will make him taste the punishment of burning.
That is for what your hands have sent ahead, and because Allah is never unjust to the servants.

Among people is one who worships Allah on the edge. If good touches him, he is reassured by it, but if a trial strikes him, he turns back on his face. He loses this world and the Hereafter. That is the clear loss.
He calls upon besides Allah what can neither harm him nor benefit him. That is the farthest straying.
He calls upon one whose harm is closer than his benefit. How wretched is the protector, and how wretched is the companion.
Allah will admit those who believe and do good deeds into Gardens beneath which rivers flow. Allah does whatever He wills.
Whoever thinks that Allah will not support him in this world and the Hereafter, let him stretch a rope to the sky, then cut it, and see whether his scheme removes what enrages him.
And in this way We have sent it down as clear verses, and Allah guides whom He wills.

Indeed, those who believe, and those who are Jews, and the Sabians, and the Christians, and the Magians, and those who associate partners, Allah will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection. Allah is witness over everything.

Do you not see that to Allah prostrates whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth, and the sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, the trees, the moving creatures, and many among people? And many deserve punishment. Whoever Allah humiliates, none can honour. Allah does whatever He wills.
These are two opponents who disputed about their Lord. For those who disbelieve, garments of fire will be cut for them. Scalding water will be poured over their heads,
melting whatever is within their bellies and their skins.
And for them are hooked rods of iron.
Every time they want to escape from it out of anguish, they will be forced back into it, and it will be said, “T aste the punishment of burning.”
Allah will admit those who believe and do good deeds into Gardens beneath which rivers flow. They will be adorned in them with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their clothing there will be silk.
They were guided to good speech, and they were guided to the path of the All-Praiseworthy.

Indeed, those who disbelieve and block others from the path of Allah and from the Sacred Mosque, which We made for all people, equal for those who reside there and those who come from outside, and whoever intends deviation in it through wrongdoing, We will make him taste a painful punishment.
And when We prepared for Ibrāhīm the site of the House, saying, “Do not associate anything with Me, and purify My House for those who circle it, those who stand, and those who bow and prostrate.”
And proclaim the pilgrimage among the people. They will come to you on foot and on every lean mount, coming from every distant pass,
so that they may witness benefits for themselves and remember the name of Allah on appointed days over the animals He has provided for them. So eat from them and feed the poor and the needy.
Then let them complete their rites, fulfil their vows, and circle the Ancient House.

That is so. And whoever honours the sacred limits of Allah, it is better for him with his Lord. And the livestock have been made lawful for you, except what is recited to you. So avoid the filth of idols, and avoid false speech.

Being devoted to Allah, not associating anything with Him. Whoever associates partners with Allah is like one who falls from the sky, then birds snatch him away, or the wind carries him off to a far place.
That is so. Whoever honours the sacred symbols of Allah, it is truly from the mindfulness of hearts.
In them there are benefits for you for a fixed term, then their place of sacrifice is at the Ancient House.
For every community We appointed rites, so that they may remember the name of Allah over the animals He has provided for them. Your god is One God, so submit to Him. And give glad news to the humble,
those whose hearts tremble when Allah is mentioned, those who are patient over what strikes them, those who uphold the prayer, and those who give from what We have provided for them.

The sacrificial camels, We have made them among the symbols of Allah for you. In them there is good for you. So mention the name of Allah over them as they stand in rows, and when they collapse on their sides, eat from them and feed the content and the needy. In this way We have subjected them to you, so that you may be grateful.

Neither their flesh nor their blood reaches Allah, but mindfulness from you reaches Him. In this way He has subjected them to you, so that you may magnify Allah for having guided you. And give glad news to those who do good.
Indeed, Allah defends those who believe. Allah does not love any ungrateful traitor.
Permission has been given to those who are fought because they were wronged. Allah is fully able to give them victory,

those who were expelled from their homes without right, only for saying, “Our Lord is Allah.” If Allah did not repel people, some by means of others, monasteries, synagogues, and mosques, in which the name of Allah is mentioned often, would surely have been destroyed. Allah will surely support those who support Him. Allah is All-Strong, All-Mighty.
Those who, if We establish them in the land, uphold the prayer, give zakah, command what is right, and forbid what is wrong. T o Allah belongs the outcome of all matters.
If they deny you, then before them the people of Nūḥ denied, and ʿĀd and Thamūd,
and the people of Ibrāhīm, and the people of Lūṭ,
and the people of Madyan. And Mūsā was denied. So I granted respite to the disbelievers, then I seized them. How severe was My punishment.
How many towns We destroyed while they were steeped in wrongdoing. They lie collapsed upon their roofs, with abandoned wells and lofty palaces.
Have they not travelled through the land, so that they may have hearts to understand with, or ears to hear with? It is not the eyes that go blind, but the hearts within the chests.
They ask you to hasten the punishment, but Allah will never fail in His promise. A single day with your Lord is like a thousand years of what you count.
How many towns I granted respite to while they were wrongdoers, then I seized them. T o Me is the final return.
Say, “O people, I am only a clear warner to you.”
So those who believe and do good deeds will have forgiveness and a noble provision.
But those who strive against Our signs, seeking to frustrate them, they are the companions of the Fire.

We did not send before you any messenger or prophet except that, when he recited, Shayṭān cast something into his recitation. But Allah removeswhat Shayṭān casts, then Allah confirms His signs. Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
So that He may make what Shayṭān casts a trial for those whose hearts are diseased and whose hearts are hardened. Indeed, the wrongdoers are in deep opposition.

And so that those who have been given knowledge may know that it is the truth from your Lord, and so they may believe in it, and their hearts may humble themselves to it. Allah surely guides those who believe to a straight path.
Those who disbelieve will not cease to be in doubt about it until the Hour comes upon them suddenly, or the punishment of a barren Day comes to them.
Sovereignty on that Day will belong to Allah. He will judge between them. Those who believe and do good deeds will be in Gardens of delight.
But those who disbelieve  and deny Our signs, for them there will be a humiliating punishment.
Those who emigrated in the path of Allah, then were killed or died, Allah will surely provide for them a good provision. Allah is truly the Best of providers.
He will surely admit them to a place with which they will be pleased. Allah is All-Knowing, Most-Forbearing.
That is so. Whoever retaliates with the like of what he was harmed with, then is wronged again, Allah will surely support him. Allah is Pardoning, Forgiving.
That is because Allah causes the night to enter into the day and causes the day to enter into the night, and because Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
That is because Allah is the T ruth, and what they call upon besides Him is false, and because Allah is the Most-High, the Most-Great.
Do you not see that Allah sends down water from the sky, and the earth becomes green? Allah is All-Subtle, All-Aware.
T o Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is Free of need, the All-Praiseworthy.

Do you not see that Allah has subjected to you whatever is on the earth, and the ships that sail through the sea by His command? He holds the sky from falling upon the earth except by His permission. Indeed, Allah is Most- Gentle and Ever-Merciful to people.
He is the One who gave you life, then causes you to die, then gives you life again. Indeed, the human being is deeply ungrateful.
For every community We have appointed a way of worship which they follow. So let them not dispute with you over this matter, and call to your Lord. You are surely upon a straight path.
And if they argue with you, then say, “ Allah knows best what you do.”
Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning what you differed over.
Do you not know that Allah knows whatever is in the heaven and the earth? That is all in a Record. That is easy for Allah.
They worship besides Allah what He has not sent down any authority for, and what they have no knowledge of. There is no helper for the wrongdoers.

When Our clear verses are recited to them, you recognise hostility on the faces of those who disbelieve. They almost lash out at those who recite Our verses to them. Say, “Shall I tell you of something worse than that? The Fire. Allah has promised it to those who disbelieve. What a wretched destination.”

O people, a comparison is set forth, so listen to it. Those whom you call upon besides Allah will never create a fly, even if they gathered together for it. And if a fly were to take something from them, they could not retrieve it from it. Weak is the seeker and weak is what is sought.
They have not honoured Allah with the honour due to Him. Indeed, Allah is All-Strong, All-Mighty.
Allah chooses messengers from among the angels and from among people. Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
He knows what lies before them and what lies behind them, and to Allah all matters are returned.
O you who believe, bow and prostrate, worship your Lord, and do good, so that you may succeed.

Strive for Allah with the striving due to Him. He has chosen you and has placed no hardship upon you in the religion, the way of your father Ibrāhīm. He named you Muslims before and in this, so that the Messenger may be a witness over you, and that you may be witnesses over people. So uphold the prayer, give zakah, and hold firmly to Allah. He is your Protector. How excellent is the Protector, and how excellent is the Helper.

surahs.name: Al-Muʾminūn (The Believers)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: The believers have truly succeeded,
those who are humble in their prayer,
those who turn away from empty talk,
those who give zakah,
those who guard their private parts,
except from their spouses or those whom their right hands possess, for then they are free from blame,
but whoever seeks beyond that, those are the ones who exceed all bounds,
those who faithfully keep their trusts and their promises,
those who carefully guard their prayers.
Those are the inheritors,
those who will inherit the highest Paradise. They will remain there forever.
We created the human being from an extract of clay.
Then We placed him as a drop in a secure resting place.

Then We made the drop into a clinging form, and We made the clinging form into a lump, and We made the lump into bones, and We clothed the bones with flesh, then We produced him as another creation. So blessed is Allah, the Best of creators.
Then after that, you will surely die.
Then on the Day of Resurrection, you will surely be raised.
We created above you seven layered paths, and We are never unaware of creation.
We sent down water from the sky in measured amount, and We settled it in the earth. We are surely able to take it away.
With it We produced for you gardens of date palms and grapes. In them you have abundant fruit, and from them you eat.
And a tree that emerges from Mount Sinai, producing oil and relish for those who eat.
And in livestock there is indeed a lesson for you. We give you to drink from what is in their bellies, and in them there are many benefits for you, and from them you eat.
And upon them and upon ships you are carried.
We certainly sent Nūḥ to his people, and he said, “O my people, worship Allah. You have no god other than Him. Will you not then be mindful?”

The leaders who disbelieved among his people said, “This is nothing but a human like yourselves, seeking to gain superiority over you. If Allah had willed, He would have sent down angels. We never heard of this among our earlier forefathers.
He is nothing but a man possessed by madness, so wait concerning him for a time.”
He said, “My Lord, support me because they have denied me.”

So We revealed to him, “Build the Ark under Our eyes and by Our revelation. Then when Our command comes and the oven overflows, load into it from every kind two, and your family, except those against whom the word has already gone forth. And do not address M e concerning those who have done wrong. They are surely to be drowned.
Then when you and those with you are settled upon the Ark, say, ‘Praise belongs to Allah, who saved us from the wrongdoing people.’
And say, ‘My Lord, settle me in a blessed landing place. You are the best of those who grant landing.’”
Indeed, in that are signs, and We are surely testing.
Then after them We raised another generation.
We sent among them a messenger from themselves, saying, “W orship Allah. You have no god other than Him. Will you not then be mindful?”

The leaders who disbelieved among his people and denied the meeting of the Hereafter, and whom We had allowed to enjoy comfort in the life of this world, said, “This is nothing but a human like yourselves. He eats from what you eat, and drinks from what you drink.
If you were to obey a human like yourselves, you would surely be losers.
Does he promise you that when you have died and become dust and bones, you will be brought out again?
Far removed, far removed is what you are promised.
There is nothing but our life of this world. We die and we live, and we will not be raised.
He is nothing but a man who has fabricated a lie against Allah, and we will not believe him.”
He said, “My Lord, support me because they have denied me.”
He said, “In a little while they will surely become regretful.”
So the blast seized them in truth, and We made them like scattered debris. So away with the wrongdoing people.
Then after them We raised other generations.
No nation can advance its appointed time, nor can they delay it.

Then We sent Our messengers one after another. Every time a messenger came to his people, they denied him, so We made them follow one another in ruin, and We turned them into stories. So away with a people who do not believe.
Then We sent Mūsā and his brother Hārūn with Our signs and clear authority
to Firʿawn and his leaders, but they acted arrogantly and were an overbearing people.
They said, “ Are we to believe two humans like ourselves, while their people are servants to us?”
So they denied them, and they were among those destroyed.
We certainly gave Mūsā the Book, so that they might be guided.
And We made the son of Maryam and his mother a sign, and We sheltered them on a raised ground, settled and flowing with water.
O messengers, eat from what is wholesome and do what is right. I fully know what you do.
This nation of yours is one single nation, and I am your Lord, so be mindful of Me.
But they split their affairs among themselves into factions, each group rejoicing in what it has.
So leave them in their heedlessness for a time.
Do they think that what We provide them with, of wealth and children,
is that We are hastening good things for them? No, but they do not realise.
Those who stand in awe out of fear of their Lord,
and those who believe in the signs of their Lord,
and those who do not associate partners with their Lord,
and those who give what they give while their hearts tremble, knowing that they will return to their Lord,
Those are the ones who hasten toward good deeds, and they are the foremost in them.
We do not burden any soul beyond its capacity. With Us is a record that speaks with truth, and they will not be wronged.
But their hearts are immersed in heedlessness about this, and they have deeds besides that which they persist in doing.
Until when We seize their affluent ones with punishment, suddenly they cry out in despair.
“Do not cry out today. You will not be helped by Us.
My verses were recited to you, but you used to turn back on your heels,
acting arrogantly toward it, speaking ill by night.”
Have they not reflected on the W ord, or has there come to them what did not come to their earlier forefathers?
Or do they not recognise their Messenger, so they deny him?
Or do they say, “He is possessed?” No, he has come to them with the truth, but most of them dislike the truth.
If the truth had followed their desires, the heavens and the earth and whoever is in them would surely have collapsed. No, We have brought them their reminder, but they turn away from their reminder.
Or do you ask them for any reward? The provision of your Lord is better, and He is the Best of providers.
And you surely call them to a straight path.
But those who do not believe in the Hereafter are indeed straying from the path.
Even if We were to show them mercy and remove the hardship that is upon them, they would persist blindly in their transgression.
We have already seized them with punishment, but they did not humble themselves to their Lord, nor did they submit.
Until when We open for them a gate of severe punishment, suddenly they will be plunged into despair within it.
He is the One who produced for you hearing, sight, and hearts. How little you give thanks.
He is the One who spread you across the earth, and to Him you will be gathered.
He is the One who gives life and causes death, and to Him belongs the alternation of night and day. Will you not then use reason?
No, they say the same as what the earlier people said.
They said, “When we have died and become dust and bones, will we truly be raised again?
We have already been promised this, we and our forefathers before. This is nothing but tales of the earlier people.”
Say, “T o whom belongs the earth and whoever is in it, if you know?”
They will say, “T o Allah.” Say, “Will you not then take heed?”
Say, “Who is the Lord of the seven heavens and the Lord of the mighty Throne?”
They will say, “ Allah.” Say, “Will you not then be mindful?”
Say, “In whose hand is the dominion of everything, and He protects while none can be protected from Him, if you know?”
They will say, “ Allah.” Say, “Then how are you deceived?”
No, We have brought them the truth, and they are surely liars.

Allah has not taken any child, nor is there any god alongside Him. If there were, each god would have taken what he created, and some would have risen over others. Glory be to Allah, far above what they describe.
Knower of the unseen and the seen, exalted above what they associate.
Say, “My Lord, if You show me what they are promised,
then, my Lord, do not place me among the wrongdoing people.”
We are fully able to show you what We promise them.
Repel evil with what is better. We know best what they describe.
And say, “My Lord, I seek refuge in You from the promptings of the devils,
and I seek refuge in You, my Lord, from their presence.”
Until when death comes to one of them, he says, “My Lord, send me back,
so that I may do good in what I left behind.” No. It is only a word he speaks, and behind them is a barrier until the Day they are raised.
Then when the T rumpet is blown, there will be no family ties between them that Day, nor will they ask one another.
So those whose scales are heavy, they are the successful.
And those whose scales are light, they are the ones who have lost themselves. In Hell they will remain forever.
The Fire will scorch their faces, and in it they will be grimacing.
“Were My verses not recited to you, yet you used to deny them?”
They will say, “Our Lord, our misery overwhelmed us, and we were a misguided people.
Our Lord, bring us out of it. If we return, then we will indeed be wrongdoers.”
He will say, “Remain in it, despised, and do not speak to Me.
There was a group of My servants who used to say, ‘Our Lord, we believe, so forgive us and have mercy on us. You are the Best of those who show mercy.’
But you took them in mockery, until they made you forget My remembrance, and you used to laugh at them.
T oday I have rewarded them for their patience. They are the ones who have triumphed.”
He will say, “How many years did you remain on the earth?”
They will say, “We remained a day, or part of a day. Ask those who count.”
He will say, “You remained only a little, if only you had known.
Did you think that We created you without purpose, and that you would not be returned to Us?”
So exalted is Allah, the T rue King. There is no god but Him, Lord of the Noble Throne.
Whoever calls upon another god alongside Allah, for which he has no proof, his reckoning is only with his Lord. The disbelievers will not succeed.
And say, “My Lord, forgive and have mercy. You are the Best of those who show mercy.”

surahs.name: An-Nūr (The Light)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: A surah We have sent down and made binding, and We have sent down in it clear verses, so that you may take heed.

The woman who commits adultery and the man who commits adultery, lash each of them a hundred lashes. Do not let compassion for them overcome you in the law of Allah, if you believe in Allah and the Last Day. And let a group of the believers witness their punishment.

The man who commits adultery does not marry except an adulteress or an associator, and the woman who commits adultery is not married except by an adulterer or an associator. That has been made forbidden for the believers.
Those who accuse chaste women, then do not bring four witnesses, lash them eighty lashes and do not accept their testimony ever. Those are the rebellious,
except those who repent after that and set matters right.  Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
Those who accuse their spouses and have no witnesses except themselves, then the testimony of one of them is four testimonies by Allah that he is among the truthful,
and the fifth that the curse of Allah be upon him if he is among the liars.
And the punishment will be turned away from her if she gives four testimonies by Allah that he is among the liars,
and the fifth that the wrath of Allah be upon her if he is among the truthful.
If not for the favour of Allah upon you and His mercy, and that Allah is Accepting of repentance, All-Wise.

Those who brought forward the slander are a group from among you. Do not think it is bad for you. Rather, it is good for you. For every person among them is what he earned of sin, and the one who took the greater share of it will have a tremendous punishment.
Why, when you heard it, did the believing men and believing women not think well of one another and say, “This is a clear lie”?
Why did they not bring four witnesses for it? Since they did not bring witnesses, then with Allah they are the liars.
If not for the favour of Allah upon you and His mercy in this world and the Hereafter, a great punishment would have touched you for what you plunged into.
When you received it with your tongues and said with your mouths what you had no knowledge of, and you thought it was something trivial, while with Allah it is immense.
Why, when you heard it, did you not say, “It is not for us to speak of this. Glory be to You. This is a tremendous slander”?
Allah warns you not to ever return to the like of it, if you are believers.
Allah makes the signs clear to you. Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
Those who love for indecency to spread among the believers will have a painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter. Allah knows, and you do not know.
If not for the favour of Allah upon you and His mercy, and that Allah is Most-Kind, Ever-Merciful.

O you who believe, do not follow the footsteps of Shayṭān. Whoever follows the footsteps of Shayṭān, then he surely commands indecency and wrongdoing. If not for the favour of Allah upon you and His mercy, not one of you would ever have been purified. But Allah purifies whom He wills. Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

Those of virtue and means among you should not swear to withhold from relatives, the poor, and those who emigrated in the path of Allah. Let them pardon and overlook. Do you not love that Allah should forgive you? Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
Those who accuse chaste, innocent, believing women are cursed in this world and the Hereafter, and for them is a tremendous punishment.
On the Day when their tongues, their hands, and their feet will testify against them about what they used to do.
That Day Allah will repay them in full with their due, and they will know that Allah is the Clear T ruth.

Impure women are for impure men, and impure men are for impure women. Good women are for good men, and good men are for good women. They are declared innocent of what they say. For them is forgiveness and a noble provision.
O you who believe, do not enter houses other than your own until you seek permission and greet their people. That is better for you, so that you may take heed.
If you find no one in them, then do not enter until permission is given to you. And if it is said to you, “Go back,” then go back. That is purer for you. Allah knows what you do.
There is no blame upon you for entering uninhabited houses in which you have some use. Allah knows what you reveal and what you conceal.
T ell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their private parts. That is purer for them. Allah is fully aware of what they do.

And tell the believing women to lower their gaze, guard their private parts, and not display their adornment except what normally appears of it. Let them draw their coverings over their chests and not reveal their adornment except to their husbands, or th eir fathers, or their husbands’ fathers, or their sons, or their husbands’ sons, or their brothers, or their brothers’ sons, or their sisters’ sons, or their women, or those whom their right hands possess, or male attendants with no desire, or children who are not yet aware of women’s private aspects. Let them not strike their feet so as to reveal what they conceal of their adornment. Turn to Allah all together, O believers, so that you may succeed.
Marry off the unmarried among you, and the righteous among your male and female servants. If they are poor, Allah will enrich them from His bounty. Allah is All Encompassing, All-Knowing.

Let those who cannot find the means to marry remain chaste until Allah enriches them from His bounty. And those who seek a contract of emancipation from those whom your right hands possess, then write it for them if you know good in them, and give them fr om the wealth of Allah which He has given you. Do not compel your slave women into prostitution if they desire chastity, seeking the fleeting gains of worldly life. If someone compels them, then Allah, after their compulsion, is Most-Forgiving, Ever- Merciful.
We have certainly sent down to you clear verses, examples of those who passed away before you, and an instruction for the mindful.

Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth. The example of His light is like a niche within which is a lamp. The lamp is in a glass, the glass as if it were a shining star, lit from a blessed olive tree, neither eastern nor western. Its oil nearly gives light even if fire does not touch it. Light upon light. Allah guides to His light whom He wills. Allah sets forth examples for people, and Allah knows everything.
In houses Allah has permitted to be raised and His name to be remembered in them. He is glorified in them morning and evening,
men whom neither trade nor sale distract from the remembrance of Allah, from upholding prayer, and from giving zakah. They fear a Day when hearts and eyes will be turned in fear.
so that Allah may reward them with the best of what they did and increase them from His bounty. Allah provides for whom He wills without measure.

As for those who disbelieve, their deeds are like a mirage in a desert. The thirsty one thinks it is water, until when he reaches it, he finds it to be nothing, but he finds Allah there, who pays him in full his account. Allah is swift in reckoning.

Or like layers of darkness in a deep sea, covered by a wave, above it a wave, above it clouds. Layers of darkness, one above another. When he stretches out his hand, he can barely see it. Whoever Allah does not grant light, for him there is no light.
Do you not see that Allah is glorified by whoever is in the heavens and the earth, and by the birds with wings outspread? Each knows its prayer and its glorification. Allah fully knows what they do.
T o Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and to Allah is the final return.

Do you not see that Allah drives clouds, then brings them together, then makes them into layers, and you see rain come out from within them? He sends down from the sky mountains of hail, striking with it whom He wills and turning it away from whom He will s. The flash of its lightning almost takes away their sight.
Allah alternates the night and the day. In that there is surely a lesson for those who have insight.

Allah created every living creature from water. Among them are those that move on their bellies, among them are those that walk on two legs, and among them are those that walk on four. Allah creates what He wills. Allah has power over everything.
We have certainly sent down clear verses. Allah guides whom He wills to a straight path.
They say, “We believe in Allah and in the Messenger, and we obey,” then a group of them turn away after that. Those are not believers.
When they are called to Allah and His Messenger so that he may judge between them, suddenly a group of them turn away.
But if the right is theirs, they come to him readily, submitting.
Is there sickness in their hearts, or have they doubted, or do they fear that Allah and His Messenger will be unjust to them? No, it is they who are the wrongdoers.
The words of the believers, when they are called to Allah and His Messenger so that he may judge between them, are only that they say, “We hear and we obey.” Those are the ones who succeed.
Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, stands in awe of Allah, and is mindful of Him, then those are the ones who are successful.
They swear by Allah with their strongest oaths that if you were to command them, they would surely go forth. Say, “Do not swear. Obedience is known. Allah is fully aware of what you do.”

Say, “Obey Allah and obey the Messenger. But if you turn away, then he is responsible only for what he has been entrusted with, and you are responsible for what you have been entrusted with. If you obey him, you will be guided. The Messenger’s duty is only clear delivery.”

Allah has promised those among you who believe and do good deeds that He will surely grant them succession in the land, just as He granted it to those before them, and that He will firmly secure for them their way of life which He has approved for them, an d that He will surely replace their fear with security. They worship Me, not associating anything with Me. Whoever disbelieves after that, then those are the rebellious.
And uphold the prayer, give zakah, and obey the Messenger, so that you may be shown mercy.
Do not think that those who disbelieve  can escape in the land. Their refuge is the Fire. What an evil destination.

O you who believe, let those whom your right hands possess and those among you who have not yet reached puberty ask permission of you at three times: before the dawn prayer, when you set aside your clothing at midday, and after the night prayer. These are three times of privacy for you. There is no blame upon you nor upon them outside of these times, as you move about among one another. In this way Allah makes the signs clear to you. Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
When the children among you reach puberty, let them ask permission as those before them asked permission. In this way Allah makes His signs clear to you. Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

As for elderly women who no longer expect marriage, there is no blame upon them if they set aside their outer garments without displaying adornment. But to remain modest is better for them. Allah is All -Hearing, All-Knowing.

There is no blame upon the blind, nor is there blame upon the lame, nor is there blame upon the sick, nor upon yourselves for eating in your own houses, or the houses of your fathers, or the houses of your mothers, or the houses of your brothers, or the h ouses of your sisters, or the houses of your paternal uncles, or the houses of your paternal aunts, or the houses of your maternal uncles, or the houses of your maternal aunts, or houses whose keys you possess, or the house of your friend. There is no blame upon you whether you eat together or separately. When you enter houses, then greet one another with a greeting from Allah, blessed and good. In this way Allah makes the signs clear to you, so that you may understand.

The true believers are only those who believe in Allah and His Messenger, and when they are with him on a matter requiring collective action, they do not leave until they seek his permission. Those who seek your permission are the ones who truly believe in Allah and His Messenger. So when they ask your permission for some of their affairs, then grant permission to whom you will of them, and seek forgiveness for them from Allah. Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

Do not make the calling of the Messenger among you like the calling of one of you to another. Allah already knows those of you who slip away under cover. So let those who go against his command beware lest a trial strike them or a painful punishment afflict them.

Surely, to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. He already knows the state you are in. And on the Day they are returned to Him, He will inform them of what they did. Allah has full knowledge of everything.

surahs.name: Al-Furqān (The Criterion)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Blessed is the One who sent down the Criterion upon His servant, so that he may be a warner to all worlds.
The One to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, who has not taken a child, who has no partner in dominion, and who created everything and measured it with precise measure.
Y et they have taken gods besides Him who create nothing but are themselves created, who hold no power to harm or benefit themselves, and who possess no power over death, life, or resurrection.
Those who disbelieve say, “This is nothing but a lie he has invented, and others have helped him in it.” They have certainly brought wrongdoing and falsehood.
And they say, “Stories of earlier people which he has had written down, and they are recited to him morning and evening.”
Say, “It has been sent down by the One who knows the secret in the heavens and the earth. He is truly Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.”
They say, “What is this Messenger who eats food and walks in the markets? Why has an angel not been sent down to him to be a warner alongside him?
Or a treasure be cast to him, or he have a garden from which he eats?” And the wrongdoers say, “You are only following a man under spell.”
See how they strike comparisons for you, so they have gone astray and cannot find a way.
Blessed is the One who, if He wills, could grant you better than that, Gardens beneath which rivers flow, and could make palaces for you.
But they deny the Hour, and We have prepared a Blaze for those who deny the Hour.
When it sees them from a distant place, they will hear its raging and roaring.
And when they are thrown into a narrow place within it, bound together, they will call there for destruction.
“Do not call today for one destruction, but call for many destructions.”
Say, “Is that better, or the Garden of lasting stay which has been promised to the mindful? It will be for them a reward and a destination.”
For them in it is whatever they wish, remaining there forever. This is a promise binding upon your Lord.
On the Day He gathers them and what they worship besides Allah, He will say, “W as it you who led My servants astray, or did they themselves lose the way?”

They will say, “Glory be to You. It was not for us to take any protectors besides You. But You allowed them and their forefathers to enjoy life until they forgot the reminder and became a ruined people.”
“So they have denied you in what you say, and you will not be able to avert it nor receive help. Whoever among you does wrong, We will make him taste a great punishment.”
We did not send before you any messengers except that they ate food and walked in the markets. We made some of you a test for others. Will you then be patient? Your Lord is All Seeing.
And those who do not expect to meet Us say, “Why have angels not been sent down to us, or why do we not see our Lord?” They have grown arrogant within themselves and have exceeded all bounds.
On the Day they see the angels, there will be no good news that Day for the criminals, and they will say, “ A barrier, completely barred.”
We will turn to the deeds they did and make them scattered dust.
The companions of the Garden on that Day will have the best place of rest and the finest dwelling.
On the Day the sky will split apart with clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive descent,
true authority that Day will belong to the Most-Merciful, and it will be a difficult Day for the disbelievers.
On the Day the wrongdoer will bite his hands, saying, “If only I had taken a path with the Messenger.
If only I had not taken so and so as a close companion.
He led me away from the reminder after it had come to me.” Shayṭān is ever a betrayer to the human being.
The Messenger will say, “My Lord, my people have taken this Qur’an as something abandoned.”
In this way We appointed for every prophet an enemy from among the criminals. But your Lord is sufficient as a guide and a helper.
Those who disbelieve say, “Why was the Qur’an not sent down to him all at once?” In this way We strengthened your heart with it, and We delivered it with measured delivery.
They do not bring you any example except that We bring you the truth and the best explanation.
Those who will be gathered on their faces toward Hell, those are the worst in position and the furthest astray in path.
We certainly gave Mūsā the Book, and We appointed with him his brother Hārūn as a support.
We said, “Go both of you to the people who denied Our signs.” Then We destroyed them with complete destruction.
And the people of Nūḥ, when they denied the messengers, We drowned them and made them a sign for people. We have prepared a painful punishment for the wrongdoers.
And ʿĀd and Thamūd, and the people of the well, and many generations between them.
For each We set forth examples, and each We destroyed with total destruction.
They have certainly passed by the town upon which the evil rain was sent down. Have they not seen it? No, they do not expect resurrection.
When they see you, they take you only in mockery, saying, “Is this the one Allah sent as a messenger?
He nearly led us astray from our gods, had we not remained steadfast to them.” They will soon know, when they see the punishment, who is most astray in path.
Have you seen the one who takes his own desire as his god? W ould you then be a guardian over him?
Or do you think that most of them hear or have reason? They are only like livestock. No, they are even further astray from the path.
Do you not see how your Lord extends the shadow? If He willed, He could have made it still. Then We made the sun its guide.
Then We gradually draw it in to Ourselves with gentle drawing.
He is the One who made the night a covering for you, and sleep for rest, and made the day a rising.
He is the One who sends the winds as glad news before His mercy, and We send down pure water from the sky,
so that We may bring a dead land to life with it, and give drink from it to what We have created, many livestock and people.
We have distributed it among them so that they may take heed, but most people refuse anything but denial.
If We had willed, We could have sent a warner to every town.
So do not obey the disbelievers, but strive against them with it, a great striving.
He is the One who released the two seas, one fresh and sweet, the other salty and bitter, and He placed between them a barrier and a firm boundary.
He is the One who created the human being from water, then made for him ties of lineage and marriage. Your Lord is fully able.
Y et they worship besides Allah what neither benefits them nor harms them. The disbeliever is ever a supporter against his Lord.
We did not send you except as a bringer of good news and a warner.
Say, “I ask you for no reward for it, except that whoever wills may take a path to his Lord.”
Put your trust in the Ever-Living who does not die, and glorify His praise. He is fully aware of the sins of His servants.
He is the One who created the heavens and the earth and everything between them in six days, then established Himself over the Throne, the Most-Merciful. Ask one who is well informed about Him.
When it is said to them, “Prostrate to the Most-Merciful,” they say, “What is the Most-Merciful? Are we to prostrate to what you command us?” And it increases them only in aversion.
Blessed is the One who placed constellations in the sky, and placed in it a lamp and a shining moon.
He is the One who made the night and the day follow one another for whoever wishes to reflect or to be grateful.
The servants of the Most-Merciful are those who walk upon the earth with humility, and when the ignorant address them, they say, “Peace.”
Those who spend the night prostrating and standing before their Lord,
and those who say, “Our Lord, turn away from us the punishment of Hell. Its punishment is indeed relentless.
It is an evil place to settle and to remain.”
Those who, when they spend, are neither wasteful nor tight fisted, but keep a balanced course between that,
and those who do not call upon another god alongside Allah, do not kill the soul which Allah has made sacred except with right, and do not commit adultery. Whoever does that will face punishment.
The punishment will be doubled for him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will remain in it humiliated forever,
except for those who repent, believe, and do good deeds. For them Allah will replace their evil deeds with good ones. Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever- Merciful.
