Whoever repents and does good has truly turned to Allah in sincere return.
Those who do not bear false witness, and when they pass by empty talk, they pass by with dignity,
and those who, when reminded of the verses of their Lord, do not fall upon them deaf and blind,
and those who say, “Our Lord, grant us from our spouses and our children comfort to our eyes, and make us leaders for the mindful of Allah.”
Those will be rewarded with the highest place for what they patiently endured, and they will be met there with greeting and peace,
remaining there forever. What a fine place to settle and remain.
Say, “My Lord would not care for you were it not for your calling upon Him. But you have denied, so the consequences remain inevitable.”

surahs.name: Ash-Shuʿarāʾ (The Poets)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Ṭā Sīn Mīm.
These are the verses of the clear Book.
Perhaps you would wear yourself down with grief because they do not believe.
And if We willed, We could send down upon them a sign from the sky, and their necks would bow to it in submission.
Y et no new reminder comes to them from the Most-Merciful except that they turn away from it.
They have denied, so the truth of what they used to mock will soon come upon them.
Have they not looked at the earth, how much We have caused to grow in it of every noble kind?
In this there is surely a sign, yet most of them do not believe.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful.
And when your Lord called out to Mūsā, saying, “Go to the wrongdoing people,
the people of Firʿawn. Will they not be mindful of Allah?”
He said, “My Lord, I fear that they will deny me,
and my chest will tighten, and my tongue will not be fluent, so send for Hārūn.
They also have a charge against me, and I fear they will kill me.”
He said, “No. Go, both of you, with Our signs. We are with you, listening.
So go to Firʿawn and say, ‘We are messengers of the Lord of all worlds,
to send with us the children of Isrāʾīl.’”
He said, “Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you remained among us for years of your life?
And you did the deed you did, and you were among the ungrateful.”
He said, “I did it then while I was among those who were unaware.
“So I fled from you when I feared you, then my Lord granted me judgement and made me among the messengers.
And is this a favour you remind me of, that you enslaved the children of Isrāʾīl?”
Firʿawn said, “ And what is the Lord of all worlds?”
He said, “The Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, if you would be certain.”
He said to those around him, “Do you not hear?”
He said, “Your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers.”
He said, “Your messenger who has been sent to you is truly mad.”
He said, “The Lord of the east and the west and whatever is between them, if you use your reason.
He said, “If you take a god other than me, I will surely make you among the imprisoned.”
He said, “Even if I bring you something clear?”
He said, “Then bring it, if you are among the truthful.”
So he threw down his staff, and suddenly it was a great serpent, clear and unmistakable.
And he drew out his hand, and it appeared white to those looking on.
He said to the chiefs around him, “This is surely a skilled magician,
who wants to drive you out of your land by his magic. So what do you advise?”
They said, “Delay him and his brother, and send gatherers to the cities,
to bring you every knowledgeable magician.”
So the magicians were gathered at a set time on an appointed day,
and it was said to the people, “Will you gather,
so that we may follow the magicians if they are the ones who prevail?”
When the magicians came, they said to Firʿawn, “Will there truly be a reward for us if we are the ones who prevail?”
He said, “Y es, and you will then be among those brought close.”
Mūsā said to them, “Throw down whatever you are going to throw.”
So they threw down their ropes and their staffs, and said, “By the might of Firʿawn, we will surely prevail.”
Then Mūsā threw down his staff, and it swallowed up what they were fabricating.
So the magicians fell down in prostration.
They said, “We believe in the Lord of all worlds,
the Lord of Mūsā and Hārūn.”

He said, “You believed in him before I gave you permission. He is surely your chief who taught you magic. But you will soon know. I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you all.”
They said, “No harm. We are returning to our Lord.”
We hope that our Lord will forgive us our sins, for we are the first of the believers.”
And We revealed to Mūsā, “Set out by night with My servants. You will surely be pursued.”
So Firʿawn sent callers through the cities,
saying, “These are only a small band,
and they have certainly enraged us,
and we are all alert and prepared.”
So We drove them out of gardens and springs,
and treasures and an honoured place,
just so. And We caused the children of Isrāʾīl to inherit them.
Then they pursued them at sunrise.
When the two groups saw one another, the companions of Mūsā said, “We will surely be overtaken.”
He said, “No. My Lord is with me. He will guide me.”
So We revealed to Mūsā, “Strike the sea with your staff.” And it split apart, and each part was like a towering mountain.
Then We brought the others close to that place,
and We saved Mūsā and all who were with him,
then We drowned the others.
In this there is surely a sign, yet most of them do not believe.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful.
And recite to them the account of Ibrāhīm,
when he said to his father and his people, “What is it that you worship?”
They said, “We worship idols, and we remain devoted to them.”
He said, “Do they hear you when you call,
or benefit you, or cause you harm?”
They said, “No, but we found our forefathers doing so.”
He said, “Have you considered what you have been worshipping,
you and your forefathers of old?
They are enemies to me, except the Lord of all worlds,
who created me and then guides me,
and who feeds me and gives me drink,
and when I fall ill, He is the One who heals me,
and who will cause me to die, then bring me back to life,
and who I hope will forgive me my error on the Day of Judgement.
“My Lord, grant me judgement and join me with the righteous.
And grant me a truthful mention among later people,
and make me among the inheritors of the Garden of delight,
and forgive my father, for he was among those who went astray,
and do not disgrace me on the Day they are raised,
the Day when neither wealth nor children will benefit,
except one who comes to Allah with a sound heart.”
And the Garden will be brought near for the mindful of Allah,
and the Blaze will be made visible to those who went astray,
and it will be said to them, “Where is what you used to worship,
besides Allah? Can they help you, or help themselves?”
Then they will be hurled into it, they and those who went astray,
and all the forces of Iblīs together.
They will say, while they argue within it,
“By Allah, we were clearly in error,
when we made you equal with the Lord of all worlds.
And it was only the criminals who led us astray.
So now we have no intercessors,
nor a devoted friend.
If only we could have another return, so that we might be among the believers.”
In this there is surely a sign, yet most of them do not believe.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful.
The people of Nūḥ denied the messengers,
when their brother Nūḥ said to them, “Will you not be mindful?
I am a trustworthy messenger to you,
so be mindful of Allah and obey me.
I ask of you no reward for it. My reward is only with the Lord of all worlds.
So be mindful of Allah and obey me.”
They said, “Should we believe you when it is the lowest among us who follow you?”
He said, “What knowledge do I have of what they used to do?
Their account is only with my Lord, if you but realised.
And I am not one to drive away the believers.
I am only a clear warner.”
They said, “If you do not stop, O Nūḥ, you will surely be among those stoned.”
He said, “My Lord, my people have denied me,
so judge decisively between me and them, and save me and those with me of the believers.”
So We saved him and those with him in the laden ship,
then We drowned the rest afterwards.
In this there is surely a sign, yet most of them do not believe.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful.
ʿĀd denied the messengers,
when their brother Hūd said to them, “Will you not be mindful?
I am a trustworthy messenger to you,
so be mindful of Allah and obey me.
I ask of you no reward for it. My reward is only with the Lord of all worlds.
Do you build on every high place a monument, acting aimlessly,
and take for yourselves strongholds, as if you were to live forever?
And when you strike, you strike as tyrants.
So be mindful of Allah and obey me.
And be mindful of the One who has supplied you with what you know,
supplied you with livestock and children,
and gardens and springs.
I truly fear for you the punishment of a mighty Day.”
They said, “It is all the same to us whether you warn us or you are not among those who warn.
This is nothing but the custom of the earlier people,
and we are not going to be punished.”
So they denied him, and We destroyed them. In this there is surely a sign, yet most of them do not believe.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful.
Thamūd denied the messengers,
when their brother Ṣāliḥ said to them, “Will you not be mindful?
I am a trustworthy messenger to you,
so be mindful of Allah and obey me.
I ask of you no reward for it. My reward is only with the Lord of all worlds.
Will you be left secure in what is here,
among gardens and springs,
and crops and date palms with soft fruit,
carving out homes from the mountains with skill?
So be mindful of Allah and obey me,
and do not obey the command of those who go beyond bounds,
those who spread corruption in the land and do not set things right.”
They said, “You are only one of those bewitched.
You are nothing but a human like us, so bring a sign, if you are among the truthful.”
He said, “This is a she camel. She has her share to drink, and you have your share to drink on a known day.
And do not touch her with harm, or the punishment of a mighty Day will seize you.”
But they hamstrung her, then became full of regret.
So the punishment seized them. In this there is surely a sign, yet most of them do not believe.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful.
The people of Lūṭ denied the messengers,
when their brother Lūṭ said to them, “Will you not be mindful?
I am a trustworthy messenger to you,
so be mindful of Allah and obey me.
I ask of you no reward for it. My reward is only with the Lord of all worlds.
Do you approach males among all people,
and leave what your Lord has created for you from your spouses? You are truly a people who go beyond bounds.”
They said, “If you do not stop, O Lū ṭ, you will surely be among those driven out.”
He said, “I am indeed among those who detest your actions.
My Lord, save me and my family from what they do.”
So We saved him and his family, all of them,
except an old woman among those who remained behind.
Then We destroyed the others,
and We rained upon them a rain. How terrible was the rain of those who were warned.
In this there is surely a sign, yet most of them do not believe.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful.
The people of the Thicket denied the messengers,
when Shuʿayb said to them, “Will you not be mindful?
I am a trustworthy messenger to you,
so be mindful of Allah and obey me.
I ask of you no reward for it. My reward is only with the Lord of all worlds.
Give full measure, and do not be among those who cause loss,
and weigh with a straight balance,
and do not deprive people of what is theirs, and do not spread corruption in the land.
Be mindful of the One who created you and the earlier generations.”
They said, “You are only one of those bewitched.
You are nothing but a human like us, and we think you are surely among the liars.
So make fragments of the sky fall upon us, if you are among the truthful.”
He said, “My Lord knows best what you do.”
So they denied him, and the punishment of the Day of the Shadow seized them. It was truly the punishment of a mighty Day.
In this there is surely a sign, yet most of them do not believe.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful.
And indeed, this is a revelation from the Lord of all worlds.
The T rustworthy Spirit brought it down,
upon your heart, so that you may be among the warners,
in a clear Arabic tongue.
And it is certainly mentioned in the scriptures of the earlier people.
W as it not a sign for them that the scholars of the children of Isrāʾīl recognised it?
And had We revealed it to one of the non Arabs,
and he had recited it to them, they still would not have believed in it.
Thus We have caused it to enter the hearts of the criminals.
They would not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.
Then it will come upon them suddenly, while they are unaware.
And they will say, “Will we be given any respite?”
So is it Our punishment that they are seeking to hasten?
Have you considered if We were to let them enjoy themselves for years,
then what they were promised came to them,
their enjoyment would not benefit them in the least.
And We never destroyed any town except that it had warners,
as a reminder. And We were not unjust.
And it was not the devils who brought it down.
It is not for them, nor are they able.
They are surely kept away from hearing it.
So do not call upon another god alongside Allah, or you will be among those punished.
And warn your closest relatives.
And lower your wing to those of the believers who follow you.
But if they disobey you, then say, “I am free of what you do.”
And place your trust in the All-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful,
who sees you when you stand,
and sees your movements among those who prostrate.
Indeed, He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
Shall I inform you about whom the devils descend upon?
They descend upon every lying sinner.
They pass on what they hear, and most of them are liars.
As for the poets, those who go astray follow them.
Do you not see that they wander aimlessly through every valley,
and that they say what they do not do,
except those who believe, do good deeds, remember Allah often, and stand up for themselves after being wronged. Those who do wrong will soon know what outcome awaits them.

surahs.name: An-Naml (The Ant)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Ṭā Sīn. These are the verses of the Qur’an, a clear Book,
a guidance and good news for the believers,
those who uphold the prayer, give zakah, and are certain of the Hereafter.
As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have made their deeds seem pleasing to them, so they wander blindly.
Those are the ones who will have the worst punishment, and in the Hereafter they will be the greatest losers.
And you are certainly receiving the Qur’an from One who is All-Wise, All- Knowing.
When Mūsā said to his family, “I have noticed a fire. I will bring you news from it, or bring you a burning brand so that you may warm yourselves.”
But when he came to it, he was called, “Blessed is the One who is at the fire, and those around it. Glory be to Allah, the Lord of all worlds.
O Mūsā, it is I am Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.”
And, “Throw down your staff.” But when he saw it moving as if it were a swift snake, he turned and fled without looking back. “O Mūsā, do not fear. Messengers do not fear in My presence,
except for one who does wrong, then replaces evil with good. I am indeed Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
And put your hand into your cloak. It will come out white without harm, among nine signs to Firʿawn and his people. They are truly a defiant people.”
But when Our signs came to them, clear to see, they said, “This is obvious magic.”
They denied them while their souls were certain of them, out of wrongdoing and arrogance. So look at the end of those who spread corruption.
And We gave knowledge to Dāwūd and Sulaymān, and they said, “ All praise belongs to Allah, who has favoured us over many of His believing servants.”
And Sulaymān inherited from Dāwūd, and he said, “O people, we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from everything. This is indeed a clear favour.”
And Sulaymān’s armies were gathered for him, of jinn, humans, and birds, and they were kept in order.
Until, when they came upon the valley of the ants, an ant said, “O ants, enter your dwellings, lest Sulaymān and his armies crush you while they are unaware.”

So he smiled, amused by her words, and said, “My Lord, inspire me to be grateful for Your favour which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do good that pleases You, and admit me by Your mercy among Your righteous servants.”
And he inspected the birds, then said, “Why do I not see the hoopoe, or is he among the absent?”
He said, “I will surely punish him with a severe punishment, or slaughter him, unless he brings me a clear excuse.”
But he did not stay long, and he said, “I have grasped what you have not grasped, and I have come to you from Sabaʾ with certain news.
I found a woman ruling over them, and she has been given from everything, and she has a magnificent throne.
I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah. Shayṭān has made their deeds seem pleasing to them and turned them away from the path, so they are not guided,
so that they do not prostrate to Allah, who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and the earth and knows what you conceal and what you reveal.
Allah, there is no god except Him, the Lord of the mighty Throne.”
He said, “We will see whether you have spoken the truth or are among the liars.
T ake this letter of mine and deliver it to them, then turn away from them and see what response they give.”
She said, “O chiefs, a noble letter has been delivered to me.
It is from Sulaymān, and it is, ‘In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.
“Do not exalt yourselves against me, and come to me in submission.”
She said, “O chiefs, advise me in my affair. I do not decide a matter until you are present.”
They said, “We possess strength and possess great might, but the decision is yours, so see what you will command.”
She said, “When kings enter a town, they ruin it and turn the honoured of its people into the humiliated. That is how they act.
So I will send them a gift and see what the messengers return with.”
So when he came to Sulaymān, he said, “ Are you offering me with wealth? What Allah has given me is better than what He has given you. Rather, you rejoice in your gift.
Return to them. We will surely come to them with forces they cannot withstand, and we will drive them out from it, humiliated and brought low.”
He said, “O chiefs, which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?”
A powerful one from the jinn said, “I will bring it to you before you rise from your place. I am strong and trustworthy for it.”

One who had knowledge from the Book said, “I will bring it to you before you can blink.” Then when he saw it set before him, he said, “This is from the favour of my Lord, to test me whether I am grateful or ungrateful. Whoever is grateful is only grateful for his own good, and whoever is ungrateful, then my Lord is Free of need, Most-Generous.”
He said, “ Alter her throne for her. Let us see whether she is guided or among those who are not guided.”
So when she came, it was said, “Is your throne like this?” She said, “It is as if it were the same.” And knowledge had been given to us before her, and we were in submission.
What she used to worship besides Allah had kept her back. She belonged to a people who disbelieved.

It was said to her, “Enter the palace.” But when she saw it, she thought it was a body of water and uncovered her legs. He said, “It is a palace paved with glass.” She said, “My Lord, I have wronged myself, and I submit with Sulaymān to Allah, the Lord of all worlds.”
And We sent to Thamūd their brother Ṣāliḥ, saying, “W orship Allah.” But they became two opposing groups.
He said, “O my people, why do you seek to hasten evil before good? Why do you not seek Allah’s forgiveness, so that you may receive mercy?”
They said, “We take you and those with you as a bad omen.” He said, “Your omen is with Allah. Rather, you are a people being tested.”
And there were in the city nine men who spread corruption in the land and did not set things right.

They said, “Swear by Allah to one another that we will surely attack him and his family by night. Then we will say to his heir, ‘We did not witness the destruction of his family, and we are indeed truthful.’”
And they plotted a plot, and We plotted a plot, while they did not realise.
So see how the outcome of their plot was, that We destroyed them and their people, all together.
Those are their homes, lying empty because of the wrong they did. In this there is surely a sign for people who know.
And We saved those who believed and were mindful.
And Lūṭ, when he said to his people, “Do you commit indecency while you see clearly?
Do you approach men with desire instead of women? Rather, you are a people who act ignorantly.”
But the only response of his people was that they said, “Drive out the family of Lūṭ from your town. They are people who keep themselves pure.”
So We saved him and his family, except his wife. We decreed her to be among those who remained behind.
And We rained upon them a rain. How terrible was the rain of those who were warned.
Say, “ All praise belongs to Allah, and peace be upon His servants whom He has chosen.” Is Allah better, or what they associate with Him?

Or who created the heavens and the earth and sent down water from the sky for you, by which We cause beautiful gardens to grow, whose trees you could not have caused to grow? Is there a god alongside Allah? Rather, they are a people who set equals.
Or who made the earth a stable place, set rivers through it, placed firm mountains upon it, and put a barrier between the two seas? Is there a god alongside Allah? Rather, most of them do not know.
Or who responds to the one in distress when he calls upon Him, removes harm, and makes you inheritors of the earth? Is there a god alongside Allah? Little do you reflect.
Or who guides you through the darkness of the land and sea, and sends the winds as good news before His mercy? Is there a god alongside Allah? Exalted is Allah above what they associate.
Or who begins creation, then brings it back, and provides for you from the sky and the earth? Is there a god alongside Allah? Say, “Bring your proof, if you are truthful.”
Say, “No one in the heavens or the earth knows the unseen except Allah.” And they do not realise when they will be raised.
Rather, their knowledge of the Hereafter has fallen short. In fact, they are in doubt about it. Indeed, they are blind to it.
Those who disbelieve  say, “When we and our forefathers have become dust, will we truly be brought out again?
This has been promised to us and to our forefathers before. This is nothing but stories of the earlier people.”
Say, “T ravel through the land and see how the end of the criminals.”
And do not grieve over them, and do not be distressed by what they plot.
And they say, “When will this promise be, if you are truthful?”
Say, “Perhaps some of what you seek to hasten is already close behind you.”
And indeed, your Lord is full of favour toward the people, but most of them are not grateful.
And indeed, your Lord knows what their chests conceal and what they reveal.
There is nothing hidden in the heaven or the earth except that it is in a clear Record.
Indeed, this Qur’an recounts to the children of Isrāʾīl much of what they differ over.
And it is certainly guidance and mercy for the believers.
Indeed, your Lord will judge between them by His judgement, and He is the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.
So place your trust in Allah. You are surely upon the clear truth.
Indeed, you cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn away retreating.
And you cannot guide the blind away from their misguidance. You can only make hear those who believe in Our signs, and they are in submission.
And when the word falls upon them, We will bring forth for them a creature from the earth, speaking to them, that the people were not certain of Our signs.
And on the Day We gather from every community a group of those who denied Our signs, and they will be restrained.
Until, when they come, He will say, “Did you deny My signs while you did not encompass them in knowledge, or what was it that you were doing?”
And the word will fall upon them because of the wrong they did, and they will not speak.
Do they not see that We made the night for them to rest in, and the day to give sight? In this there are surely signs for a people who believe.
And on the Day the trumpet is blown, those in the heavens and the earth will be struck with terror, except whom Allah wills, and all will come to Him humbled.
And you see the mountains, thinking them solid, while they pass like clouds. The work of Allah, who perfected everything. He is truly aware of what you do.
Whoever comes with a good deed will have better than it, and they will be safe from terror on that Day.
And whoever comes with an evil deed, their faces will be thrown into the Fire. Will you be repaid except for what you used to do?
Say, “I have only been commanded to worship the Lord of this land, whom He has made sacred, and to whom belongs everything. And I have been commanded to be among those who submit,
and to recite the Qur’an.” Whoever is guided is only guided for his own good, and whoever goes astray, then say, “I am only among the warners.”
And say, “ All praise belongs to Allah. He will show you His signs, and you will recognise them.” And your Lord is not unaware of what you do.

surahs.name: Al-Qaṣaṣ (The Stories)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Ṭā Sīn Mīm.
These are the verses of the clear Book.
We recite to you, in truth, some of the story of Mūsā and Firʿawn, for people who believe.

Indeed, Firʿawn exalted himself in the land and divided its people into factions, weakening one group among them, slaughtering their sons and sparing their women. He was truly among those who spread corruption.
And We intended to show favour to those who were oppressed in the land, to make them leaders, and to make them inheritors,
and to establish them in the land, and to show Firʿawn and Hāmān and their forces, through them, what they feared.
And We inspired the mother of Mūsā, “Suckle him, and when you fear for him, cast him into the river, and do not fear nor grieve. We will surely return him to you and make him among the messengers.”
So the family of Firʿawn picked him up, so that he would become for them an enemy and a source of sorrow. Indeed, Firʿawn, Hāmān, and their forces were in grave error.
And the wife of Firʿawn said, “ A comfort to the eye for me and for you. Do not kill him. Perhaps he will benefit us, or we may take him as a son.” And they were unaware.
And the heart of the mother of Mūsā became empty. She almost revealed him, had We not strengthened her heart, so that she would be among the believers.
And she said to his sister, “Follow him.” So she watched him from a distance, while they were unaware.
And We had already prevented him from accepting wet nurses, so she said, “Shall I direct you to a household who will care for him for you and be devoted to him?”
So We returned him to his mother, so that her eyes might be comforted and she would not grieve, and so that she would know that the promise of Allah is true. But most of them do not know.
And when he reached full strength and maturity, We gave him judgement and knowledge. That is how We reward those who do good.

And he entered the city at a time when its people were unaware, and he found in it two men fighting, one from his own people and one from his enemy. The one from his people called upon him for help against the one from his enemy. So Mūsā struck him with h is fist and killed him. He said, “This is from the work of Shayṭān. He is indeed a clear, misleading enemy.”
He said, “My Lord, I have wronged myself, so forgive me.” So He forgave him. He is indeed the Most-Forgiving, the Ever-Merciful.
He said, “My Lord, for the favour You have bestowed upon me, I will never again support the criminals.”
So he remained in the city fearful, watching cautiously. Then the one who had sought his help the day before cried out to him again. Mūsā said to him, “You are indeed clearly misguided.”

Then, when he was about to strike the one who was an enemy to them both, he said, “O Mūsā, do you want to kill me as you killed a person yesterday? You only want to be a tyrant in the land, and you do not want to be among those who set things right.”
And a man came running from the far end of the city. He said, “O Mūsā, the chiefs are consulting about you to kill you, so leave. I am truly giving you sincere advice.”
So he left it, fearful and alert. He said, “My Lord, save me from the wrongdoing people.”
And when he turned toward Madyan, he said, “Perhaps my Lord will guide me to the right path.”

And when he reached the water of Madyan, he found there a group of people drawing water, and he found besides them two women holding back. He said, “What is your situation?” They said, “We do not draw water until the shepherds leave, and our father is an old man.”
So he drew water for them, then turned aside to the shade and said, “My Lord, I am indeed in need of whatever good You send down to me.”

Then one of the two women came to him, walking with modesty. She said, “My father invites you so that he may reward you for drawing water for us.” So when he came to him and related the story, he said, “Do not fear. You have been saved from the wrongdoing people.”
One of the two women said, “My father, hire him. Indeed, the best one you can hire is the strong and trustworthy.”

He said, “I wish to marry you to one of these two daughters of mine on the condition that you serve me for eight years. But if you complete ten, then that is from you. I do not wish to make things difficult for you. You will find me, if Allah wills, among the righteous.”
He said, “That is agreed between me and you. Whichever of the two terms I complete, there will be no injustice upon me. And Allah is Witness over what we say.”

So when Mūsā completed the term and set out with his family, he noticed a fire at the side of the mountain. He said to his family, “Stay here. I have noticed a fire. Perhaps I will bring you news from it, or a burning brand from the fire so that you may warm yourselves.”
But when he came to it, he was called from the right side of the valley, from the tree in the blessed spot, “O Mūsā, indeed I am Allah, the Lord of all worlds.”
And, “Throw down your staff.” But when he saw it moving as if it were a serpent, he turned and fled without looking back. “O Mūsā, come forward and do not fear. You are among those who are safe.

Put your hand into your cloak. It will come out white without harm. And draw your arm close to yourself against fear. These are two proofs from your Lord to Firʿawn and his chiefs. They are truly a rebellious people.”
He said, “My Lord, I killed a soul among them, so I fear that they will kill me.
And my brother Hārūn is more fluent in speech than I am, so send him with me as support, to confirm me. I truly fear that they will deny me.”
He said, “We will strengthen you through your brother, and We will grant you both authority, so they will not reach you. With Our signs, you and those who follow you will prevail.”
But when Mūsā came to them with Our clear signs, they said, “This is nothing but fabricated magic, and we have never heard of this among our earlier forefathers.”
And Mūsā said, “My Lord knows best who comes with guidance from Him and who will have the final outcome of the home. Indeed, the wrongdoers will not succeed.”

Firʿawn said, “O chiefs, I do not know of any god for you other than me. So kindle for me a fire over clay, O Hāmān, and build me a tower, that I may look upon the god of Mūsā. I truly think he is among the liars.”
And he and his forces behaved arrogantly in the land without right, and they thought that they would not be returned to Us.
So We seized him and his forces and cast them into the sea. So see how the end of the wrongdoers was.
And We made them leaders calling to the Fire, and on the Day of Resurrection they will not be helped.
And We caused a curse to follow them in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection they will be among those made repulsive.
And We certainly gave Mūsā the Book after We had destroyed the earlier generations, as insight for the people, and as guidance and mercy, so that they might reflect.
And you were not on the western side when We decreed the matter to Mūsā, nor were you among those who witnessed it.
But We brought forth generations, and their lifespans were extended. And you were not dwelling among the people of Madyan, reciting Our verses to them, but We were the Ones who sent messengers.
And you were not at the side of the mountain when We called out, but it is a mercy from your Lord, so that you may warn a people to whom no warner came before you, so that they might reflect.

Otherwise, if a calamity were to strike them because of what their hands have sent ahead, they would say, “Our Lord, why did You not send us a messenger, so that we might have followed Your verses and been among the believers?”

But when the truth came to them from Us, they said, “Why has he not been given the like of what was given to Mūsā?” Did they not deny what was given to Mūsā before? They said, “T wo acts of magic supporting one another,” and they said, “We deny them all.”
Say, “Then bring a book from Allah that gives better guidance than these two, and I will follow it, if you are truthful.”

But if they do not respond to you, then know that they only follow their desires. And who is more misguided than one who follows his desire without guidance from Allah? Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
And We have certainly conveyed the word to them repeatedly, so that they might reflect.
Those to whom We gave the Book before it, they believe in it.
And when it is recited to them, they say, “We believe in it. It is the truth from our Lord. Indeed, we were already in submission before it.”
Those will be given their reward twice, for what they patiently endured, and they repel evil with good, and from what We have provided them they spend.
And when they hear empty talk, they turn away from it and say, “For us are our deeds, and for you are your deeds. Peace be upon you. We do not seek the ignorant.”
Indeed, you do not guide whom you love, but Allah guides whom He wills. And He knows best those who are guided.

And they say, “If we were to follow the guidance with you, we would be seized from our land.” Have We not established for them a secure sanctuary, to which fruits of all kinds are brought as provision from Us? But most of them do not know.
And how many a town We destroyed that exulted in its livelihood. Those are their dwellings, not inhabited after them except briefly. And We were the inheritors.

And your Lord would not destroy the towns until He had sent to their central town a messenger reciting Our verses to them. And We would not destroy the towns except while their people were wrongdoers.
And whatever you have been given is only the enjoyment of the life of this world and its adornment. But what is with Allah is better and more lasting. Will you not then use reason?

Is one to whom We have given a good promise, which he will surely meet, like one whom We have given enjoyment in the life of this world, then on the Day of Resurrection he will be among those brought forth?
And on the Day He calls out to them and says, “Where are My partners that you used to claim?”
Those upon whom the word has come will say, “Our Lord, these are the ones we led astray. We led them astray as we ourselves went astray. We disown them before You. They were not worshipping us.”
And it will be said, “Call upon your partners.” So they will call upon them, but they will not respond to them, and they will see the punishment. If only they had been guided.
And on the Day He calls out to them and says, “What answer did you give the messengers?”
Then all arguments will be lost to them on that Day, and they will not question one another.
But as for one who repents, believes, and does good, it may be that he will be among the successful.
And your Lord creates whatever He wills and chooses. They have no choice. Glory be to Allah, exalted above what they associate.
And your Lord knows what their chests conceal and what they reveal.
And He is Allah. There is no god except Him. All praise belongs to Him in this world and the Hereafter. His is the judgement, and to Him you will be returned.
Say, “Have you considered if Allah were to make the night continuous over you until the Day of Resurrection? Which god besides Allah could bring you light? Will you not then listen?”

Say, “Have you considered if Allah were to make the day continuous over you until the Day of Resurrection? Which god besides Allah could bring you night, so that you may rest in it? Will you not then see?”
And out of His mercy, He made for you the night and the day, so that you may rest in it and seek from His favour, and so that you may be grateful.
And on the Day He calls out to them and says, “Where are My partners that you used to claim?”
And We will draw forth from every community a witness, and We will say, “Bring your proof.” Then they will know that the truth belongs to Allah, and what they used to invent will leave them.

Indeed, Qārūn was from the people of Mūsā, but he tyrannised against them. And We gave him treasures whose keys would burden a group of strong men. When his people said to him, “Do not exult. Indeed, Allah does not love the exultant.

But seek, through what Allah has given you, the Home of the Hereafter, and do not forget your share of this world. And do good as Allah has done good to you, and do not seek corruption in the land. Indeed, Allah does not love those who spread corruption.”

He said, “I was only given it because of knowledge I possess.” Did he not know that Allah had destroyed before him generations stronger than him in power and greater in accumulation? And the criminals will not be questioned about their sins.
So he went out to his people in his splendour. Those who desired the life of this world said, “If only we had the like of what Qārūn has been given. He truly has great fortune.”
But those who were given knowledge said, “W oe to you. The reward of Allah is better for one who believes and does good. And none are granted it except those who are patient.”
So We caused the earth to swallow him and his home, and he had no group to help him against Allah, nor was he among those who could defend themselves.

And those who had wished for his position the day before began to say, “How Allah extends provision to whom He wills and restricts it. Had Allah not been gracious to us, He would have caused it to swallow us too. How the disbelievers do not succeed.”
That Home of the Hereafter We assign to those who do not seek exaltation in the land nor corruption. And the final outcome belongs to the mindful of Allah.
Whoever comes with a good deed will have better than it, and whoever comes with an evil deed, then those who commit evil deeds will not be repaid except for what they used to do.
Indeed, the One who imposed the Qur’an upon you will surely return you to a place of return. Say, “My Lord knows best who comes with guidance and who is in clear misguidance.”
And you did not expect that the Book would be cast to you, except as a mercy from your Lord. So do not be a supporter of the disbelievers.
And do not let them turn you away from the verses of Allah after they have been sent down to you. And call to your Lord, and do not be among the associators.
And do not call upon another god alongside Allah. There is no god except Him. Everything will perish except His Face. His is the judgement, and to Him you will be returned.

surahs.name: Al-ʿAnkabūt (The Spider)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Mīm.
Do people think they will be left alone simply because they say, “We believe,” and will not be tested?
We certainly tested those before them, so Allah will surely make clear who are truthful and He will surely make clear who are false.
Or do those who commit evil deeds think they can outpace Us? How badly they judge.
Whoever hopes to meet Allah, then the appointed time set by Allah is surely coming. And He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
Whoever strives, strives only for his own soul. Indeed, Allah is Free of need of all creation.
And those who believe and do good deeds, We will certainly wipe away their bad deeds from them, and We will reward them with the best of what they used to do.

And We have instructed the human being to be good to his parents. But if they strive to make you associate with Me what you have no knowledge of, then do not obey them. T o Me is your return, and I will inform you of what you used to do.
And those who believe and do good deeds, We will surely admit them among the righteous.

And among people are those who say, “We believe in Allah,” but when they are harmed for the sake of Allah, they treat the persecution of people as though it were the punishment of Allah. Y et if help comes from your Lord, they will surely say, “We were with you.” Does Allah not know best what is within the hearts of all creation?
Allah will surely make known those who believe, and He will surely make known the hypocrites.
Those who disbelieve say to those who believe, “Follow our way, and we will carry your sins.” But they will not carry any of their sins. They are surely liars.
They will carry their own burdens, and burdens along with their burdens. And they will surely be questioned on the Day of Resurrection about what they used to invent.
We certainly sent Nūḥ to his people, and he remained among them a thousand years less fifty. Then the flood seized them while they were doing wrong.
So We rescued him and those with him in the Ark, and We made it a sign for all worlds.
And Ibrāhīm, when he said to his people, “W orship Allah and be mindful of Him. That is better for you, if you only knew.

You worship idols besides Allah and create falsehood. Those you worship besides Allah have no power to provide for you. So seek provision from Allah, worship Him, and be grateful to Him. T o Him you will be returned.
And if you deny, nations before you also denied. The Messenger is only responsible for delivering the message clearly.
Have they not seen how Allah begins creation, then brings it back again? Surely that is easy for Allah.
Say, “T ravel through the land and observe how He began creation. Then Allah will bring about the final creation. Indeed, Allah  has power over everything.
He punishes whom He wills and shows mercy to whom He wills. And to Him you will be returned.
You cannot escape Him on earth or in the sky, and you have no protector or helper besides Allah.
Those who disbelieve in the signs of Allah and in the meeting with Him, they have lost hope of My mercy. And for them is a painful punishment.
But the only response of his people was that they said, “Kill him or burn him.” So Allah saved him from the fire. Surely in that are signs for people who believe.

And he said, “You have taken idols besides Allah as bonds of affection between you in this worldly life. Then on the Day of Resurrection you will disown one another and curse one another, and your home will be the Fire, and you will have no helpers.”
So Lūṭ believed in him, and he said, “I am migrating to my Lord. Indeed, He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.”

And We granted him Isḥāq and Y aʿqūb, and We placed prophethood and the Book among his descendants. And We gave him his reward in this world, and in the Hereafter he will surely be among the righteous.
And Lūṭ, when he said to his people, “You commit an outrage that none in all the worlds committed before you.
Do you approach men, cut off the road, and commit wrongdoing in your gatherings?” But the only response of his people was that they said, “Bring us the punishment of Allah, if you are truthful.”
He said, “My Lord, support me against the people who spread corruption.”
When Our messengers came to Ibrāhīm with good news, they said, “We are going to destroy the people of this town. Its people have truly been wrongdoers.”
He said, “But Lūṭ is there.” They said, “We know best who is there. We will surely save him and his household, except his wife. She is among those who will remain behind.”

And when Our messengers came to Lūṭ, he was distressed for them and felt powerless to protect them. They said, “Do not fear, and do not grieve. We will save you and your household, except your wife. She is among those who will remain behind.
We are about to bring down a punishment from the sky upon the people of this town, because of the rebellion they used to commit.”
And We have left from it a clear sign for people who use reason.
And to Madyan, their brother Shuʿayb. He said, “My people, worship Allah and hope for the Last Day, and do not recklessly spread corruption in the land.”
But they denied him, so a violent tremor seized them, and they were left lifeless in their homes.
And ʿĀd and Thamūd. Their ruins have already become clear to you. Shayṭān made their deeds appealing to them and blocked them from the path, even though they were people of insight.
And Qārūn, and Firʿawn, and Hāmān. Mūsā came to them with clear signs, but they acted arrogantly in the land, and they could not escape.

Each We seized for their sin. Among them were those upon whom We sent a storm of stones, and among them were those seized by the blast, and among them were those whom We caused the earth to swallow, and among them were those We drowned. Allah did not wrong them, but they were wronging themselves.
The example of those who take protectors besides Allah is like the spider that makes a home. And the weakest of homes is the spider’s home, if only they knew.
Allah knows whatever they call upon besides Him. And He is the All- Mighty, the All-Wise.
These examples We set out for people, but none understand them except those with knowledge.
Allah created the heavens and the earth in truth. Surely in that is a sign for the believers.
Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book, and uphold the prayer. Indeed, p rayer restrains from indecency and wrongdoing. And the remembrance of Allah is greater. Allah knows what you do.

And do not argue with the People of the Book except in the best manner, except with those among them who act unjustly. And say, “We believe in what was sent down to us and what was sent down to you. Our God and your God is One, and to Him we submit.”
And in this way We sent down the Book to you. Those to whom We gave the Book believe in it, and among these are those who believe in it. And none reject Our signs except the disbelievers.
You did not recite any book before it, nor did you write it with your right hand. Otherwise the deniers would have doubted.
Rather, it is clear signs within the hearts of those who are given knowledge. And none reject Our signs except the wrongdoers.
And they say, “Why are signs not sent down to him from his Lord?” Say, “The signs are only with Allah, and I am only a clear warner.”
Is it not enough for them that We have sent down to you the Book which is recited to them? Surely in that is mercy and a reminder for people who believe.
Say, “ Allah is sufficient as a witness between me and you. He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Those who believe in falsehood and disbelieve in Allah, they are the losers.”
They ask you to hasten the punishment. If it were not for an appointed time, the punishment would have come to them already. It will surely come upon them suddenly, while they do not realise.
They ask you to hasten the punishment, while Hell will surely surround the disbelievers.
On the Day when the punishment will cover them from above them and from beneath their feet, and He will say, “T aste what you used to do.”
O My servants who believe, My earth is vast, so worship Me alone.
Every soul will taste death. Then to Us you will be returned.
Those who believe and do good deeds, We will surely settle them in lofty dwellings of the Garden, beneath which rivers flow, remaining there forever. How excellent is the reward of those who act.
Those who are patient and place their trust in their Lord.
How many creatures do not carry their own provision. Allah provides for them and for you. And He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
If you ask them who created the heavens and the earth and placed the sun and the moon under control, they will surely say, “ Allah.” So how are they turned away?
Allah expands provision for whom He wills and restricts it for whom He wills. Indeed, Allah has full knowledge of everything.

And if you ask them who sends down water from the sky and brings the earth back to life after its death, they will surely say, “ Allah.” Say, “ All praise belongs to Allah.” Y et most of them do not understand.
This worldly life is nothing but distraction and play, while the Home of the Hereafter is the true life, if only they knew.
When they board a ship, they call upon Allah with sincere devotion to Him. But when He brings them safely to land, they associate others with Him,
so that they may be ungrateful for what We have given them and enjoy themselves. They will soon come to know.
Have they not seen that We made a secure sanctuary, while people around them are being seized? Do they still believe in falsehood and deny the blessing of Allah?
Who is more unjust than one who invents lies against Allah or denies the truth when it comes to him? Is there not a dwelling in Hell for the disbelievers?
Those who strive for Our sake, We will surely guide them to Our paths. And Allah is truly with those who do good.

surahs.name: Ar-Rūm (The Romans)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Mīm.
The Romans have been defeated
in the nearest land. Y et after their defeat, they will overcome
within a few years. T o Allah belongs the command before and after. On that day, the believers will rejoice
in the help of Allah. He gives help to whom He wills. And He is the All- Mighty, the Ever-Merciful.
This is the promise of Allah. Allah does not fail in His promise, but most people do not know.
They know what is apparent of the worldly life, but of the Hereafter they are unaware.

Do they not reflect within themselves? Allah did not create the heavens and the earth and what lies between them except with truth and for an appointed term. Y et many people deny the meeting with their Lord.

Have they not travelled through the land and seen what became of those before them? They were stronger than them in power, they cultivated the land more, and built it up more than these have built it. Their messengers came to them with clear signs. Allah did not wrong them, but they were wronging themselves.
Then the end of those who committed evil was the worst end, because they denied the signs of Allah and used to mock them.
Allah begins creation, then brings it back again. Then to Him you will be returned.
On the Day the Hour is established, the criminals will be struck silent in despair.
They will have no intercessors from those they associated with Allah, and they will deny those they once associated.
On the Day the Hour is established, that Day they will be separated.
As for those who believed and did good deeds, they will be made joyful in a garden of delight.
But as for those who disbelieved and denied Our signs and the meeting of the Hereafter, they will be brought into the punishment.
So glorify Allah when you reach the evening and when you rise in the morning.
And praise belongs to Him in the heavens and the earth, and in the late afternoon and when you reach midday.
He brings the living out of the dead and brings the dead out of the living, and He brings the earth to life after its death. And in this way you will be brought out.
And among His signs is that He created you from dust, then suddenly you were human beings spreading out.

And among His signs is that He created for you spouses from among yourselves, so that you may find calm with them, and He placed between you affection and mercy. Surely in that are signs for people who reflect.
And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the diversity of your languages and your colours. Surely in that are signs for those who know.
And among His signs is your sleep by night and by day, and your seeking of His bounty. Surely in that are signs for people who listen.

And among His signs is that He shows you lightning, causing fear and hope, and sends down water from the sky, bringing the earth back to life after its death. Indeed, in that are signs for people who use their reason.
And among His signs is that the sky and the earth stand by His command. Then when He calls you with a single call from the earth, you will come out at once.
T o Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient to Him.
He is the One who begins creation, then brings it back again, and that is easier for Him. T o Him belongs the highest description in the heavens and the earth. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

He sets forth for you an example from yourselves: do you have among those your right hands possess any partners in what We have provided you, so that you would be equal in it and fear them as you fear one another? In this way We make the signs clear for people who use reason.
But those who do wrong follow their desires without knowledge. Who then can guide those Allah leaves to go astray? They will have no helpers.

So direct your face firmly to the way of life, the natural way Allah has set upon which He created people. There is no altering the creation set by Allah. That is the upright way of life, but most people do not know.
Turn back to Him, be mindful of Him, uphold the prayer, and do not be among those who associate others with Him,
among those who split their religion and became factions, each group pleased with what it has.
When harm touches people, they call upon their Lord, turning to Him alone. But when He lets them taste mercy from Him, a group of them associate others with their Lord,
showing ingratitude for what We have given them. So enjoy yourselves; you will soon know.
Or have We sent down to them an authority that speaks of what they associate with Him?
When We let people taste mercy, they rejoice in it. But if harm touches them because of what their own hands have sent ahead, they immediately fall into despair.
Have they not seen that Allah expands provision for whom He wills and restricts it? Surely in that are signs for people who believe.
So give the relative his due, and the poor, and the traveller. That is best for those who seek the Face of Allah. Those are the ones who will succeed.

Whatever you give in interest so that it may increase through people’s wealth does not increase with Allah. But whatever you give in charity, seeking the Face of Allah, those are the ones who will receive many times more.

Allah is the One who created you, then provided for you, then will cause you to die, then will bring you back to life. Is there any of those you associate with Him who can do any of that? Glory be to Him. He is far above what they associate with Him.
Corruption has appeared on land and sea because of what people’s hands have earned, so that He may let them taste some of what they have done, so that they may return.
Say, “T ravel through the land and see what was the end of those before. Most of them were associators.”
So set your face firmly toward the upright way of life before a Day comes from Allah that cannot be turned back. On that Day they will be divided.
Whoever disbelieves, his disbelief is against himself. And whoever does good, they are preparing for themselves,
so that He may reward those who believe and do good deeds from His favour. Indeed, He does not love the disbelievers.

And among His signs is that He sends the winds as bringers of good news, so that He may let you taste His mercy, and so that ships may sail by His command, and so that you may seek of His bounty, and so that you may be grateful.

We certainly sent messengers before you to their peoples, and they came to them with clear signs. Then We took retribution from those who committed crimes, and it was binding upon Us to help the believers.

Allah is the One who sends the winds, and they stir up clouds. Then He spreads them in the sky as He wills, and breaks them into fragments, and you see rain coming out from within them. When He lets it fall upon whom He wills of His servants, they rejoice,
even though before it was sent down upon them, they were in despair.
So look at the effects of Allah’s mercy, how He brings the earth back to life after its death. Indeed, He is the One who gives life to the dead, and He has power over everything.
Y et if We were to send a wind and they saw it turning yellow, they would persist afterwards in disbelief.
You cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs.
And you cannot guide the blind away from their misguidance. You can only make those hear who believe in Our signs and who submit.

Allah is the One who created you from weakness, then after weakness gave strength, then after strength gave weakness and grey hair. He creates what He wills. And He is the All-Knowing, the All-Powerful.
On the Day the Hour is established, the criminals will swear they remained only an hour. In this way they were always turned away.
But those who were given knowledge and faith will say, “You remained according to the decree of Allah until the Day of Resurrection. This is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know.”
On that Day, the excuses of those who did wrong will not benefit them, nor will they be allowed to seek favour.
We have presented for people in this Qur’an every kind of example. Y et if you bring them a sign, those who disbelieve  will surely say, “You are only bringing falsehood.”
In this way Allah seals the hearts of those who do not know.
So be patient. Indeed, the promise of Allah is true. And do not let those who are not certain shake your firmness.

surahs.name: Luqmān
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Mīm.
These are the verses of the wise Book,
a guidance and a mercy for those who do good,
those who uphold the prayer, give zakah, and are certain of the Hereafter.
They are upon guidance from their Lord, and they are the ones who succeed.
And among people are those who purchase distracting speech to mislead others from the way of Allah without knowledge, and they take it in mockery. For them is a humiliating punishment.
When Our verses are recited to him, he turns away arrogantly, as if he had not heard them, as if there were heaviness in his ears. So give him news of a painful punishment.
Surely those who believe and do good deeds will have Gardens of delight,
remaining there forever. The promise of Allah is true. And He is the All- Mighty, the All-Wise.

He created the heavens without pillars that you can see, and placed firm mountains on the earth so that it would not shake with you, and spread across it every kind of creature. And We sent down water from the sky and caused every kind of noble growth to grow in it.
This is the creation of Allah. So show Me what those besides Him have created. Rather, the wrongdoers are in clear misguidance.
We certainly gave Luqmān wisdom: “Be grateful to Allah.” Whoever is grateful is grateful only for his own benefit. And whoever is ungrateful, then surely Allah is Free of need, All-Praiseworthy.
When Luqmān said to his son, while advising him, “O my son, do not associate partners with Allah. Indeed, associating partners with Him is a great wrongdoing.”

And We instructed the human being regarding his parents. His mother carried him in weakness upon weakness, and his weaning was in two years. “Be grateful to Me and to your parents. T o Me is the return.”

But if they strive to make you associate with Me what you have no knowledge of, then do not obey them. Y et keep good company with them in this world with kindness, and follow the way of those who turn to Me. Then to Me is your return, and I will inform you of what you used to do.
“O my son, even if it is the weight of a mustard seed, hidden within a rock, or in the heavens, or in the earth, Allah will bring it forth. Indeed, Allah is All-Subtle, All-Aware.
O My son, uphold the prayer, command what is right, forbid what is wrong, and be patient over what befalls you. Indeed, that is of the matters requiring resolve.
Do not turn your cheek away from people in pride, and do not walk through the land arrogantly. Surely Allah does not love any self -deluded boaster.
Be moderate in your walk and lower your voice. Indeed, the harshest of voices is the voice of donkeys.”

Do you not see that Allah has subjected to you whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and has poured out upon you His blessings, both visible and hidden? Y et among people are those who argue about Allah without knowledge, guidance, or an enlightening Book.
When it is said to them, “Follow what Allah has sent down,” they say, “Rather, we follow what we found our forefathers upon.” Even if Shayṭān is calling them to the punishment of the Blaze?
Whoever submits his face to Allah while doing good has surely grasped the firmest handhold. And to Allah belongs the outcome of all matters.
Whoever disbelieves, let his disbelief not grieve you. T o Us is their return, and We will inform them of what they did. Surely Allah knows what lies within hearts.
We let them enjoy for a little while, then We will force them into a severe punishment.
If you ask them who created the heavens and the earth, they will surely say, “ Allah.” Say, “ All praise belongs to Allah.” Y et most of them do not know.
T o Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Indeed, Allah is Free of need, All-Praiseworthy.
And if all the trees on the earth were pens, and the sea, with seven more seas added to it, were ink, the words of Allah would not be exhausted. Indeed, Allah is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
Your creation and your resurrection are only like that of a single soul. Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

Do you not see that Allah merges the night into the day and merges the day into the night, and has subjected the sun and the moon, each running for an appointed term, and that Allah is fully aware of what you do?
That is because Allah is the T ruth, and whatever they call upon besides Him is falsehood, and because Allah is the Most-High, the Great.
Do you not see that the ships sail through the sea by the favour of Allah, so that He may show you some of His signs? Indeed, in that are signs for everyone who is patient and deeply grateful.

When waves cover them like canopies, they call upon Allah with sincere devotion to Him. But when He brings them safely to land, some of them take a middle course. And none deny Our signs except every treacherous, ungrateful one.

O people, be mindful of your Lord and fear a Day when no parent will avail a child, nor will a child avail a parent at all. Indeed, the promise of Allah is true. So do not let the worldly life deceive you, and do not let the Deceiver deceive you about Allah.

Indeed, Allah alone has knowledge of the Hour. He sends down the rain and knows what is in the wombs. No soul knows what it will earn tomorrow, and no soul knows in which land it will die. Indeed, Allah is All -Knowing, Fully Aware.

surahs.name: As-Sajdah (The Prostration)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Alif Lām Mīm.
The sending down of the Book is without doubt from the Lord of all the worlds.
Or do they say, “He invented it”? Rather, it is the truth from your Lord, so that you may warn a people to whom no warner came before you, so that they may be guided.

Allah is the One who created the heavens and the earth and everything between them in six days, then established Himself over the Throne. You have no protector or intercessor besides Him. Will you not then reflect?
He directs the command from the heaven to the earth, then it rises back to Him in a Day whose measure is a thousand years by what you count.
That is the Knower of the unseen and the seen, the All-Mighty, the Ever- Merciful,
who perfected everything He created, and began the creation of the human being from clay.
Then He made his offspring from an extract of a humble fluid.
Then He shaped him, breathed into him from His spirit, and gave you hearing, sight, and hearts. Little are you grateful.
They say, “When we are lost in the earth, will we really be created anew?” Rather, they disbelieve in the meeting with their Lord.
Say, “The Angel of Death, who has been appointed over you, will take your souls, then you will be returned to your Lord.”
If only you could see when the criminals hang their heads before their Lord, saying, “Our Lord, we have seen and we have heard, so send us back, we will do good. Surely we are now convinced.”
If We had willed, We could have given every soul its guidance. But the word from Me has come true: “I will surely fill Hell with jinn and humans together.”
So taste, because you forgot the meeting of this Day of yours. We have forgotten you. T aste the lasting punishment for what you used to do.
Only those believe in Our verses who, when they are reminded of them, fall down in prostration, glorify their Lord with praise, and are not arrogant.
Their sides leave their beds as they call upon their Lord in fear and hope, and they spend from what We have provided them.
No soul knows what has been kept hidden for them of joy as a reward for what they used to do.
Is the one who believes like the one who rebels? They are not equal.
As for those who believe and do good deeds, for them are Gardens of refuge, a welcome for what they used to do.

But as for those who were rebellious, their refuge is the Fire. Every time they try to come out of it, they will be returned to it, and it will be said to them, “T aste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny.”
We will surely let them taste the nearer punishment before the greater punishment, so that they may return.
Who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the verses of his Lord, then turns away from them? Indeed, We will take retribution from the criminals.
We certainly gave Mūsā the Book, so do not be in doubt about meeting it, and We made it a guidance for the Children of Isrā’īl.
We appointed leaders from among them, guiding by Our command when they were patient and were certain of Our signs.
Surely your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection regarding what they used to differ over.
Has it not guided them how many generations We destroyed before them, in whose dwellings they now walk about? Indeed, in that are signs. Will they not listen?
Have they not seen that We drive water to barren land and bring forth crops by it, from which their livestock eat and they themselves eat? Will they not see?
And they say, “When will this judgement be, if you are truthful?”
Say, “On the Day of judgement, the belief of those who disbelieved will not benefit them, nor will they be given respite.”
So, turn away from them and wait. Indeed, they too are waiting.

surahs.name: Al-Aḥzāb (The Confederates)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: O Prophet, be mindful of Allah, and do not obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
And follow what has been revealed to you from your Lord. Indeed, Allah is fully aware of what you do.
And place your trust in Allah. Allah is sufficient as a trustee.

Allah has not placed two hearts within any man. Nor has He made your wives, whom you declare to be like your mothers, your actual mothers. Nor has He made your adopted sons your true sons. These are merely words from your mouths. Allah speaks the truth, and He guides to the right way.

Call them by the names of their fathers; that is more just in the sight of Allah. But if you do not know their fathers, then they are your brothers in faith and those under your care. There is no blame upon you for mistakes you make in this matter, but on ly for what your hearts intend. Allah is Most - Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

The Prophet has a greater claim over the believers than they have over themselves, and his wives are their mothers. Blood relatives have a stronger claim over one another, according to the Book of Allah, than the believers and the emigrants, except that y ou may show kindness to your close allies. This is written in the Book.
And when We took the covenant from the prophets, and from you, and from Nūḥ, and Ibrāhīm, and Mūsā, and ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, We took from them a firm covenant,
so that He may question the truthful about their truthfulness. And He has prepared for the disbelievers a painful punishment.
O you who believe, remember the blessing of Allah upon you when forces came against you, and We sent against them a wind and forces you could not see. Allah sees all that you do.
When they came upon you from above you and from below you, when eyes grew strained and hearts reached the throats, and you began to think thoughts about Allah.
There the believers were tested and shaken with a severe shaking.
When the hypocrites and those with sickness in their hearts said, “ Allah and His Messenger promised us nothing but delusion.”

And when a group among them said, “ O people of Y athrib, there is no place for you here, so return,” and a group of them asked permission from the Prophet, saying, “Our homes are exposed,” while they were not exposed. They only wanted to flee.
If they had been entered upon from all sides, then asked for sedition, they would have carried it out, and they would not have hesitated except briefly.
They had already given Allah a pledge before, that they would not turn their backs. And the pledge to Allah will be questioned.
Say, “Flight will not benefit you if you flee from death or killing. Then you would only be allowed brief enjoyment.”
Say, “Who is it that can protect you from Allah if He intends harm for you, or if He intends mercy for you?” They will find no protector or helper for themselves besides Allah.
Allah already knows those among you who hinder others and those who say to their brothers, “Come to us,” while they do not come to battle except a little,

being stingy towards you. Then when fear comes, you see them looking at you, their eyes rolling like one overcome by death. But when fear departs, they strike you with sharp tongues, greedy for gains. They did not believe, so Allah rendered their deeds worthless. That is easy for Allah.

They think the allied forces have not gone. And if the allied forces were to return, they would wish to be out in the desert among the Bedouins, asking about your news from afar. And if they were among you, they would not fight except a little.
There is indeed for you in the Messenger of Allah an excellent example for whoever hopes in Allah and the Last Day and remembers Allah often.
When the believers saw the allied forces, they said, “This is what Allah and His Messenger promised us. Allah and His Messenger spoke the truth.” And it only increased them in faith and submission.
Among the believers are men who were true to what they pledged to Allah. Some of them have fulfilled their vow, and some are still waiting. They did not change in the least.
So that Allah may reward the truthful for their truthfulness and punish the hypocrites if He wills, or turn to them in mercy. Indeed, Allah is Most- Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
Allah repelled the disbelievers in their rage without them gaining any good. Allah was sufficient for the believers in battle. Allah is All-Strong, All- Mighty.
He brought down those among the People of the Book who supported them from their fortresses and cast terror into their hearts. Some you killed, and some you took captive.
He caused you to inherit their land, their homes, their wealth, and a land you had not yet set foot on. Allah has power over everything.
O Prophet, say to your wives, “If you desire the worldly life and its adornment, then come, I will provide for you and release you with a gracious release.
But if you desire Allah and His Messenger and the Home of the Hereafter, then surely Allah has prepared for those among you who do good a great reward.”
O wives of the Prophet, whoever among you commits a clear indecency, the punishment for her will be doubled twice over. And that is easy for Allah.
But whoever among you is obedient to Allah and His Messenger and does good, We will give her her reward twice over, and We have prepared for her a generous provision.
O wives of the Prophet, you are not like any other women. If you are mindful, then do not be soft in speech, lest the one in whose heart is sickness should desire. Speak in a fitting manner.

Remain within your homes, and do not display yourselves as in the former days of ignorance. Uphold the prayer, give zakah, and obey Allah and His Messenger. Allah only intends to remove impurity from you, people of the household, and to purify you completely.
Remember what is recited in your homes of the verses of Allah and wisdom. Indeed, Allah is All-Subtle, All-Aware.

Indeed, the Muslim men and Muslim women, the believing men and believing women, the devoted men and devoted women, the truthful men and truthful women, the patient men and patient women, the humble men and humble women, the charitable men and charitable women, th e fasting men and fasting women, the men who guard their chastity and the women who guard their chastity, and the men who remember Allah much and the women who remember, Allah has prepared for them forgiveness and a great reward.
It is not for a believing man or a believing woman, when Allah and His Messenger have decided a matter, to have any choice in it. Whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger has clearly gone astray.

When you said to the one upon whom Allah bestowed favour and you also bestowed favour, “Keep your wife and be mindful of Allah,” while you concealed within yourself what Allah would reveal, and you feared people, while Allah has more right that you fear H im. Then when Zayd had ended his bond with her, We married her to you, so that there would be no difficulty for the believers regarding the wives of their adopted sons when they have ended their bond with them. And the command of Allah is carried out.
There is no blame upon the Prophet in what Allah has made obligatory for him. This was the way of Allah with those who passed before. And the command of Allah is a determined decree.
Those who convey the messages of Allah and fear Him, and fear none except Allah. Allah is sufficient as a reckoner.
Muhammad is not the father of any of your men, but he is the Messenger of Allah and the seal of the prophets. Allah has full knowledge of everything.
O you who believe, remember Allah with much remembrance,
and glorify Him morning and evening.
He is the One who sends blessings upon you, and His angels too, to bring you out from darkness into light. And He is Ever-Merciful to the believers.
Their greeting on the Day they meet Him will be, “Peace.” And He has prepared for them a generous reward.
O Prophet, We have sent you as a witness, a bearer of good news, and a warner,
and as one who calls to Allah by His permission, and as a radiant light.
Give good news to the believers that for them from Allah is great favour.
And do not obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites. Ignore their harm, and place your trust in Allah. Allah is sufficient as a trustee.

O you who believe, when you marry believing women and then divorce them before you have touched them, there is no waiting period for you to count for them. So provide for them and release them in a gracious release.

O Prophet, We have made lawful for you your wives whom you have given their dowries, and those whom your right hand possesses from what Allah has granted you, and the daughters of your paternal uncles and aunts, and the daughters of your maternal uncles a nd aunts, who migrated with you, and any believing woman who offers herself to the Prophet if the Prophet wishes to marry her, a privilege for you alone, not for the believers. We know what We have made obligatory upon them concerning their wives and those whom their right hands possess, so that there would be no difficulty for you. Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

You may set aside whom you will among them, and take to yourself whom you will. And whoever you wish of those you had set aside, there is no blame upon you. That is more suitable that their eyes be comforted, that they not grieve, and that all of them be pleased with what you give them. Allah knows what is in your hearts. Allah is All-Knowing, Most-Forbearing.

It is not lawful for you to take women beyond this, nor to exchange them for other wives, even if their beauty pleases you, except those whom your right hand possesses. Allah is ever watchful over everything.

O you who believe, do not enter the homes of the Prophet unless permission is given to you for a meal, without waiting for its preparation. But when you are invited, then enter, and when you have eaten, disperse without lingering for conversation. That was causing the Prophet discomfort, and he was shy of you, but Allah is not shy of the truth. And when you ask them for something, ask them from behind a screen. That is purer for your hearts and their hearts. It is not for you to harm the Messenger of Al lah, nor to ever marry his wives after him. Indeed, that would be grave in the sight of Allah.
Whether you reveal something or conceal it, surely Allah has full knowledge of everything.

There is no blame upon them regarding their fathers, their sons, their brothers, the sons of their brothers, the sons of their sisters, their women, or those whom their right hands possess. Be mindful of Allah. Indeed, Allah is Witness over everything.
Indeed, Allah sends blessings upon the Prophet, and His angels too. O you who believe, send blessings upon him and greet him with full greetings.
Those who harm Allah and His Messenger, Allah has cursed them in this world and the Hereafter, and has prepared for them a humiliating punishment.
Those who harm believing men and believing women without them having earned it, they have taken upon themselves slander and clear sin.

O Prophet, tell your wives, your daughters, and the women of the believers to draw their outer garments closely over themselves. That is more suitable so that they may be recognised and not harmed. Allah is Most- Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

If the hypocrites, those with sickness in their hearts, and those who spread rumours in the city do not desist, We will surely incite you against them. Then they will not remain your neighbours there except briefly.
