Accursed wherever they are found, they will be seized and killed completely.
This is the established way of Allah with those who passed before, and you will never find any change in the way of Allah.
People ask you about the Hour. Say, “Its knowledge is only with Allah.” And what will make you know? Perhaps the Hour is near.
Indeed, Allah has cursed the disbelievers and prepared for them a blazing Fire,
remaining in it forever. They will find no protector and no helper.
On the Day their faces are turned about in the Fire, they will say, “If only we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger.”
And they will say, “Our Lord, we obeyed our leaders and our great ones, and they led us astray from the path.
Our Lord, give them double punishment and curse them with a great curse.”
O you who believe, do not be like those who harmed Mūsā. Allah cleared him of what they said, and he was honourable in the sight of Allah.
O you who believe, be mindful of Allah and speak words that are upright.
He will set right your deeds for you and forgive your sins for you. Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger has achieved a great success.
We certainly offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to carry it and feared it. Y et the human being carried it. He is indeed unjust and deeply ignorant,

so that Allah may punish the hypocrite men and hypocrite women, and the associating men and associating women, and so that Allah may turn in mercy to the believing men and believing women. Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

surahs.name: Sabaʾ (Sheba)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: All praise belongs to Allah, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. And to Him belongs all praise in the Hereafter. He is the All-Wise, the All-Aware.
He knows what enters into the earth and what comes out of it, what descends from the sky and what rises into it. He is the Ever-Merciful, the Most-Forgiving.

Those who disbelieve say, “The Hour will not come to us.” Say, “Y es, by my Lord, it will surely come to you.” He is the Knower of the unseen. Not even the weight of an atom escapes Him in the heavens or in the earth, nor anything smaller than that or greater, except that it is in a clear Record,
so that He may reward those who believe and do good deeds. For them is forgiveness and a noble provision.
But those who strive against Our verses, trying to undermine them, for them is a punishment of painful torment.
Those who have been given knowledge see that what has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth and guides to the path of the All-Mighty, the All-Praiseworthy.
Those who disbelieve  say, “Shall we show you a man who tells you that when you are completely torn apart, you will truly be in a new creation?
Has he invented a lie against Allah, or is there madness in him?” Rather, those who do not believe in the Hereafter are in punishment and far astray.

Do they not see what is before them and what is behind them of the sky and the earth? If We willed, We could cause the earth to swallow them or make pieces of the sky fall upon them. Surely in that is a sign for every servant who turns back to Allah.
We certainly gave Dāwūd favour from Us: “Mountains, echo with him, and birds as well.” And We made iron soft for him.
“Make full coats of armour and measure the links carefully.” And do good. Indeed, I see what you do.

And to Sulaymān the wind, its morning journey was a month and its evening journey was a month. And We caused a spring of molten copper to flow for him. And among the jinn were those who worked before him by the permission of his Lord. Whoever among them turned away from Our command, We made him taste the punishment of the Blaze.

They made for him whatever he wished of lofty structures, statues, basins like reservoirs, and heavy cooking pots fixed in place. “W ork gratefully, family of Dāwūd, in gratitude.” Few of My servants are truly grateful.

When We decreed death for him, nothing showed them his death except a creature of the earth that was eating away at his staff. When he fell, the jinn realised that if they had known the unseen, they would not have remained in humiliating punishment.
There was certainly for Sabaʾ in their dwelling a sign: two gardens on the right and on the left. “Eat from the provision of your Lord and be grateful to Him. A good land and a Most-Forgiving Lord.”
But they turned away, so We sent against them the flood of the dam, and We replaced their two gardens with gardens bearing bitter fruit, tamarisk, and a few lote trees.
That was how We repaid them for their ingratitude. And We do not repay in that way except the ungrateful.
And We placed between them and the towns We had blessed visible towns, and We measured the journey between them. “T ravel between them by night and day in safety.”

But they said, “Our Lord, lengthen the distance between our journeys,” and they wronged themselves. So We made them stories and scattered them completely. Indeed, in that are signs for everyone who is patient and grateful.
And Iblīs certainly proved his assumption about them to be true, and they followed him, except a group among the believers.
He had no authority over them, except that We might make clear who believes in the Hereafter from those who are in doubt about it. Your Lord is Guardian over everything.

Say, “Call upon those you claim besides Allah. They do not possess even the weight of an atom in the heavens or in the earth. They have no share in either of them, nor does He have any supporter from among them.”

Intercession with Him does not benefit except for the one He permits. When fear is lifted from their hearts, they say, “What has your Lord said?” They say, “The truth.” And He is the Most-High, the Most-Great.
Say, “Who provides for you from the heavens and the earth?” Say, “ Allah. And surely either we or you are upon guidance or in clear misguidance.”
Say, “You will not be asked about what we committed, nor will we be asked about what you do.”
Say, “Our Lord will gather us together, then judge between us with truth. And He is the Decisive Judge, the All-Knowing.”
Say, “Show me those you have attached to Him as partners. No. Rather, He is Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.”
We did not send you except as a bringer of good news and a warner to all people, but most people do not know.
They say, “When will this promise be, if you are truthful?”
Say, “For you is an appointed Day that you cannot delay by even an hour, nor can you bring it forward.”

Those who disbelieve  say, “We will never believe in this Qur’an, nor in what came before it.” If only you could see when the wrongdoers are made to stand before their Lord, throwing accusations back and forth. Those who were oppressed will say to those who were arrogant, “If not for you, we would have been believers.”
Those who were arrogant will say to those who were oppressed, “Did we stop you from guidance after it came to you? Rather, you yourselves were criminals.”

Those who were oppressed will say to those who were arrogant, “Rather, it was your plotting night and day, when you were ordering us to  disbelieve in Allah and set up equals to Him.” They will conceal their regret when they see the punishment. We will place chains around the necks of those who disbelieved. Are they repaid except for what they used to do?
We did not send a warner to any town except that its affluent ones said, “We disbelieve in what you have been sent with.”
And they said, “We have more wealth and children, and we will not be punished.”
Say, “My Lord expands provision for whom He wills and restricts it, but most people do not know.”

It is not your wealth nor your children that bring you nearer to Us in rank, but whoever believes and does good. For them is multiplied reward for what they did, and they will be secure in lofty dwellings.
But those who strive against Our verses, trying to undermine them, they will be brought into the punishment.
Say, “Indeed, my Lord expands provision for whom He wills of His servants and restricts it for him. Whatever you spend, He will replace it. He is the best of providers.”
On the Day He will gather them all together, then He will say to the angels, “W as it you these people used to worship?”
They will say, “Glory be to You. You are our Protector, not them. Rather, they used to worship the jinn. Most of them were believers in them.”
So today none of you has power to benefit or harm another. And We will say to those who did wrong, “T aste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny.”

When Our clear verses are recited to them, they say, “This is nothing but a man who wants to turn you away from what your forefathers used to worship.” And they say, “This is nothing but invented falsehood.” And those who disbelieve say of the truth when it comes to them, “This is nothing but obvious magic.”
We did not give them any books to study, nor did We send to them any warner before you.
Those before them denied, and these have not reached even a tenth of what We gave them. Y et they denied My messengers, so how was My punishment?
Say, “I only advise you of one thing: that you stand for Allah, in pairs and individually, then reflect. There is no madness in your companion. He is only a warner to you before a severe punishment.”
Say, “Whatever reward I may have asked of you, it is yours. My reward is only with Allah. He is Witness over everything.”
Say, “Indeed, my Lord casts the truth. He is the Knower of the unseen.”
Say, “The truth has come, and falsehood can neither begin nor return.”
Say, “If I go astray, I stray only against myself. And if I am guided, it is by what my Lord reveals to me. Indeed, He is All-Hearing, Near.”
If only you could see when they are terrified, with no escape, and are seized from a nearby place.
And they will say, “We believe in it.” But how can they reach it from such a distant place,
when they had already disbelieved in it before and were casting doubts from afar?
A barrier will be placed between them and what they desire, as was done with those like them before. Indeed, they were in unsettling doubt.

surahs.name: Fāṭir (The Originator)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: All praise belongs to Allah, Originator of the heavens and the earth, who made the angels messengers with wings, two, three, and four. He increases in creation what He wills. Indeed, Allah has power over everything.
Whatever mercy Allah opens to people, none can withhold it. And whatever He withholds, none can release after Him. He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
O people, remember the blessing of Allah upon you. Is there any creator other than Allah who provides for you from the heaven and the earth? There is no god except Him. So how are you turned away?
If they deny you, messengers before you were denied. And to Allah all matters are returned.
O people, surely the promise of Allah is true. So do not let the worldly life deceive you, and do not let the Deceiver deceive you about Allah.
Indeed, Shayṭān is an enemy to you, so take him as an enemy. He only calls his followers to be among the companions of the Blaze.
Those who disbelieve will have a severe punishment, and those who believe and do good deeds will have forgiveness and a great reward.

Is the one whose evil deed has been made attractive to him so that he sees it as good like one rightly guided? Indeed, Allah leaves astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. So do not let yourself be consumed in sorrow over them. Indeed, Allah knows what they do.
Allah is the One who sends the winds, and they raise clouds. Then We drive them to a lifeless land and bring the earth to life after its death. In that way is the resurrection.
Whoever seeks honour, all honour belongs to Allah. T o Him rise good words, and righteous deeds lift them up. But those who plot evil will have a severe punishment, and their plotting will perish.

And Allah created you from dust, then from a drop of fluid, then made you pairs. No female conceives or gives birth except with His knowledge. No one’s life is extended nor shortened except that it is in a Record. Indeed, that is easy for Allah.

The two bodies of water are not the same: one is fresh and sweet, pleasant to drink, and the other is salty and bitter. Y et from each you eat fresh meat and extract ornaments you wear. And you see ships cutting through them so that you may seek of His bounty and so that you may be grateful.

He merges the night into the day and merges the day into the night. He has subjected the sun and the moon, each running for an appointed term. That is Allah, your Lord. T o Him belongs all sovereignty. Those you call upon besides Him do not possess even the thin membrane of a date seed.

If you call upon them, they do not hear your call. And even if they heard, they could not respond to you. On the Day of Resurrection they will deny your association with them. None can inform you like the One who is All- Aware.
O people, you are in need of Allah, and Allah is Free of need, All- Praiseworthy.
If He wills, He can remove you and bring about a new creation.
That is not difficult for Allah.

No bearer of burdens will carry the burden of another. If a heavily burdened soul calls for help with its load, nothing of it will be carried, even if it is a close relative. You can only warn those who fear their Lord unseen and uphold the prayer. Whoever purifies himself does so only for himself. And to Allah is the destination.
The blind and the seeing are not equal,
nor are darkness and light.
Nor are shade and scorching heat.
Nor are the living and the dead equal. Indeed, Allah makes whom He wills hear, but you cannot make those in the graves hear.
You are only a warner.
We have sent you with the truth as a bearer of good news and a warner. There has never been a community without a warner having passed among them.
If they deny you, those before them also denied. Their messengers came to them with clear signs, with scriptures, and with the enlightening Book.
Then I seized those who disbelieved, and how severe was My punishment.
Do you not see that Allah sends down water from the sky, and with it We bring forth fruits of different colours? And in the mountains are streaks of white and red, of various shades, and deep black.
And among people, creatures, and livestock are colours likewise varied. Only those among His servants who have knowledge truly fear Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, Most-Forgiving.
Those who recite the Book of Allah, uphold the prayer, and spend from what We have provided them, secretly and openly, hope for a trade that will never fail,
so that He may give them their full rewards and increase them from His favour. Surely, He is Most-Forgiving, Most-Appreciative.
What We have revealed to you of the Book is the truth, confirming what came before it. Indeed, Allah is Fully Aware of His servants and All-Seeing.

Then We caused those We chose from among Our servants to inherit the Book. Among them are those who wrong themselves, among them are those who are moderate, and among them are those who race ahead in good deeds by the permission of Allah. That is the great favour.
Gardens of lasting residence which they will enter, adorned therein with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their clothing therein will be silk.
And they will say, “ All praise belongs to Allah, who has removed sorrow from us. Indeed, our Lord is Most-Forgiving, Most-Appreciative,
who has settled us in the Home of lasting stay by His favour. No fatigue will touch us there, nor will weariness touch us.”
But those who disbelieve, for them is the Fire of Hell. It will not be decreed for them that they die, nor will its punishment be lightened for them. In that way We repay every ungrateful one.

They will cry out in it, “Our Lord, bring us out, we will do good, not what we used to do.” Did We not give you a life long enough for whoever would remember to remember, and the warner came to you? So taste. There is no helper for the wrongdoers.
Indeed, Allah is Knower of the unseen of the heavens and the earth. He truly knows what lies within hearts.

He is the One who made you successors in the earth. So whoever disbelieves, his disbelief is against himself. The disbelief of the disbelievers only increases them in the sight of their Lord in hatred, and the disbelief of the disbelievers only increases them in loss.

Say, “Have you seen your partners whom you call upon besides Allah? Show me what they created of the earth. Or do they have any share in the heavens? Or have We given them a Book so that they stand upon clear proof from it?” Rather, the wrongdoers promise one another nothing but delusion.
Indeed, Allah holds the heavens and the earth lest they cease. And if they were to cease, none could hold them after Him. Indeed, He is Most - Forbearing, Most-Forgiving.
They swore by Allah their strongest oaths that if a warner came to them, they would be more guided than any community. But when a warner came to them, it only increased them in aversion,

acting arrogantly in the land and plotting evil. Evil plotting only engulfs its own people. So do they expect anything except the way of those who passed before? You will never find any change in the way of Allah, and you will never find any alteration in the way of Allah.

Have they not travelled through the land and seen what was the end of those before them, though they were stronger than them in power? Nothing in the heavens or the earth can escape Allah. Indeed, He is All-Knowing, All- Powerful.

If Allah were to seize people for what they earned, He would not leave upon it a single living creature. But He delays them until an appointed term. Then when their term comes, Indeed, Allah is All-Seeing of His servants.

surahs.name: Yā Sīn
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Yā Sīn.
By the Qur’an, full of wisdom,
you are indeed among the messengers,
upon a straight path.
A revelation sent down by the All-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful,
so that you may warn a people whose fathers were not warned, so they are unaware.
The word has already come into effect against most of them, so they will not believe.
We have placed shackles around their necks reaching up to their chins, so their heads are forced up.
And We have set a barrier before them and a barrier behind them, covering them, so they cannot see.
It is the same for them whether you warn them or do not warn them, they will not believe.
You can only warn those who follow the Reminder and fear the Most- Merciful without seeing Him. So give them good news of forgiveness and a generous reward.
Indeed, We give life to the dead, and We record what they have sent ahead and what they left behind. We have counted everything in a clear  master record.
And present to them the example of the people of the town, when the messengers came to it.
When We sent two to them, but they denied them, so We strengthened them with a third. They said, “We have Indeed been sent to you.”
They said, “You are nothing but men like us, and the Most-Merciful has not sent down anything. You are only lying.”
They said, “Our Lord knows that we have indeed been sent to you,
and we are only responsible for delivering the message clearly.”
They said, “We see you as a bad omen. If you do not stop, we will surely stone you, and a painful punishment from us will touch you.”
They said, “Your omen is with yourselves. Is it because you were reminded? Rather, you are a people who go beyond all limits.”
And a man came running from the far end of the city. He said, “My people, follow the messengers.
Follow those who ask no reward from you, and who are rightly guided.
Why should I not worship the One who created me, and to whom you will all be returned?
Should I take besides Him other gods? If the Most-Merciful intends harm for me, their intercession will not help me at all, nor can they rescue me.
Then I would clearly be in error.
I have believed in your Lord, so listen to me.”
It was said, “Enter the Garden.” He said, “If only my people knew
how my Lord has forgiven me and made me among those honoured.”
We did not send down upon his people any army from the sky after him, nor was it Our way to send one.
It was only a single blast, and suddenly they were silent, lifeless.
Oh, what regret for the servants. No messenger came to them without them mocking him.
Have they not seen how many generations We destroyed before them, that they do not return to them?
And all of them will surely be brought before Us.
A sign for them is the dead earth. We bring it to life and produce from it grain, from which they eat.
And We placed in it gardens of date palms and grapevines, and We caused springs to flow within it,
so that they may eat from its fruit. Their hands did not make it. Will they not then be grateful?
Glory be to the One who created all pairs, from what the earth grows, from themselves, and from what they do not know.
And a sign for them is the night. We strip the day away from it, and suddenly they are in darkness.
And the sun runs to its resting place. That is the decree of the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.
And the moon, We have measured for it phases until it returns like an old date stalk.
The sun cannot overtake the moon, nor does the night outstrip the day. Each floats along in its own orbit.
And a sign for them is that We carried their descendants in the loaded ship,
and We created for them the like of it in which they ride.
If We willed, We could drown them, then there would be no one to answer their cry, nor would they be rescued,
except by mercy from Us and as a provision for a time.
And when it is said to them, “Be mindful of what is before you and what is behind you, so that you may receive mercy,”
not a single sign from the signs of their Lord comes to them without them turning away from it.

And when it is said to them, “Spend from what Allah has provided you,” those who disbelieve  say to those who believe, “Should we feed someone whom Allah could feed if He willed? You are clearly in error.”
And they say, “When is this promise, if you are truthful?”
They are only waiting for a single blast that will seize them while they are arguing.
Then they will not be able to make a will, nor will they return to their families.
And the T rumpet will be blown, and suddenly they will rush from the graves to their Lord.
They will say, “W oe to us! Who has raised us from our resting place?” This is what the Most-Merciful promised, and the messengers spoke the truth.
It will be only a single blast, and suddenly they will all be brought before Us.
So today no soul will be wronged at all, and you will only be repaid for what you used to do.
Indeed, the people of the Garden today will be fully occupied in joy,
they and their spouses, in shade, reclining on couches.
For them there will be fruit, and for them whatever they ask for.
“Peace,” a word from the Lord, Ever-Merciful.
And stand apart today, you who were criminals.
Did I not command you, children of Ādam, that you must not worship Shayṭān, for he is a clear enemy to you,
and that you must worship Me alone? This is a straight path.
He has already led many of you astray. Did you not use your reason?
This is Hell which you were promised.
Enter it today because you used to disbelieve.
T oday We will seal their mouths, and their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will testify to what they used to earn.
If We willed, We could erase their sight, and they would rush toward the path, but how would they see?
And if We willed, We could transform them in their places, so they would not be able to move forward or return.
Whoever We grant long life, We reverse him in creation. Will they not then understand?
We did not teach him poetry, nor is it fitting for him. It is nothing but a Reminder and a clear Qur’an,
so that he may warn whoever is alive and so that the word may come into effect against the disbelievers.
Have they not seen that We created for them, from what Our hands have made, livestock which they own?
And We made them manageable for them, so some of them they ride and some they eat.
And for them in them are other benefits and drinks. Will they not then be grateful?
Y et they have taken gods besides Allah, hoping to be helped.
They cannot help them, though they are brought forward as soldiers for them.
So do not let their words grieve you. Indeed, We know what they conceal and what they reveal.
Does man not see that We created him from a drop, and yet he becomes a clear opponent?
And he sets up comparisons for Us and forgets his own creation. He says, “Who will give life to bones when they have decayed?”
Say, “The One who created them the first time will give them life, and He is fully aware of every creation,
the One who made for you fire from the green tree, and suddenly you kindle from it.”
Is not the One who created the heavens and the earth able to create the like of them? Y es T ruly. He is the All-Creating, the All-Knowing.
His command, when He wills a thing, is only that He says to it, “Be,” and it is.
So glory be to the One in whose hand is the dominion of everything, and to Him you will be returned.

surahs.name: Aṣ-Ṣāffāt (Those Who Line Up)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By those who line up in rows,
and those who drive forcefully,
and those who recite the Reminder,
Indeed, your God is One,
Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever lies between them, Lord of the places of rising.
We have adorned the lowest heaven with the beauty of stars,
and as protection against every defiant devil.
They cannot listen to the highest assembly and are pelted from every side,
driven away, and for them is a lasting punishment,
except one who snatches a fragment, then a piercing flame pursues him.
So ask them, are they harder to create, or those others We have created? We created them from sticky clay.
Y et you are astonished, while they mock,
and when they are reminded, they do not take heed,
and when they see a sign, they ridicule it,
and say, “This is nothing but obvious magic.
When we have died and become dust and bones, will we really be raised?
And our forefathers as well?”
Say, “Y es, and you will be humbled.”
It will only be a single cry, and suddenly they will be watching,
and they will say, “W oe to us, this is the Day of Recompense.”
This is the Day of Decision which you used to deny.
Gather those who did wrong, along with their kind and what they used to worship
besides Allah, and lead them to the path of Hell.
And stop them, they will be questioned.
What is the matter with you that you do not help one another?
Rather, today they are in full submission.
And they will turn to one another, questioning each other.
They will say, “You used to come to us from the right.”
They will reply, “No, you yourselves were not believers.
And we had no authority over you. Rather, you were a people who went beyond all limits.”
So the word of our Lord has come true against us. We will surely taste it.
We led you astray, for we ourselves were astray.
So on that Day they will all share in the punishment.
That is how We deal with the criminals.
When it was said to them, “There is no god except Allah,” they were arrogant,
and said, “ Are we to abandon our gods for a mad poet?”
Rather, he came with the truth and confirmed the messengers.
You will surely taste the painful punishment,
and you will only be repaid for what you used to do,
except the sincere servants of Allah.
For them is a known provision,
fruits, and they will be honoured,
in Gardens of Bliss,
facing one another on couches.
A cup will be passed around among them from a flowing spring,
clear and delightful for those who drink,
with no harm in it, nor will they be intoxicated by it.
And with them will be those who lower their gaze, with wide, beautiful eyes,
as if they were well-protected pearls.
Then they will turn to one another, questioning each other.
One of them will say, “I had a companion
who used to say, ‘ Are you really among those who believe?
When we have died and become dust and bones, will we truly be repaid?’”
He will say, “Will you look?”
Then he will look and see him in the midst of the Fire.
He will say, “By Allah, you nearly destroyed me.
If not for the favour of my Lord, I too would have been among those brought in.”
“ Are we then not to die,
except for our first death, and will we not be punished?”
Indeed, this is the great triumph.
For the like of this, let those who work strive.
Is that a better welcome, or the tree of Zaqqūm?
We have made it a test for the wrongdoers.
It is a tree that grows from the depths of Hell.
Its fruits are like the heads of devils.
They will surely eat from it and fill their bellies with it.
Then over it they will have a mixture of boiling water.
Then their return will be to the Fire.
They found their fathers astray,
and they rushed to follow in their footsteps.
Most of the earlier peoples also went astray before them.
We had already sent warners among them,
so look how the end was for those who were warned,
except the sincere servants of Allah.
And certainly, Nū ḥ called upon Us, and how excellent are We in answering.
We saved him and his family from the great distress,
and We made his descendants the survivors,
and We left for him a good mention among later generations:
“Peace be upon Nūḥ among all people.”
That is how We reward those who do good.
He was ind one of Our believing servants.
Then We drowned the others.
And among those who followed his way was Ibrāhīm.
When he came to his Lord with a sound heart,
he said to his father and his people, “What is it that you worship?
Is it false gods you desire instead of Allah?
What then is your thought about the Lord of all worlds?”
Then he cast a glance at the stars
and said, “I am unwell.”
So they turned away from him and left.
Then he slipped away to their gods and said, “Will you not eat?
What is the matter with you that you do not speak?”
Then he struck them with his right hand.
So they rushed towards him.
He said, “Do you worship what you carve,
while Allah created you and what you make?”
They said, “Build for him a furnace and throw him into the blazing fire.”
They intended a plot against him, but We made them the lowest.
He said, “I am going to my Lord, He will guide me.
My Lord, grant me from among the righteous.”
So We gave him good news of a forbearing son.

Then when he reached the age to work with him, he said, “My son, I have seen in a dream that I am sacrificing you. So consider what you think.” He said, “My father, do as you are commanded. You will find me, if Allah wills, among the patient.”
Then when they had both submitted and he laid him down on his forehead,
We called out to him, “O Ibrāhīm,
you have fulfilled the vision.” In this way We reward those who do good.
This was clearly a decisive test.
And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice,
and We left for him a good mention among later generations:
“Peace be upon Ibrāhīm.”
That is how We reward those who do good.
He was truly one of Our believing servants.
And We gave him good news of Isḥāq, a prophet from among the righteous.
And We blessed him and Isḥāq. And among their descendants are those who do good and those who clearly wrong themselves.
And We certainly showed favour to Mūsā and Hārūn.
We saved them and their people from the great distress,
and We supported them, so they became victorious.
And We gave them the clear Book,
and We guided them to the straight path.
And We left for them a good mention among later generations:
“Peace be upon Mūsā and Hārūn.”
In this way We reward those who do good.
They were truly among Our believing servants.
And Ilyās was surely among the messengers.
When he said to his people, “Will you not be mindful?
Do you call upon Baʿl and leave the Best of creators,
Allah, your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers?”
But they denied him, so they will surely be brought forward,
except the sincere servants of Allah.
And We left for him a good mention among later generations:
“Peace be upon Ilyāseen.”
In this way We reward those who do good.
He was truly among Our believing servants.
And Lūṭ was surely among the messengers.
When We saved him and all his family,
except an old woman who stayed behind.
Then We destroyed the others.
And you pass by them in the morning,
and at night. Will you not then use your reason?
And Yūnus was surely among the messengers.
When he fled to the laden ship,
Then he drew lots and was among those cast off.
Then the great fish swallowed him while he was blameworthy.
If he had not been among those who glorify Allah,
he would have remained inside it until the Day they are raised.
But We cast him onto the open shore while he was ill,
and We caused a gourd plant to grow over him.
And We sent him to a hundred thousand, or even more.
And they believed, so We granted them enjoyment for a time.
So ask them, does your Lord have daughters while they have sons?
Or did We create the angels as females while they were witnesses?
Indeed, it is from their fabrication that they say,
“ Allah has begotten,” and they are surely lying.
Has He chosen daughters over sons?
What is wrong with you? How do you judge?
Will you not then reflect?
Or do you have clear authority?
Then bring your book, if you are truthful.
And they have made between Him and the jinn a kinship, but the jinn know that they will surely be brought forward.
Glory be to Allah above what they describe,
except the sincere servants of Allah.
So you and what you worship
cannot tempt anyone away from Him,
except one who is destined for the Blaze.
“There is none among us except that he has a known place.
And we are indeed those lined up in ranks.
And we are indeed those who glorify.”
And they used to say,
“If we had a reminder like that of the former peoples,
we would certainly be sincere servants of Allah.”
Y et they disbelieved in it, so they will come to know.
And Our word had already gone forth for Our servants, the messengers,
that they would surely be given victory,
and that Our forces would surely prevail.
So turn away from them for a time,
and watch them, they too will soon see.
Do they seek to hasten Our punishment?
But when it descends into their courtyard, evil will be the morning of those who were warned.
So turn away from them for a time,
and watch, they too will soon see.
Glory be to your Lord, the Lord of Might, above what they describe.
Peace be upon the messengers.
And all praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all worlds.

surahs.name: Ṣād
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Ṣād. By the Qur’an, full of reminder.
But those who disbelieve are in pride and opposition.
How many generations We destroyed before them, and they cried out when there was no time to escape.
They are amazed that a warner has come to them from among themselves, and the disbelievers say, “This is a magician and a liar.
Has he made the gods into one God? This is something astonishing.”
And their leaders went off saying, “Go and remain firm with your gods. This is something planned.
We have not heard of this in the later faith. This is nothing but invention.
Has the Reminder been sent down to him among us?” Rather, they are in doubt about My Reminder. Rather, they have not yet tasted My punishment.
Or do they possess the treasures of the mercy of your Lord, the All-Mighty, the All-Bestower?
Or do they possess the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them? Then let them ascend through the means.
They are nothing but a defeated force from the confederates.
Before them, the people of Nūḥ denied, and ʿĀd and Firʿawn, the possessor of stakes,
and Thamūd, and the people of Lūṭ, and the people of the Thicket. Those were the confederates.
Each of them denied the messengers, so My punishment became due.
These are only waiting for a single blast, there will be no delay in it.
And they say, “Our Lord, hasten for us our share before the Day of Reckoning.”
Be patient over what they say, and remember Our servant Dāwūd, the one of strength. He constantly turned back to Allah.
We made the mountains join him in glorifying in the evening and at sunrise,
and the birds gathered together, all turning back with him.
And We strengthened his kingdom and gave him wisdom and decisive speech.
Has the news of the disputants reached you, when they climbed over the wall into the prayer chamber?

When they entered upon Dāwūd and he was alarmed by them, they said, “Do not fear. We are two disputants. One of us has wronged the other, so judge between us with truth and do not be unjust, and guide us to the straight way.
This is my brother. He has ninety-nine ewes, and I have one ewe. Y et he says, ‘Entrust her to me,’ and he overpowered me in speech.”

He said, “He has certainly wronged you by asking for your ewe to add to his ewes. Many partners wrong one another, except those who believe and do good deeds, and they are few.” And Dāwūd realised that We had tested him, so he sought forgiveness from his Lord, fell down bowing, and turned back in repentance.
So We forgave him for that, and for him with Us is closeness and a good return.

O Dāwūd, We have made you a successor in the earth, so judge between people with truth and do not follow desire, lest it lead you away from the path of Allah. Indeed, Those who go astray from the path of Allah will have a severe punishment because they forgot the Day of Reckoning.
And We did not create the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them without purpose. That is the assumption of those who disbelieve. So woe to those who disbelieve from the Fire.
Or shall We treat those who believe and do good deeds like those who spread corruption in the earth? Or shall We treat those mindful of Allah like the wicked?
This is a blessed Book We have sent down to you so that they may reflect on its verses and so that those of understanding may take heed.
And We granted Dāwūd Sulaymān. What an excellent servant. He constantly turned back.
When the fine horses were presented before him in the late afternoon,
he said, “I have loved the love of good over the remembrance of my Lord,” until they were hidden from sight.
“Bring them back to me.” Then he began passing his hand over their legs and necks.
And We certainly tested Sulaymān, and We placed upon his throne a body, then he turned back in repentance.
He said, “My Lord, forgive me and grant me a kingdom that will not belong to anyone after me. You are indeed the All-Bestower.”
So We subjected the wind to him, flowing gently by his command wherever he intended,
and the devils, every builder and diver,
and others bound together in chains.
“This is Our gift, so grant or withhold without account.”
And for him with Us is closeness and a good return.
And remember Our servant Ayyūb, when he called to his Lord, “Shayṭān has touched me with hardship and suffering.”
“Strike with your foot. Here is cool water to wash with and to drink.”
And We restored his family to him and the like of them with them, as mercy from Us and a reminder for those of understanding.
“T ake in your hand a bundle and strike with it, and do not break your oath.” We found him patient. What an excellent servant. He constantly turned back to Allah.
And remember Our servants Ibrāhīm, Isḥāq, and Y aʿqūb, people of strength and insight.
We purified them with a pure quality, the remembrance of the final Home.
And they are with Us among the chosen and the best.
And remember Ismāʿīl, Alyasaʿ, and Dhul-Kifl. All are among the best.
This is a reminder. And for those mindful of Allah is a good return,
Gardens of lasting stay whose gates will be open for them.
Reclining there, they will call for abundant fruit and drink.
And with them will be those who lower their gaze, of equal age.
This is what you are promised for the Day of Reckoning.
Indeed, this is Our provision, it will never run out.
This is so. But for those who transgress is an evil return,
Hell, where they will burn. What a miserable resting place.
This is so. So let them taste it, boiling water and pus,
and other punishments of similar kind.
“This is a crowd rushing in with you.” There is no welcome for them. They will burn in the Fire.
They will say, “No, rather there is no welcome for you. You brought this upon us. What a miserable place to settle.”
They will say, “Our Lord, whoever brought this upon us, increase him in double punishment in the Fire.”
And they will say, “Why do we not see men whom we used to count among the worst?
Did we mock them, or have our eyes missed them?”
Surely this is true, the arguing of the people of the Fire.
Say, “I am only a warner. There is no god except Allah, the One, the All- Prevailing,
Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the All- Mighty, the Most-Forgiving.”
Say, “It is great news
from which you turn away.
I had no knowledge of the highest assembly when they were disputing.
It is only revealed to me that I am a clear warner.”
When your Lord said to the angels, “I am creating a human from clay.
When I have formed him and breathed into him from My spirit, then fall down before him in prostration.”
So the angels all prostrated together,
except Iblīs. He was arrogant and became one of the disbelievers.
He said, “O Iblīs, what prevented you from prostrating to what I created with My hands? Were you arrogant, or were you among the exalted?”
He said, “I am better than him. You created me from fire and created him from clay.”
He said, “Then get out of it, for you are cast out,
and upon you is My curse until the Day of Judgement.”
He said, “My Lord, then grant me respite until the Day they are raised.”
He said, “You are among those given respite
until the Day of the known time.”
He said, “By Your might, I will surely mislead them all,
except Your sincere servants among them.”
He said, “The truth is, and the truth I say,
I will surely fill Hell with you and those of them who follow you, all together.”
Say, “I do not ask you for any reward for it, nor am I one who pretends.
It is only a reminder for all worlds.
And you will certainly know its reality after a time.”

surahs.name: Az-Zumar (The Groups)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: The sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
We have sent down to you the Book with truth, so worship Allah, making the religion purely for Him.

Surely, pure religion belongs to Allah alone. Those who take protectors besides Him say, “We only worship them so they may bring us nearer to Allah.” Allah will judge between them concerning what they differ about. Indeed, Allah does not guide one who is a liar and ungrateful.
If Allah had intended to take a son, He could have chosen what He willed from what He creates. Glory be to Him. He is Allah, the One, the All- Prevailing.

He created the heavens and the earth with truth. He wraps the night over the day and wraps the day over the night. He subjected the sun and the moon, each running for a fixed term. Surely, He is the All -Mighty, the Most - Forgiving.

He created you from a single soul, then made from it its mate, and sent down for you eight pairs of livestock. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers, creation after creation, in three layers of darkness. That is Allah, your Lord. T o Him belongs dominion. There is no god except Him. So how are you turned away?

If you disbelieve, Allah has no need of you. Y et He is not pleased with disbelief for His servants. But if you are grateful, He is pleased with it for you. No bearer of burden will bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you of what you used to do. Indeed, He knows what lies within hearts.

When hardship touches a person, he calls upon his Lord, turning to Him. Then when He grants him favour from Himself, he forgets what he used to call upon before and sets up equals with Allah to lead others away from His path. Say, “Enjoy your disbelief for a while. You are among the people of the Fire.”

Is one who stands in devotion during the night, prostrating and standing, mindful of the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord, like one who does not? Say, “ Are those who know equal to those who do not know?” Only those of understanding take heed.

Say, “O My servants who believe, be mindful of your Lord. For those who do good in this world is good, and the earth of Allah is spacious. Surely the patient will be given their reward without measure.”
Say, “I have been commanded to worship Allah, making the religion purely for Him,
and I have been commanded to be the first of those who submit.”
Say, “I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the punishment of a mighty Day.”
Say, “ Allah alone I worship, making my religion purely for Him.
So worship whatever you wish besides Him.” Say, “The true losers are those who lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. That is the clear loss.”
Above them are layers of Fire, and beneath them are layers. That is what Allah warns His servants about. O My servants, be mindful of Me.
And those who avoid false worship and turn back to Allah, for them is good news. So give good news to My servants,
those who listen to the word and follow the best of it. They are the ones whom Allah has guided, and they are people of understanding.
Is one upon whom the word of punishment has become due, can you then rescue one who is in the Fire?
But those who are mindful of their Lord will have chambers built high above one another, beneath which rivers flow. This is the promise of Allah. Allah does not fail in His promise.

Do you not see that Allah sends down water from the sky, then guides it as springs into the earth? Then with it He brings out crops of different colours. Then they dry, and you see them turn yellow. Then He makes them scattered fragments. Indeed, in that is a reminder for people of understanding.
Is one whose chest Allah has opened to Islām, so he is upon a light from his Lord? So woe to those whose hearts are hardened against the remembrance of Allah. They are in clear error.

Allah has sent down the best statement: a Book consistent and repeated. The skins of those who fear their Lord tremble at it; then their skins and their hearts soften at the remembrance of Allah. That is the guidance of Allah. He guides with it whom He wills. And whoever Allah leaves astray has no guide.
Is one who shields himself with his face from the worst punishment on the Day of Resurrection like one who is secure? And it will be said to the wrongdoers, “T aste what you used to earn.”
Those before them denied, and punishment came upon them from where they did not expect.
So Allah made them taste disgrace in worldly life, and the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if only they knew.
We have set forth for people in this Qur’an every kind of example so that they may take heed.
It is a Qur’an in clear Arabic, without distortion, so that they may be mindful.
Allah presents an example: a man owned by quarrelling partners and a man belonging wholly to one master. Are they equal in comparison? All praise belongs to Allah. Y et most of them do not know.
You will certainly die, and they too will die.
Then on the Day of Resurrection, you will dispute before your Lord.
Who is more unjust than one who lies about Allah and denies the truth when it comes to him? Is there not in Hell a dwelling for the disbelievers?
And the one who brings the truth and the one who confirms it, they are the ones mindful of Allah.
For them with their Lord is whatever they wish. That is the reward of those who do good,
so that Allah may remove from them the worst of what they did and reward them according to the best of what they used to do.
Is Allah not enough for His servant? Y et they frighten you with those besides Him. And whoever Allah leaves astray has no guide.
And whoever Allah guides, none can mislead. Is Allah not Mighty, Possessor of retribution?

If you ask them who created the heavens and the earth, they will surely say, “ Allah.” Say, “Have you then considered what you call upon besides Allah? If Allah intends harm for me, can they remove His harm? Or if He intends mercy for me, can they withhold  His mercy?” Say, “ Allah is enough for me. In Him those who trust place their trust.”
Say, “My people, act according to your position. I am acting. You will soon know
who will receive a punishment that disgraces him and upon whom a lasting punishment will descend.”
We have sent down to you the Book for people with truth. So whoever is guided, it is for his own soul, and whoever goes astray, it is only against it. You are not a guardian over them.

Allah takes souls at the time of their death, and those that do not die during their sleep. He keeps those for which He has decreed death and releases the others until a fixed term. Surely in that are signs for people who reflect.
Or have they taken intercessors besides Allah? Say, “Even if they have no power over anything and do not understand?”
Say, “ All intercession belongs to Allah alone. T o Him belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. Then to Him you will be returned.”
When Allah alone is mentioned, the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter shrink with aversion, but when those besides Him are mentioned, they rejoice.
Say, “O Allah, Originator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the unseen and the seen, You will judge between Your servants concerning what they used to differ over.”

If those who did wrong possessed everything in the earth and the like of it with it, they would offer it to ransom themselves from the worst punishment on the Day of Resurrection. And there will appear to them from Allah what they had never expected.
And the evils of what they earned will appear to them, and what they used to mock will surround them.
When hardship touches a person, he calls upon Us. Then when We grant him favour from Us, he says, “I was only given this because of knowledge.” Rather, it is a test, but most of them do not know.
Those before them also said it, but what they used to earn did not benefit them.
So the evil consequences of what they earned struck them. And those among these who do wrong will also be struck by the evil of what they earned, and they will not escape.
Do they not know that Allah extends provision for whom He wills and restricts it? Surely in that are signs for people who believe.
Say, “O My servants who have wronged themselves, do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Surely Allah forgives all sins. He is the Most-Forgiving, the Ever-Merciful.
Turn back to your Lord and submit to Him before the punishment comes upon you, then you will not be helped.
And follow the best of what has been sent down to you from your Lord before the punishment comes upon you suddenly while you do not realise,
lest a soul should say, ‘How regretful I am for what I neglected regarding Allah, and I was indeed among those who mocked,’
or it should say, ‘If only Allah had guided me, I would have been among those mindful,’
or it should say, when it sees the punishment, ‘If only I had another chance, I would be among those who do good.’
No indeed. My signs came to you, but you denied them, were arrogant, and became among the disbelievers.
And on the Day of Resurrection you will see those who lied about Allah, their faces darkened. Is there not in Hell a dwelling for the arrogant?
And Allah will save those who were mindful by their success. No harm will touch them, nor will they grieve.
Allah is the Creator of everything, and He is Guardian over everything.
T o Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth. Those who disbelieve in the signs of Allah, they are the true losers.
Say, “Is it other than Allah that you command me to worship, O ignorant ones?”
It has been revealed to you and to those before you that if you associate anything with Allah, your deeds will surely be lost, and you will surely be among the losers.
Rather, worship Allah alone and be among the grateful.

They did not measure Allah with His true measure, while the whole earth will be in His grasp on the Day of Resurrection, and the heavens will be folded in His right hand. Glory be to Him, far above what they associate with Him.

And the T rumpet will be blown, and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is in the earth will fall down, except whom Allah wills. Then it will be blown again, and suddenly they will be standing, looking on.

And the earth will shine with the light of its Lord, and the record will be set down, and the prophets and the witnesses will be brought, and judgement will be made between them with truth, and they will not be wronged.
And every soul will be paid in full for what it did, and He knows best what they do.

Those who disbelieved will be driven to Hell in groups. When they reach it, its gates will be opened and its keepers will say to them, “Did messengers not come to you from among yourselves, reciting to you the verses of your Lord and warning you of the meeting of this Day?” They will say, “Y es.” But the word of punishment has come true against the disbelievers.
It will be said, “Enter the gates of Hell, remaining there forever.” How miserable is the dwelling of the arrogant.

And those who were mindful of their Lord will be led to the Garden in groups. When they reach it and its gates are opened, its keepers will say to them, “Peace be upon you. You have done well, so enter it, remaining there forever.”

And they will say, “ All praise belongs to Allah, who has fulfilled His promise to us and made us inherit the earth so we may settle in the Garden wherever we wish.” How excellent is the reward of those who worked.

And you will see the angels surrounding the Throne, glorifying with praise of their Lord. Judgement will be made between them with truth, and it will be said, “ All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all worlds.”

surahs.name: Ghāfir (The Forgiver)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Hā Mīm.
This Book is sent down from Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.
The One who forgives sin, accepts repentance, is firm in punishment, and vast in generosity. There is no god except Him. T o Him is the final return.
No one disputes the signs of Allah except those who disbelieve, so do not let their movement throughout the land deceive you.

Before them, the people of Nū ḥ denied, and after them came other factions. Every nation intended to seize its messenger, and they argued with falsehood to wipe out the truth. So I seized them. How was My punishment?
And so the word of your Lord was fulfilled against those who disbelieve: they are the people of the Fire.

Those who carry the Throne and those around it glorify their Lord with praise, believe in Him, and ask forgiveness for those who believe: “Our Lord, You encompass everything in mercy and knowledge, so forgive those who repent and follow Your path, and protect them from the punishment of the Blaze.

Our Lord, admit them into Gardens of lasting stay which You have promised them, along with those who were righteous among their fathers, their spouses, and their offspring. You alone are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
Shield them from evil deeds. Whoever You shield from evil deeds on that Day, You have truly shown mercy to them. That is the great success.”
Indeed, those who disbelieved will be called out: “The hatred of Allah towards you was greater than your hatred of yourselves when you were called to faith but chose to disbelieve.”
They will say, “Our Lord, You caused us to die twice and gave us life twice. Now we confess our sins. Is there any way out?”
That is because when Allah alone was called upon, you disbelieved, but when partners were associated with Him, you believed. So judgement belongs to Allah, the Most-High, the Most-Great.
He is the One who shows you His signs and sends down provision for you from the sky. Y et none take heed except those who turn back to Him.
So call upon Allah, sincere in your devotion to Him, even if the disbelievers dislike it.
The Exalted above all ranks, Owner of the Throne. He sends the Spirit by His command upon whom He wills of His servants, to warn of the Day of Meeting.
The Day they will come out, nothing about them hidden from Allah. T o whom belongs the dominion today? T o Allah, the One, the Supreme over all.
T oday every soul will be repaid for what it earned. No injustice today. Allah is swift in reckoning.
W arn them of the Day that draws near, when hearts will rise to the throats, choked with distress. The wrongdoers will have no close friend and no intercessor who is obeyed.
He knows the betrayal of the eyes and what the chests conceal.
Allah judges with truth, while those they call upon besides Him judge nothing. Allah alone is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.

Have they not travelled through the earth and seen how those before them ended? They were greater than them in strength and in the marks they left on the earth, yet Allah seized them for their sins, and they had no protector against Allah.
That was because their messengers came to them with clear proofs, but they disbelieved. So Allah seized them. He is Powerful and firm in punishment.
And We certainly sent Mūsā with Our signs and clear authority,
T o Firʿawn, Hāmān, and Qārūn, but they said, “ A lying magician.”
When he brought them the truth from Us, they said, “Kill the sons of those who believe with him and spare their women.” But the plan of those who disbelieve leads only to ruin.
Firʿawn said, “Leave me to kill Mūsā and let him call upon his Lord. I fear that he will change your religion or cause corruption to spread in the land.”
And Mūsā said, “I seek refuge with my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant one who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.”

And a believing man from the family of Firʿawn, who had kept his faith hidden, said, “Will you kill a man simply because he says, ‘My Lord is Allah,’ when he has brought you clear proofs from your Lord? If he is lying, his lie is upon him. But if he is truthful, some of what he warns you of will reach you. Indeed, Allah does not guide one who is excessive and a liar.”

“My people, today authority is yours, dominant in the land. But who will help us against the punishment of Allah if it comes to us?” Firʿawn said, “I only show you what I see, and I only guide you to the path of right judgement.”
The one who believed said, “My people, I fear for you a day like the day that befell the factions before,
like the fate of the people of Nū ḥ, ʿĀd, Thamūd, and those after them. Allah does not intend injustice for His servants.
And O my people, I fear for you the Day of Calling,
the Day you will turn and flee, with no one to protect you from Allah. Whoever Allah leads astray, no one can guide.

And certainly Yūsuf came to you before with clear proofs, yet you remained in doubt about what he brought, until when he died you said, “ Allah will never send a messenger after him.” In this way Allah leads astray those who are excessive and full of doubt.

Those who dispute the signs of Allah without any authority given to them are deeply hateful in the sight of Allah and in the sight of those who believe. In this way Allah seals the heart of every arrogant tyrant.
And Firʿawn said, “Hāmān, build for me a tower so that I may reach the pathways,

the pathways of the heavens, and look upon the God of Mūsā, though I am certain he is a liar.” In this way the evil of his deeds was made appealing to Firʿawn, and he was turned away from the path. The plan of Firʿawn led only to ruin.
The one who believed said, “My people, follow me and I will guide you to the path of right guidance.
My people, this worldly life is only temporary enjoyment, but the Hereafter is the lasting home.
Whoever does an evil deed will only be repaid with its like, but whoever does what is right, male or female, and is a believer will enter the Garden and be provided there without measure.
O my people, why is it that I call you to salvation while you call me to the Fire?
You call me to disbelieve in Allah and to associate with Him what I have no knowledge of, while I call you to the All-Mighty, the Most-Forgiving.
There is no doubt that what you call me to has no claim in this world or the Hereafter. Our return is to Allah, and those who go beyond limits are the people of the Fire.
You will remember what I say to you. I entrust my affair to Allah. Allah is All-Seeing of His servants.”
So Allah protected him from the evils they planned, while a terrible punishment surrounded the family of Firʿawn.
The Fire. They are exposed to it morning and evening. And on the Day the Hour is established, it will be said, “ Admit the family of Firʿawn into the most severe punishment.”
When they argue within the Fire, the weak will say to those who were arrogant, “We were your followers, so can you relieve us of a portion of the Fire?”
Those who were arrogant will say, “We are all in it together. Allah has already judged between the servants.”
Those in the Fire will say to the keepers of Hell, “Call upon your Lord to lighten the punishment for us for just one day.”
They will say, “Did your messengers not come to you with clear proofs?” They will say, “Y es.” They will say, “Then call.” But the call of those who disbelieve leads only to loss.
We will indeed support Our messengers and those who believe, in the life of this world and on the Day when the witnesses stand.
The Day when the excuses of the wrongdoers will not benefit them. For them is the curse, and for them is the worst home.
And certainly, We gave Mūsā guidance and made the Children of Isrāʾīl inherit the Scripture,
as guidance and a reminder for those of understanding.
So be patient. The promise of Allah is true. Seek forgiveness for your sin and glorify your Lord with praise in the evening and in the morning.

Those who dispute the signs of Allah without any authority given to them have nothing in their chests except pride they will never attain. So seek refuge in Allah. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.
The creation of the heavens and the earth is greater than the creation of people, but most people do not know.
The blind and the seeing are not equal, nor are those who believe and do what is right equal to one who does evil. Little do you reflect.
The Hour is certainly coming. There is no doubt about it. Y et most people do not believe.
Your Lord says, “Call upon Me and I will respond to you. Those who are too proud to worship Me will enter Hell humiliated.”
Allah is the One who made the night for you so you may rest in it, and the day giving sight. Allah is full of favour to people, yet most people are not grateful.
That is Allah, your Lord, Creator of everything. There is no god except Him. So how are you turned away?
In this way those who used to reject the signs of Allah were turned away.

Allah is the One who made the earth a settled place for you and the sky a canopy, shaped you and perfected your form, and provided you with good things. That is Allah, your Lord. So blessed is Allah, Lord of all worlds.
He is the Living. There is no god except Him. So call upon Him, sincere in devotion to Him. All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all worlds.
Say, “I have been forbidden from worshipping those you call upon besides Allah once clear proofs came to me from my Lord, and I have been commanded to submit to the Lord of all worlds.”

He is the One who created you from dust, then from a drop of fluid, then from a clinging form, then brings you out as a child. Then you reach maturity, then you become elderly, though some of you are taken earlier, so that you may reach an appointed term and perhaps you will understand.
He is the One who gives life and causes death. When He decides a matter, He only says to it, “Be,” and it is.
Have you not seen those who dispute the signs of Allah? How are they turned away?
Those who deny the Book and what We sent with Our messengers will soon know,
when shackles will be around their necks and chains dragging them,
into boiling water, then into the Fire they will be fed.
Then it will be said to them, “Where is what you used to associate
besides Allah?” They will say, “They have deserted us. Rather, we were not calling upon anything before.” In this way Allah lets the disbelievers stray.
That is because you used to rejoice on the earth without right and because you were arrogant.
“Enter the gates of Hell, remaining there.” Miserable is the dwelling of the arrogant.
So be patient. The promise of Allah is true. Whether We show you some of what We have promised them or We take you back, to Us they will be returned.

We have already sent messengers before you. Among them are those whose stories We have told you, and among them are those We have not told you. No messenger could bring a sign except by the permission of Allah. When the command of Allah comes, judgement i s made with truth, and those who follow falsehood lose.
Allah is the One who made livestock for you, so that you may ride some of them and eat from some of them.
In them you have other benefits, and through them you reach needs within your hearts. On them and on ships you are carried.
He shows you His signs. So which of the signs of Allah will you deny?

Have they not travelled through the land and seen how those before them ended? They were more numerous than them, greater in strength, and in the marks they left upon the earth. Y et what they used to earn did not benefit them.
When their messengers came to them with clear proofs, they were content with the knowledge they already had, and what they used to mock surrounded them.
When they saw Our punishment, they said, “We believe in Allah alone and reject what we used to associate with Him.”
But their faith did not benefit them once they saw Our punishment. That is the way of Allah which has passed among His servants. There the disbelievers lost.

surahs.name: Fuṣṣilat (Explained in Detail)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Hā Mīm.
A revelation sent down from the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.
A Book whose verses are clearly explained, an Arabic Qur’an for people who know,
bringing good news and giving warning. Y et most of them turn away, so they do not listen.
They say, “Our hearts are covered from what you call us to, in our ears is heaviness, and between us and you is a barrier. So act, and we too are acting.”
Say, “I am only a man like you. It has been revealed to me that your god is One God. So take a straight path to Him and seek His forgiveness. W oe to those who associate others with Him,
those who do not give zakah and disbelieve in the Hereafter.”
Those who believe and do what is right will have a reward that never ends.
Say, “Do you really disbelieve in the One who created the earth in two days and set up equals to Him? That is the Lord of all worlds.”
He placed firm mountains upon it, blessed it, and measured out its provisions within it in four days, equal for those who ask.
Then He turned to the sky while it was smoke and said to it and to the earth, “Come willingly or unwillingly.” They said, “We come willingly.”

So He completed them as seven heavens in two days and assigned to each heaven its command. We adorned the lowest heaven with lamps and protection. That is the decree of the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.
But if they turn away, say, “I warn you of a thunderbolt like the thunderbolt of ʿĀd and Thamūd.”

When messengers came to them from before them and from behind them, saying, “Do not worship except Allah.” They said, “If our Lord had willed, He would have sent down angels. So we disbelieve in what you have been sent with.”
As for ʿĀd, they were arrogant upon the earth without right and said, “Who is stronger than us?” Did they not see that Allah who created them was stronger than them? They used to reject Our signs.

So We sent against them a furious wind in days of misfortune, to make them taste the punishment of disgrace in the life of this world. The punishment of the Hereafter is even more disgraceful, and they will not be helped.
As for Thamūd, We guided them, but they preferred blindness over guidance. So the thunderbolt of humiliating punishment seized them for what they used to earn.
We saved those who believed and were mindful.
The Day the enemies of Allah are gathered to the Fire, they will be driven in ranks.
When they reach it, their hearing, their sight, and their skins will testify against them about what they used to do.
They will say to their skins, “Why did you testify against us?” They will say, “ Allah made us speak, the One who makes everything speak. He created you the first time, and to Him you are returned.”
You did not try to hide yourselves lest your hearing, your sight, and your skins testify against you. Rather, you thought Allah did not know much of what you were doing.
That thought of yours about your Lord has destroyed you, and you have become among the losers.
If they endure, the Fire is their home. If they seek to make amends, they will not be among those accepted.

We assigned to them companions who made attractive to them what was before them and what was behind them. The word was fulfilled against them along with nations that passed before them of jinn and mankind. They were losers.
Those who disbelieved said, “Do not listen to this Qur’an and talk over it so that you may prevail.”
We will certainly make those who disbelieved taste a severe punishment, and We will repay them with the worst of what they used to do.
That is the reward of the enemies of Allah, the Fire. For them in it is the home of permanence, a reward for denying Our signs.
Those who disbelieved will say, “Our Lord, show us those among jinn and mankind who led us astray, so we may place them beneath our feet and they will be among the lowest.”
Indeed, those who say, “Our Lord is Allah,” then remain steadfast, angels descend upon them saying, “Do not fear and do not grieve, but receive good news of the Garden you were promised.
We are your allies in the life of this world and in the Hereafter. You will have there whatever your souls desire, and you will have there whatever you ask for,
as a welcoming gift from the Most-Forgiving, the Ever-Merciful.”
Who speaks better than one who calls to Allah, does what is right, and says, “I am among those who submit?”
Good and evil are not equal. Repel evil with what is better, and the one between whom and you was hostility will become as though a close friend.
None are granted it except those who are patient, and none are granted it except one with a great share.
If a whisper from Shay ṭān stirs you, seek refuge in Allah. He is the All - Hearing, the All-Knowing.
Among His signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate to the sun or the moon, but prostrate to Allah who created them, if it is Him you worship.
But if they are arrogant, those near your Lord glorify Him night and day, and they never become weary.
Among His signs is that you see the earth lifeless, but when We send down water upon it, it stirs and grows. The One who gives it life will surely give life to the dead. He has power over everything.
Those who distort Our signs are not hidden from Us. Is the one thrown into the Fire better, or the one who comes secure on the Day of Resurrection? Do as you wish. He sees what you do.
Those who disbelieve in the Reminder when it comes to them will face the truth, for it is a mighty Book.
Falsehood cannot approach it from before it or from behind it. It is sent down from One who is All-Wise and All-Praiseworthy.
Nothing is said to you except what was already said to the messengers before you. Your Lord is Owner of forgiveness and Owner of painful punishment.

If We had made it a non-Arabic Qur’an, they would have said, “Why are its verses not clearly explained? A non -Arabic and an Arab?” Say, “It is guidance and healing for those who believe. As for those who do not believe, there is heaviness in their ears, and it is blindness for them. They are being called from a distant place.”

And We surely gave Mūsā the Scripture, but differences arose about it. Had it not been for a prior word from your Lord, judgement would have been made between them. They remain in deep doubt about it.
Whoever does what is right, it is for his own soul; and whoever does evil, it is against it. And your Lord is never unjust to the servants.

Knowledge of the Hour belongs to Him alone. No fruits emerge from their coverings, and no female conceives or gives birth except with His knowledge. On the Day He calls to them, “Where are My partners?” They will say, “We declare to You that none of us can testify to that.”
What they used to call upon before will abandon them, and they will realise there is no escape for them.
The human being  does not tire of asking for good, but when harm touches him, he becomes despairing and hopeless.

If We let him taste mercy from Us after hardship has touched him, he surely says, “This is mine, and I do not think the Hour will occur. But even if I am returned to my Lord, I will have the best with Him.” We will inform those who disbelieved of what they did, and We will make them taste a harsh punishment.
And w hen We grant favour to the human being, he turns away and distances himself, but when harm touches him, he is full of lengthy supplication.
Say, “Have you considered, if it is from Allah and yet you disbelieve in it, who is further astray than one who is in far-reaching opposition?”
We will show them Our signs on the horizons and within themselves until it becomes clear to them that it is the truth. Is it not enough that your Lord is Witness over everything?
Indeed, they are in doubt about meeting their Lord. Indeed, He encompasses everything.

surahs.name: Ash-Shūrā (Consultation)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Ḥā Mīm.
ʿAyn Sīn Qāf.
In this way Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise, reveals to you and to those before you.
T o Him belongs everything in the heavens and everything on the earth. He is the Most-High, the Most-Great.
The heavens almost break apart from above them, while the angels glorify with praise of their Lord and ask forgiveness for those on the earth. Surely, Allah is the Most-Forgiving, the Ever-Merciful.
And as for those who take protectors besides Him, Allah watches over them. You are not placed over them as a guardian.

And in this way, We have revealed to you an Arabic Qur’an, so that you may warn the Mother of Cities and those around it, and warn of the Day of Gathering, about which there is no doubt. A group will be in the Garden, and a group will be in the Blaze.
Had Allah willed, He could have made them one single community, but He admits whom He wills into His mercy. And the wrongdoers will have no protector and no helper.
Or have they taken protectors besides Him? Y et Allah alone is the true Protector. He gives life to the dead, and He has power over everything.
Whatever you differ about, its judgement belongs to Allah. That is Allah, my Lord. In Him I place my trust, and to Him I turn.

Originator of the heavens and the earth. He made for you spouses from among yourselves, and mates among the livestock, multiplying you through this. Nothing is like Him. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.
T o Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth. He extends provision to whom He wills and restricts it. He has full knowledge of everything.

He has laid down for you in faith what He instructed Nūḥ, and what We revealed to you, and what We instructed Ibrāhīm, Mūsā, and ʿĪsā: uphold the faith and do not divide within it. What you call them to is heavy upon those who associate others with Allah. Allah chooses for Himself whom He wills and guides to Himself those who turn back to Him.

They did not split into factions until after knowledge had come to them, out of rivalry among themselves. Had it not been for a prior word from your Lord setting a fixed term, judgement would already have been passed between them. And indeed, Those who inherited the Book after them are in unsettling doubt about it.

So to this call, and stand firm as you are commanded. Do not follow their desires, but say, “I believe in every Book Allah has sent down, and I am commanded to act justly between you. Allah is our Lord and your Lord. For us are our deeds, and for you are your deeds. There is no argument between us. Allah will gather us together, and to Him is the final return.”
Those who argue about Allah after He has been responded to, their argument is worthless with their Lord. Upon them is anger, and for them is a severe punishment.
It is Allah who sent down the Book with truth, and the Balance. And what will make you realise? The Hour may be near.
Those who do not believe in it seek to hasten it, while those who believe are in awe of it and know it is the truth. Surely, those who dispute about the Hour are far astray.
Allah is All-Subtle with His servants. He provides for whom He wills. He is the All-Strong, the All-Mighty.
Whoever desires the harvest of the Hereafter, We increase him in his harvest. And whoever desires the harvest of this world, We give him from it, but he will have no share in the Hereafter.

Or have they taken partners who have laid down for them a way of faith that Allah did not permit? If it were not for the decisive word, judgement would already have been passed between them. Indeed, the wrongdoers will have a painful punishment.

You will see the wrongdoers fearful because of what they have earned, and it will fall upon them. But those who believe and do what is right will be in the gardens of Paradise. They will have whatever they wish with their Lord. That is the great favour.

That is the good news Allah gives to His servants who believe and do what is right. Say, “I ask of you no reward for it except affection among close kin.” Whoever earns a good deed, We increase him in good through it. Allah is Most-Forgiving, Most-Appreciative.
Or do they say, “He has invented a lie about Allah”? If Allah willed, He could seal your heart. Allah erases falsehood and confirms the truth by His words. He fully knows what lies within the chests.
He is the One who accepts repentance from His servants and pardons misdeeds. He knows what you do.
He answers those who believe and do what is right, and increases them from His favour. As for the disbelievers, for them is a severe punishment.

And if Allah were to expand provision for His servants without limit, they would overstep throughout the earth. But He sends it down in due measure as He wills. He is fully aware of His servants and sees them clearly.
And He is the One who sends down rain after they have despaired and spreads His mercy. He is the T rue Protector, the One worthy of all praise.
Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and whatever living creatures He has spread throughout them. He is able to gather them together whenever He wills.
Whatever hardship strikes you is because of what your own hands have earned, and He overlooks much.
You cannot escape on the earth, and you have no protector or helper besides Allah.
Among His signs are the ships sailing the sea like mountains.
If He wills, He can calm the wind  and they remain motionless on its surface. In that are signs for everyone patient and grateful.
Or He can cause them to sink because of what they have earned, yet He pardons much.
And so those who argue about Our signs may know that they have no refuge.
