Whatever you have been given is only the enjoyment of this worldly life. But what is with Allah is better and more lasting for those who believe and rely upon their Lord,
those who avoid major sins and indecency, and when they are angered, they forgive,
those who respond to their Lord, uphold the prayer, conduct their affairs by mutual consultation among themselves, and spend from what We have provided them,
and those who, when oppression strikes them, defend themselves.
The reward of a wrong is a wrong equal to it. But whoever forgives and makes peace, his reward rests with Allah. He does not love the wrongdoers.
Whoever defends himself after being wronged, there is no blame upon them.
Blame is only upon those who oppress people and act unjustly on the earth without right. For them is a painful punishment.
But whoever is patient and forgives, that is indeed  a matter requiring firm resolve.
Whoever Allah leaves astray has no protector after Him. You will see the wrongdoers, when they see the punishment, saying, “Is there any way back?”

You will see them brought before it, humbled by disgrace, looking with hidden glances. Those who believed will say, “The true losers are those who lost themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection.” Surely, the wrongdoers are in lasting punishment.
They will have no protectors to help them besides Allah. And whoever Allah leaves astray has no path.
Respond to your Lord before a Day comes from Allah that cannot be turned back. On that Day you will have no refuge and no denial.

But if they turn away, We have not sent you over them as a guardian. Your duty is only to deliver. When We let a person taste mercy from Us, he rejoices in it. But if hardship strikes them because of what their own hands have sent ahead, then the human being is ungrateful.
T o Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. He creates what He wills. He grants daughters to whom He wills, and grants sons to whom He wills,
or He grants them both sons and daughters, and He makes whom He wills unable to have children. He is All-Knowing, All-Powerful.

It is not for any human being that Allah should speak to him except by revelation, or from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger who reveals by His permission what He wills. He is Most-High, All-Wise.

In this way We revealed to you a spirit from Our command. You did not know what the Book was, nor what faith was, but We made it a light by which We guide whom We will among Our servants. And you surely guide to a straight path,
the path of Allah, to whom belongs everything in the heavens and everything on the earth. Indeed, to Allah all matters return.

surahs.name: Az-Zukhruf (Ornaments of Gold)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Ḥā Mīm.
By the clear Book.
We have made it an Arabic Qur’an so that you may use your reason.
It is preserved with Us in the Mother of the Book, exalted and full of wisdom.
Should We then turn the Reminder away from you because you are a people who go beyond limits?
How many prophets We sent to the former peoples,
yet no prophet came to them without them mocking him.
So We destroyed those stronger in power than them, and the example of the earlier peoples has already passed.
If you were to ask them who created the heavens and the earth, they would surely say, “The All-Mighty, the All-Knowing created them.”
The One who made the earth a resting place for you and placed pathways in it so that you may find your way.
The One who sends down water from the sky in measured amount, and with it We bring dead land back to life. In this way you will be brought out.
The One who created all pairs, and made for you ships and livestock upon which you ride,

so that you may settle firmly upon their backs, then remember the favour of your Lord when you are settled upon them, and say, “Glory be to the One who has placed this at our service, for we could never have controlled it ourselves.
And surely, to our Lord we will return.”
Y et they assign to Him a portion from His own servants. Indeed, the human being is clearly ungrateful.
Or has He taken daughters from what He creates and chosen sons for you?
When one of them is given news of what he attributes to the Most - Merciful, his face darkens and he is filled with grief.
Someone brought up in adornment and unable to express clearly in dispute?
They have made the angels, who are servants of the Most -Merciful, female. Did they witness their creation? Their claim will be recorded, and they will be questioned.
And they say, “If the Most-Merciful had willed, we would not have worshipped them.” They have no knowledge of this. They are only guessing.
Or did We give them a book before this to which they now hold fast?
No, they say, “We found our forefathers following a way, and we are guided by their footsteps.”
In the same way, We never sent a warner before you to any town except that its wealthy ones said, “We found our forefathers on a path, and we are following in their tracks.”
He said, “Even if I bring you better guidance than what you found your forefathers upon?” They replied, “We disbelieve in what you have been sent with.”
So We took retribution from them. Look then how the end of those who denied turned out.
When Ibrāhīm said to his father and his people, “I am free from what you worship,
except the One who created me. He will surely guide me.”
And he made it a lasting word among his descendants so that they might return.
Y et I allowed these people and their fathers to enjoy life until the truth and a clear messenger came to them.
But when the truth came to them, they said, “This is magic, and we reject it.”
They said, “Why was this Qur’an not sent down to a great man from one of the two towns?”

Is it they who distribute the mercy of your Lord? We have distributed their livelihood among them in the life of this world, and raised some of them above others in rank so that some may make use of others in service. The mercy of your Lord is better than what they gather.
If it were not that people might become one community, We would have made for those who deny the Most-Merciful roofs of silver for their houses, and stairways upon which they climb,
and doors for their houses, and couches upon which they recline,
and ornaments of gold. Y et all of that is nothing but the enjoyment of this worldly life. The Hereafter with your Lord is for those who are mindful of Him.
Whoever turns away from the remembrance of the Most-Merciful, We assign to him a devil who becomes his close companion.
They turn them away from the path, yet they think they are guided.
Until, when he comes to Us, he says, “If only there were between me and you the distance of the two easts.” What a miserable companion.
It will not benefit you that Day, since you did wrong, that you share in the punishment.
Can you make the deaf hear, or guide the blind and one who is in clear error?
Whether We take you away, We will still take retribution from them.
Or We show you what We have promised them, We are fully able over them.
So hold firmly to what has been revealed to you. You are upon a straight path.
It is a reminder for you and for your people, and you will be questioned.
Ask those of Our messengers whom We sent before you, did We ever appoint gods to be worshipped besides the Most-Merciful?
We certainly sent Mūsā with Our signs to Firʿawn and his chiefs. He said, “I am the messenger of the Lord of all worlds.”
But when he came to them with Our signs, they laughed at them.
We did not show them any sign except that it was greater than the one before it, and We seized them with punishment so that they might return.
They said, “Magician, call upon your Lord for us by the covenant He made with you. We will surely be guided.”
But when We removed the punishment from them, they broke their word.
And Firʿawn called out to his people, saying, “My people, does not the rule of Egypt belong to me, and these rivers flowing beneath me? Do you not see?
Am I not better than this man who is insignificant and can hardly speak clearly?
Why have bracelets of gold not been placed upon him, or angels come with him in procession?”
So he misled his people, and they obeyed him. Indeed, t hey were a rebellious people.
When they provoked Us to anger, We took retribution from them and drowned them all.
We made them a lesson from the past and an example for those who came after.
And when the son of Maryam was presented as an example, your people reacted with loud protest.
They said, “ Are our gods better, or is he?” They only put this forward to argue. They are a quarrelsome people.
He was only a servant upon whom We granted favour, and We made him a sign for the Children of Isrā’īl.
If We willed, We could have made angels among you, succeeding one another on the earth.
He is a sign of the Hour, so do not doubt it, and follow Me. This is a straight path.
Do not let Shayṭān turn you away. He is to you a clear enemy.
When ʿĪsā came with clear signs, he said, “I have come to you with wisdom, and to make clear to you some of that over which you differ. So be mindful of Allah and obey me.
Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight path.”
Y et the factions among them differed. So woe to those who did wrong from the punishment of a painful Day.
Are they waiting for the Hour to come upon them suddenly while they are unaware?
Close friends on that Day will be enemies to one another, except those who were mindful.
“O My servants, there will be no fear upon you today, nor will you grieve,
those who believed in Our signs and were in willing submission.
Enter the Garden, you and your spouses, delighted.”
Circulated among them will be plates and cups of gold. There will be whatever souls desire and eyes delight in. You will remain there forever.
That is the Garden you are given as inheritance because of what you used to do.
For you there will be abundant fruit, from which you will eat.
As for the criminals, they will remain forever in the punishment of Hell.
It will not be lightened for them, and they will be in despair within it.
We did not wrong them, but they were the wrongdoers.
They will call out, “O Mālik, let your Lord finish us.” He will say, “You will remain.”
We brought you the truth, but most of you disliked the truth.
Or have they decided on a plan? We too are planning.
Or do they think that We do not hear their secrets and their private talks? Y es indeed, and Our messengers are with them recording.
Say, “If the Most-Merciful had a son, I would be the first to worship.”
Exalted is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, far above what they describe.
So leave them to talk and play until they meet their Day which they are promised.
He is the One who is worshipped in the heaven and worshipped on the earth. He is the All-Wise, the All-Knowing.
Blessed is the One to whom belongs the rule of the heavens and the earth and whatever lies between them. With Him is knowledge of the Hour, and to Him you will be returned.
Those they call upon besides Him hold no power of intercession, except those who testify to the truth knowingly.
If you ask them who created them, they will surely say, “ Allah.” So how are they turned away?
And his saying, “My Lord, these are a people who do not believe.”
So turn away from them and say, “Peace.” They will come to know.

surahs.name: Ad-Dukhān (The Smoke)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Ḥā Mīm.
By the clear Book.
We sent it down in a blessed night. We have always been the W arner.
In it every decisive matter is set apart,
as a command from Us. We have always sent messengers,
as mercy from your Lord. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing,
Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything between them, if you would be certain.
There is no god except Him. He gives life and causes death, your Lord and the Lord of your earliest forefathers.
Y et they play in doubt.
So watch for the Day when the sky will bring a visible smoke.
It will cover the people. This is a painful punishment.
“Our Lord, lift the punishment from us. We truly believe.”
How will they take heed, when a clear Messenger had already come to them,
yet they turned away from him and said, “T aught by others, possessed.”
We will remove the punishment for a short while. You will return.
On the Day We seize with the greatest seizure, We will take full retribution.
Before them We tested the people of Firʿawn. A noble messenger came to them,
saying, “Hand over to me the servants of Allah. I am a trustworthy messenger to you.
Do not rise up against Allah. I come to you with clear authority.
I seek refuge with my Lord and your Lord, that you do not stone me.
But if you do not believe me, then leave me be.”
So he called upon his Lord, “These are a criminal people.”
“T ravel by night with My servants. You will be pursued.
Leave the sea parted and still. They are an army to be drowned.”
How many gardens and springs they left behind,
and fields and fine dwellings,
and comfort in which they took delight.
That is how it was. And We made another people inherit it.
The heaven and the earth did not weep for them, nor were they given respite.
We saved the Children of Isrā’īl from the humiliating torment,
from Firʿawn. He was arrogant, one of those who overstep all bounds.
We chose them knowingly above the worlds of their time,
and gave them signs containing a clear trial.
These people say,
“There is nothing except our first death. We will not be raised again.
Bring back our forefathers, if you are truthful.”
Are they better, or the people of Tubbaʿ and those before them? We destroyed them. They were criminals.
We did not create the heavens and the earth and everything between them in mere play.
We created them only with truth, but most of them do not know.
The Day of Decision is their appointed time, all of them together,
the Day when no close friend will benefit a close friend in any way, nor will they be helped,
except those upon whom Allah shows mercy. He is the Al l-Mighty, the Ever-Merciful.
The tree of Zaqqūm
will be food for the sinful,
like molten metal boiling in the stomachs,
boiling like scalding water.
“Seize him and drag him into the heart of the Blaze.
Then pour over his head from the torment of boiling water.
T aste it. You were the mighty, the honoured.
This is what you used to doubt.”
The mindful will be in a place of security,
among gardens and springs,
dressed in fine silk and brocade, facing one another.
That is how it will be. And We will join them with companions of pure, wide eyes.
There they will call for every fruit in safety.
They will not taste death there beyond the first death, and He will shield them from the punishment of the Blaze,
as favour from your Lord. That is the great success.
We have made it easy in your tongue, so that they may take heed.
So wait. They too are waiting.

surahs.name: Al-Jāthiyah (The Kneeling)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Ḥā Mīm.
The sending down of this Book is from Allah, the All -Mighty, the All- Wise.
In the heavens and the earth are clear signs for those who believe.
In your own creation, and in the living creatures He spreads throughout the earth, are signs for people who are certain.

In the alternation of night and day, and in what Allah sends down from the sky as provision, bringing the earth to life after its death, and in the shifting of the winds, are signs for people who use their reason.
These are the signs of Allah which We recite to you in truth. So in what message after Allah and His signs will they believe?
W oe to every sinful liar,
who hears the signs of Allah being recited to him, then persists in arrogance as if he never heard them. So give him news of a painful punishment.
When he learns anything of Our signs, he turns it into mockery. For such people there is a humiliating punishment.
Ahead of them is Hell. What they earned will not benefit them in the least, nor will those they took as protectors besides Allah. For them is a great punishment.
This is guidance. And those who disbelieve in the signs of their Lord will have a punishment of severe torment.
Allah is the One who made the sea serve you, so ships sail upon it by His command, that you may seek from His bounty and be grateful.
He made everything in the heavens and everything in the earth serve you, all from Him. In this are signs for people who reflect.
T ell those who believe to forgive those who do not expect the Days of Allah, so that He may repay people for what they used to earn.
Whoever does what is right, it is for his own soul. And whoever does wrong, it is against it. Then to your Lord you will be returned.
And We certainly gave the Children of Isrā’īl the Book, judgement, and prophethood. We provided them with good things and favoured them above the worlds.

We gave them clear proofs in the matter. They did not differ until knowledge had come to them, out of rivalry among themselves. Your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ.
Then We placed you upon a clear path of the command, so follow it and do not follow the desires of those who do not know.
They will not avail you against Allah in the least. Indeed, the wrongdoers are allies of one another, but Allah is the Protector of the mindful.
This is insight for people, and guidance and mercy for people who are certain.
Do those who commit evil think We will make them like those who believe and do what is right, equal in their life and in their death? How wrongly they judge.
Allah created the heavens and the earth in truth, so that every soul may be repaid for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged.

Have you seen the one who takes his own desire as his god? Allah leaves him astray despite knowledge, seals his hearing and his heart, and places a covering over his sight. Who then can guide him after Allah? Will you not reflect?
They say, “There is nothing but our worldly life. We die and we live, and nothing destroys us except time.” They have no knowledge of this. They only speculate.
And when Our clear signs are recited to them, their only argument is to say, “Bring back our forefathers, if you are truthful.”
Say, “ Allah gives you life, then causes you to die, then He will gather you for the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt. Y et most people do not know.”
T o Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. On the Day the Hour is established, that Day the falsifiers will be lost.
You will see every community kneeling. Every community will be called to its record. “T oday you will be repaid for what you used to do.”
“This is Our record. It speaks against you in truth. We were recording all that you used to do.”
As for those who believed and did what is right, their Lord will admit them into His mercy. That is the clear success.
But as for those who disbelieved: “Were My signs not recited to you? Y et you were arrogant and became a criminal people.”
When it was said, “The promise of Allah is true, and there is no doubt about the Hour,” you said, “We do not know what the Hour is. We only think it is an assumption, and we are not convinced.”
The evil of what they did will appear to them, and what they used to mock will surround them.
It will be said, “T oday We forget you as you forgot the meeting of this Day of yours. Your refuge is the Fire, and you have no helpers.”
“That is because you took the signs of Allah as mockery, and the life of this world deceived you.” So today they will not be brought out from it, nor will they be allowed to seek favour.
All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of all worlds.
T o Him belongs all majesty in the heavens and the earth. He is the All - Mighty, the All-Wise.

surahs.name: Al-Aḥqāf (The Sand Dunes)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: * Ḥā Mīm.
The sending down of this Book is from Allah, the All -Mighty, the All - Wise.
We did not create the heavens and the earth and everything between them except in truth and for a set term. Y et those who disbelieve turn away from what they are warned about.

Say, “Have you seen what you call upon besides Allah? Show me what they have created of the earth, or do they have a share in the heavens. Bring me a book before this, or a trace of knowledge, if you are truthful.”
Who is further astray than one who calls upon something besides Allah that will not respond to him until the Day of Resurrection, and they are unaware of their calls?
When people are gathered, they will be enemies to them and will deny their worship.
When Our clear signs are recited to them, those who disbelieve say about the truth when it comes to them, “This is obvious magic.”

Or they say, “He has invented it.” Say, “If I have invented it, you have no power to protect me from Allah. He knows best what you are saying about it. He is enough as a Witness between me and you. He is the Most-Forgiving, the Ever-Merciful.”
Say, “I am not something new among the messengers, and I do not know what will be done with me or with you. I only follow what is revealed to me, and I am only a clear warner.”

Say, “Have you considered: if it is from Allah and you reject it, while a witness from the Children of Isrā’īl testifies to something similar and believes, yet you are arrogant? Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.”
Those who disbelieve say about those who believe, “If it were good, they would not have reached it before us.” And since they are not guided by it, they say, “This is an old lie.”
And before it was the Book of Mūsā, a guide and a mercy. And this is a confirming Book in the Arabic language, to warn those who do wrong and to bring good news to those who do what is right.
Indeed, those who say, “Our Lord is Allah,” then remain steadfast, no fear will be upon them, nor will they grieve.
They are the people of Paradise, remaining in it  forever, as a reward for what they used to do.

And We have instructed the human being  to show excellence to his parents. His mother carried him with hardship and gave birth to him with hardship. His carrying and his weaning last thirty months. When he reaches maturity and reaches forty years, he says, “My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favour which You have given to me and to my parents, and to do what is right that pleases You. Set right for me my offspring. I turn to You, and I am among those who submit.”
Those are the ones from whom We accept the best of what they did and overlook their wrongs. They will be among the people of Paradise, a true promise which they were promised.

But the one who says to his parents, “Enough of you both. Do you promise me that I will be brought out, when generations before me have already passed away?” While they both call upon Allah for help, “W oe to you, believe. The promise of Allah is true.” Y et he says, “This is nothing but tales of the former people.”
Those are the ones upon whom the word has become binding among communities that passed away before them of jinn and humankind. They were indeed losers.
For each there are ranks according to what they have done, so that He may repay them in full for their deeds, and they will not be wronged.

On the Day those who disbelieved are exposed to the Fire, it will be said, “You exhausted your good things in your worldly life and enjoyed them. So today you will be repaid with a humiliating punishment for the arrogance you showed on earth without right and for your rebellion.”

Mention the brother of ʿĀd, when he warned his people at Al-Aḥqāf, and warners had already passed before him and after him, saying, “Do not worship except Allah. I fear for you the punishment of a mighty Day.”
They said, “Have you come to turn us away from our gods? Then bring us what you threaten us with, if you are truthful.”
He said, “The knowledge is only with Allah. I only deliver to you what I was sent with, but I see that you are an ignorant people.”
When they saw it as a cloud approaching their valleys, they said, “This is a cloud bringing us rain.” Rather, it is what you were impatient for, a wind carrying a painful punishment,
destroying everything by the command of its Lord. By morning nothing could be seen except their homes. Thus We repay the criminal people.

We established them in ways We have not established you, and We gave them hearing, sight, and hearts. Y et their hearing, their sight, and their hearts did not benefit them in the least when they disbelieved in the signs of Allah, and what they used to mock closed in around them.
We destroyed the towns around you, and We repeated the signs, so that they might return.
Why then did those they took as gods besides Allah, as a means of nearness, not help them? Rather, they disappeared from them. That was their falsehood and what they used to invent.
When We directed a group of jinn towards you to listen to the Qur’an, when they attended it they said, “Be silent.” When it was finished, they returned to their people as warners.
They said, “Our people, we have heard a Book sent down after Mūsā, confirming what came before it, guiding to the truth and to a straight path.”
“Our people, respond to the caller of Allah and believe in him. He will forgive you some of your sins and protect you from a painful punishment.”
Whoever does not respond to the caller of Allah cannot escape on earth, and he has no protectors besides Him. Such people are in clear error.
Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and the earth and was not wearied by their creation, is able to give life to the dead? Y es indeed. He has power over everything.

On the Day those who disbelieved are exposed to the Fire, it will be said, “Is this not the truth?” They will say, “Y es, by our Lord.” He will say, “Then taste the punishment for what you used to disbelieve.”

So be patient as the resolute among the messengers were patient, and do not seek to hasten it for them. On the Day they see what they are promised, it will seem as if they had remained only an hour of a day. This is a clear message. And none are destroyed except the rebellious people.

surahs.name: Muḥammad
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Those who disbelieve and turn others away from the path of Allah, He will bring their deeds to nothing.
And those who believe, do what is right, and believe in what has been sent down to Muhammad, and it is the truth from their Lord, He will erase their sins and set their condition right.
That is because those who disbelieve follow falsehood, while those who believe follow the truth from their Lord. In this way Allah makes clear to people their realities.

So when you meet those who disbelieve in battle, strike at their necks until you have subdued them, then bind them firmly. After that, either release them as a favour or for ransom, until the war lays down its burdens. That is how it is. If Allah had will ed, He could have taken retribution from them Himself, but He tests some of you through others. And those who are killed in the path of Allah, He will never let their deeds be lost.
He will guide them, set their condition right,
and admit them into the Garden which He has made known to them.
O you who believe, if you support Allah, He will support you and make your steps firm.
But those who disbelieve, ruin awaits them, and He will bring their deeds to nothing.
That is because they hate what Allah has sent down, so He has made their deeds worthless.
Have they not travelled through the land and seen how the end was for those before them? Allah destroyed them, and for the disbelievers there will be the same.
That is because Allah is the Protector of those who believe, and the disbelievers have no protector.
Allah will admit those who believe and do what is right into Gardens beneath which rivers flow. But those who disbelieve enjoy themselves and eat as animals eat, and the Fire will be their home.
How many towns stronger than your town which drove you out have We destroyed. There was no one to help them.
Is one who stands upon clear proof from his Lord like one whose evil deeds are made attractive to him and who follows his own desires?

The likeness of the Garden promised to those who are mindful: in it are rivers of water never stagnant, rivers of milk whose taste never changes, rivers of wine delightful to those who drink, and rivers of purified honey. For them in it are fruits of every kind and forgiveness from their Lord. Is that like one who will remain in the Fire forever and be given boiling water to drink that tears apart their insides?

Among them are those who listen to you, but when they leave your presence they say to those given knowledge, “What did he just say?” They are the ones whose hearts Allah has sealed, and they follow their own desires.
But those who are guided, He increases them in guidance and grants them their mindfulness.
Are they waiting for the Hour to come upon them suddenly? Its signs have already come. So what good will their reminder be when it arrives?
So know that there is no god except Allah, and seek forgiveness for your sin and for the believing men and believing women. Allah knows your movements and your resting place.

Those who believe say, “Why has a chapter not been sent down?” But when a decisive chapter is sent down and fighting is mentioned in it, you see those with sickness in their hearts looking at you like one fainting from death. So ruin is closer to them.
Obedience and fair speech. But when the matter is settled, then if they are true to Allah, it would be better for them.
W ould you then, if you were given authority, spread corruption in the land and break your family ties?
Those are the ones Allah has cursed, so He has made them deaf and blinded their sight.
Do they not reflect upon the Qur’an, or are there locks upon their hearts?
Those who turned back after guidance had become clear to them, Shayṭān made it appealing to them and filled them with false hopes.
That is because they said to those who hate what Allah has sent down, “We will obey you in some matters.” But Allah knows what they conceal.
So how will it be when the angels take them at death, striking their faces and their backs?
That is because they followed what angers Allah and disliked what pleases Him, so He made their deeds worthless.
Or do those with sickness in their hearts think that Allah will not expose their hidden grudges?
If We willed, We could show them to you, and you would recognise them by their mark. But you will surely recognise them by the tone of their speech. And Allah knows your deeds.
We will surely test you until We make known those among you who strive and remain steadfast, and We will reveal your true record.

Those who disbelieve and turn others away from the path of Allah, and oppose the Messenger after guidance has become clear to them, will not harm Allah in the least, and He will bring their deeds to nothing.
O you who believe, obey Allah and obey the Messenger, and do not let your deeds be wasted.
Those who disbelieve and turn others away from the path of Allah, then die while they are disbelievers, Allah will never forgive them.
So do not lose heart or call for peace while you hold the upper hand. Allah is with you and He will never deprive you of your deeds.
The life of this world is only play and distraction. But if you believe and are mindful, He will give you your rewards and will not demand your wealth from you.
If He were to demand it from you and press you for it, you would become tight fisted, and He would bring out your hidden resentment.

Here you are, invited to spend in the path of Allah, yet among you are those who hold back. Whoever holds back only withholds from himself. Allah is Free of need, and you are the ones in need. If you turn away, He will replace you with another people, and they will not be like you.

surahs.name: Al-Fatḥ (The Victory)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: We have granted you a clear victory,
so that Allah may forgive you your past and future shortcomings, complete His favour upon you, and guide you on a straight path,
and that Allah may support you with mighty support.

He is the One who sent tranquillity into the hearts of the believers so that they may grow stronger in faith alongside their faith. T o Allah belong the forces of the heavens and the earth. Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

So that He may admit believing men and believing women into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, where they will remain, and remove from them their sins. That, in the sight of Allah, is a great success.

And that He may punish the hypocrite men and hypocrite women, and the men and women who associate others with Allah, those who hold evil assumptions about Allah. Upon them is a turn of evil. Allah is angry with them, has cursed them, and has prepared Hell  for them. What an evil destination.
T o Allah belong the forces of the heavens and the earth. Allah is All- Mighty, All-Wise.
We have sent you as a witness, a bearer of good news, and a warner,
so that you may believe in Allah and His Messenger, support him, honour him, and glorify Him morning and evening.

Those who pledge allegiance to you are in fact pledging allegiance to Allah. The hand of Allah is over their hands. Whoever breaks his pledge only breaks it against himself, and whoever fulfils what he has pledged to Allah, He will grant him a great reward.

Those of the Bedouin Arabs who stayed behind will say to you, “Our wealth and our families kept us occupied, so ask forgiveness for us.” They say with their tongues what is not in their hearts. Say, “Who can control anything for you against Allah, if He intends harm for you or intends benefit for you? Allah is fully aware of what you do.”
Rather, you assumed that the Messenger and the believers would never return to their families, and that was made pleasing in your hearts. You held a bad assumption and became a ruined people.
Whoever does not believe in Allah and His Messenger, then We have prepared for the disbelievers a blazing Fire.
T o Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills. Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever- Merciful.

When you set out to take spoils, those who stayed behind will say, “Let us follow you.” They want to change the words of Allah. Say, “You will not follow us. Allah has already said so.” They will say, “No, you are jealous of us.” Rather, they understand very little.

Say to those Bedouin Arabs  who stayed behind, “You will be called against a people of great strength. You will fight them, unless they submit. If you obey, Allah will give you a good reward. But if you turn away as you turned away before, He will punish you with a painful punishment.”

There is no blame upon the blind, nor is there blame upon the lame, nor is there blame upon the sick. Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, He will admit him into Gardens beneath which rivers flow. But whoever turns away, He will punish him with a painful punishment.

Allah was certainly pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you under the tree. He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent down tranquillity upon them and rewarded them with a near victory,
and abundant spoils that they would take. Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

Allah has promised you many spoils that you will take, and He hastened this for you and restrained the hands of people from you, so that it may be a sign for the believers and that He may guide you to a straight path.
And other gains which you have not yet been able to reach, Allah has already encompassed them. Allah has power over everything.
If those who disbelieve had fought you, they would have turned their backs, then they would have found neither protector nor helper.
Such is the way of Allah which has passed before. You will never find any change in the way of Allah.
He is the One who held their hands back from you and your hands back from them within Makkah, after He had given you victory over them. Allah sees what you do.

They are the ones who disbelieved and barred you from the Sacred Mosque and prevented the sacrificial animals from reaching their place of offering. If not for believing men and believing women whom you did not know, whom you might have trampled and thus harm would have come upon you unknowingly, so that Allah may admit into His mercy whom He wills. If they had been clearly separated, We would have punished those who disbelieved among them with a painful punishment.

When those who disbelieved placed arrogance in their hearts, the arrogance of the time of ignorance, Allah sent down His tranquillity upon His Messenger and upon the believers, and bound them to the word of mindfulness. They were most worthy of it and des erving of it. And Allah knows everything.

Indeed, Allah has fulfilled for His Messenger the true vision. You will surely enter the Sacred Mosque, if Allah wills, in safety, with your heads shaved or hair shortened, not fearing. He knew what you did not know, and He granted before that a near victory.
He is the One who sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth to make it prevail over all religion. Allah is sufficient as Witness.

Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah. Those with him are firm against the disbelievers and merciful among themselves. You see them bowing and prostrating, seeking favour from Allah and His pleasure. Their mark is on their faces from the trace of prostration. That is their description in the Tawrāh. And their description in the Injīl is like a seed that sends out its shoot, then strengthens it, so it grows thick and stands upright on its stem, delighting the sowers, so that through them He may anger the di sbelievers. Allah has promised those among them who believe and do what is right forgiveness and a great reward.

surahs.name: Al-Ḥujurāt (The Private Chambers)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: O you who believe, do not put yourselves forward before Allah and His Messenger, and be mindful of Allah. Indeed, Allah is All -Hearing, All - Knowing.
O you who believe, do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet, nor speak loudly to him as you speak loudly to one another, or your deeds may become worthless without you realising.
Those who lower their voices in the presence of the Messenger of Allah are the ones whose hearts Allah has tested for mindfulness. For them is forgiveness and a great reward.
Those who call out to you from behind the private chambers, most of them do not understand.
If they had been patient until you came out to them, it would have been better for them. Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
O you who believe, if a corrupt person comes to you with news, verify it, lest you harm people in ignorance and then regret what you have done.

Know that the Messenger of Allah is among you. If he were to obey you in many matters, you would fall into hardship. But Allah has made faith beloved to you and made it beautiful in your hearts, and He has made disbelief, corruption, and disobedience hateful to you. Those are the rightly guided.
It is a favour and blessing from Allah. Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

If two groups of believers fight, make peace between them. If one of them transgresses against the other, fight the one that transgresses until it returns to the command of Allah. If it returns, then make peace between them with justice and act fairly. Indeed, Allah loves those who act justly.
The believers are but brothers, so make peace between your brothers and be mindful of Allah so that you may receive mercy.

O you who believe, let not one group mock another group, perhaps they may be better than them. Nor let women mock other women, perhaps they may be better than them. Do not defame one another nor insult each other with offensive names. How bad is the name of rebellion after faith. Whoever does not repent, they are the wrongdoers.

O you who believe, avoid much suspicion. Some suspicion is sin. Do not spy, nor backbite one another. W ould one of you like to eat the flesh of his dead brother? You would detest it. Be mindful of Allah. Indeed, Allah is Accepting of repentance, Ever-Merciful.

O people, We created you from a male and a female and made you into peoples and tribes so that you may know one another. The most honoured of you in the sight of Allah is the most mindful of Him. Allah is All - Knowing, All-Aware.

The Bedouin Arabs say, “We believe.” Say, “You have not believed. Rather say, ‘We have submitted,’ for faith has not yet entered your hearts. If you obey Allah and His Messenger, He will not reduce anything from your deeds. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.”
The believers are only those who believe in Allah and His Messenger and then do not doubt, but strive with their wealth and their lives in the path of Allah. Those are the truthful.
Say, “ Are you informing Allah about your religion while Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth? Allah has full knowledge of everything.”
They consider it a favour to you that they have accepted Islām. Say, “Do not count your Islām as a favour to me. Rather, Allah has favoured you by guiding you to faith, if you are truthful.”
Indeed, Allah knows the unseen of the heavens and the earth, and Allah sees what you do.

surahs.name: Qāf
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Qāf. By the glorious Qur’an.
Y et they are astonished that a warner has come to them from among themselves, and the disbelievers say, “This is something strange.
When we have died and become dust, will we return? That is a far return.”
We know what the earth takes away from them, and with Us is a preserving record.
But they denied the truth when it came to them, so they are in a state of confusion.
Have they not looked at the sky above them, how We built it and adorned it, and it has no cracks?
And the earth, We spread it out, set firm mountains upon it, and caused every beautiful kind of plant to grow from it,
as insight and reminder for every servant who turns back to Allah.
And We sent down blessed water from the sky and brought forth with it gardens and grain for harvest,
and tall palm trees with layered clusters,
as provision for the servants. And with it We revived a dead land. Thus will be the coming forth.
Before them, the people of Nūḥ denied, and the companions of ar-Rass and Thamūd,
and ʿĀd, and Firʿawn, and the brothers of Lūṭ,
and the companions of the thicket, and the people of Tubbaʿ. All denied the messengers, so My warning was fulfilled.
Were We then tired by the first creation? Y et they are in doubt about a new creation.
We created the human being, and We know what his soul whispers to him. We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.
When the two receivers receive, seated on the right and on the left,
not a word does he utter except that there is a watcher ready with him.
The agony of death will come with the truth. That is what you tried to escape.
And the T rumpet will be blown. That is the Day of the warning.
Every soul will come with a driver and a witness.
“You were unaware of this, so We have removed your covering from you, and today your sight is sharp.”
And his companion will say, “This is what I have ready.”
“Throw into Hell every stubborn disbeliever,
every preventer of good, transgressor, full of doubt,
who set up another god alongside Allah. So throw him into the severe punishment.”
His companion will say, “Our Lord, I did not make him transgress, but he was far astray.”
He will say, “Do not argue before Me, when I had already sent you the warning.
The word is not changed with Me, and I am not unjust to the servants.”
On the Day We will say to Hell, “ Are you filled?” and it will say, “ Are there any more?”
And the Garden will be brought near to those who were mindful, not far away.
“This is what you were promised, for everyone who turns back and guards himself,
who feared the Most-Merciful unseen and came with a returning heart.
Enter it in peace. That is the Day of lasting life.”
For them there is whatever they wish in it, and with Us is even more.
How many generations before them We destroyed, stronger than them in power, and they travelled through the lands. W as there any escape?
Indeed, in that is a reminder for whoever has a heart or listens while fully present.
We created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six days, and no weariness touched Us.
So be patient over what they say, and glorify your Lord with praise before the rising of the sun and before its setting,
and during the night glorify Him, and after the prostrations.
And listen on the Day when the caller will call from a near place.
The Day they will hear the blast in truth. That is the Day of coming forth.
It is We who give life and cause death, and to Us is the final return.
The Day the earth will split from them as they rush out. That gathering is easy for Us.
We know best what they say, and you are not over them as a compeller. So remind with the Qur’an whoever fears My warning.

surahs.name: Adh-Dhāriyāt (The Scattering Winds)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the winds that scatter far and wide,
and those that carry a heavy load,
and those that glide with ease,
and those that distribute by command,
what you are promised is certainly true,
and the Judgement will surely take place.
By the sky full of pathways,
you are in deeply divided speech.
Turned away from it is whoever is turned away.
Ruined are the fabricators,
those who are lost in confusion and unaware.
They ask, “When is the Day of Judgement?”
The Day they are tried in the Fire:
“T aste your trial. This is what you were demanding.”
The mindful will be among gardens and springs,
receiving what their Lord has given them. Before that, they were doers of good.
They used to sleep only a little of the night,
and before dawn they would seek forgiveness,
and in their wealth there was a rightful share for the one who asked and the one deprived.
On the earth are signs for those who are certain,
and within yourselves. Do you not see?
And in the heaven is your provision and what you are promised.
So by the Lord of the heaven and the earth, it is the truth, just as surely as you speak.
Has the story reached you of the honoured guests of Ibrāhīm?
When they entered upon him and said, “Peace.” He said, “Peace,” and thought, “a people unknown.”
Then he went quietly to his family and brought a fat roasted calf.
He placed it before them and said, “Will you not eat?”
He felt fear within himself from them. They said, “Do not fear,” and they gave him good news of a knowledgeable boy.
Then his wife came forward with a cry, struck her face, and said, “ An old woman, barren!”
They said, “Thus has your Lord said. He is the All -Wise, the All - Knowing.”
He said, “What is your mission, O messengers?”
They said, “We have been sent to a criminal people,
to send down upon them stones of clay,
marked by your Lord for the excessive ones.”
So We brought out those who were in it among the believers,
but We found in it only one household of those who had submitted.
And We left in it a sign for those who fear the painful punishment.
And in Mūsā, when We sent him to Firʿawn with clear authority,
but he turned away with his power and said, “ A magician or a madman.”
So We seized him and his soldiers and cast them into the sea, and he was blameworthy.
And in ʿĀd, when We sent against them the barren wind,
leaving nothing it came upon without turning it into ruin.
And in Thamūd, when it was said to them, “Enjoy yourselves for a time,”
but they were rebellious against the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on.
They were not able to rise up, nor could they defend themselves.
And the people of Nūḥ before that. Indeed, they were a rebellious people.
And the heaven, We built it with strength, and We are surely expanding it.
And the earth, We spread it out. How excellent are We in preparing it.
And of everything We created pairs, so that you may reflect.
So flee to Allah. I am to you from Him a clear warner.
And do not set up another god alongside Allah. I am to you from Him a clear warner.
Likewise, no messenger came to those before them without them saying, “ A magician or a madman.”
Did they pass this down to one another? Rather, they are a transgressing people.
So turn away from them, for you are not to be blamed.
And remind, for the reminder benefits the believers.
I did not create jinn and humankind except to worship Me.
I do not seek provision from them, nor do I want them to feed Me.
Indeed, Allah is the Provider, the Possessor of strength, the Firm.
For those who have wronged is a share like the share of their companions, so let them not rush Me.
So woe to those who disbelieve from their Day which they are promised.

surahs.name: Aṭ-Ṭūr (The Mount)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the Mount,
and by a Book inscribed,
on parchment spread open,
and by the inhabited House,
and by the raised ceiling,
and by the sea set ablaze,
the punishment of your Lord will surely take place.
There is nothing to repel it.
On the Day when the sky will shake violently,
and the mountains will move swiftly.
So woe that Day to those who deny,
those who engage in pointless talk,
the Day they will be pushed into the Fire of Hell forcefully:
“This is the Fire which you used to deny.
Is this magic, or do you not see?
Enter it and burn in it. It is all the same whether you endure patiently or not endure. You are only being repaid for what you used to do.”
The mindful will be in gardens and delight,
enjoying what their Lord has given them, and their Lord will shield them from the punishment of the Fire.
“Eat and drink with satisfaction for what you used to do.”
Reclining on couches arranged in rows, and We will pair them with companions of wide, beautiful eyes.

And those who believed, and whose descendants followed them in faith, We will join their descendants with them, and We will not reduce anything from their deeds. Every person is held by what he earned.
And We will supply them with fruit and meat from whatever they desire.
They will pass among themselves a cup in which there is no empty talk and no sin.
And there will circulate among them young servants of theirs, like protected pearls.
And they will turn to one another, asking each other.
They will say, “Before this, we used to be fearful among our families.
But Allah was gracious to us and protected us from the punishment of the scorching wind.
We used to call upon Him before. He is the Most -Kind, the Ever- Merciful.”
So remind, for by the favour of your Lord you are not a fortune teller nor a madman.
Or do they say, “ A poet for whom we await the passing of fate?”
Say, “W ait, for I too am with you among those who wait.”
Or do their minds command them to this, or are they a people who overstep all bounds?
Or do they say, “He made it up”? Rather, they do not believe.
Then let them produce a statement like it, if they are truthful.
Or were they created from nothing, or are they the creators?
Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather, they are not certain.
Or do they possess the treasures of your Lord, or are they in control?
Or do they have a stairway by which they listen? Then let their listener bring clear proof.
Or does He have daughters while you have sons?
Or do you ask them for reward, so they are weighed down by debt?
Or do they have knowledge of the unseen, so they write it down?
Or do they intend a plan? But those who disbelieve, they are the ones being planned against.
Or do they have a god other than Allah? Glorified is Allah above what they associate.
Even if they were to see a piece of the sky falling, they would say, “Clouds piled up.”
So leave them until they meet their Day in which they will be struck down,
the Day when their scheme will not benefit them at all, nor will they be helped.
And indeed, for those who have wronged there is a punishment before that, but most of them do not know.
So be patient under the judgement of your Lord, for you are under Our watch. And glorify your Lord with praise when you rise,
and in part of the night glorify Him, and at the fading of the stars.

surahs.name: An-Najm (The Star)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the star when it descends,
your companion has not strayed, nor has he erred.
He does not speak from his own desire.
It is nothing but revelation sent down,
taught to him by one intense in strength,
full of power, who rose to his true form,
while he was on the highest horizon.
Then he drew near and came closer,
until he was two bow lengths away, or even nearer.
And he revealed to His servant what He revealed.
The heart did not lie about what it saw.
Will you then argue with him over what he saw?
He saw him on another descent,
near the Lote T ree of the furthest boundary,
near it is the Garden of Refuge,
when the Lote T ree was covered by what covered it.
The sight did not swerve, nor did it overstep.
He certainly saw some of the greatest signs of his Lord.
Have you considered al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā,
and Manāt, the third, the other one?
Is the male for you and the female for Him?
That would then be an unfair division.

They are nothing but names you have named, you and your fathers, for which Allah has sent down no authority. They follow only assumption and what their souls desire, even though guidance has come to them from their Lord.
Or does the human being have whatever he wishes?
T o Allah belongs the Hereafter and this life.
How many angels are in the heavens whose intercession does not benefit at all, except after Allah permits for whom He wills and is pleased with.
Those who do not believe in the Hereafter give the angels female names.
They have no knowledge of that. They follow only assumption, and assumption does not avail against the truth in any way.
So turn away from whoever turns away from Our reminder and desires only the life of this world.
That is the extent of their knowledge. Indeed, Your Lord knows best who has strayed from His path, and He knows best who is guided.
T o Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, so that He may repay those who do evil for what they have done, and reward those who do good with the best.

Those who avoid the major sins and immoral acts, except for minor slips, your Lord is vast in forgiveness. He knows you best since He brought you from the earth and when you were unborn in your mothers’ wombs. So do not claim yourselves to be pure. He knows best who is mindful.
Have you seen the one who turns away,
and gives a little, then withholds?
Does he have knowledge of the unseen, so he sees?
Or has he not been informed of what was in the scrolls of Mūsā,
and Ibrāhīm, who fulfilled his duty:
that no bearer of burden will carry the burden of another,
and that the human being will have only what he strives for,
and that his effort will be seen,
then he will be repaid in full,
and that to your Lord is the final end,
and that He makes one laugh and makes one weep,
and that He gives death and gives life,
and that He created the two mates, male and female,
from a drop when it is emitted,
and that upon Him is the other creation,
and that He enriches and gives provision,
and that He is the Lord of Sirius,
and that He destroyed the former ʿĀd,
and Thamūd, and He left none remaining,
and the people of Nūḥ before that. They were indeed more unjust and more transgressive.
And the overturned towns, He brought them down,
and covered them with what covered them.
So which of the favours of your Lord do you doubt?
This is a warner like the former warners.
The approaching event has drawn near.
None besides Allah can remove it.
Do you then marvel at this speech,
and laugh and not weep,
while you are heedless?
So prostrate to Allah and worship Him.

surahs.name: Al-Qamar (The Moon)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: The Hour has drawn near, and the moon has split.
Y et whenever they see a sign, they turn away and say, “Ongoing magic.”
They denied and followed their own desires, but every matter is settled.
There has already come to them reports containing restraint,
profound wisdom, yet warnings do not benefit.
So turn away from them. On the Day the caller calls to something terrible,
their eyes lowered, they will come out from the graves like scattered locusts,
rushing toward the caller. The disbelievers will say, “This is a difficult Day.”
Before them, the people of Nūḥ denied. They denied Our servant and said, “ A madman,” and he was rebuked.
So he called upon his Lord, “I am overpowered, so help.”
So We opened the gates of the sky with pouring water,
and caused the earth to burst with springs, and the waters met for a matter already decreed.
And We carried him upon planks and nails,
sailing under Our watch as a reward for the one who had been denied.
And We left it as a sign, so is there anyone who will take heed?
So how were My punishment and My warnings?
We have made the Qur’an easy for remembrance, so is there anyone who will reflect?
ʿĀd denied, so how were My punishment and My warnings?
We sent against them a screaming wind on a day of unending misfortune,
pulling people away as if they were uprooted trunks of palm trees.
So how were My punishment and My warnings?
We have made the Qur’an easy for remembrance, so is there anyone who will reflect?
Thamūd denied the warnings.
They said, “Shall we follow one man from among us? Then we would be in error and madness.
Has the reminder been sent down to him alone from among us? Rather, he is a lying braggart.”
T omorrow they will know who is the lying braggart.
We are sending the she-camel as a test for them, so watch them and be patient.
Inform them that the water is to be shared between them, each share attended in turn.
But they called their companion, and he seized her and slaughtered her.
So how were My punishment and My warnings?
We sent against them a single blast, and they became like dry twigs trampled in an animal pen.
We have made the Qur’an easy for remembrance, so is there anyone who will reflect?
The people of Lūṭ denied the warnings.
We sent upon them a storm of stones, except for the family of Lūṭ. We saved them before dawn,
as a favour from Us. That is how We reward those who are grateful.
He had warned them of Our grasp, but they disputed the warnings.
They even demanded his guests from him, so We blinded their eyes: “T aste My punishment and My warnings.”
And a settled punishment came upon them at dawn.
“T aste My punishment and My warnings.”
We have made the Qur’an easy for remembrance, so is there anyone who will reflect?
The warnings also came to the people of Firʿawn.
They denied all Our signs, so We seized them with the grip of the All- Mighty, the All-Powerful.
Are your disbelievers better than those, or do you have immunity in the scriptures?
Or do they say, “We are a united group who will prevail”?
Their group will be defeated, and they will turn their backs.
But the Hour is their appointed time, and the Hour is more disastrous and more bitter.
Indeed, the criminals are in error and madness.
The Day they are dragged into the Fire on their faces: “T aste the touch of Hell.”
Indeed, We created everything with precise measure.
Our command is only one word, like the blink of an eye.
We have destroyed those like you, so is there anyone who will take heed?
Everything they did is recorded in the scrolls.
Every small and great thing is written down.
Indeed, the mindful will be in gardens and rivers,
in a seat of truth, with a Sovereign of absolute power.

surahs.name: Ar-Raḥmān (The Most-Merciful)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: The Most-Merciful.
He taught the Qur’an.
He created the human.
He taught him clear expression.
The sun and the moon move with precise measure.
The stars and the trees bow in submission.
He raised the sky high and set the balance,
so that you do not transgress the balance.
Uphold the weight with fairness and do not fall short in the balance.
And the earth, He laid it out for living beings.
In it are fruits, and date palms with sheathed clusters,
and grain with husk, and fragrant plants.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
He created the human from dried clay like fired pottery,
and He created the jinn from a smokeless flame of fire.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
Lord of the two sunrises and Lord of the two sunsets.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
He released the two seas, meeting side by side.
Between them is a barrier they do not cross.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
From both of them come pearls and coral.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
His are the ships, raised high on the sea like mountains.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
Everyone on the earth will perish,
and the Face of your Lord will remain, full of Majesty and Honour.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
All who are in the heavens and the earth ask of Him. Every day He is engaged in a matter.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
We will attend to you fully, O two kinds of beings.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
O company of jinn and humankind, if you can pass beyond the boundaries of the heavens and the earth, then pass. You will not pass except with authority.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
A flame of fire and molten smoke will be sent against you, and you will not be able to defend yourselves.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
When the heavens are torn open and becomes red like burning oil,
so which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
On that Day neither human nor jinn will be questioned about their sin.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
The criminals will be recognised by their marks, and they will be seized by the forelocks and the feet.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
This is Hell which the criminals denied.
They will circle between it and scalding water at its fiercest heat.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
But for the one who stood in awe of standing before his Lord, there will be two gardens.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
Both full of spreading branches.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
In both are two flowing springs.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
In both are pairs of every fruit.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
Reclining on furnishings lined with rich brocade, and the fruits of the two gardens hanging close within reach.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
In them are those who lower their gaze, untouched before them by any human or jinn.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
As though they were rubies and coral.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
Is the reward of excellence anything other than excellence?
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
And beyond those two are two other gardens.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
Deep green in colour.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
In them are two springs, constantly flowing.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
In them are fruits, date palms, and pomegranates.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
In them are women of goodness and beauty.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
Pure companions kept reserved in pavilions.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
Untouched before them by any human or jinn.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
Reclining on green cushions and splendid carpets.
So which of your Lord’s blessings will you both deny?
Blessed is the Name of your Lord, full of Majesty and Honour.

surahs.name: Al-Wāqiʿah (The Inevitable Event)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: When the Inevitable Event occurs,
there will be no denying its happening.
It will lower some and raise others.
When the earth is shaken with a violent shaking,
and the mountains are shattered completely,
becoming scattered dust,
and you are divided into three groups:
those of the right, what of those of the right?
and those of the left, what of those of the left?
and those foremost, the foremost.
They are the ones brought near,
in gardens of lasting bliss.
A large number from the earlier generations,
and a few from the later ones.
On woven couches, adorned,
reclining on them, facing one another.
Youths of everlasting freshness will move among them,
with cups, pitchers, and a drink flowing from a spring,
from which they will feel no headache, nor will they lose their senses,
and fruits of whatever they choose,
and meat of any bird they desire.
And companions with wide, beautiful eyes,
like protected pearls,
a reward for what they used to do.
They will hear in it no empty talk and no sinful speech,
only words of peace, peace.
And those of the right, what of those of the right?
They will be among thornless lote trees,
and layered banana trees,
and extended shade,
and flowing water,
and abundant fruit,
neither cut off nor forbidden,
and raised couches.
We have brought them into being in a new creation,
and made them pure and devoted,
equal in age,
for those of the right.
A large number from the earlier generations,
and a large number from the later ones.
And those of the left, what of those of the left?
They will be in scorching wind and boiling water,
and in the shade of black smoke,
neither cool nor refreshing.
Before that, they lived in comfort,
and they persisted in grave wrongdoing,
and they used to say, “When we die and become dust and bones, will we truly be raised again?
And our earlier forefathers as well?”
Say, “The earlier and the later
will surely be gathered at the appointed time of a known Day.”
Then you who went astray and denied,
you will surely eat from a tree of Zaqqūm,
filling your stomachs with it,
then drinking over it boiling water,
drinking as thirsty camels drink.
That will be their hospitality on the Day of Judgement.
We created you, so why do you not accept the truth?
Have you considered what you release?
Is it you who create it, or are We the Creator?
We have set death among you, and We are never outmatched,
so that We may replace you with your likes and bring you forth in a form you do not know.
You already know the first creation, so why do you not reflect?
Have you considered what you sow?
Is it you who make it grow, or are We the One who makes it grow?
If We willed, We could turn it into dry debris, and you would remain in shock,
saying, “We are burdened with loss.
Rather, we are deprived.”
Have you considered the water you drink?
Is it you who send it down from the clouds, or are We the One who sends it down?
If We willed, We could make it bitter, so why are you not grateful?
Have you considered the fire you kindle?
Is it you who produced its tree, or are We the Producer?
We made it a reminder and a provision for travellers in the wilderness.
So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most-Great.
No, I swear by the setting of the stars,
and it is indeed a mighty oath, if only you knew,
this is indeed a noble Qur’an,
in a protected Record,
none touch it except those purified,
a revelation sent down from the Lord of all worlds.
Do you then treat this speech lightly,
and make your denial your share of provision?
So why, when the soul reaches the throat,
and you are at that moment watching,
while We are nearer to him than you, yet you do not see,
why then, if you are not under authority,
do you not return it, if you are truthful?
Then if he is among those brought near,
there is rest, provision, and a garden of lasting bliss.
And if he is among those of the right,
then peace for you, from those of the right.
But if he is among the deniers who went astray,
then a hospitality of boiling water,
and burning in Hellfire.
This is the certain truth.
So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most-Great.

surahs.name: Al-Ḥadīd (Iron)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah. He is the All - Mighty, the All-Wise.
His is the dominion of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and causes death. He has power over everything.
He is the First and the Last, the Manifest and the Hidden. He has full knowledge of everything.

He is the One who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then established Himself over the Throne. He knows what enters the earth and what comes out of it, what descends from the sky and what rises into it. He is with you wherever you are, and Allah sees all that you do.
His is the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and to Allah all matters return.
He merges the night into the day and merges the day into the night. He knows what lies within the hearts.
Believe in Allah and His Messenger, and spend from what He has entrusted to you. Those of you who believe and spend will have a great reward.
What is the matter with you that you do not believe in Allah, when the Messenger calls you to believe in your Lord, and He has already taken your covenant, if you are truly believers?
He is the One who sends down clear revelations to His servant to bring you out from darkness into light. Indeed, Allah is Most-Gentle and Ever- Merciful towards you.

What is the matter with you that you do not spend in the way of Allah, when to Allah belongs the inheritance of the heavens and the earth? Those who spent and fought before the victory are not equal to those who did so afterwards. They are greater in rank than those who spent and fought later. Y et Allah has promised good to all. Allah is fully aware of what you do.
Who is it that will lend to Allah a good loan, so He may multiply it for him and grant him a generous reward?

On the Day you will see believing men and believing women, their light running before them and on their right: “Good news for you today, gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever.” That is the supreme success.

On that Day the hypocrite men and hypocrite women will say to those who believed, “W ait for us so we may take from your light.” It will be said, “Go back behind you and seek light.” Then a wall will be placed between them with a gate; inside it is mercy, and outside it, facing them, is punishment.

They will call out to them, “Were we not with you?” They will reply, “Y es, but you put yourselves to trial, you hesitated, you doubted, and false hopes deceived you until the command of Allah came, and the deceiver deceived you about Allah.”
So today no ransom will be taken from you nor from those who disbelieved. Your refuge is the Fire; it is your master. What a miserable destination.

Has the time not come for those who believe that their hearts should humble themselves to the remembrance of Allah and what has come down of the truth, and not be like those who were given the Scripture before, whose time was prolonged so their hearts har dened, and many of them are rebellious?
Know that Allah gives life to the earth after its death. We have made the signs clear to you so that you may understand.
Indeed, the men and women who give in charity and lend to Allah a good loan will have it multiplied for them, and they will have a generous reward.

Those who believe in Allah and His Messengers, they are the truthful and the witnesses before their Lord. They will have their reward and their light. But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs, they are the companions of Hellfire.

Know that the life of this world is only play, amusemnet, adornment, boasting among you, and competition in wealth and children. It is like rain whose growth pleases the farmers; then it dries and you see it turn yellow, then it becomes debris. In the Hereafter there is severe punishment and forgiveness from Allah and His pleasure. The life of this world is nothing but a fleeting enjoyment.

Race towards forgiveness from your Lord and a garden as vast as the heavens and the earth, prepared for those who believe in Allah and His Messengers. That is the favour of Allah; He gives it to whom He wills. Allah is the Possessor of immense favour.
No disaster strikes the earth or yourselves except that it is in a record before We bring it into being. That is easy for Allah.
So that you do not despair over what has escaped you, nor exult over what He has given you. Allah does not love any arrogant boaster,
Those who are stingy and command others to stinginess. Whoever turns away, then Allah is free of all need, the All-Praiseworthy.

We sent Our Messengers with clear proofs and sent down with them the Book and the balance so that people may uphold justice. And We sent down iron, in which there is great strength and benefits for people, and so that Allah may make known who supports Him  and His Messengers unseen. Indeed, Allah is All-Strong and All-Mighty.
We sent Nūḥ and Ibrāhīm, and placed prophethood and the Book among their descendants. Some of them were guided, but many were rebellious.

Then We sent after them Our Messengers, and followed them with ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, and We gave him the Injīl. We placed compassion and mercy in the hearts of those who followed him. But monasticism, they invented it; We did not prescribe it for them, except seeking the pleasure of Allah, yet they did not observe it as it should have been observed. So We gave those who believed among them their reward, but many of them are rebellious.

O you who believe, be mindful of Allah and believe in His Messenger. He will grant you a double share of His mercy, give you a light by which you will walk, and forgive you. Allah is Most-Forgiving and Ever-Merciful.

So that the People of the Scripture may know that they have no control over any of Allah’s favour, and that all favour is in the hand of Allah. He gives it to whom He wills. Allah is the Possessor of immense favour.

surahs.name: Al-Mujādilah (The Pleading W oman)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Allah has heard the words of the woman who argued with you about her husband and complained to Allah. Allah hears your exchange. Allah is All - Hearing, All-Seeing.

Those among you who declare their wives unlawful to themselves are not their mothers. Their mothers are only those who gave them birth. What they say is a wrongful and false statement. Y et Allah is Most-Pardoning, Most- Forgiving.

Those who declare their wives unlawful in this way, then go back on what they said, must free a slave before they touch one another. This is what you are instructed to do. Allah is fully aware of what you do.

Whoever cannot find one must fast for two consecutive months before they touch one another. And whoever is unable must feed sixty needy people. This is so that you may truly believe in Allah and His Messenger. These are the limits set by Allah. And for the disbelievers is a painful punishment.
Those who oppose Allah and His Messenger will be humiliated just as those before them were humiliated. We have already sent down clear verses. And for the disbelievers is a humiliating punishment.
On the Day Allah raises them all, He will inform them of what they did. Allah has counted it, though they have forgotten it. Allah is Witness over everything.

Do you not see that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth? There is no private conversation of three except that He is their fourth, nor of five except that He is their sixth, nor fewer nor more than that except that He is wi th them wherever they are. Then He will inform them of what they did on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed, Allah has full knowledge of everything.

Have you not seen those who were forbidden from secret talks, yet they return to what they were forbidden from? They whisper together about sin, hostility, and disobedience to the Messenger. And when they come to you, they greet you in a way Allah does no t greet you, and they say within themselves, “Why does Allah not punish us for what we say?” Hell is enough for them. They will burn in it. What a miserable destination.

O you who believe, when you speak privately, do not speak about sin, hostility, and disobedience to the Messenger. Speak about righteousness and mindfulness of Allah. Be mindful of Allah, to whom you will be gathered.
Secret talks are only from Shay ṭān, so that he may grieve those who believe. But he cannot harm them at all except by Allah’s permission. So let the believers place their trust in Allah.

O you who believe, when it is said to you, “Make room in gatherings,” then make room. Allah will make room for you. And when it is said, “Rise,” then rise. Allah will raise those of you who believe and those given knowledge in rank. Allah is fully aware of what you do.

O you who believe, when you wish to speak privately with the Messenger, offer a charity before your private conversation. That is better for you and purer. But if you cannot find anything to give, then Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

Are you afraid to offer charity before your private conversation? If you do not do so and Allah turns to you, then uphold the prayer, give zakah, and obey Allah and His Messenger. Allah is fully aware of what you do.
Have you not seen those who take as allies a people with whom Allah is angry? They are neither of you nor of them. They swear to falsehood while they know.
Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment. How evil is what they have been doing.
They use their oaths as a shield, and so they turn others away from the path of Allah. For them is a humiliating punishment.
Neither their wealth nor their children will benefit them at all against Allah. They are the people of the Fire. They will remain in it forever.
On the Day Allah raises them all, they will swear to Him as they swear to you, thinking they stand on something. They are indeed the liars.
Shayṭān has taken hold of them and made them forget the remembrance of Allah. They are the party of Shayṭān. Indeed, the party of Shayṭān are the losers.
Those who oppose Allah and His Messenger are among the most humiliated.
Allah has written, “I will surely prevail, I and My messengers.” Allah is All-Strong, All-Mighty.

You will not find a people who believe in Allah and the Last Day loving those who oppose Allah and His Messenger, even if they are their fathers, their sons, their brothers, or their relatives. Those are the ones in whose hearts He has written faith and strengthened them with a spirit from Him. He will admit them into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining there forever. Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him. They are the party of Allah. Indeed, the party of Allah are the successful.

surahs.name: Al-Ḥashr (The Gathering)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.
