verses.english: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah. He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

He is the One who drove out those who disbelieved among the People of the Scripture from their homes at the first gathering. You did not think they would leave, and they thought their fortresses would protect them from Allah. But Allah came upon them from  where they did not expect and cast fear into their hearts. They ruined their own houses with their own hands and the hands of the believers. So take heed, you who have insight.
Had Allah not decreed exile for them, He would have punished them in this world. And in the Hereafter they will have the punishment of the Fire.
That is because they opposed Allah and His Messenger. Whoever opposes Allah, then Allah is severe in punishment.
Whatever palm trees you cut down or left standing on their roots, it was by Allah’s permission, so that He might disgrace the rebellious.
Whatever Allah restored to His Messenger from them, you did not spur for it any horses or camels, but Allah gives His messengers authority over whom He wills. Allah has power over everything.

Whatever Allah restored to His Messenger from the people of the towns belongs to Allah, the Messenger, close relatives, orphans, the needy, and the traveller, so that it does not circulate only among the wealthy among you. Whatever the Messenger gives you, take it. Whatever he forbids you from, leave it. Be mindful of Allah. Allah is severe in punishment.
It is for the poor emigrants who were driven out from their homes and their wealth, seeking favour from Allah and His pleasure, and supporting Allah and His Messenger. They are the truthful.

And those who settled in the home and in faith before them love those who emigrated to them. They find no desire in their hearts for what the emigrants were given, and they prefer them over themselves even if they are in need. Whoever is protected from th e greed of his own soul, they are the successful.

And those who came after them say, “Our Lord, forgive us and our brothers who preceded us in faith. Do not place in our hearts any resentment towards those who believe. Our Lord, You are Most-Gentle, Ever-Merciful.”

Have you not seen those who act hypocritically, saying to their brothers among the People of the Scripture who disbelieve, “If you are driven out, we will surely leave with you, and we will never obey anyone against you. And if you are fought, we will surely support you”? But Allah bears witness that they are liars.

If they are driven out, they will not leave with them. And if they are fought, they will not support them. And even if they were to support them, they would turn their backs, then they would not be helped.
You strike greater fear into their hearts than Allah, because they are a people who do not understand.

They will not fight you together except within fortified towns or from behind walls. Their hostility among themselves is intense. You think they are united, but their hearts are divided. That is because they are a people who do not reason.
Like those shortly before them, they tasted the consequence of their actions. And for them is a painful punishment.
Like Shay ṭān when he says to a person, “Disbelieve.” But when he disbelieves, he says, “I am free of you. I fear Allah, Lord of all worlds.”
So the end of both of them will be that they are in the Fire, remaining in it forever. That is the reward of the wrongdoers.
O you who believe, be mindful of Allah. Let every soul look to what it has sent forward for tomorrow. Be mindful of Allah. Allah is fully aware of what you do.
Do not be like those who forgot Allah, so He made them forget themselves. They are the rebellious.
The people of the Fire and the people of the Garden are not equal. The people of the Garden are the successful.
If We had sent down this Qur’an upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled and split apart from the fear of Allah. These examples We present to people so that they may reflect.
He is Allah, there is no god except Him, Knower of the unseen and the seen. He is the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

He is Allah, there is no god except Him, the King, the Most -Pure, the Source of Peace, the Giver of Security, the Guardian, the Mighty, the Compeller, the Supremely Great. Exalted is Allah above what they associate with Him.
He is Allah, the Creator, the Originator, the Fashioner. T o Him belong the most beautiful names. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Him. He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

O you who believe, do not take My enemies and your enemies as close allies, offering them affection while they have disbelieved in the truth that has come to you. They drove out the Messenger and yourselves because you believe in Allah, your Lord. If you have gone out striving in My cause and seeking My pleasure, do not show them secret affection. I fully know what you conceal and what you declare. Whoever among you does that has strayed from the straight path.
If they gain the upper hand over you, they will treat you as enemies. They will stretch out their hands and their tongues against you with harm, and they would love for you to disbelieve.
Your relatives and your children will not benefit you on the Day of Resurrection. He will judge between you. Allah sees what you do.

You have had a fine example in Ibrāhīm and those with him when they said to their people, “We are free of you and of what you worship besides Allah. We disown you, and hostility and hatred have appeared between us and you forever, until you believe in Allah alone,” except for the words of Ibrāhīm to his father, “I will ask forgiveness for you, though I cannot avail you at all against Allah.” “Our Lord, in You we place our trust, to You we turn, and to You is the final return.”
“Our Lord, do not make us a test for those who disbelieve. Forgive us, our Lord. You are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.”
There has certainly been in them a good example for whoever hopes in Allah and the Last Day. But whoever turns away, then Allah is free of all need, the All-Praiseworthy.
It may be that Allah will place affection between you and those you now consider enemies. Allah is fully capable. Allah is Most -Forgiving, Ever - Merciful.
Allah does not forbid you from being kind and just towards those who did not fight you because of your faith nor drive you from your homes. Allah loves those who act justly.
Allah only forbids you from taking as allies those who fought you because of your faith, drove you from your homes, or supported your expulsion. Whoever takes them as allies, they are the wrongdoers.

O you who believe, when believing women come to you as emigrants, examine them. Allah knows best about their faith. If you know them to be believers, do not return them to the disbelievers. They are not lawful for them, nor are those men lawful for them. Give the disbelievers what they had spent. There is no blame on you if you marry them once you have given them their due dowry. Do not hold on to marriages with disbelieving women. Ask for what you have spent, and let them ask for what they have spent. That is the judgement of Allah; He judges between you. Allah is All-Knowing, All- Wise.

If any of your wives leave you to join the disbelievers, and you later obtain compensation, then give to those whose wives left the equivalent of what they had spent. Be mindful of Allah, in whom you believe.

O Prophet, when believing women come to you pledging that they will not associate anything with Allah, will not steal, will not commit adultery, will not kill their children, will not bring a false claim they have invented between their hands and feet, and will not disobey you in what is right, then accept their pledge and ask Allah to forgive them. Allah is Most -Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
O you who believe, do not take as allies a people with whom Allah is angry. They have despaired of the Hereafter just as the disbelievers have despaired of those in the graves.

surahs.name: Aṣ-Ṣaff (The Row)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah. He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
O you who believe, why do you say what you do not do?
It is deeply hateful in the sight of Allah that you say what you do not do.
Allah loves those who fight in His cause in a solid row, as though they are a firmly joined structure.

And when Mūsā said to his people, “My people, why do you hurt me when you know that I am Allah’s messenger to you?” So when they turned away, Allah turned their hearts away. Allah does not guide the rebellious.

And when ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, said, “O Children of Isrāʾīl, I am Allah’s messenger to you, confirming what came before me of the Tawrāh and giving good news of a messenger who will come after me whose name is Aḥmad.” But when he came to them with clear proofs, they said, “This is obvious magic.”
Who does greater wrong than one who invents a lie about Allah while he is being called to Islām? Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
They want to extinguish the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah will perfect His light, even if the disbelievers hate it.
He is the One who sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth to make it prevail over all religion, even if the associators dislike it.
O you who believe, shall I direct you to a trade that will save you from a painful punishment?
Believe in Allah and His Messenger, and strive in the cause of Allah with your wealth and your lives. That is better for you, if only you knew.
He will forgive your sins and admit you into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, and into fine homes in Gardens of lasting stay. That is the great success.
And another thing you love: help from Allah and a near victory. Give good news to the believers.

O you who believe, be helpers of Allah, as ʿĪsā, son of Maryam, said to the disciples, “Who are my helpers for Allah?” The disciples said, “We are the helpers of Allah.” So a group of the Children of Isrāʾīl believed and another group disbelieved. We supported those who believed against their enemy, and they became dominant.

surahs.name: Al-Jumuʿah (Friday Prayer)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah, the King, the Most-Pure, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

He is the One who raised among the unlettered a Messenger from among themselves, reciting to them His verses, purifying them, and teaching them the Book and wisdom, though before that they were clearly astray.
And also others among them who have not yet joined them. He is the All- Mighty, the All-Wise.
That is the favour of Allah. He grants it to whom He wills. Allah is the Possessor of immense favour.

The example of those who were entrusted with the Tawrāh but then failed to carry it is like a donkey carrying books. Miserable is the example of people who deny Allah’s signs. Allah does not guide wrongdoing people.
Say, “O you who follow the Jewish way, if you claim that you alone are Allah’s close allies, excluding all others, then wish for death, if you are truthful.”
But they will never wish for it because of what their hands have sent ahead. Allah knows the wrongdoers.
Say, “The death you try to escape will surely meet you. Then you will be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the seen, and He will inform you of what you used to do.”
O you who believe, when the call is made for prayer on Friday, then move swiftly to the remembrance of Allah and leave trade aside. That is better for you, if only you knew.
Once the prayer has concluded, then spread out through the land and seek from Allah’s bounty, and remember Allah often so that you may succeed.
But when they see trade or amusement, they rush towards it and leave you standing. Say, “What is with Allah is better than amusement and trade, and Allah is the Best of providers.”

surahs.name: Al-Munāfiqūn (The Hypocrites)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: When the hypocrites come to you, they say, “We bear witness that you are indeed the Messenger of Allah.” Allah knows that you are His Messenger, and Allah bears witness that the hypocrites are liars.
They use their oaths as a shield and so they turn people away from the path of Allah. What they have been doing is evil.
That is because they believed, then disbelieved, so their hearts were sealed and they do not understand.

When you see them, their appearance impresses you, and if they speak, you listen to their words. Y et they are like propped up pieces of timber. They think every shout is against them. They are the enemy, so beware of them. May Allah deal with them. How they are turned away.
When it is said to them, “Come, the Messenger of Allah will ask forgiveness for you,” they turn their heads aside, and you see them turning away in arrogance.
It is the same for them whether you ask forgiveness for them or do not ask forgiveness for them. Allah will never forgive them. Indeed, Allah does not guide the rebellious people.

They are the ones who say, “Do not spend on those who are with the Messenger of Allah until they disperse.” Y et to Allah belong the treasures of the heavens and the earth, but the hypocrites do not understand.
They say, “If we return to the city, the more honoured will surely drive out the weaker.” Y et honour belongs to Allah, His Messenger, and the believers, but the hypocrites do not know.
O you who believe, do not let your wealth or your children distract you from the remembrance of Allah. Whoever does that, they are the losers.
And spend from what We have provided you before death comes to one of you and he says, “My Lord, if only You would delay me for a short time, I would give in charity and be among the righteous.”
But Allah will never delay a soul when its appointed time has come. Allah is fully aware of what you do.

surahs.name: At-Taghābun (Mutual Loss and Gain)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah. T o Him belongs all dominion and all praise. He has power over everything.
He is the One who created you. Among you are those who disbelieve and among you are those who believe. Allah sees what you do.
He created the heavens and the earth in truth, shaped you and perfected your forms, and to Him is the final return.
He knows what is in the heavens and the earth. He knows what you conceal and what you reveal. Allah knows what lies within the hearts.
Has the news not reached you of those who disbelieved before? They tasted the consequence of their actions, and for them is a painful punishment.

That was because their messengers came to them with clear proofs, yet they said, “ Are humans to guide us?” So they disbelieved and turned away. Allah had no need of them. Allah is Free of all need, All-Praiseworthy.
Those who disbelieve claim that they will never be raised again. Say, “Y es indeed, by my Lord, you will surely be raised, then you will be informed of what you did. That is easy for Allah.”
So believe in Allah and His Messenger and in the light We have sent down. Allah is fully aware of what you do.

On the Day He gathers you for the Day of Gathering, that is the Day of mutual loss and gain. Whoever believes in Allah and does what is right, He will remove their sins and admit them into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining there forever. That is the great success.
But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs, they are the people of the Fire, remaining there forever. What a miserable destination.
No calamity strikes except by Allah’s permission. Whoever believes in Allah, He guides their heart. Allah has full knowledge of everything.
Obey Allah and obey the Messenger. But if you turn away, then upon Our Messenger is only to convey the message clearly.
Allah, there is no god except Him. In Allah let the believers place their trust.
O you who believe, among your spouses and your children are some who are enemies to you, so beware of them. But if you pardon, overlook, and forgive, then Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
Your wealth and your children are only a test, and with Allah is a great reward.
So be mindful of Allah as much as you are able. Listen and obey, and spend for your own good. Whoever is protected from the greed of their own soul, they are the successful.
If you lend to Allah a good loan, He will multiply it for you and forgive you. Allah is Most-Appreciative, Most-Forbearing.
Knower of the unseen and the seen, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

surahs.name: Aṭ-Ṭalāq (Divorce)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: O Prophet, when you divorce women, divorce them for their prescribed waiting period, and count the waiting period carefully. Be mindful of Allah, your Lord. Do not turn them out of their homes, nor should they leave, unless they commit a clear act of indecency. These are the limits set by Allah. Whoever oversteps the limits set by Allah has wronged himself. You do not know, perhaps Allah will bring about something new after that.

When they reach the end of their waiting period, either retain them in a fair manner or part from them in a fair manner. Call two just men from among you as witnesses, and uphold the testimony for Allah. That is instructed to whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day. Whoever is mindful of Allah, He will make for him a way out,
and provide for him from where he does not expect. Whoever places his trust in Allah, He is enough for him. Allah carries out His command. Allah has set a measure for everything.

As for your women who no longer expect menstruation, if you are unsure, their waiting period is three months, and the same applies to those who have not menstruated. For those who are pregnant, their term is until they deliver their burden. Whoever is mindful of Allah, He will make his matter easy for him.
That is the command of Allah which He has sent down to you. Whoever is mindful of Allah, He will remove his sins from him and increase his reward.

Let them live where you live, according to your means. Do not harm them in order to make things difficult for them. If they are pregnant, then spend on them until they deliver their burden. If they breastfeed your children for you, then give them their reward and consult together in a proper manner. If you make things difficult for one another, then another woman may breastfeed for him.

Let the one who has abundance spend from his abundance, and the one whose provision is restricted should spend from what Allah has given him. Allah does not burden a soul beyond what He has given it. Allah will bring ease after hardship.
How many a town rebelled against the command of its Lord and His messengers, so We called it to a severe account and punished it with a terrible punishment.
It tasted the consequence of its conduct, and the outcome of its affair was loss.
Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment. So be mindful of Allah, O people of understanding who believe. T ruly, Allah has sent down to you a reminder,

a Messenger who recites to you the clear signs of Allah, bringing those who believe and do what is right out from darkness into light. Whoever believes in Allah and does what is right, He will admit him into gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining th ere forever. Allah has made his provision excellent.

Allah is the One who created seven heavens and from the earth the like of them. The command descends among them so that you may know that Allah has power over everything and that Allah has encompassed everything in knowledge.

surahs.name: At-Taḥrīm (Prohibition)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: O Prophet, why do you forbid what Allah has made lawful for you, seeking to please your wives? Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.
Allah has already set for you the way to release yourselves from your oaths. Allah is your Guardian, and He is the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.

When the Prophet confided a matter to one of his wives, and she disclosed it, and Allah made it known to him, he informed part of it and overlooked part. When he told her of it, she said, “Who informed you of this?” He said, “The All-Knowing, the All-Aware informed me.”

If you both turn to Allah in repentance, your hearts have inclined. But if you support one another against him, then Allah is his Protector, and Jibrīl, and the righteous among the believers, and after that the angels are his support.
If he were to divorce you, his Lord may replace you with wives better than you, submissive, believing, obedient, constantly returning, worshipping, fasting, previously married and virgins.

O you who believe, protect yourselves and your families from a Fire whose fuel is people and stones, over it are angels, stern and severe. They do not disobey Allah in what He commands them, and they do exactly what they are ordered.
O you who disbelieve, do not make excuses today. You are only being repaid for what you used to do.

O you who believe, turn to Allah in sincere repentance. Perhaps your Lord will remove your sins from you and admit you into gardens beneath which rivers flow, on the Day when Allah will not disgrace the Prophet and those who believe with him. Their light will run before them and on their right. They will say, “Our Lord, complete our light for us and forgive us. You have power over everything.”
O Prophet, strive against the disbelievers and the hypocrites, and be firm with them. Their refuge is Hell, and what a terrible destination.

Allah sets forth the example of the wife of Nūḥ and the wife of Lūṭ for those who disbelieve. They were under two of Our righteous servants, yet they betrayed them, but they could not avail them at all against Allah. It was said, “Enter the Fire with those who enter.”

And Allah sets forth the example of the wife of Firʿawn for those who believe, when she said, “My Lord, build for me with You a house in Paradise, and save me from Firʿawn and his deeds, and save me from the wrongdoing people.”
And Maryam, daughter of ʿImrān, who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her from Our spirit, and she affirmed the words of her Lord and His Books, and she was among the devoted.

surahs.name: Al-Mulk (The Dominion)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Blessed is the One in whose hand is all dominion, and He has power over everything.
He created death and life to test you as to which of you is best in action. He is the All-Mighty, the Most-Forgiving.
He created seven heavens layered one above another. You will not see any flaw in the creation of the Most-Merciful. Look again. Do you see any imperfection?
Then look again and again. Your sight will return to you humbled and worn out.
We have adorned the nearest heaven with lamps, and made them as projectiles against the devils, and We have prepared for them the punishment of the Blaze.
For those who disbelieve in their Lord is the punishment of Hell, and what a dreadful destination.
When they are thrown into it, they will hear it roaring as it boils,
almost bursting with rage. Every time a group is cast into it, its keepers will ask them, “Did no warner come to you?”
They will say, “Y es, a warner did come to us, but we denied and said, ‘ Allah has not sent down anything. You are only in great error.’”
And they will say, “If only we had listened or used our reason, we would not be among the people of the Blaze.”
So they will admit their sin. Away with the people of the Blaze.
Indeed, those who fear their Lord unseen will have forgiveness and a great reward.
Whether you keep your speech secret or speak openly, He knows what lies within the hearts.
Does He who created not know? He is the All-Subtle, the All-Aware.
He is the One who made the earth manageable for you, so walk in its paths and eat from His provision. T o Him is the resurrection.
Do you feel secure from the One who is above the heaven that He will not cause the earth to swallow you while it trembles?
Or do you feel secure from the One who is above the heaven that He will not send against you a storm of stones? Then you will know how My warning was.
Those before them also denied, and how severe was My punishment.
Have they not seen the birds above them, spreading and folding their wings? None holds them except the Most-Merciful. He sees everything.
Who is it that could be an army for you, helping you besides the Most- Merciful? The disbelievers are only in delusion.
Who is it that could provide for you if He were to withhold His provision? Y et they persist in defiance and aversion.
Is one who walks with his face fallen forward more guided, or one who walks upright on a straight path?
Say, He is the One who brought you into being, and gave you hearing, sight, and hearts. Little thanks you give.
Say, He is the One who spread you throughout the earth, and to Him you will be gathered.
They say, “When is this promise, if you are truthful?”
Say, The knowledge is only with Allah. I am only a clear warner.
But when they see it drawing near, the faces of those who disbelieve will be distressed, and it will be said, “This is what you used to call for.”
Say, Have you considered: whether Allah destroys me and those with me, or shows mercy to us, who will protect the disbelievers from a painful punishment?
Say, He is the Most-Merciful. We believe in Him and place our trust in Him. You will soon know who is in clear error.
Say, Have you considered: if your water were to sink deep into the earth, who could bring you flowing water?

surahs.name: Al-Qalam (The Pen)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Nūn. By the pen and what they write,
by the favour of your Lord, you are not mad.
You will surely have a reward that never ends.
And you are truly upon an immense character.
You will see, and they will see,
which of you is afflicted.
Your Lord knows best who has strayed from His way, and He knows best who is guided.
So do not yield to those who deny.
They wish that you would soften, so they would soften.
Do not obey every worthless swearer,
a slanderer, going about with gossip,
withholding good, transgressing, sinful,
harsh, and after all that, of doubtful lineage,
simply because he has wealth and sons.
When Our signs are recited to him, he says, “Stories of the former people.”
We will brand him upon the snout.
We tested them as We tested the people of the garden, when they swore that they would harvest it in the morning,
without making any exception.
So a calamity from your Lord passed over it while they slept,
and it became like a field stripped bare.
They called out to one another at dawn,
“Go early to your crop if you are going to harvest.”
So they went off, speaking in low tones,
“No poor person must enter it upon you today.”
They set out early, convinced they had power over it.
But when they saw it, they said, “Surely we have lost our way.”
“Rather, we have been deprived.”
The most balanced among them said, “Did I not tell you to glorify Allah?”
They said, “Glory be to our Lord. We were indeed wrongdoers.”
Then they turned against one another, blaming each other.
They said, “W oe to us. We were the ones who overstepped.”
Perhaps our Lord will give us something better than it in its place. We turn to our Lord in hope.”
Such is the punishment. But the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if only they knew.
For those who are mindful of Allah are gardens of delight with their Lord.
Should We treat those who submit like those who are criminals?
What is the matter with you? How do you judge?
Or do you have a book in which you study,
that you will have whatever you choose?
Or do you have binding oaths from Us, lasting until the Day of Resurrection, that you will have whatever you decide?
Ask them which of them will guarantee that.
Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they are truthful.
On the Day when the shin will be uncovered and they will be called to prostrate, but they will not be able,
their eyes lowered, humiliation covering them. They used to be called to prostrate while they were sound.
So leave Me with whoever denies this message. We will draw them on gradually from where they do not realise.
I grant them respite. My plan is firm.
Or do you ask them for reward, so that they are burdened with debt?
Or do they have knowledge of the unseen, and so they write it down?
So be patient for the judgement of your Lord, and do not be like the companion of the fish when he called out while he was distressed.
Had a favour from his Lord not reached him, he would have been cast upon the bare shore while he was blamed.
But his Lord chose him and made him among the righteous.
Those who disbelieve almost strike you down with their eyes when they hear the reminder, and they say, “He is surely mad.”
But it is nothing except a reminder for all worlds.

surahs.name: Al-Ḥāqqah (The Inevitable Reality)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: The Inevitable Reality.
What is the Inevitable Reality?
And what will make you understand what the Inevitable Reality is?
Thamūd and ʿĀd denied the Striking Calamity.
As for Thamūd, they were destroyed by an overwhelming blast.
And as for ʿĀd, they were destroyed by a furious, raging wind,
which He unleashed against them for seven nights and eight days in succession. You would have seen the people fallen there as though they were hollow trunks of palm trees.
Do you see any of them remaining?
Firʿawn, those before him, and the overturned towns all committed wrongdoing.
They disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so He seized them with a crushing grip.
When the water overflowed, We carried you in the floating vessel,
so that We might make it a reminder for you, and that attentive ears might retain it.
Then when the T rumpet is blown with a single blast,
and the earth and the mountains are lifted up and crushed with one crushing,
on that Day the Event will occur.
The sky will split apart, for that Day it will be fragile.
The angels will be at its edges, and eight will carry the Throne of your Lord above them that Day.
That Day you will be presented. None of your secrets will be hidden.
As for the one who is given his record in his right hand, he will say, “Here, read my record.
I was certain that I would meet my account.”
So he will be in a pleasant life,
in a lofty garden,
its fruits hanging low within reach.
“Eat and drink with satisfaction for what you sent ahead in the past days.”
But as for the one who is given his record in his left hand, he will say, “If only I had not been given my record,
and had not known what my account was.
If only it had been the end.
My wealth has not benefited me.
My authority has left me.”
“Seize him and shackle him.”
Then burn him in the blazing Fire.
Then bind him in a chain whose length is seventy cubits.
For he did not believe in Allah, the Most-Great,
nor did he urge the feeding of the poor.
So today he has no close friend here,
nor any food except from foul discharge,
which none will eat except the sinners.
So I swear by what you see,
and what you do not see,
that it is truly the word of a noble Messenger.
It is not the word of a poet. Little do you believe.
Nor is it the word of a fortune teller. Little do you reflect.
It is a revelation sent down from the Lord of all worlds.
If he had fabricated any sayings against Us,
We would have seized him by the right hand,
then cut from him the life vein,
and none of you could have shielded him.
It is indeed a reminder for those who are mindful of Allah.
We know that among you are those who deny.
It will be a source of regret for the disbelievers.
It is the certain truth.
So glorify the name of your Lord, the Most-Great.

surahs.name: Al-Maʿārij (The Ascending Ways)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: A questioner asked about a punishment bound to fall,
upon the disbelievers. None can repel it,
from Allah, Lord of the ways of ascent.
The angels and the Spirit ascend to Him in a Day whose measure is fifty thousand years.
So be patient with a dignified patience.
They see it as distant,
but We see it as near.
On the Day when the sky will be like molten metal,
and the mountains like fluffed wool,
and no close friend will ask about a close friend,
though they will be made to see one another. The criminal will wish he could ransom himself from the punishment of that Day with his children,
his wife and his brother,
his closest kin who sheltered him,
and everyone on earth altogether, if only it could save him.
No. It is a raging flame,
stripping away the skin of the head.
It calls out to whoever turned his back and walked away,
and gathered and hoarded.
Indeed, the human being was created anxious,
when harm touches him, he is distressed,
and when good reaches him, he withholds,
except those devoted to prayer,
who are constant in their prayer,
and those in whose wealth there is a recognised share,
for the one who asks and the one deprived,
and those who affirm the Day of Judgement,
and those who are fearful of the punishment of their Lord,
for the punishment of their Lord is not something one feels secure from,
and those who guard their chastity,
except from their spouses or those whom their right hands possess, for then they are free from blame.
But whoever seeks beyond that, then they are the ones who cross the limits.
And those who honour their trusts and their pledges,
and those who stand firm in their testimonies,
and those who guard their prayer,
they will be in gardens, honoured.
So what is the matter with those who disbelieve, rushing towards you,
from the right and from the left, in groups?
Does each of them hope to be admitted into a garden of bliss?
No. We created them from what they know.
So I swear by the Lord of the east and the west, We are fully able
to replace them with better than them, and none can outstrip Us.
So leave them to plunge and play until they meet their Day which they are promised,
the Day they will come out from the graves swiftly, as if racing towards a goal,
their eyes lowered, humiliation covering them. That is the Day which they were promised.

surahs.name: Nūḥ (Noah)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: We sent Nūḥ to his people: “W arn your people before a painful punishment comes to them.”
He said, “O My people, I am a clear warner to you.
W orship Allah, be mindful of Him, and obey me.
He will forgive you some of your sins and grant you respite until an appointed time. When the time set by Allah comes, it will not be delayed, if only you knew.”
He said, “My Lord, I have called my people night and day,
but my call has only made them flee further.
Every time I called them so that You might forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears, covered themselves with their garments, persisted, and were arrogant with great arrogance.
Then I called them openly.
Then I spoke to them publicly and spoke to them privately.
I said, ‘Seek forgiveness from your Lord. He is Ever-Forgiving.
He will send down rain upon you in abundance,
and increase you in wealth and children, and provide for you gardens, and provide for you rivers.
What is the matter with you that you do not hold hope in the majesty of Allah,
when He created you in stages?
Have you not seen how Allah created seven heavens layered one above another,
and placed the moon within them as a light, and made the sun a shining lamp?
Allah caused you to grow from the earth like a growth,
then He will return you to it, and bring you out again.
Allah made the earth a wide expanse for you,
so that you may travel along its broad paths.”
Nūḥ said, “My Lord, they have disobeyed me and followed those whose wealth and children only increase them in loss.
They have plotted a mighty plot.
They said, ‘Do not abandon your gods, and do not abandon W add, nor Suwāʿ, nor Y aghūth, nor Y aʿūq, nor Nasr.’
They have led many astray. And do not increase the wrongdoers except in misguidance.”
Because of their sins they were drowned and made to enter a Fire, and they found no helpers besides Allah.
And Nūḥ said, “My Lord, do not leave upon the earth any of the disbelievers.
If You leave them, they will mislead Your servants and give birth only to corrupt, ungrateful offspring.
My Lord, forgive me and my parents, and whoever enters my house as a believer, and the believing men and believing women. And do not increase the wrongdoers except in ruin.”

surahs.name: Al-Jinn (The Jinn)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Say, it has been revealed to me that a group of the jinn listened and said, “We have heard a remarkable Qur’an.
It guides  to what is right, so we have believed in it, and we will never associate anyone with our Lord.
He, exalted is the majesty of our Lord, has not taken a wife nor a child.
And the foolish among us used to speak outrageous lies about Allah.
We had assumed that neither humans nor jinn would ever speak lies about Allah.
There were men among humans who sought refuge with men among the jinn, and so they only increased them in burden.
They thought, just as you thought, that Allah would never send anyone.
And we tried to reach the heaven, but we found it filled with strong guards and flaming fires.
We used to sit there in places to listen, but whoever listens now finds a flaming fire lying in wait for him.
We do not know whether harm is intended for those on earth, or whether their Lord intends guidance for them.
Among us are the righteous, and among us are others less than that. We follow different paths.
We realised that we could never escape Allah on earth, nor could we flee from Him.
When we heard the guidance, we believed in it. Whoever believes in his Lord need not fear loss nor injustice.
Among us are those who submit, and among us are those who deviate. Whoever submits has chosen the right path.
But those who deviate will be fuel for Hell.”
And if they had remained upright on the way, We would have given them abundant water,
to test them through it. Whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, He will lead him into a severe punishment.
And the mosques belong to Allah, so do not call upon anyone alongside Allah.
When the servant of Allah stood calling upon Him, they almost crowded over him.
Say, I call only upon my Lord, and I do not associate anyone with Him.
Say, I do not control harm or guidance for you.
Say, no one can protect me from Allah, and I will never find any refuge besides Him.
I only convey from Allah and His messages. Whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger will have the Fire of Hell, remaining in it forever.
Until, when  they see what they are promised, they will know who is weaker in support and fewer in number.
Say, I do not know whether what you are promised is near, or whether my Lord will appoint for it a distant term.
He is the Knower of the unseen, and He does not reveal His unseen to anyone,
except to a messenger whom He has chosen. Then He places before him and behind him watchers,
so that He may know that they have delivered the messages of their Lord. He encompasses all that is with them and has counted everything in exact number.

surahs.name: Al-Muzzammil (The One Wrapped Up)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: O you wrapped in garments,
stand in prayer at night, except a little,
half of it, or reduce from it a little,
or add to it, and recite the Qur’an with measured recitation.
We will cast upon you a weighty word.
The rising at night is stronger in discipline and more upright in speech.
Indeed, you have during the day prolonged engagement.
Remember the name of your Lord and devote yourself to Him with full devotion.
Lord of the east and the west. There is no god except Him, so take Him as your Guardian.
Be patient over what they say, and withdraw from them with a dignified withdrawal.
Leave Me with those who deny and live in comfort, and give them a little time.
With Us are heavy chains and a blazing Fire,
and food that chokes, and a painful punishment,
on the Day when the earth and the mountains will shake, and the mountains will become a heap of shifting sand.
We have sent to you a Messenger as a witness over you, just as We sent to Firʿawn a messenger.
But Firʿawn disobeyed the messenger, so We seized him with a crushing grip.
How then will you guard yourselves, if you disbelieve, against a Day that will make children’s hair turn white?
The sky will be split apart by it. His promise will be fulfilled.
This is a reminder. So whoever wills, let him take a path to his Lord.

Your Lord knows that you stand nearly two-thirds of the night, or half of it, or a third of it, along with a group of those with you. Allah measures the night and the day. He knows that you will not be able to sustain it, so He turned to you in mercy. So recite from the Qur’an what is easy for you. He knows that among you are those who are ill, others travelling through the earth seeking from the bounty of Allah, and others fighting in the cause of Allah. So recite from it what is easy for you, uphold the prayer, give zakah, and lend to Allah a good loan. Whatever good you send ahead for yourselves, you will find it with Allah, better and greater in reward. Seek forgiveness from Allah. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful.

surahs.name: Al-Muddaththir (The One Covered)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: O you wrapped up,
rise and warn,
and magnify your Lord,
purify your garments,
and keep away from impurity,
and do not give expecting more in return,
and be patient for the sake of your Lord.
When the T rumpet is blown,
that Day will be a difficult Day,
not easy for the disbelievers.
Leave Me with the one I created alone,
and to whom I gave extended wealth,
and sons present before him,
and made things smooth for him,
yet he hopes that I will give him more.
No. He has been stubborn towards Our signs.
I will burden him with a steep ascent.
He thought and plotted,
so ruin to him for how he plotted,
again, ruin to him for how he plotted,
then he looked,
then he frowned and scowled,
then he turned his back and was arrogant,
and said, “This is nothing but magic handed down.
This is nothing but the speech of a human.”
I will cast him into Saqar.
And what will make you know what Saqar is?
It leaves nothing and spares nothing,
scorching the skin.
Over it are nineteen.

We have appointed only angels as keepers of the Fire, and We have made their number only as a test for those who disbelieve, so that those who were given the Book may be certain, and those who believe may increase in faith, and that those who were given the Book and the believers may not doubt, and that those in whose hearts is disease and the disbelievers may say, “What does Allah intend by this example?” Thus Allah leaves astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. None knows the forces of your Lor d except Him. It is nothing but a reminder for humanity.
No. By the moon,
and the night as it retreats,
and the morning as it brightens,
it is surely one of the greatest warnings,
a warning to humanity,
to whoever among you wishes to move forward or stay behind.
Every soul is held in pledge for what it has earned,
except the people of the right,
in gardens, asking one another,
about the criminals,
“What led you into Saqar?”
They will say, “We were not among those who prayed,
nor did we feed the poor,
and we used to plunge into falsehood with those who plunged,
and we used to deny the Day of Judgement,
until certainty came to us.”
So the intercession of those who intercede will not benefit them.
What is the matter with them that they turn away from the reminder,
as though they were startled donkeys,
fleeing from a lion?
Rather, each of them wants pages spread out before him.
No. They do not fear the Hereafter.
No. It is truly a reminder.
So whoever wills may remember it.
But they will not remember unless Allah wills. He is worthy of mindfulness and worthy of forgiveness.

surahs.name: Al-Qiyāmah (The Resurrection)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: I swear by the Day of Resurrection,
and I swear by the self-reproaching soul.
Does the human being think We will not assemble his bones?
Y es indeed. We are able even to shape his fingertips.
But the human being wants to continue in wrongdoing ahead of him.
He asks, “When is the Day of Resurrection?”
But when the sight is dazzled,
and the moon is eclipsed,
and the sun and the moon are brought together,
that Day the human being will say, “Where is the escape?”
No. There is no refuge.
T o your Lord that Day is the final return.
The human being will be informed that Day of what he sent ahead and what he left behind.
Rather, the human being is a witness against himself,
even if he puts forward his excuses.
Do not move your tongue with it to hasten it.
It is upon Us to gather it and to recite it.
So when We have recited it, follow its recitation.
Then upon Us is its clarification.
No. You love what is immediate,
and you leave the Hereafter.
Faces that Day will be radiant,
looking towards their Lord,
and faces that Day will be gloomy,
expecting that a crushing blow will be dealt to them.
No. When it reaches the collarbone,
and it is said, “Who can heal him?”
and he realises it is the separation,
and the leg is wrapped around the leg,
to your Lord that Day is the driving.
He neither affirmed nor prayed,
but denied and turned away,
then went to his family walking proudly.
Ruin is nearer to you, and nearer,
Again, ruin is nearer to you, and nearer.
Does the human being think he will be left neglected?
W as he not a drop of fluid emitted?
Then he was a clinging form, and He created and shaped him,
and made from him the two kinds, male and female.
Is He not able to bring the dead to life?

surahs.name: Al-Insān (The Human Being)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Has there not come upon the human being a stretch of time when he was nothing worth mentioning?
We created the human being from a blended drop, testing him, so We gave him hearing and sight.
We showed him the way, whether he is grateful or ungrateful.
For the disbelievers We have prepared chains, shackles, and a blazing Fire.
Indeed, the righteous will drink from a cup mixed with camphor,
a spring from which the servants of Allah will drink, making it flow as they wish.
They fulfil their vows and fear a Day whose harm will be widespread.
They give food, out of love for Him, to the poor, the orphan, and the captive,
saying, “We feed you only for the sake of Allah. We seek from you neither reward nor thanks.
We fear from our Lord a Day, grim and distressing.”
So Allah will protect them from the harm of that Day and grant them brightness and joy.
He will reward them for their patience with a garden and silk,
reclining within it on couches. They will not see there scorching heat or bitter cold.
Its shade will be close over them, and its fruits will hang low within reach.
V essels of silver will circulate among them, and cups of crystal,
crystal clear, made of silver, measured precisely.
They will be given to drink a cup mixed with ginger,
from a spring within it called Salsabīl.
Young attendants will move around them. When you see them, you would think them scattered pearls.
When you look there, you will see delight and a vast kingdom.
They will wear garments of fine green silk and rich brocade, and they will be adorned with bracelets of silver. Their Lord will give them a pure drink.
This is your reward, and your effort has been appreciated.
Indeed, We have sent down the Qur’an to you in stages.
So be patient for the judgement of your Lord, and do not obey any sinner or ungrateful one among them.
Remember the name of your Lord morning and evening,
and during part of the night prostrate to Him and glorify Him through long hours of the night.
These people love what is immediate and leave behind them a heavy Day.
We created them and strengthened their build. If We will, We can replace them with others like them.
This is a reminder. So whoever wills may take a path to his Lord.
But you do not will unless Allah Wills. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All- Wise.
He admits whom He wills into His mercy. As for the wrongdoers, He has prepared for them a painful punishment.

surahs.name: Al-Mursalāt (Those Sent Forth)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By those sent forth one after another,
driving strongly,
spreading widely,
separating decisively,
delivering a reminder,
as an excuse or a warning,
what you are promised will surely occur.
When the stars are wiped away,
when the sky is split open,
when the mountains are blown away,
and when the messengers are gathered at their appointed time.
For what Day has it been delayed?
For the Day of Decision.
And what will make you understand what the Day of Decision is?
W oe that Day to those who deny.
Did We not destroy the former people?
Then We will follow them with the later ones.
Thus We deal with the criminals.
W oe that Day to those who deny.
Did We not create you from a humble fluid,
and place it in a secure resting place,
for a known measure?
We determined, and how excellent are We in determining.
W oe that Day to those who deny.
Did We not make the earth a holding place,
for the living and the dead,
and place upon it towering mountains, and give you fresh water to drink?
W oe that Day to those who deny.
“Go to what you used to deny.
Go to a shadow of three columns,
neither cool nor of any use against the flame.
It throws sparks like great castles,
as though they were yellow camels.”
W oe that Day to those who deny.
This is a Day when they will not speak,
nor will they be allowed to make excuses.
W oe that Day to those who deny.
This is the Day of Decision. We have gathered you and the former people.
If you have a plan, then plan against Me.
W oe that Day to those who deny.
Indeed, the mindful will be among shade and springs,
and fruits from whatever they desire.
“Eat and drink with satisfaction for what you used to do.”
Thus We reward those who do good.
W oe that Day to those who deny.
“Eat and enjoy yourselves a little. You are criminals.”
W oe that Day to those who deny.
When it is said to them, “Bow down,” they do not bow.
W oe that Day to those who deny.
In what message after this will they believe?

surahs.name: An-Nabaʾ (The Great News)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: What are they asking one another about?
About the great news,
the one over which they differ.
No. They will soon know.
Then again, no. They will soon know.
Have We not made the earth a resting place,
and the mountains as stabilising anchors,
and created you in pairs,
and made your sleep for rest,
and made the night as a covering,
and made the day for livelihood,
and built above you seven strong realms,
and placed within them a blazing lamp,
and sent down from the rain clouds pouring water,
so that We bring forth with it grain and vegetation,
and dense, entwined gardens.
Indeed, the Day of Decision is an appointed time,
the Day the T rumpet is blown and you come in multitudes,
and the sky is opened and becomes gateways,
and the mountains are set in motion and become like a mirage.
Indeed, Hell lies in waiting,
a return place for the transgressors,
remaining in it for ages upon ages,
tasting in it neither coolness nor drink,
except scalding water and a filthy fluid,
a fitting recompense.
They never expected a reckoning,
and they denied Our signs outright.
But We have recorded everything in writing.
So taste. We will only increase you in punishment.
Indeed, for those mindful of Allah there is triumph,
gardens and grapevines,
and full-bosomed companions of equal age,
and an overflowing cup.
They will hear in it no empty talk and no lies,
a reward from your Lord, a generous gift in due measure,
Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything between them, the Most-Merciful. They will have no authority to address Him.
On the Day when the Spirit and the angels stand in rows, none will speak except the one permitted by the Most -Merciful, and he will speak what is right.
That is the T rue Day. So whoever wishes may take a path to his Lord.
We have warned you of a near punishment, the Day a person will look at what his hands have sent ahead, and the disbeliever will say, “If only I were dust.”

surahs.name: An-Nāziʿāt (Those Who Pull Out)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By those who tear out forcefully,
and those who draw out gently,
and those who glide swiftly,
racing ahead with urgency,
managing the affair as commanded,
on the Day the violent blast strikes,
followed by the second blast,
hearts that Day will tremble,
their eyes lowered in fear.
They say, “ Are we really to be restored to our former state,
even after we have become crumbled bones?”
They say, “That would be a losing return.”
Y et it will only be a single shout,
and at once they will be on the open plain.
Has the story of Mūsā reached you?
When his Lord called to him in the sacred valley of Ṭuwā,
“Go to Firʿawn. He has transgressed.
Say, ‘W ould you be willing to purify yourself,
and let me guide you to your Lord so that you may stand in awe of Him?’”
Then he showed him the greatest sign.
But he denied and disobeyed.
Then he turned away, striving against the truth.
He gathered his people and called out,
and said, “I am your lord, the highest.”
So Allah seized him with the punishment of the Hereafter and the worldly life.
In that is surely a lesson for whoever stands in awe.
Are you harder to create, or the sky? He built it,
raised its height and perfected it,
darkened its night and brought out its daylight,
and after that He spread out the earth,
brought forth from it its water and its pasture,
and set the mountains firmly,
as provision for you and your livestock.
But when the overwhelming calamity comes,
the Day a person will remember what he strove for,
and Hell is brought into view for whoever sees,
then as for the one who transgressed
and preferred the life of this world,
Hell will be his refuge.
But as for the one who feared standing before his Lord and restrained himself from desire,
Paradise will be his refuge.
They ask you about the Hour, when will it arrive?
What have you to do with its mention?
Its final knowledge rests with your Lord.
You are only a warner for whoever fears it.
The Day they see it, it will seem as though they had stayed only an evening or its morning.

surahs.name: ʿAbasa (He Frowned)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: He frowned and turned away,
because the blind man came to him.
But what would make you know? Perhaps he might grow in purity,
or take heed, and the reminder would benefit him.
As for the one who thinks himself self-sufficient,
you gave him your attention,
though you are not accountable if he does not purify himself.
But as for the one who came to you striving,
while he feared,
you were distracted from him.
No. This is a reminder.
So whoever wishes may take it to heart.
It is in honoured pages,
raised high and purified,
in the hands of scribes,
noble and devoted.
Ruined is the human being, how ungrateful he is.
From what did He create him?
From a drop of fluid He created him and proportioned him,
then made the path easy for him,
then caused him to die and placed him in a grave,
then, when He wills, He will raise him again.
No. He has not yet fulfilled what He commanded him.
Let the human being look at his food:
how We pour down water abundantly,
then split the earth open,
and grow within it grain,
and grapes and fresh produce,
and olives and date palms,
and lush gardens,
and fruit and fodder,
provision for you and your livestock.
But when the Deafening Blast comes,
the Day a man will flee from his brother,
and from his mother and his father,
and from his wife and his children,
that Day each person will have enough concern of his own.
Faces that Day will be radiant,
laughing, rejoicing.
And faces that Day will be covered in dust,
overshadowed by darkness.
Those are the disbelievers, the openly defiant.

surahs.name: At-Takwīr (The Folding Up)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: When the sun is folded up,
and the stars fall, losing their light,
and the mountains are set in motion,
and heavily pregnant camels are left untended,
and wild creatures are gathered together,
and the seas are set ablaze,
and the souls are paired,
and the girl buried alive is asked
for what crime she was killed,
and the records are laid open,
and the sky is stripped away,
and Hell is set aflame,
and Paradise is brought near,
each soul will know what it has put forward.
So I swear by the retreating stars,
moving swiftly and disappearing,
and by the night as it deepens,
and by the dawn as it breathes,
this is truly the word of a noble messenger,
endowed with power, secure in rank with the Lord of the Throne,
obeyed there and trustworthy.
Your companion is not mad.
He certainly saw him on the clear horizon.
And he is not withholding what is unseen.
Nor is it the word of a cursed devil.
So where are you heading?
It is nothing but a reminder to all worlds,
for whoever among you wishes to remain upright.
But you will not will unless Allah, Lord of all worlds, Wills.

surahs.name: Al-Infiṭār (The Splitting Open)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: When the sky is split open,
and the stars are scattered,
and the seas burst forth,
and the graves are overturned,
each soul will know what it sent ahead and what it left behind.
O human being, what has deceived you about your Generous Lord,
who created you, shaped you, and formed you in balance,
in whatever form He willed, He assembled you?
Y et you deny the Day of Judgement.
Indeed, over you are watchers,
noble recorders,
who know all that you do.
Indeed, the righteous will be in bliss,
and the wicked will be in blazing fire.
They will enter it on the Day of Judgement,
and they will not be absent from it.
What will make you understand what the Day of Judgement is?
Again, what will make you understand what the Day of Judgement is?
It is the Day when no soul will possess anything for another soul, and the command that Day belongs entirely to Allah.

surahs.name: Al-Muṭaffifīn (The Defrauders)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: W oe to the defrauders,
those who, when they take measure from people, take in full,
but when they give by measure or weight, they reduce.
Do they not think they will be raised,
for a great Day,
the Day when people will stand before the Lord of all worlds?
No. The record of the wicked is in Sijjīn.
And what will make you understand what Sijjīn is?
A written record.
W oe that Day to the deniers,
those who deny the Day of Judgement.
None deny it except every sinful aggressor.
When Our signs are recited to him, he says, “T ales of the former peoples.”
No. Rather, what they used to earn has rusted over their hearts.
No. That Day they will be veiled from their Lord.
Then they will enter the blazing fire.
Then it will be said, “This is what you used to deny.”
No. The record of the righteous is in ʿIlliyyīn.
And what will make you understand what ʿIlliyyīn is?
A written record,
witnessed by those brought near.
Indeed, the righteous will be in bliss,
on couches, looking on.
You will recognise on their faces the radiance of delight.
They will be given to drink pure sealed nectar,
sealed with musk. For that let those who compete, compete.
And its mixture will be from T asnīm,
a spring from which those brought near drink.
Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed,
and when they passed by them, they would wink at one another,
and when they returned to their people, they returned amused,
and when they saw them, they said, “These people are surely astray.”
Y et they were not sent as watchers over them.
So today those who believed are laughing at the disbelievers,
on couches, looking on.
Have the disbelievers been repaid for what they used to do?

surahs.name: Al-Inshiqāq (The Splitting Apart)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: When the sky is split apart,
listening to its Lord, as it must,
and when the earth is stretched out,
and casts out what is within it and becomes empty,
listening to its Lord, as it must,
O human being, you are striving towards your Lord with effort, and you will meet Him.
Then as for the one who is given his record in his right hand,
he will be judged with an easy reckoning,
and will return to his people joyful.
But as for the one who is given his record behind his back,
he will cry out for destruction,
and will enter a blazing fire.
He had once been joyful among his people,
thinking he would never return.
No. His Lord was always watching him.
So I swear by the twilight glow,
and by the night and what it gathers,
and by the moon when it becomes full,
you will surely move from stage to stage.
So what is wrong with them that they do not believe,
and when the Qur’an is recited to them, they do not prostrate?
Rather, those who disbelieve deny.
And Allah knows best what they conceal.
So give them news of a painful punishment,
except for those who believe and do what is right. For them is a reward without end.

surahs.name: Al-Burūj (The Constellations)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the sky filled with constellations,
and by the Promised Day,
and by the witness and what is witnessed,
ruined were the people of the trench,
of the fire fed with fuel,
when they sat beside it,
watching what they were doing to the believers.
They resented them only because they believed in Allah, the Al l-Mighty, the One worthy of All-Praise,
to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is witness over all things.
Those who persecuted believing men and believing women and then did not repent will have the punishment of Hell and the punishment of burning.
Indeed, those who believe and do what is right will have gardens beneath which rivers flow. That is the great triumph.
Indeed, the seizure of your Lord is severe.
He originates and He restores,
and He is the Most-Forgiving, the Most-Loving,
Lord of the Throne, the Most-Glorious,
Doer of whatever He wills.
Has the story reached you of the forces,
of Firʿawn and Thamūd?
Y et those who disbelieve persist in denial,
while Allah surrounds them from behind.
Rather, it is a glorious Qur’an,
in a tablet preserved.

surahs.name: Aṭ-Ṭāriq (The Night Visitor)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the sky and the night visitor,
and what will make you understand what the night visitor is?
The piercing star.
There is no soul without a watcher over it.
So let the human being consider from what he was created.
He was created from a fluid that surges forth,
emerging from between the backbone and the ribs.
Indeed, He is fully able to bring him back,
on the Day when secrets are laid bare,
and he will have no strength and no helper.
By the sky that returns again and again,
and by the earth that splits open,
this is truly a decisive word,
and it is not a joke.
They are certainly plotting a plan,
and I am planning a plan.
So grant the disbelievers respite, leave them for a short while.

surahs.name: Al-Aʿlā (The Most-High)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Glorify the name of your Lord, the Most-High,
who created and proportioned,
who determined and guided,
who brings out pasture,
then makes it dry and darkened.
We will make you recite, and you will not forget,
except what Allah wills. He knows what is open and what is hidden.
And We will ease you towards what is easiest.
So remind, if the reminder benefits.
The one who stands in awe will take heed,
but the most wretched will turn away,
the one who will enter the greatest fire,
where he will neither die nor live.
Successful indeed is the one who purifies himself,
and remembers the name of his Lord and prays.
Y et you prefer the life of this world,
while the Hereafter is better and more lasting.
This is certainly in the earlier scriptures,
the scriptures of Ibrāhīm and Mūsā.
Has the news reached you of the Overwhelming Event?
Faces that Day will be humbled,
labouring, exhausted,
entering a blazing fire,
given drink from a scalding spring.
They will have no food except thorny plants,
which neither nourish nor satisfy hunger.
Other faces that Day will be radiant,
pleased with their striving,
in a lofty garden,
where they will hear no empty speech.
Within it, there is a flowing spring.
There are raised couches,
and cups set out,
and cushions arranged in rows,
and carpets spread wide.
Do they not look at the camels, how they are created,
and at the sky, how it is raised,
and at the mountains, how they are set firmly,
and at the earth, how it is laid out?
So remind. You are only a reminder.
You are not over them as a controller.
But whoever turns away and disbelieves,
Allah will punish him with the greatest punishment.
T o Us is their return,
then upon Us is their reckoning.

surahs.name: Al-Fajr (The Dawn)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the dawn,
and by the ten nights,
and by the even and the odd,
and by the night as it passes,
is there not in that an oath for one who understands?
Have you not seen how your Lord dealt with ʿĀd,
Iram of the towering pillars,
the like of whom had never been created in the land,
and Thamūd, who carved out the rock in the valley,
and Firʿawn, master of the stakes?
They all exceeded limits in the land,
and spread corruption within it.
So your Lord poured upon them a scourge of punishment.
Indeed, your Lord is ever watchful.
As for the human being, when his Lord tests him by granting him honour and favour, he says, “My Lord has honoured me.”
But when He tests him by restricting his provision, he says, “My Lord has humiliated me.”
No. Rather, you do not honour the orphan,
nor do you urge one another to feed the poor,
and you consume inheritance greedily,
and you love wealth with intense love.
No. When the earth is crushed again and again,
and your Lord comes, and the angels in rows upon rows,
and Hell is brought near that Day, on that Day the human being will remember, but how will the remembrance benefit him?
He will say, “If only I had sent something ahead for my life.”
That Day none will punish as He punishes,
and none will bind as He binds.
O tranquil soul,
return to your Lord, pleased and pleasing,
enter among My servants,
and enter My Paradise.

surahs.name: Al-Balad (The City)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: I swear by this city,
and you are settled in this city,
and by a father and what he fathered,
We have created the human being in hardship.
Does he think no one has power over him?
He says, “I have spent vast wealth.”
Does he think no one has seen him?
Have We not given him two eyes,
and a tongue and two lips,
and shown him the two paths?
Y et he has not attempted the steep path.
And what will make you understand what the steep path is?
It is freeing a slave,
or feeding on a day of severe hunger,
an orphan of close relation,
or a needy person in distress,
and then being among those who believe and encourage one another to patience and encourage one another to compassion.
Those are the people of the right.
But those who disbelieve in Our signs, they are the people of the left.
Over them will be a sealed fire.

surahs.name: Ash-Shams (The Sun)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the sun and its brightness,
and by the moon when it follows it,
and by the day when it reveals it,
and by the night when it covers it,
and by the sky and the One who built it,
and by the earth and the One who spread it,
and by the soul and the One who formed it,
then inspired it with its wrong and its right,
successful indeed is the one who purifies it,
and failed is the one who corrupts it.
Thamūd denied in their arrogance,
when their most wretched one rose up,
and the messenger of Allah said to them, “Leave the she -camel of Allah and her drinking turn.”
But they denied him and hamstrung her, so their Lord crushed them for their sin and levelled them,
and He does not fear the consequence.

surahs.name: Al-Layl (The Night)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the night as it covers,
and by the day as it appears,
and by the One who created male and female,
your striving is surely diverse.
As for the one who gives and is mindful of Allah,
and affirms the best,
We will ease him towards ease.
But as for the one who withholds and thinks himself self-sufficient,
and denies the best,
We will ease him towards hardship.
And his wealth will not benefit him when he falls.
Indeed, guidance rests with Us,
and indeed, to Us belong the Hereafter and the first life.
So I have warned you of a blazing fire,
none will enter it except the most wretched,
the one who denied and turned away.
But the most mindful will be kept away from it,
the one who gives his wealth to purify himself,
not seeking repayment from anyone for a favour,
but only seeking the face of his Lord, the Most High.
And he will surely be satisfied.

surahs.name: Ad-Ḍuḥā (The Morning Brightness)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the morning brightness,
and by the night when it grows still,
your Lord has not abandoned you, nor has He hated you.
And the Hereafter is better for you than the first life.
Your Lord will soon give to you, and you will be satisfied.
Did He not find you an orphan and give you shelter,
and find you searching and guide you,
and find you in need and enrich you?
So as for the orphan, do not treat them harshly,
and as for the one who asks, do not turn them away harshly,
and as for the favour of your Lord, speak of it.

surahs.name: Ash-Sharḥ (The Expansion)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Did We not expand your chest for you,
and remove from you your burden,
which weighed heavily upon your back,
and raise high for you your mention?
Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
So when you have finished, stand in devotion,
and turn your longing towards your Lord.

surahs.name: At-Tīn (The Fig)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the fig and the olive,
and by Mount Sinai,
and by this secure city,
We certainly created the human being in the best form,
then We returned him to the lowest of the low,
except for those who believe and do what is right. For them is a reward without end.
So what then makes you deny the Judgement?
Is not Allah the most just of judges?

surahs.name: Al-ʿAlaq (The Clot)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Recite in the name of your Lord who created,
created the human being from a clinging clot.
Recite, and your Lord is the Most-Generous,
who taught by the pen,
taught the human being what he did not know.
No. The human being oversteps all limits,
when he sees himself self-sufficient.
Surely to your Lord is the return.
Have you seen the one who prevents
a servant when he prays?
Have you considered if he is upon guidance,
or commands mindfulness?
Have you considered if he denies and turns away?
Does he not know that Allah sees?
No. If he does not stop, We will seize him by the forelock,
a lying, sinful forelock.
So let him call his supporters.
We will call the guards.
No. Do not obey him. Prostrate and draw near.

surahs.name: Al-Qadr (The Decree)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: We sent it down on the Night of Decree.
And what will make you realise what the Night of Decree is?
The Night of Decree is better than a thousand months.
The angels and the Spirit descend in it by permission of their Lord for every matter.
It is peace until the rising of dawn.

surahs.name: Al-Bayyinah (The Clear Proof )
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Those who disbelieved from the People of the Scripture and the associators were not going to desist until the clear proof came to them,
a messenger from Allah reciting purified pages,
within them upright writings.
Those who were given the Scripture did not divide except after the clear proof had come to them.
They were only commanded to worship Allah, sincere to Him in devotion, turning away from all false worship, to uphold prayer and give zakah. That is the upright way.
Indeed, those who disbelieved from the People of the Scripture and the associators will be in the fire of Hell, remaining in it  forever. They are the worst of creation.
Indeed, those who believe and do what is right, they are the best of creation.

Their reward with their Lord is gardens of lasting residence beneath which rivers flow, remaining in them forever. Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. That is for the one who stands in awe of his Lord.

surahs.name: Az-Zalzalah (The Earthquake)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: When the earth is shaken with its violent shaking,
and the earth brings out its burdens,
and the human being says, “What is happening to it?”,
that Day it will report its news,
because your Lord has inspired it.
That Day people will come forward in scattered groups to be shown their deeds.
So whoever does an atom’s weight of good will see it,
and whoever does an atom’s weight of evil will see it.

surahs.name: Al-ʿĀdiyāt (The Charging Horses)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By the chargers that race, panting,
striking sparks as they strike,
launching an attack at dawn,
stirring up dust with it,
and breaking through into the midst of the ranks,
indeed the human being is deeply ungrateful to his Lord,
and he himself is a witness to that,
and he is intense in his love of wealth.
Does he not know that when what is in the graves is brought out,
and what is in the hearts is laid bare,
that their Lord, that Day, will be fully aware of them?

surahs.name: Al-Qāriʿah (The Striking Calamity)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: The Striking Event.
What is the Striking Event?
And what will make you realise what the Striking Event is?
On the Day people will be like scattered moths,
and the mountains will be like carded wool.
Then as for the one whose scales are heavy,
he will be in a pleasant life.
But as for the one whose scales are light,
his refuge will be the abyss.
And what will make you realise what it is?
A blazing fire.

surahs.name: At-Takāthur (Competing for More)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Competing for more distracts you,
until you reach the graves.
No. You will soon know.
Again, No. You will soon know.
No. If you only knew with certain knowledge,
you would surely see the blazing fire.
Then you will surely see it with the eye of certainty.
Then that Day you will certainly be questioned about the blessings.

surahs.name: Al-ʿAṣr (Time)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: By time,
the human being is surely in loss,
except those who believe and do what is right, and encourage one another to truth, and encourage one another to patience.

surahs.name: Al-Humazah (The Slanderer)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: W oe to every slanderer and backbiter,
who gathers wealth and keeps counting it,
thinking that his wealth will make him live forever.
No. He will surely be thrown into the Crusher.
And what will make you realise what the Crusher is?
The fire of Allah, kindled,
which rises over the hearts.
It will be closed over them,
in towering columns.

surahs.name: Al-Fīl (The Elephant)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Have you not seen how your Lord dealt with the companions of the elephant?
Did He not turn their scheme into ruin,
and send against them birds in flocks,
striking them with stones of baked clay,
and make them like eaten straw?

surahs.name: Quraysh
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: For the settled security of Quraysh,
their settled security in the winter and summer journeys,
let them worship the Lord of this House,
who fed them against hunger and gave them safety from fear.

surahs.name: Al-Māʿūn (Small Help)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Have you seen the one who denies the Judgement?
That is the one who pushes away the orphan
and does not encourage feeding the poor.
So woe to those who pray
yet are careless about their prayer,
those who show off
and withhold simple acts of help.

surahs.name: Al-Kawthar (Abundant Good)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Indeed, We have given you abundance.
So pray to your Lord and sacrifice.
Indeed, the one who hates you is the one cut off.

surahs.name: Al-Kāfirūn (The Disbelievers)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Say, O disbelievers,
I do not worship what you worship,
nor do you worship what I worship,
nor will I worship what you have worshipped,
nor will you worship what I worship.
For you is your way, and for me is mine.

surahs.name: An-Naṣr (Divine Help)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: When the help of Allah comes and the victory,
and you see people entering the religion of Allah in crowds,
then glorify your Lord with praise and seek His forgiveness. Surely, He is ever accepting of repentance.

surahs.name: Al-Masad (The Palm Fibre)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Ruined are the hands of Abū Lahab, and ruined is he.
His wealth and what he earned did not benefit him.
He will enter a blazing fire,
and his wife as well, the carrier of firewood,
around her neck will be a rope of twisted palm fibre.

surahs.name: Al-Ikhlāṣ (Sincerity)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Say, He is Allah, One,
Allah, the Absolute.
He does not beget, nor is He born,
and there is nothing equal to Him.

surahs.name: Al-Falaq (The Daybreak)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Say, I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak,
from the evil of what He created,
and from the evil of darkness when it settles,
and from the evil of those who blow into knots,
and from the evil of an envier when he envies.

surahs.name: An-Nās (Mankind)
surahs.basmala: In the name of Allah, the Most-Merciful, the Ever-Merciful.

verses.english: Say, I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind,
the King of mankind,
the One worthy of worship by mankind,
from the evil of the whisperer who withdraws,
who whispers into the hearts of mankind,
from among jinn and mankind. The End All Surahs
